10.1055_s-0041-1738439.rxn_class_summary: Nickel-catalyzed electrochemical reductive cyclizative carboxylation of alkene-tethered carbamoyl chlorides with atmospheric CO2 to give 2-oxoindolin-3-ylacetic acids and 2-oxotetrahydroquinoline-4-carboxylic acids
10.1055_s-0041-1738439.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of NiCl2(bpp) to Ni(0), which performs oxidative addition into the C(acyl)-Cl bond; further single-electron cathodic reductions turn over the Ni catalyst across the cycle. Mg sacrificial anode balances charge.
10.1055_s-0041-1738439.electron_flow: Cathode reduces NiCl2(bpp) to Ni(0); Ni(0) does oxidative addition into the carbamoyl C-Cl bond of 1 to give Ni(II) acyl A. A is reduced at the cathode (path a: SET first to Ni(I) B, then alkene cyclization to D/D'; path b: alkene cyclization first to Ni(II) C/C', then SET to D/D'). CO2 inserts into the Ni-C bond to give carboxylate E/E'; a final cathodic reduction releases Ni(0) and, after aqueous workup, the carboxylic acid product. Mg anode is sacrificially oxidized.

key_intermediates.name: Ni(II)-acyl chloride intermediate A from oxidative addition of carbamoyl chloride 1a
Ni(I)-acyl intermediate B (from SET reduction of A, path a)
Ni(II) cyclized alkyl intermediates C/C' (intramolecular alkene migratory insertion, path b)
Ni(I) cyclized alkyl intermediates D/D' after SET reduction
Ni-carboxylate intermediates E/E' after CO2 insertion into Ni-C bond

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of NiCl2(bpp), 1a, and CO2
probe_reagents.observation: NiCl2(bpp) shows two reversible reductive peaks at -1.71 V and -2.31 V (Ni(II)->Ni(I)->Ni(0)); adding 1a produces strong catalytic current near -2.50 V indicating oxidative addition with in situ Ni(0); saturating with CO2 further increases the reductive current, consistent with carboxylation turnover.

10.1055_s-0041-1738439.notes: Two cyclization pathways are proposed and considered competitive: path a (SET reduction of Ni(II) acyl A to Ni(I) B before alkene migratory insertion) and path b (alkene insertion first to give Ni(II) C/C', then SET to D/D'). The paper does not designate one as strictly favored; both converge on D/D' prior to CO2 insertion. Substrate scope shows ortho/meta/para electronic effects and poor performance with aryl chloride/bromide substituents on the arene (competing reactivity), and 1n (bromo on indoline-forming arene) gives no carboxylation product, consistent with competing Ni insertion into the Ar-Br bond.

10.1002_ejoc.202301246_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [3+2] annulation of 3-aza-1,5-dienes with dimethyl malonate to access 4-pyrrolin-2-ones with cathodic H2 evolution
10.1002_ejoc.202301246_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation generates a malonyl radical from deprotonated dimethyl malonate; cathodic reduction of protons produces H2
10.1002_ejoc.202301246_sup_1.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the malonate anion (formed by n-Bu4NOAc base) to a malonyl C-radical that adds to the methacrylamide C=C of the 3-aza-1,5-diene; the resulting carbon radical cyclizes onto the pendant styrenyl alkene, and a further SET oxidation/deprotonation furnishes the 4-pyrrolin-2-one, with protons reduced at the stainless-steel cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: malonyl-stabilized C-centered radical from dimethyl malonate
N-acyl alpha-amino allyl/C-centered radical after radical addition to the methacrylamide alkene
cyclized N-acyl iminium / radical cation intermediate prior to deprotonation/aromatization to 4-pyrrolin-2-one

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO/BHT (radical quenching experiments)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed in the presence of radical scavengers, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: electricity on/off
probe_reagents.observation: Product formation only progresses while current is applied, indicating continuous electrolysis is required

probe_reagents.name: H2 detection (GC)
probe_reagents.observation: H2 detected as the cathodic counter-product

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of substrates/malonate
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation event consistent with anodic oxidation of the malonate anion being the initiating SET step

10.1002_ejoc.202301246_sup_1.notes: Mechanism inferred from the SI section headings (quenching, on/off, H2 detection, CV) and the gram-scale electrolysis scheme using C(+)/stainless-steel(-) electrodes with n-Bu4NOAc in MeCN; full mechanistic schemes were not visible on the supplied pages, so specific intermediate SMILES beyond the malonyl radical are left null.

10.1002_ejoc.202301246.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical dehydrogenative regioselective cyclization of 3-aza-1,5-dienes with 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds to construct 4-pyrrolin-2-ones via anodic generation of 1,3-dicarbonyl radicals.
10.1002_ejoc.202301246.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the 1,3-dicarbonyl compound (Eox p/2 = 1.49 V vs Ag/AgCl) to a 1,3-dicarbonyl radical; a second anodic SET oxidizes the cyclized C-radical to a cation; concomitant cathodic reduction of protons evolves H2.
10.1002_ejoc.202301246.electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the 1,3-dicarbonyl compound to a C-centered radical (A), which adds regioselectively to the acryloyl alkene of the 3-aza-1,5-diene to give radical B; intramolecular cyclization onto the enaminic C=C gives radical C, which is further oxidized at the anode to cation D, and deprotonation delivers the pyrrolinone while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: open-chain radical adduct (B) from radical addition onto the acryloyl C=C of the 3-aza-1,5-diene
cyclized C-radical (C) after intramolecular radical trapping by the enaminic double bond
cationic intermediate (D) from SET oxidation of C at the anode

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a suppressed to <1%, indicating a radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield <1%, consistent with radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (0% yield).

probe_reagents.name: p-dinitrobenzene (pDNB)
probe_reagents.observation: SET scavenger; 0% yield, supporting single-electron transfer.

probe_reagents.name: CuCl2
probe_reagents.observation: <1% yield, single-electron scavenging suppresses reaction.

probe_reagents.name: CCl4
probe_reagents.observation: 0% yield; single-electron quencher inhibits reaction.

probe_reagents.name: current on/off experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction halts during off cycles, excluding a radical chain mechanism.

probe_reagents.name: H2 headspace detection
probe_reagents.observation: H2 concentration increases over time, confirming cathodic proton reduction paired with anodic oxidation.

10.1002_ejoc.202301246.notes: Regioselectivity favors radical addition onto the acryloyl alkene rather than the aryl-substituted enaminic alkene, with cyclization closing through the enaminic double bond rather than via aryl radical attack. On/off current experiments rule out a radical chain propagation; instead a stepwise anodic SET / radical addition / cyclization / second anodic SET / deprotonation sequence is invoked, paired with cathodic H2 evolution.

10.1002_ejoc.202500222_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical reductive aryl radical cyclization/cross-coupling of aryl iodide-tethered alkenes with 1,4-dicyanobenzene to form chroman-type products bearing arylated quaternary centers
10.1002_ejoc.202500222_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction: aryl iodide 1a is reduced at the carbon cloth cathode to generate an aryl radical (via C-I cleavage), while 1,4-dicyanobenzene 2a is reduced to its persistent radical anion; the Mg anode is sacrificial
10.1002_ejoc.202500222_sup_1.electron_flow: Cathode reduces 1,4-dicyanobenzene 2a to its radical anion (CV: ca. -1.49/-1.60 V) and reduces aryl iodide 1a (more negative, ca. -2.45/-2.52 V) to give an aryl radical after loss of iodide; the aryl radical undergoes intramolecular 5-exo-trig cyclization onto the alkene, and the resulting tertiary C-radical couples with the dicyanobenzene radical anion, followed by loss of cyanide to deliver the arylated chroman 3aa.
10.1002_ejoc.202500222_sup_1.rate_determining_step: Cathodic SET to the aryl iodide generating the aryl radical (highest reduction potential observed by CV)

key_intermediates.name: aryl radical from C-I cleavage of 1a
alkyl radical from 5-exo-trig cyclization onto pendant prenyl ether (chroman tertiary C-radical)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO adducts 9 (chroman alkyl-OTMP), 10 (dicyanocyclohexadienyl-OTMP) and 11 (aryl-OTMP from 2a) each detected by HRMS (<5%); reaction to 3aa suppressed, supporting radical intermediates

10.1002_ejoc.202500222_sup_1.notes: Current on/off experiments (Figure S1c) show yield increases only during current-on periods and plateaus when current is off, indicating no radical chain propagation and that continuous electron input is required. CV titration of 1a into 2a shows progressive cathodic shift of the 2a reduction wave (-1.62 to -1.63 V), consistent with interaction/consumption of the 2a radical anion in the presence of 1a. Sacrificial Mg anode balances charge.

10.1002_ejoc.202500222.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical cyclization of O-tethered aryl iodides (alkenyl ethers) with terephthalonitrile to access chromanes/isochromanes bearing arylated quaternary carbons, using a Mg anode and carbon cloth cathode.
10.1002_ejoc.202500222.electrochemical_role: Cathode reduces terephthalonitrile to its radical anion, which then mediates single-electron reduction of the aryl C–I bond to generate an aryl radical; Mg anode is sacrificially oxidized to Mg(II).
10.1002_ejoc.202500222.electron_flow: Cathode delivers one electron to terephthalonitrile to give its radical anion, which transfers an electron to the aryl iodide (the iodide has a more negative reduction potential), cleaving C–I to give an aryl radical; 5- or 6-exo-trig cyclization onto the pendant alkene forms an exocyclic alkyl radical that couples with a second terephthalonitrile radical anion, then rearomatizes by extrusion of cyanide to give the ipso-arylated product. Mg anode is sacrificially oxidized to Mg(2+).

key_intermediates.name: aryl radical from C–I cleavage of O-tethered iodoarene
exocyclic tertiary C-radical (II) after 5- or 6-exo-trig cyclization onto the tethered alkene
radical-radical coupling adduct / cyclohexadienyl anion that rearomatizes by loss of CN-

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO adduct 9 of the exocyclic alkyl radical detected by HRMS (<5%); TEMPO adducts 10/11 of terephthalonitrile-derived radical species also detected (<5%); reaction suppressed, supporting both aryl/alkyl C-radical and arene radical-anion intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 2a, 1a, 4a
probe_reagents.observation: E_red(2a) = -1.61 V vs Ag/AgCl, less negative than aryl iodobenzene (E_red ≈ -2.5 V), indicating preferential cathodic reduction of terephthalonitrile; reduction peak of 2a grows with increasing [1a], consistent with mediated SET from 2a•- to 1a.

10.1002_ejoc.202500222.notes: Net transformation is an ipso-cyanide substitution on terephthalonitrile (loss of CN-) coupled to intramolecular aryl-radical cyclization. Selectivity for 5- vs 6-exo-trig set by tether length (m, n). Mg(II) released from the sacrificial anode likely stabilizes the cyclohexadienyl anion and/or cyanide leaving group. No DFT or KIE reported.

10.1002_ejoc.202300506.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical thiolative cyclization of lapachol/C-allyl-lawsone with diaryl disulfides to form fused sulfide-containing pyran (6-membered) or furan (5-membered) lapachones, followed by Ru/NaIO4 oxidation to the corresponding sulfones.
10.1002_ejoc.202300506.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of PhSSPh to [(PhS)4]2+/[(PhS)3]+ thiiranium-type electrophile which activates the alkene; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode (paired electrolysis).
10.1002_ejoc.202300506.electron_flow: At the anode PhSSPh is oxidized (E = -0.41 V vs Ag) to its radical cation, which dimerizes to [(PhS)4]2+ and fragments to electrophilic [(PhS)3]+; this sulfenyl cation adds to the alkene of lapachol/C-allyl-lawsone to give a sulfonium/carbocation that is intramolecularly trapped by the naphthoquinone enol-OH (6-endo for lapachol, 5-exo for less substituted C-allyl-lawsone), releasing H+ which is reduced to H2 at the cathode.
10.1002_ejoc.202300506.rate_determining_step: Nucleophilic attack of the alkene on the electrogenerated sulfenyl cation / formation of the carbocation intermediate (slower for the less substituted C-allyl-lawsone, consistent with lower yields).

key_intermediates.name: thiiranium / sulfonium adduct on the prenyl/allyl alkene of lapachol
tertiary carbocation from Markovnikov opening (lapachol case)

probe_reagents.name: diphenyl disulfide (2a) alone
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows semi-reversible oxidation; radical cation [PhSSPh]•+ at E = -0.41 V vs Ag, evolving to [(PhS)4]2+ at -0.06 V vs Ag.

probe_reagents.name: lapachol (1) alone
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation at E = +0.70 V vs Ag; cathodic response at -1.22 V vs Ag assigned to quinone reduction/rearrangement.

probe_reagents.name: lapachol + disulfide 2a mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Lapachol oxidation peak disappears, indicating consumption/coupling with electrogenerated sulfenyl species; supports disulfide-as-mediator pathway rather than direct lapachol oxidation.

probe_reagents.name: isolated product 3a
probe_reagents.observation: Reversible reductions at -0.59 and -1.01 V and reduction at E1/2 = -0.80 V vs Ag confirm the quinone is recovered in its oxidized form after electrolysis.

10.1002_ejoc.202300506.notes: Mechanism is supported primarily by CV studies and product/structural (ORTEP) characterization; no radical traps, KIE, DFT, or EPR were reported. Regiochemical divergence (6-endo vs 5-exo) is rationalized by carbocation stability — lapachol's prenyl group gives a tertiary cation enabling 6-membered ring closure, while C-allyl-lawsone gives a less stabilized cation favoring 5-membered ring closure. Subsequent (non-electrochemical) RuCl3/NaIO4 oxidation converts sulfides 3/5 to sulfones 6.

10.1002_ejoc.202100640.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical bromide-mediated oxybromination of allylic oximes to form 2-bromomethyl-2-isoxazolines using DMSO as oxygen-free mediator partner
10.1002_ejoc.202100640.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide (Br-) to bromine/tribromide (Br3-), which serves as the active brominating mediator; cathode reduces water to H2 and generates hydroxide that deprotonates the oxime
10.1002_ejoc.202100640.electron_flow: Anode performs 1e- oxidation of Br- to Br•, which dimerizes to Br2 and is sequestered by Br- as Br3-; Br3- brominates DMSO to electrophile A, which transfers Br+ to the alkene of the allylic oxime to give bromonium/sulfonium adduct B (major) or bromonium C (minor); intramolecular oxa-cyclization by the oxime hydroxyl (5-exo-trig) delivers the isoxazoline, while the cathode reduces H2O to H2 and OH- to deprotonate the oxime.

key_intermediates.name: open-chain bromonium-DMSO adduct B from alkene addition
bromonium intermediate C of allylic oxime

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully suppressed (0% of 2a) with 3 equiv TEMPO, implying involvement of a radical species in the catalytic cycle

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Bromine-trapped adduct 5 (Ph2C=CHBr / bromo-DPE) detected by GCMS, supporting participation of a bromine radical

10.1002_ejoc.202100640.notes: Two cyclization pathways are proposed from intermediate A: major pathway goes through DMSO-bromonium adduct B and then ring-closes to 2a; minor pathway invokes a free bromonium C from the oxime which can either close 5-exo-trig (path a → 2a) or 6-endo-trig (path b → by-product 3a, 1,2-oxazine). DMSO acts as Br+ shuttle; in-situ generated hydroxide at the cathode serves as base for oxime deprotonation.

10.1002_ejoc.202200977.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical iron-catalyzed azidoesterification of alkenes using carbazates as alkoxycarbonyl radical precursors and TMSN3 as the azide source to give beta-azido esters.
10.1002_ejoc.202200977.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of PcFe(II) to PcFe(III), which oxidizes methyl carbazate to generate the methoxycarbonyl radical (with loss of N2); a second metal-azide complex (Fe or Mn) delivers N3 to the alkyl radical after C-C bond formation.
10.1002_ejoc.202200977.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PcFe(II) to PcFe(III), which oxidizes methyl carbazate to give methoxycarbonyl radical (releasing N2); this C-radical adds to the alkene to form a secondary C-radical that is trapped by an azido-M(III) complex via inner-sphere azide group transfer, regenerating M(II). Protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: beta-(alkoxycarbonyl) carbon-centered radical (I) after addition to alkene
M(III)-N3 azido metal complex (M = Fe or Mn)
M(II)-N3 complex after azide group transfer

probe_reagents.name: (1-phenyl-2-methylcyclopropyl)methylene alkene (radical clock 29)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 30 obtained in 53% yield (E/Z = 2.5:1), consistent with a carbon-centered radical intermediate after addition.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of TMSN3, methyl carbazate (1), PcFe, and MnBr2
probe_reagents.observation: Methyl carbazate oxidizes at 670 mV and TMSN3 at 340 mV; addition of PcFe to TMSN3 gives a new wave at ca. 160 mV (Fe(II)/Fe(III) with azide as ligand); this wave increases with added carbazate indicating redox communication between Fe(III) and 1. MnBr2 does not show analogous behavior.

probe_reagents.name: Controlled potential electrolysis at Eanode = 0.10 V vs Fc+/0
probe_reagents.observation: Azidoesterification of 4-tBu-styrene proceeded smoothly to give product 3 in 64% yield, confirming low-potential Fe-mediated oxidation is sufficient.

10.1002_ejoc.202200977.notes: Two metal catalysts are used cooperatively: PcFe is proposed as the redox-active mediator that oxidizes the carbazate to the alkoxycarbonyl radical, while MnBr2·4H2O (or PcMn in some cases) is invoked as the azide-delivery metal (M(III)-N3 -> M(II)). Mn alone is not catalytically competent (Table 1, entry 3). Cathode reaction is H2 evolution.

10.1002_ejoc.202200857_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical bromination of limonene (olefin) via anodic oxidation of bromide to bromine, which adds across the alkene
10.1002_ejoc.202200857_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide to bromine (Br2), which then reacts with the olefin; tribromide (Br3-) serves as a reservoir in equilibrium with Br2
10.1002_ejoc.202200857_sup_1.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Br- to Br2 (E ~0.49 V); Br2 either equilibrates with excess Br- to form Br3- (resting state) or is consumed by the olefin via electrophilic addition. In the presence of substrate the Br3- oxidation wave diminishes and no reduction wave is observed, indicating Br2 is trapped by the alkene.

probe_reagents.name: NaBr (bromide source) CV
probe_reagents.observation: Quasi-reversible oxidation at 0.49 V (Br-/Br2) and second oxidation at 0.86 V (Br3-/Br2); reduction wave at -0.21 V

probe_reagents.name: (S)-(-)-limonene with NaBr CV
probe_reagents.observation: First oxidation shifts from 0.49 V to 0.58 V, second oxidation wave reduced, and reduction wave absent — consistent with electrogenerated Br2 being consumed by the olefin

10.1002_ejoc.202200857_sup_1.notes: Mechanism supported solely by CV experiments comparing NaBr alone vs NaBr + limonene; Br3-/Br2 equilibrium acts as a bromine reservoir. No radical traps, KIE, or DFT reported on this page.

10.1002_ejoc.202300477.rxn_class_summary: Enantioselective electrochemical 1,2-diacetoxylation of styrenes mediated by a chiral iodoarene (Ishihara-Muñiz-type iodoresorcinol-lactamide) catalyst
10.1002_ejoc.202300477.electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes the chiral iodoarene catalyst (ArI) to a hypervalent I(III) species (ArI(OAc)2/ArI-OAc radical/cation), which then acts as the stoichiometric oxidant transferring two OAc groups to the styrene; direct anodic oxidation of the styrene to a radical cation is a competing background pathway leading to racemic product 3 and dimeric side-product 4
10.1002_ejoc.202300477.electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the chiral iodoarene (S,S)-1 stepwise in the presence of AcOH to give a chiral λ3-iodane ArI(OAc)2; this I(III) species then engages styrene to deliver two acetoxy groups enantioselectively, regenerating ArI. A competing direct anodic SET oxidation of styrene to its radical cation produces racemic 3 and dimer 4.
10.1002_ejoc.202300477.rate_determining_step: Anodic oxidation of the iodoarene catalyst to the I(III) state (catalyst E_p/2 ~2.0 V vs Fc+/Fc is at the high end of the electrochemical window, identified as the key obstacle)

key_intermediates.name: styrene radical cation (from direct anodic oxidation, background racemic pathway)
β-acetoxy benzylic radical (from AcOH addition to styrene radical cation)
β-acetoxy benzylic cation (further oxidation, gives rac-3 after AcOH trap)
dimeric distonic radical cation leading to side-product 4
ArI(•)-OAc (anodically generated iodine(II) radical intermediate of chiral catalyst)
ArI(OAc)2 (chiral hypervalent iodine(III) diacetate, active enantioselective oxidant)
styrene–I(III) adduct / acetoxy-iodonium intermediate that delivers OAc enantioselectively

probe_reagents.name: glassy carbon anode (vs graphite)
probe_reagents.observation: Switching from graphite to glassy carbon anode changed yields and ee (e.g. 3a: 23%/84% ee → 71%/56% ee; 3c: 19%/78% ee → 30%/10% ee; 3e: 36%/81% ee → 40%/28% ee; 3j: 15%/33% ee → 31%/3% ee), showing electrode material modulates competition between catalyst-mediated enantioselective pathway and direct styrene oxidation

10.1002_ejoc.202300477.notes: Two competing mechanisms are presented in Scheme 4: (A) direct anodic oxidation of styrene generating a radical cation that is trapped by AcOH, oxidized further to a cation, and trapped again to give racemic 3 (with a dimeric C–C coupled side-product 4) — based on Schäfer's work; (B) the desired enantioselective pathway via anodically generated chiral I(III) species (ArI(OAc)2) that transfers two OAc groups to styrene — based on Ishihara/Muñiz precedent. Pathway B is the favored/intended mechanism. The authors note the catalyst's high oxidation potential (~2.0 V vs Fc+/Fc) is the main reason direct styrene oxidation competes and ee is moderate; this suggests the Ishihara–Muñiz iodoresorcinol-lactamide scaffold is not ideal for electrochemistry. No radical traps, KIE, or DFT were performed; mechanism is proposed by analogy to literature.

10.1002_ejoc.202200857.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-dibromination of terpenes and alkenes using NaBr as bromide source in an undivided cell with glassy carbon electrodes
10.1002_ejoc.202200857.electrochemical_role: Anodic 2-electron oxidation of bromide to Br2 (in equilibrium with tribromide Br3-); cathodic reduction of water to H2 (and hypobromite formation in undivided cell)
10.1002_ejoc.202200857.electron_flow: At the anode, 2 Br- is oxidized (2 e-) to Br2, which equilibrates with Br3-; Br2 reacts with the alkene to form a bromonium ion that is opened by Br- to give the 1,2-dibromide. The cathode reduces 2 H2O (2 e-) to H2 and hydroxide, balancing charge.
10.1002_ejoc.202200857.rate_determining_step: Anodic generation of Br2 from bromide (slow, controlled in-situ release accounts for high selectivity)

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of NaBr
probe_reagents.observation: Two oxidation events observed: Br- to Br2/Br3- equilibrium, then Br3- to Br2

probe_reagents.name: pH monitoring (divided vs undivided cell)
probe_reagents.observation: Cathodic chamber pH rises from 6 to 14 in divided cell (OH-/H2 evolution); undivided cell pH remains constant, consistent with hypobromite formation

probe_reagents.name: X-ray crystallography of 2b
probe_reagents.observation: Confirmed (1R,2R,4S,8S)-tetrabromide diastereomer, supporting stereospecific anti-addition via bromonium intermediates

probe_reagents.name: Br2 control (conventional)
probe_reagents.observation: Br2 gives 22% 2a and 13% 2b (poor selectivity vs 54% / 0% for electrochemical)

probe_reagents.name: DMSO/HBr control
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 39% 2a and 10% 2b; lower selectivity than electrochemical method

10.1002_ejoc.202200857.notes: Selectivity rationalized by slow, controlled in-situ generation of Br2 at the anode versus bulk Br2 addition. Electron-withdrawing groups on alkene reduce reactivity at that position, directing bromination to more nucleophilic alkenes in polyenes (e.g. terminal alkene of carvone/perillaldehyde). Cathodic lability of C-Br bonds required a divided PTFE cell for tetrabromination of limonene to 2b. Hypobromite is invoked as a side species in undivided cells based on constant pH.

10.1002_ejoc.202300927.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical bromide-mediated sulfoximido-selenylation of unsaturated sulfoximines with diselenides to give cyclic sulfoximidoyl heterocycles bearing -SeR groups
10.1002_ejoc.202300927.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Br- to Br3+ which then oxidizes PhSeSePh to a selenium radical/radical cation; bromide acts as redox mediator
10.1002_ejoc.202300927.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Br- to Br3+, which oxidizes PhSeSePh to PhSe radical/cation; this adds to the alkene of the unsaturated sulfoximine generating intermediate I, which is further oxidized to a cation (II) and then trapped by intramolecular nucleophilic attack of the sulfoximine N followed by deprotonation to give the cyclic product.

key_intermediates.name: PhSe radical and/or PhSe cation from anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh
Carbon-centered radical/cation adduct I from PhSe addition to alkene of unsaturated sulfoximine
Further oxidized intermediate II (carbocation) or aziridinium-type III after deprotonation

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a still formed in 35% yield; only partial suppression, suggesting radical pathway may not be dominant

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a formed in 67% yield; little suppression observed

probe_reagents.name: nBu4NBr (cyclic voltammetry)
probe_reagents.observation: Lower oxidation peak and obvious catalytic current observed, confirming Br- acts as redox mediator

10.1002_ejoc.202300927.notes: Authors propose two pathways in Scheme 4: (A) anodic oxidation pathway via Br3+ mediator generating PhSe radical/cation, favored and discussed first; (B) alternative cathodic reduction of PhSeSePh to give PhSe- + PhSe radical, supported by prior literature. The incomplete suppression by TEMPO/BHT led authors to suggest a radical process may not be the main pathway, implying the cationic (path a via radical cation) route may dominate.

10.1002_ejoc.201101313.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intermolecular [2+2] cycloaddition between an enol ether (1-(prop-1-en-1-yloxy)-4-propylbenzene) and an olefinic carboxylic acid (hex-5-enoic acid) via an anodically generated radical cation, with the olefin nucleophile noncovalently condensed at the electrode surface.
10.1002_ejoc.201101313.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the electron-rich aryl enol ether (1) to its radical cation, which is trapped at the electrode surface by the surface-condensed olefin (2) to give a [2+2] cycloadduct.
10.1002_ejoc.201101313.electron_flow: The anode removes one electron from the electron-rich aryl enol ether 1 to give a short-lived radical cation; the pendant olefin of hex-5-enoic acid 2, noncovalently condensed at the electrode surface, traps this radical cation and ring-closes to the cyclobutane radical cation, which is reduced back to the neutral [2+2] adduct 3.
10.1002_ejoc.201101313.rate_determining_step: trapping of the transient radical cation of 1 by the olefin nucleophile 2 (limited by its local concentration at the electrode surface)

key_intermediates.name: aryl enol ether radical cation of 1-(prop-1-en-1-yloxy)-4-propylbenzene
distonic radical cation / 1,4-radical cation intermediate from C-C bond formation with hex-5-enoic acid

probe_reagents.name: high-equivalent loading of hex-5-enoic acid (20 mol equiv., 200 mM)
probe_reagents.observation: Required excess for effective trapping of the short-lived radical cation under conventional conditions; yields of 3 dropped rapidly as the olefin amount was decreased, whereas with surface-condensed electrodes high yields were retained even with much less olefin.

10.1002_ejoc.201101313.notes: Mechanism is presented as a proposal supported indirectly by the dependence of yield on olefin loading and by the comparison between conventional vs surface-condensed electrode conditions; no TEMPO/BHT trapping, CV, EPR, or DFT data shown on the provided page. Only the first page of the communication was supplied, so later mechanistic details (if any) are not captured.

10.1002_ejoc.202201032.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical vicinal fluoroselenenylation of alkenes using diaryl diselenides and Et3N·3HF to give β-fluoroselenides.
10.1002_ejoc.202201032.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diaryl diselenide (ArSeSeAr) to its radical cation, which fragments into an ArSe• radical and an ArSe+ cation; the alkene-derived carbon radical is then further oxidized at the anode to a selenonium cation that is trapped by fluoride.
10.1002_ejoc.202201032.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes ArSeSeAr to its radical cation, which fragments to ArSe• + ArSe+; ArSe• adds across the alkene to give a β-selenyl C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a selenonium (seleniranium) cation, and fluoride from Et3N·3HF opens it to deliver the β-fluoroselenide.

probe_reagents.name: (1-(2-phenylcyclopropyl)-vinyl)benzene (radical clock 29)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 30 obtained in 74% yield, indicating involvement of a selenium radical adding to the alkene.

probe_reagents.name: Dark control (electrolysis without light)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction still proceeded in >99% yield, excluding photochemical generation of ArSe• and supporting anodic oxidation as the radical source.

probe_reagents.name: No-electricity control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed, confirming electrochemical requirement.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1 and 2
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peaks: α-methylstyrene 2.48 V vs SCE; PhSeSePh 1.91 V vs SCE — diselenide is oxidized first under electrolysis.

10.1002_ejoc.202201032.notes: Reductive homo-coupling of the ArSe+ cation at the cathode is proposed to regenerate ArSeSeAr, closing a redox loop. An alternative photochemical pathway (visible-light homolysis of PhSeSePh to ArSe•) was explicitly excluded by the dark experiment.

10.1002_ejoc.202400351.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-dihalogenation (F, Cl, Br) of gem-difluoroalkenes using HX (HF, HCl, HBr) as halide source to deliver CF2X-CHX-R type products including 18F-labeled tracers
10.1002_ejoc.202400351.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of halide (X-) from HX to halogen radical/halogen species which adds to the gem-difluoroalkene; the resulting carbon radical/cation is trapped by a second halide
10.1002_ejoc.202400351.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes X- (from HX) to a halogen radical that adds to the terminal CF2=CHR alkene to give a CF2X-substituted carbon radical; this radical is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation and trapped by a second halide to yield the vicinal dihalide.

key_intermediates.name: halide-derived halogen radical (X•) from anodic oxidation of HX
β-fluorinated carbon radical from X• addition to gem-difluoroalkene (CF2X-C•(H)-R)

probe_reagents.name: K[18F]F-K222 (Kryptofix-complexed [18F]fluoride)
probe_reagents.observation: Enables 18F-labeling of the dihalogenated product, supporting halide incorporation pathway and giving 18F-labeled tracers

10.1002_ejoc.202400351.notes: Only the introductory/abstract page was provided; the detailed mechanistic scheme (radical traps, CV, DFT) is not shown here. Mechanism inferred from Scheme 1b (HX + electrolysis, classification as Method III electrochemical dihalogenation) and analogy to prior electrochemical olefin dihalogenations cited in the introduction.

10.1002_ejoc.202101189.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular halo-amination (iodo- and bromo-) of unactivated N-alkenyl sulfonamides to give 3-, 5-, and 6-membered halogenated N-heterocycles using LiI or LiBr as halide source and redox mediator.
10.1002_ejoc.202101189.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (or bromide) to molecular I2 (Br2), which acts as the halogen-transfer mediator; the sulfonamide substrate itself is not oxidized at the anode (confirmed by CV). Cathode reduces protons to H2.
10.1002_ejoc.202101189.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes I- to I2; I2 reacts with the pendant alkene to form an iodonium ion, which undergoes intramolecular nucleophilic attack by the sulfonamide nitrogen to give the halogenated N-heterocycle with loss of H+; protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: molecular I2 generated in situ from anodic oxidation of I-
cyclic iodonium ion from alkene + I2 (intermediate I)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction almost completely inhibited by 2.0 equiv TEMPO; starting material decomposed to 4-methylbenzenesulfonamide (detected by GC-MS); only trace 2a formed. Authors note this may reflect a radical pathway OR suppression via TEMPO-iodine adduct formation.

10.1002_ejoc.202101189.notes: CV shows two oxidation peaks for LiI (0.89 and 1.57 V vs Ag/AgCl) while sulfonamide 1a shows no anodic oxidation in the operating window, supporting iodide-mediator (not direct substrate oxidation) pathway. Authors favor an ionic iodonium/nucleophilic cyclization mechanism (Scheme 3), but acknowledge the TEMPO inhibition could alternatively implicate a radical pathway; they attribute it to TEMPO–I adduct formation rather than C-radical trapping.

10.1002_ejoc.202500634.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical tandem trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-(but-3-en-1-yl)anilines with Togni's reagent II to give 4-(trifluoromethylmethyl)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroquinolines
10.1002_ejoc.202500634.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of Togni's reagent II (facilitated by TFA, shifting reduction from -2.73 V to -1.65 V vs SCE) generates a CF3 radical; subsequent anodic oxidation of a cyclohexadienyl-type radical intermediate closes the catalytic cycle
10.1002_ejoc.202500634.electron_flow: Cathode reduces TFA-activated Togni's reagent II to release a CF3 radical, which adds to the alkene of the aniline substrate to give C-radical I; intramolecular radical addition onto the arene gives cyclohexadienyl radical II, which is oxidized at the anode to cation III and deprotonated to rearomatize, affording the tetrahydroquinoline product.

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical generated by cathodic reduction of Togni's reagent II
Carbon-centered radical I from CF3 addition to terminal alkene of N-(but-3-en-1-yl)aniline
Cyclohexadienyl radical II from intramolecular radical cyclization onto the arene
Cyclohexadienyl cation III after anodic oxidation of II, prior to rearomatizing deprotonation

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; no product 3a formed, indicating radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; supports radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited, consistent with radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: no-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed without applied current; confirms electrochemistry is essential

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, TFA and mixtures
probe_reagents.observation: 1a and TFA show no reduction peak; 2a reduces at -2.73 V vs SCE; 2a+TFA shows new reduction peak at -1.65 V vs SCE, showing TFA facilitates cathodic reduction of Togni's reagent II

10.1002_ejoc.202500634.notes: TFA plays a critical role by protonating/activating Togni's reagent II, lowering its reduction potential by ~1 V and enabling efficient cathodic CF3 radical generation under mild conditions. Mechanism is supported by radical trapping, current on/off, and CV but no DFT or KIE was reported.

10.1039_c2ra20078c.rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic Michael-initiated ring closure (MIRC) of N,N'-dialkylbarbituric acids with benzylidenemalononitriles or benzylidenecyanoacetates in methanol/NaBr (or NaI) to give spirocyclopropylbarbiturates (4,6,8-trioxo-5,7-diazaspiro[2.5]octanes)
10.1039_c2ra20078c.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of halide mediator (Br- or I-) to molecular halogen (Hal2), which halogenates the Michael adduct at the barbiturate C5 position; methoxide generated at the cathode (from MeOH reduction) deprotonates the substrate and the halogenated intermediate to drive Michael addition and intramolecular SN2 ring closure
10.1039_c2ra20078c.electron_flow: At the graphite anode, halide (Br-/I-) is oxidized to Hal2; at the iron cathode, MeOH is reduced to generate MeO- and H2. MeO- deprotonates the barbituric acid to give C5-anion A which undergoes Michael addition to the arylidene; the resulting adduct C is halogenated by Hal2 at C5, then MeO- deprotonates the alpha-CN/COOR carbon to form anion D which performs intramolecular SN2 displacement of the halide, closing the cyclopropane and regenerating halide for the mediator cycle.

key_intermediates.name: barbiturate C5 anion A (deprotonated N,N'-dialkylbarbituric acid)
Michael adduct anion B (unstable, undergoes retro-Michael)
Michael adduct anion C (deprotonated at malononitrile/cyanoacetate carbon)
conjugated barbiturate anion D (after methoxide deprotonation of halogenated adduct)
spirocyclopropylbarbiturate 3 (product after intramolecular ring closure with halide expulsion)

probe_reagents.name: low-charge electrolysis (0.1 F/mol)
probe_reagents.observation: Detected retro-Michael products (barbituric acid and benzylidenemalononitrile) and dihydropyrano[2,3-d]pyrimidine from intramolecular cyclization of adduct B, confirming Michael adduct C as a key intermediate

probe_reagents.name: single-crystal X-ray of product 3q
probe_reagents.observation: Confirmed (E)-configuration of aryl and carbomethoxy substituents on the cyclopropane ring, supporting stereoselective intramolecular SN2 ring closure from anion D

probe_reagents.name: NaI vs NaBr mediator screen
probe_reagents.observation: NaI less efficient than NaBr (50% vs 57% at 0 C); supports halide-mediated (inner-sphere) mechanism via in situ Hal2

probe_reagents.name: current density / electricity passed variation
probe_reagents.observation: No improvement above 2 F/mol; consistent with catalytic halide mediator turnover (2 e- per cyclopropanation)

10.1039_c2ra20078c.notes: Stereoselective formation of single (E)-diastereomer attributed to steric repulsion between aryl and carboalkoxy groups during the intramolecular SN2 cyclization from anion D, which dictates the conformation placing those groups on opposite faces of the forming cyclopropane. The mediator could in principle act via outer-sphere SET, but the authors invoke discrete Hal2 formation and ionic alpha-halogenation (inner-sphere/ionic mediator pathway). Off-pathway: at low charge, adduct B can undergo retro-Michael or 6-endo O-cyclization to a dihydropyrano[2,3-d]pyrimidine.

10.1039_d3ra07282g.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative radical cascade trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-(2-alkenylphenyl)benzamides with CF3SO2Na to give CF3-containing benzoxazines and oxazolines
10.1039_d3ra07282g.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na (Eox = 0.83 V) to CF3SO2 radical, which fragments to CF3 radical and SO2; cathodic reduction of HFIP generates H2; second anodic oxidation of the cyclized alkyl radical to a cation
10.1039_d3ra07282g.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3SO2 radical which loses SO2 to give CF3 radical; CF3 radical adds to the alkene of the N-arylbenzamide to give alkyl radical I, which cyclizes onto the amide oxygen; the resulting radical is oxidized at the anode to cation II and deprotonated to deliver the benzoxazine, with cathodic H2 evolution from HFIP.

key_intermediates.name: CF3SO2 radical from anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na
Intermediate I: alkyl C-radical from CF3 addition to alkene of N-(2-isopropenylphenyl)benzamide
Intermediate II: benzylic/oxocarbenium cation after radical cyclization onto amide oxygen and anodic oxidation

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of TEMPO completely suppressed formation of 2a (0% yield), supporting a radical pathway involving CF3 radical

10.1039_d3ra07282g.notes: CV shows CF3SO2Na oxidizes at 0.83 V while substrate 1a oxidizes at 1.32 V, indicating preferential anodic oxidation of the sulfinate rather than the alkene (distinguishing this from alkene radical-cation pathways). Control entry without electricity gave no product, confirming electrochemistry is essential. CF2HSO2Na can replace CF3SO2Na to give the difluoromethyl analogue (2v, 31%).

10.3390_molecules29050973_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical cascade fluoroalkylation/cyclization of N-acryloyl 2-arylbenzimidazoles (and 2-arylindoles) with sodium fluoroalkylsulfinates to construct fluoroalkylated benzimidazo[2,1-a]isoquinolinones
10.3390_molecules29050973_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of MeCF2SO2Na generates the MeCF2SO2 radical that loses SO2 to give the MeCF2 radical; a second anodic oxidation converts the cyclized aryl radical B into aryl cation C
10.3390_molecules29050973_1.electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the sulfinate to a sulfonyl radical that extrudes SO2 to give a MeCF2 radical, which adds to the acrylamide alkene to form C-radical A; A cyclizes onto the pendant aryl ring giving aryl radical B, which is further oxidized at the anode to aryl cation C, then deprotonated to product. Protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: MeCF2SO2 radical (sulfonyl radical from anodic oxidation of sulfinate)
MeCF2 radical (1,1-difluoroethyl radical after SO2 extrusion)
Carbon-centered radical A (addition of MeCF2 to acrylamide alkene)
Aryl radical B (after intramolecular cyclization onto arene)
Aryl cation C (after second anodic oxidation of B)

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product not detected; BHT–CF2Me adduct detected by HRMS, supporting a free MeCF2 radical

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-Diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Product yield dropped to 16%; MeF2C–CH=CPh2 type adduct detected by HRMS, confirming MeCF2 radical intermediate

10.3390_molecules29050973_1.notes: Cyclic voltammetry shows an oxidation onset for 2a (sulfinate) and an enhanced current for the 1a+2a mixture, consistent with anodic oxidation of the sulfinate initiating the cascade rather than direct substrate oxidation. Product structures confirmed by X-ray (CCDC 2303467 and 2307007). The scope extends to cyclopropyl-CF2 sulfinate (compound 5i).

10.31635_ccschem.021.202100826.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical aziridination of alkenes with ammonia to give NH-aziridines, with solvent-controlled chemoselectivity (MeOH favors aziridination over NH3 oxidation/N2 formation seen in DMF)
10.31635_ccschem.021.202100826.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the alkene (e.g., triphenylethene 1v) to its radical cation, followed by nucleophilic addition of NH3 and a second anodic oxidation of the resulting aminyl radical to a nitrogen-centered cation that closes to the aziridine; in MeOH, hydrogen-bond donation by solvent raises Eox(NH3), suppressing competing NH3 oxidation
10.31635_ccschem.021.202100826.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the alkene to a radical cation (Eox = 6.64 eV); NH3 nucleophilically adds to the less-substituted carbon (TS1-a, 8.7 kcal/mol), and the adduct is deprotonated to a neutral β-amino C-radical; a second anodic oxidation (Eox = 5.72 eV) gives a β-amino carbocation that undergoes near-barrierless ring closure to the aziridinium, which is deprotonated to the NH-aziridine. In DMF the competing pathway is direct anodic oxidation of NH3 to NH3•+ leading to N2/H2 formation.
10.31635_ccschem.021.202100826.rate_determining_step: Nucleophilic addition of NH3 to the alkene radical cation via TS1-a (ΔG‡ ≈ 8.7 kcal/mol in MeOH)

key_intermediates.name: alkene radical cation (1v•+, triphenylethene radical cation)
β-ammonio carbon radical (Int2-a, NH3 adduct of radical cation, after deprotonation)

probe_reagents.name: triphenylethene (1v) as model substrate
probe_reagents.observation: Greatest contrast between MeOH (aziridination) and DMF (hydrogenation/N2 formation), used to probe solvent-dependent chemoselectivity

probe_reagents.name: Ammonia (NH3)
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows NH3 is oxidized preferentially in DMF but 1v is oxidized prior to NH3 in MeOH, indicating MeOH H-bonding stabilizes NH3 lone pair

10.31635_ccschem.021.202100826.notes: Two solvent-dependent pathways are computed: in MeOH, NH3 addition to 1v•+ (TS1-a, 8.7 kcal/mol) outcompetes endergonic NH3 oxidation (+17.9 kcal/mol), leading to aziridine; in DMF, NH3•+ formation is only +3.6 kcal/mol and leads to barrierless deprotonation/hydrazine/N2 formation (N2 hydrogenation pathway), explaining loss of aziridination selectivity. The MeOH selectivity is rationalized by Kamlet–Taft α (H-bond donor) parameter (α=0.98 vs 0 for DMF). Tetraphenyl aziridine 2u structure confirmed by X-ray.

10.31635_ccschem.021.202100933.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical mediator-assisted [4+2] annulation of anethole derivatives with α-methylstyrenes to form polysubstituted dihydronaphthalenes via radical cation intermediates
10.31635_ccschem.021.202100933.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of (4-BrPh)3N mediator to its aminium radical cation, which then performs single-electron oxidation of the electron-rich anethole (2a) to generate an alkene radical cation; cathode reduces protons to H2
10.31635_ccschem.021.202100933.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes (4-BrPh)3N to its aminium radical cation, which single-electron oxidizes anethole to alkene radical cation I; nucleophilic addition of α-methylstyrene 1a yields distonic cation II, which undergoes intramolecular Friedel–Crafts-type cyclization to arenium III; deprotonation gives radical IV that loses one more electron/proton to deliver dihydronaphthalene 3aa, while H+ is reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline N-oxide)
probe_reagents.observation: EPR signals of mixed DMPO-carbon-radical adducts (g=2.0063, AN=15.20 G, AH=21.50 G; second set AN=14.87 G, AH1=AH2=19.62 G) consistent with carbon-radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, (4-BrPh)3N
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(2a)=1.41 V < Eox(1a)=1.91 V and Eox((4-BrPh)3N)=1.20 V, indicating mediator is oxidized first and selectively oxidizes anethole 2a

probe_reagents.name: no-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed without electricity (Entry 12), confirming electrochemical initiation

10.31635_ccschem.021.202100933.notes: Electron-deficient styrenes fail because they cannot be oxidized to a radical cation, supporting the radical-cation initiation pathway. β-methylstyrene gives only the dehydrogenated (fully aromatized) product, indicating IV/3aa is prone to overoxidation. X-ray structures of several products corroborate connectivity and stereochemistry of the proposed cyclization.

10.1007_s11030-019-09979-8.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical spirocyclopropanation of indane-1,3-dione with aromatic aldehydes or benzylidenemalononitriles via anodically-generated bromine mediator to afford spiro[indene-cyclopropane]tetraones and dihydrospiro[cyclopropane-indene]dicarbonitriles.
10.1007_s11030-019-09979-8.electrochemical_role: Cathode reduces propanol to propoxide (with H2 evolution) generating the base; anode oxidizes Br- to Br2 which acts as a halogenating mediator on the Knoevenagel/Michael adduct enolate.
10.1007_s11030-019-09979-8.electron_flow: Cathodic reduction of ROH gives RO- (and 1/2 H2) which deprotonates indane-1,3-dione; Knoevenagel condensation with ArCHO followed by Michael addition of a second enolate builds a 1,3-dicarbonyl bis-adduct, whose enolate is brominated by Br2 generated from anodic oxidation of Br-; intramolecular C-alkylation (SN2-like displacement of Br by the second enolate carbon) closes the cyclopropane.

key_intermediates.name: Michael adduct of two indane-1,3-diones with aldehyde (intermediate 5)

probe_reagents.name: 13C NMR comparison of products 3j vs 9j (C- vs O-cyclization)
probe_reagents.observation: Experimental 13C shifts 37.9 and 51.4 ppm match calculated cyclopropane carbons of 3j (21.6, 51.2); no peak near 108 ppm expected for spirodihydrofuran 9j, confirming intramolecular C-attack (path a) over O-attack (path b).

10.1007_s11030-019-09979-8.notes: Authors explicitly considered two divergent cyclization modes from the brominated enolate: path a (C-attack -> cyclopropane 3) vs path b (O-attack -> spirodihydrofuran 9); selectivity for path a supported by calculated vs experimental 13C NMR. An analogous bifurcation is invoked for the malononitrile series (cyclopropane 14 vs chromene-aminonitrile 15). No radical traps, KIE, or CV experiments were reported; mechanism is largely proposed by analogy.

10.1002_anie.202501424.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical amino-oxygenation cyclization of aryl alkenes with sulfonamide-tethered alcohol bisnucleophiles to form saturated 6-, 7-, and 8-membered N/O-heterocycles (morpholines, 1,4-oxazepanes, 1,4-oxazocanes).
10.1002_anie.202501424.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the aryl alkene to an alkene radical cation; cathodic reduction of protons to H2; no redox mediator and undivided cell.
10.1002_anie.202501424.electron_flow: DBU deprotonates the sulfonamide to give an N-centered anion (I); the anode oxidizes the aryl alkene (2b) to an alkene radical cation (II); I attacks II to give carbon-centered radical III with high regioselectivity; III is oxidized at the anode to cation IV; the tethered hydroxyl of 1a closes onto IV with proton release to form the saturated N/O-heterocycle, while H2 evolves at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: N-centered sulfonamide anion (I) from DBU deprotonation of N-(2-hydroxyethyl)benzenesulfonamide 1a
Aryl alkene radical cation (II) from anodic oxidation of 2b (alpha-methylstyrene)
Carbon-centered radical III from N-anion addition to radical cation II
Carbocation IV from further anodic oxidation of radical III
Cyclized morpholine product 4ab after intramolecular O-attack and deprotonation

probe_reagents.name: HFIP (added cosolvent / oxidation probe)
probe_reagents.observation: HFIP lowers oxidation potential of alkene 2b; with DBU+HFIP the DBU-induced lowering of sulfonamide oxidation is diminished, suggesting alkene is preferentially oxidized.

probe_reagents.name: DBU (base)
probe_reagents.observation: Effectively reduces onset oxidation potential of sulfonamide 1a (CV), consistent with deprotonation to N-anion.

probe_reagents.name: Na2CO3 (base in SWV control)
probe_reagents.observation: Base significantly facilitates formation of intermediate III from I + II coupling without electrode SET.

probe_reagents.name: 1a + 2b mixing (SWV coupling test)
probe_reagents.observation: Adding 1a to 2b under SWV decreases the 2b oxidation current, indicating chemical coupling between N-anion I and radical cation II rather than independent SET.

10.1002_anie.202501424.notes: Key mechanistic support comes from CV/SWV (Figure 4) showing onset potentials 1a 2.21 V, 2b 1.46 V, 6b 1.67 V (vs Ag/AgCl) — alkene is oxidized preferentially — plus in situ EC-APCI-MS (Figure 5) detecting protonated intermediate III ([M+H]+ m/z 333.1393) and product 4ab ([M+H]+ m/z 332.1315) with real-time EIC profiles showing III rising then declining as 4ab forms. An alternative sulfonamide-N-radical addition pathway (oxidation of 1a first) is ruled out because HFIP/DBU experiments and lower potential of 2b indicate the alkene radical cation, not the N-radical, is the key oxidized species.

10.1002_anie.202110616_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Ru/Os-catalyzed C-H activation/annulation of benzoic acids with n-butyl acrylate to form isobenzofuranone (phthalide) products via Heck-type insertion and lactonization
10.1002_anie.202110616_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation regenerates the active high-valent Ru(II)/Os(II) catalyst (reoxidation of reduced metal species after C-H metalation/migratory insertion/β-H elimination); the metal carboxylate mediates C-H cleavage via CMD
10.1002_anie.202110616_sup_1.electron_flow: Metal carboxylate performs concerted metalation-deprotonation of the ortho C-H, then migratory insertion into n-butyl acrylate gives an alkyl-metal species which lactonizes to give the phthalide; the reduced metal is reoxidized at the anode (graphite felt) with H2 evolution at the Pt cathode.
10.1002_anie.202110616_sup_1.rate_determining_step: C-H metalation is not rate-limiting (KIE ~1.1-1.2 from intermolecular competition and parallel experiments)

key_intermediates.name: alkyl-metal intermediate after migratory insertion of acrylate

probe_reagents.name: D2O (H/D exchange on substrate IV)
probe_reagents.observation: Significant ortho-aryl H/D incorporation (8-10%D) on recovered benzoic acid IV and high D incorporation at benzylic/α positions of product 4 (47%D, 31%D, 11%D), indicating reversible C-H metalation

probe_reagents.name: Product 4 resubjected with D2O under electro-Ru catalysis
probe_reagents.observation: 0%D incorporation; [D]n-4 recovered 99% - product is not re-activated, ruling out post-catalysis exchange and confirming on-cycle C-H activation

probe_reagents.name: Product 4 resubjected with D2O under electro-Os catalysis
probe_reagents.observation: 0%D, [D]n-4 99% - same conclusion for Os system

probe_reagents.name: [D]7-I (perdeuterated substrate) intermolecular competition with I
probe_reagents.observation: KIE = 1.1, suggesting C-H cleavage is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: I vs [D]7-I parallel kinetic experiments
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD = 0.3033/0.26 ≈ 1.2; consistent with non-rate-limiting C-H activation

probe_reagents.name: HR-ESI-MS detection of organometallic intermediates
probe_reagents.observation: Detected Os-p-cymene species bound to carboxylate and with cyclometalated naphthoate/alkyne adducts ([M+Na] = 519.0976, 725.2071, 761.2799, and lactone product at 399.1361), supporting metallacycle pathway

10.1002_anie.202110616_sup_1.notes: Both Ru and Os catalysts probed in parallel and give analogous mechanistic behavior (reversible ortho C-H metalation, low KIE, no product re-exchange). High-resolution MS identifies plausible metallacyclic intermediates including carboxylate-bound and alkyne/alkenyl-inserted species (drawn for a related naphthoic acid/alkyne system in Fig S12). Electrochemistry (CCE 4.0 mA, GF anode / Pt cathode) serves to reoxidize the metal rather than to generate substrate-centered radicals, consistent with inner-sphere electrocatalysis.

10.1002_anie.201902315.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [4+2] annulation-rearrangement-aromatization of styrenes to multi-substituted naphthalenes mediated by TBPA, forming polycyclic aromatics from simple styrene building blocks
10.1002_anie.201902315.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of TBPA (tris(4-bromophenyl)amine) to a stable radical-cation TBPA•+, which then oxidizes electron-rich styrene to its radical cation via SET
10.1002_anie.201902315.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes TBPA to TBPA•+, which performs SET on electron-rich styrene to give styrene radical cation 69; a second styrene attacks 69 (radical cyclization) to form 70, deprotonation/further oxidation yields dehydrodimer 68; TBPA•+ oxidizes 68 to radical 73 then cation 74, which undergoes 1,2-aryl (para-anisole) migration to tertiary cation 75, and oxidative aromatization delivers naphthalene 2. Protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: radical cyclization adduct (70) - C-C bonded dimeric cation-radical
rearranged tertiary carbocation (75) after para-anisole migration
cathodic H2 evolution from HFIP/proton reduction

probe_reagents.name: isolated dehydrodimer 68 (intermediate resubjection)
probe_reagents.observation: 68 detected by GC during reaction, isolated in 50% yield; resubjecting 68 to electrolysis gave product 2 in 62% yield, confirming it as an on-cycle intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 68 and of TBPA/1 mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Two anodic peaks for 68 at 1.45 V and 1.90 V (sequential 2e- losses); CV of TBPA + 68 indicates TBPA mediates oxidation of 68, paralleling TBPA/1 behavior

10.1002_anie.201902315.notes: Para-anisole 1,2-migration is favored over methyl migration due to electron-rich aryl, generating the more stable tertiary carbocation 75 prior to aromatization. HFIP serves as proton source/solvent enabling cathodic H2 evolution. Mechanism is proposed without radical-trap experiments (no TEMPO/BHT reported on these pages) or DFT; key support comes from CV and isolation/resubjection of intermediate 68.

10.1002_anie.202207059.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical mediator-induced intermolecular selective (3+2) annulation of allylmalonates with alkynes/alkenes to give cyclopentene/cyclopentane derivatives via radical intermediates
10.1002_anie.202207059.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of mediator (I- to I• or Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+); the oxidized mediator then performs SET on deprotonated allylmalonate to generate a C-centered radical, while H2/methoxide or water is generated at the cathode
10.1002_anie.202207059.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes I- to I• (or Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+); the oxidized mediator removes an electron from the base-deprotonated allylmalonate to form malonyl radical A, which adds to the alkyne to give vinyl radical B, undergoes kinetically favored 5-exo-trig cyclization onto the pendant alkene to give cyclopentenyl radical C, which is trapped by I• (path a, NaI system) or abstracts H from solvent/1,4-CHD (path b, Cp2Fe system); the cathode reduces protons/methanol to H2 and methoxide.
10.1002_anie.202207059.rate_determining_step: Addition of malonyl radical A to phenylacetylene to give vinyl radical B (DFT barrier 14.6 kcal/mol); regioselectivity is set by the kinetically preferred 5-exo-trig cyclization TS-2 vs higher-barrier 6-endo TS-3

key_intermediates.name: malonyl carbon radical A from allylmalonate after deprotonation/SET
alkenyl/vinyl radical B from addition of A to phenylacetylene
cyclopentenyl carbon radical C (5-exo cyclization product)
cyclohexenyl radical D (6-endo cyclization product, thermodynamic)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction almost completely inhibited in both NaI and Cp2Fe systems; trace TEMPO adduct detected by GC-MS

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; no adduct detected

probe_reagents.name: N-iodosuccinimide (NIS)
probe_reagents.observation: No product 7 formed with or without electricity, ruling out I+ as the active species

probe_reagents.name: I2
probe_reagents.observation: No product 7 formed with or without electricity, ruling out molecular I2

probe_reagents.name: dimethyl 2-allyl-2-iodomalonate (1a-I)
probe_reagents.observation: No annulation under standard conditions; only 14% yield when added to anode in divided cell, ruling out 1a-I as a key intermediate

probe_reagents.name: ethyl 2-((phenylsulfonyl)methyl)acrylate
probe_reagents.observation: Trapped alkyl radical C addition product detected by HRMS in Cp2Fe system

probe_reagents.name: 2-methylthiophene
probe_reagents.observation: Trapped radical C arylation product detected by HRMS

probe_reagents.name: 1a-D (deuterated allylmalonate)
probe_reagents.observation: Only non-deuterated product 75 detected; 75-D not observed, indicating the exocyclic methyl H comes from solvent/1,4-CHD rather than from 1a

10.1002_anie.202207059.notes: Two mediator systems give complementary products: NaI affords iodo-cyclopentenes (path a, I• traps radical C); Cp2Fe with 1,4-CHD as H-source affords protodemetalated cyclopentenes (path b, HAT from 1,4-CHD/solvent). DFT shows the 6-endo TS-3 leads to thermodynamically more stable D but has a 5.8 kcal/mol higher barrier than 5-exo TS-2, so selectivity is kinetic. CV confirms catalytic current of mediator + 1a + base. EPR signal (g=2.0073, AαN=14.30 G, AβH=23.60 G) supports a trapped carbon radical.

10.1002_anie.202117188.rxn_class_summary: Rhodium(III)-catalyzed electrooxidative C8–H alkenylation of 1-naphthols (and hydroxyquinolines) with styrenes under anodic conditions
10.1002_anie.202117188.electrochemical_role: Anode reoxidizes the reduced Rh(II) species (and Rh(III)→Rh(IV) en route to reductive elimination), generating H2 at the cathode; electricity acts beyond a simple sacrificial oxidant, with current-dependent rate indicating turnover-limiting electron transfer
10.1002_anie.202117188.electron_flow: Rh(III) carboxylate performs concerted metalation–deprotonation at C8 of deprotonated 1-naphthol to form a rhodacycle; after styrene coordination and migratory insertion, anodic oxidation lifts Rh(III) to Rh(IV), enabling facile reductive elimination/β-H elimination to release the alkenylated product and Rh(II), which is reoxidized at the anode while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.
10.1002_anie.202117188.rate_determining_step: Turnover-limiting single-electron transfer (anodic oxidation of the Rh intermediate), supported by strong current dependence and negligible KIE

key_intermediates.name: Cp*Rh(OPiv)2 / L-Rh(OPiv) active catalyst (species A precursor)
Five-membered peri-cyclorhodated naphtholate rhodacycle A (Cp*Rh(III) bound via O and C8)
Seven-membered alkyl–Rh(IV) rhodacycle C (after migratory insertion and anodic oxidation)
Cp*Rh(II) species D (post β-H elimination, prior to anodic reoxidation)

probe_reagents.name: D2O co-solvent (H/D exchange on 1-naphthol 1a)
probe_reagents.observation: Reversible C8 and C2 H/D scrambling with Rh catalyst (no alkene); only C2 scrambling without Rh; with alkene 2a, irreversible C–C bond formation and selective C8 functionalization

probe_reagents.name: Deuterated 1-naphthol (KIE study with 2e)
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 1.1, indicating C–H metalation is fast and not rate-limiting

probe_reagents.name: Competition alkenes 2a (Me) vs 2m (CF3)
probe_reagents.observation: 3aa:3am = 1.00:2.33, electron-poor alkene preferred

probe_reagents.name: Variable current (CCE = 2, 4, 6 mA)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction rate scales strongly with current (ρ = 0.074, 0.102, 0.183), consistent with turnover-limiting ET

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric Rh(III) (50 mol%) with 1a + 2l, no current
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3al obtained in only 40% yield, showing electricity has a role beyond sacrificial oxidation

10.1002_anie.202117188.notes: DFT (PW6B95-D4/def2-TZVP+SMD(i-PrOH)//PBE0-D3BJ/def2-SVP) compared Rh(III)/Rh(I) vs Rh(IV)/Rh(II) reductive elimination manifolds; the Rh(IV/II) pathway is preferred by 2.1 kcal/mol and gives a more stable Rh(II) intermediate (17.7 kcal/mol). The alternative Rh(III)/Rh(I) RE pathway is disfavored. Substrate 1d gave oxa-Michael and oxidative C–H/O–H cyclization products (pyranoquinolines 6) instead of alkenylation, indicating a competing cyclization pathway with hydroxyquinolines.

10.1002_anie.202406017_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-allyl benzamides with CF3SO2Na to access 3,4-dihydroisoquinolin-1(2H)-ones via anodic CF3 radical generation and subsequent radical cyclization with decarbonylative/dearomative rearrangement
10.1002_anie.202406017_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2- (Langlois reagent 2a) to generate CF3 radical (with loss of SO2); subsequent anodic oxidations of cyclohexadienyl/aminyl radical intermediates to cations that are trapped by MeOH or undergo intramolecular SNAr-type rearrangement
10.1002_anie.202406017_sup_1.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3 radical (with SO2 extrusion), which adds to the allyl alkene of the N-acyl substrate to give a C-radical B; intramolecular ipso-addition onto the aryl ring forms spirocyclic radical C (5-exo), which rearranges to the dihydroisoquinolinone-type radical C' via neophyl-like C-C migration; C' is oxidized at the anode to cation G, and deprotonation/aromatization affords the dihydroisoquinolinone. With N-OMe substrates, the carbamoyl cation E is intercepted by MeOH to give carbamate F, accounting for the rearranged ester byproduct S1.
10.1002_anie.202406017_sup_1.rate_determining_step: Radical cyclization/rearrangement step TS_BC (5-exo ipso-spirocyclization, ~16.4 kcal/mol for Ts; ~8.5 kcal/mol for t-Bu by DLPNO-CCSD(T)) is the highest barrier on the productive pathway

key_intermediates.name: Carbon-centered radical B (CF3-addition adduct to allyl alkene)
Spirocyclic cyclohexadienyl radical C (5-exo ipso-cyclization adduct)
Ring-expanded dihydroisoquinolinone radical C' (6-endo / decarbonylative-rearranged adduct)
Carbamoyl/N-acyliminium-type cation E (after second anodic oxidation)
Methoxy carbamate cation F (MeOH addition product)
N-acyl oxazolidinium intermediate (from N-OMe substrate 1d, leading to benzoate ester S1)

probe_reagents.name: N-OMe allyl benzamide 1d
probe_reagents.observation: Formation of rearranged benzoic ester S1 (29%) along with cyclized 4d (10%) supports trapping of a carbamoyl/N-acyliminium cation by intramolecular OMe migration via an oxazolidinium intermediate

probe_reagents.name: MeOH co-solvent (with N-Ts and N-t-Bu substrates 1ab/1ae)
probe_reagents.observation: With Ts (1ab): linear methoxycarbonyl product 5b (27%) and cyclized 4b (24%); with t-Bu (1ae): 5e (63%) and no cyclized 4e, indicating that the cation intermediate is intercepted by MeOH and that the protecting group governs partitioning between rearrangement and trapping

probe_reagents.name: DFT (B3LYP-D3/def2-SV(P); DLPNO-CCSD(T)/def2-TZVPP)
probe_reagents.observation: Computed competitive Truce-Smiles (desulfonylative) pathway TS_AB' is 6.8 kcal/mol above the productive decarbonylative TS_BC; C-C cleavage of spirocycle C to C' has ~70 kcal/mol barrier, ruling out direct radical ring expansion and supporting a stepwise cation-mediated rearrangement

10.1002_anie.202406017_sup_1.notes: A competitive Truce-Smiles (desulfonylative) rearrangement was considered but is disfavored by DFT (TS_AB' 6.8 kcal/mol above TS_BC). Direct radical-radical C-C bond cleavage from spirocycle C to ring-expanded radical C' is energetically prohibitive (~70 kcal/mol), so rearrangement is proposed to occur via the cation manifold after a second anodic oxidation (E -> F or E -> G). The N-protecting group strongly biases the outcome: t-Bu favors open-chain methoxycarbonyl product, Ts allows cyclization to dihydroisoquinolinone.

10.1002_anie.202304434_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [3+2] cycloaddition of styrenes with nitromethane to form 4,5-dihydroisoxazole N-oxides (oximes) via in situ generation of a 1,2,5-oxadiazole 2-oxide intermediate
10.1002_anie.202304434_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (n-Bu4NI) to I2/I., which mediates HAT from deprotonated nitromethane (CH2=NO2-, intermediate II) to generate fulminate-like HC≡N+-O- (intermediate III); cathode regenerates iodide and produces H2
10.1002_anie.202304434_sup_1.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes iodide to I./I2, which abstracts H from the t-BuOK-deprotonated nitromethane (II) via HAT to give the nitrile-oxide-like intermediate III; two molecules of III undergo self [3+2] cycloaddition to furoxan IV, which then performs a [3+2] cycloaddition with styrene to afford the dihydroisoxazole N-oxide product. Cathode reduces protons to H2 and regenerates I-.
10.1002_anie.202304434_sup_1.rate_determining_step: HAT from intermediate II (aci-nitromethane) by iodine radical to form nitrile oxide III (inverse KIE ~0.74 observed, consistent with sp3→sp rehybridization at the HAT step)

probe_reagents.name: styrene-d8
probe_reagents.observation: 2a-d8 isolated in 87-91% yield; deuterium fully retained in product, confirming styrene is incorporated intact via [3+2]

probe_reagents.name: CD3NO2 / CH3NO2 competition
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD = 0.74 (avg of 4 runs: 0.72, 0.75, 0.72, 0.75), inverse secondary KIE consistent with C-H/D cleavage at nitromethane and sp3→sp rehybridization

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Dihydroisoxazole product 4e isolated in 60%; a 1,1-diphenyl-2-CF3 alkene byproduct detected by HRMS, indicating .CF3 radical (from CF3CO2-) is generated and trapped

probe_reagents.name: litmus paper / limewater headspace test
probe_reagents.observation: Litmus turns red and limewater becomes turbid over time, indicating CO2 evolution (from Kolbe-type decarboxylation of CF3CO2-) and acidic gas release

probe_reagents.name: HRMS detection of furoxan IV
probe_reagents.observation: ESI-HRMS m/z (M+Na)+ calcd 109.0008, obsd 109.0009, confirming 1,2,5-oxadiazole 2-oxide IV is present in the reaction mixture; trapping with styrene after electrolysis gave 2a in 25-30% yield

10.1002_anie.202304434_sup_1.notes: DFT compared three HAT pathways from II to III: by I. (ΔH‡ = 51.3 kJ/mol, most favorable), by I2 (94.8 kJ/mol), and by .CF3 (79.1 kJ/mol), supporting iodine-radical-mediated HAT as the operative pathway. Self [3+2] of two III to furoxan IV has ΔH‡ ≈ 112 kJ/mol; subsequent [3+2] of IV with styrene has ΔH‡ ≈ 109 kJ/mol. Alternative direct nitrile-oxide + alkene [3+2] without furoxan intermediate is disfavored relative to the observed pathway through IV. CF3CO2Na acts as electrolyte and undergoes some Kolbe decarboxylation (CO2 + .CF3 observed) but .CF3 is not the dominant HAT agent.

10.1002_anie.202012209.rxn_class_summary: Anode-material-tuned electrochemical oxidative [4+2] annulation or dioxygenation of olefins with hydroxamic acids to give 3,4-dihydro-1H-benzo[c][1,2]oxazines or 1,2-dioxygenated alkenes
10.1002_anie.202012209.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of hydroxamic acid (and deprotonation) generates an O-centered amidoxyl radical; electrode material (RVC vs C felt) dictates whether the intermediate C-radical is further oxidized to a cation (RVC, annulation) or trapped by a second amidoxyl radical (C felt, dioxygenation). For indole substrates, both hydroxamic acid and indole are simultaneously anodically oxidized to amidoxyl radical and indole radical cation respectively.
10.1002_anie.202012209.electron_flow: Anode performs single-electron oxidation of the hydroxamic acid; after -H+ the resulting amidoxyl O-radical adds to the olefin to give a C-radical. On RVC the C-radical is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation that undergoes intramolecular electrophilic substitution (IES) onto the N-aryl ring giving the benzo[c][1,2]oxazine; on C felt, the C-radical is instead intercepted by a second amidoxyl radical giving the 1,2-dioxygenated product. Cathode reduces protons to H2.
10.1002_anie.202012209.rate_determining_step: Not the C-H cleavage of the phenyl moiety of the hydroxamic acid (KIE ≈ 1.0 with styrene); likely the amidoxyl radical addition / anodic oxidation step

key_intermediates.name: C-centered radical II from amidoxyl addition to olefin
benzylic/alkyl carbocation III (after further anodic oxidation on RVC)
indole radical cation IV (for N-pivaloyl indole substrates)
radical-radical-coupling cation V (amidoxyl + indole radical cation)
MNP-trapped aminoxyl adduct Rc (spin-trap nitroxide of C-radical)

probe_reagents.name: a1-D5 (deuterated hydroxamic acid) competition KIE with styrene b1
probe_reagents.observation: KIE = 1.0; phenyl C-H cleavage of hydroxamic acid is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: a1-D5 competition KIE with norbornene b22
a1-D5 competition KIE with N-pivaloyl indole b24
2-methyl-2-nitrosopropane (MNP)
probe_reagents.observation: Spin-trapped a transient C-centered radical giving sextuple EPR signal of aminoxyl Rc (aN=15.31 G, aHβ=3.46 G, g=2.0060), confirming amidoxyl addition onto styrene

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of a1, b1, b22, b24
probe_reagents.observation: a1 oxidized at 1.32 V; b1 and b22 not oxidized within 2 V (only a1 oxidized); b24 oxidized at 1.58 V (both a1 and b24 oxidized simultaneously); reductive peak of amidoxyl radical at 0.78 V disappears upon adding b1 or b24, indicating interception by olefin/indole

probe_reagents.name: Constant-potential electrolysis at +1.32 V (a1 + b1, undivided RVC/Pt cell)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave annulation product c1 (35%) and dioxygenation product d1 (20%), confirming both pathways branch from the same C-radical

probe_reagents.name: EPR of electrolyzed a1
probe_reagents.observation: Triplet signal (aN=8.03 G, g=2.0058) assigned to metastable amidoxyl radical; signal decreases on adding styrene

10.1002_anie.202012209.notes: Anode-material selectivity is rationalized as easier further oxidation of the C-radical to cation on RVC than on C felt; on C felt the C-radical instead couples with a second amidoxyl radical. For indole substrate b24, because its oxidation potential is close to that of a1, a radical-radical-coupling (RCC) pathway between amidoxyl radical and indole radical cation V is proposed rather than (or in addition to) amidoxyl radical addition followed by oxidation. Indole was unsuitable for dioxygenation. The MNP spin-adduct Rc is an off-pathway diagnostic species, not a productive intermediate.

10.1002_anie.202218533_sup_3.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Co-catalyzed enantioselective C-H activation forming an isolable chiral cobaltacycle intermediate via anodic oxidation
10.1002_anie.202218533_sup_3.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (RVC anode, CPE @ 1.5 V vs Ag+/Ag) oxidizes Co(II) to higher-valent Co(III)/Co species enabling C-H metalation and formation of the cobaltacycle
10.1002_anie.202218533_sup_3.electron_flow: Co(OAc)2 coordinates with the (R)-Salox ligand L1-7 and benzamide 1a; anodic oxidation at the RVC electrode oxidizes Co(II) to Co(III), which enables concerted metalation-deprotonation (CMD) of the ortho C-H bond (assisted by pivalate) to generate the isolable five-membered chiral cobaltacycle (RC)-Co-1. Protons are reduced at the Pt cathode.
10.1002_anie.202218533_sup_3.rate_determining_step: C-H metalation (CMD) at the high-valent Co center

key_intermediates.name: Chiral cobaltacycle (RC)-Co-1 with Salox ligand, benzamide substrate, and quinoline coordination
High-valent Co(III) species after anodic oxidation

probe_reagents.name: Isolation of cobaltacycle (RC)-Co-1
probe_reagents.observation: Cobaltacycle isolated in 28% yield (50.3 mg) as dark yellow solid, characterized by 1H/13C NMR and HRMS ([M+Na]+ calcd 622.1511, found 622.1507), confirming chiral C-H metalated Co species

10.1002_anie.202218533_sup_3.notes: Only the synthesis/characterization of the chiral cobaltacycle intermediate via constant potential electrolysis is shown on this page; subsequent mechanistic experiments (catalytic competence, KIE, DFT) may appear elsewhere in the SI but are not present here. NaOPiv serves as the carboxylate base assisting CMD.

10.1002_ange.201913332.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical four-component 1,2-difunctionalization of arylalkenes with carboxylic acids, acetonitrile, and alcohols to form functionalized imides via a cascade Mumm rearrangement
10.1002_ange.201913332.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of styrene generates a radical cation; cathodic reduction of methanol (and of PhCOOH) generates methoxide (and benzoate) nucleophiles plus H2; a second anodic oxidation converts the alpha-methoxy benzyl radical to a benzyl cation
10.1002_ange.201913332.electron_flow: At the anode, styrene is oxidized by SET to a radical cation, which is trapped by methoxide (generated by cathodic reduction of MeOH) to give a C-radical; a second anodic SET produces a benzyl cation that is intercepted by CH3CN (Ritter) to form a nitrilium; capture by cathodically generated benzoate gives an O-acyl isoamide that undergoes intramolecular Mumm rearrangement to the imide.
10.1002_ange.201913332.rate_determining_step: Neither MeO incorporation nor CH3CN incorporation is rate-determining (parallel CH3OH/CD3OD and CH3CN/CD3CN experiments both gave 1:1 product ratios); likely the initial anodic oxidation of styrene

key_intermediates.name: nitrilium ion / Ritter-type cation from CH3CN trapping (D)

probe_reagents.name: CD3OD (vs CH3OH)
probe_reagents.observation: Parallel CH3OH/CD3OD reaction gave 3ao/d3-3ao = 1:1 (84% combined), indicating MeO incorporation is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: CD3CN (vs CH3CN)
probe_reagents.observation: Parallel CH3CN/CD3CN reaction gave 3ao/d3-3ao = 1:1 (82% combined), indicating CH3CN incorporation is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: [18O]-4-cyanobenzoic acid
probe_reagents.observation: 18O is incorporated into the imide carbonyl of [18O]-3ak, confirming the acid oxygen ends up in the imide and supporting a Mumm rearrangement from an O-acyl isoamide

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Styrene has the lowest oxidation potential (1.60 V vs SCE) versus MeOH and benzoic acid (no peak 0-2.5 V), supporting initial SET oxidation of styrene

10.1002_ange.201913332.notes: The paper highlights generation of an unstable O-acyl isoamide (E) as a rare in situ precursor for a 1,3-(O→N) acyl Mumm rearrangement. Authors compare against alternative literature electrochemical alkene difunctionalization manifolds (Scheme 1, paths a-f: external-oxidant radical addition, direct anodic oxidation of substrate vs alkene, and mediator-based variants); the favored pathway here is direct anodic oxidation of the alkene to the radical cation (path d-type) coupled with cathodic generation of nucleophiles (paired electrolysis).

10.1002_anie.202425634.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical cyclopropanation of unactivated alkenes with active methylene compounds (malonates) using ferrocene as a redox mediator
10.1002_anie.202425634.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of ferrocene (Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+) which then oxidizes the malonate (deprotonated by cathodically generated OH-) to the alpha-carbonyl radical; cathode reduces water/protons to generate H2 and OH- base in situ
10.1002_anie.202425634.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+, which oxidizes the malonate (deprotonated by cathodically generated OH-) to alpha-carbonyl radical A; A adds anti-Markovnikov to the alkene to give alkyl radical B, which is trapped by iodide (from anodic I-/I. cycle) to give beta-iodide 96; intramolecular SN2 closure by the malonyl carbanion expels iodide to yield the cyclopropane. Cathode reduces water to H2 + OH-.

key_intermediates.name: secondary alkyl radical adduct B (anti-Markovnikov C-radical from alkene addition)
iodide / iodine (I- to I. via anodic oxidation)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclopropane 1 not formed; TEMPO adducts 91 (malonyl-TEMPO) and 92 (alkyl-TEMPO from radical addition to alkene) detected by HRMS; 91 isolated in 88% yield in absence of alkene

probe_reagents.name: PBN (N-tert-butyl-alpha-phenylnitrone)
