key_intermediates.name: alpha-iodoamine / iminium iodide pair from XAT
alkyl radical (primary, secondary, or tertiary) from alkyl iodide
alpha-EWG carbon-centered radical after Giese addition

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropylmethyl iodide radical clock (substrate 88)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 89 obtained in 36% yield with only 12% unrearranged 90, confirming a free alkyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: Deuterated product 91 in 89% yield with >95% D incorporation at the alpha-EWG position, indicating cathodic reduction to a carbanion that is protonated by water

probe_reagents.name: tethered alkene radical probe (substrate 85)
probe_reagents.observation: 5-exo cyclization to pyrrolidine 86 (with intermolecular Giese products 87a/87b when external acceptor added), supporting intramolecular radical addition pathway

probe_reagents.name: amine screen (Et3N, iPr2NEt, DABCO, TMP, iPr2NH, Bn3N, Ph2NPMP, 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine)
probe_reagents.observation: Only alkylamines with secondary alpha-H and suitable Eox (~+0.67 to +0.77 V) gave high yields; amines lacking alpha-H gave no reaction, supporting alpha-aminoalkyl radical as the XAT agent

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of Et3N vs alkyl iodides/acrylates
probe_reagents.observation: Et3N oxidizes at much lower potential than reduction of alkyl iodides or Michael acceptors, ruling out direct SET to substrate and supporting mediated XAT

10.1038_s41467-023-42566-y.notes: Authors cite prior DFT (ref 37) showing the XAT step proceeds via a polarized transition state with charge-transfer character; no new DFT performed here. Method avoids sacrificial anodes by replacing cathodic reduction of R-I with anodic-mediator XAT, with cathode reserved for the radical-polar crossover step (reduction of the Giese adduct radical to a carbanion).

10.1038_s41467-018-03246-4.rxn_class_summary: Cobalt-catalyzed electrooxidative dehydrogenative C-H/N-H [4+2] annulation of aromatic/vinyl amides with ethylene or ethyne to give dihydroisoquinolinones/isoquinolinones
10.1038_s41467-018-03246-4.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of substrate-coordinated Co(II) complex to Co(III) (and later Co(I) to Co(II)) regenerates the active high-valent cobalt catalyst, replacing a chemical oxidant
10.1038_s41467-018-03246-4.electron_flow: Co(II) binds the bidentate amide with pivalate assistance; the anode oxidizes this Co(II) complex to Co(III), which performs intramolecular C-H activation (CMD with pivalate); ethylene/ethyne insertion followed by C-N reductive elimination releases product and Co(I), which is re-oxidized at the anode to Co(II); protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.
10.1038_s41467-018-03246-4.rate_determining_step: Electrochemical oxidation of the coordinated Co(II) complex to Co(III) (C-H cleavage is kinetically relevant but not rate-determining; only secondary KIE in parallel experiments)

key_intermediates.name: Co(II)-amide complex (bidentate N,N-coordinated, 8-aminoquinoline directing group) - complex A
Co(III)-amide complex B formed by anodic oxidation of A
Cyclic Co(III) metallacycle C after C-H activation (CMD with pivalate)
Co(III) alkyl intermediate D after ethylene insertion
Co(I) species after reductive elimination, reoxidized at anode to Co(II)

probe_reagents.name: [D5]-1a (pentadeuterio benzamide) intermolecular competition
probe_reagents.observation: 3aa:[D4]-3aa = 3.5:1 in intermolecular competition, indicating C-H cleavage is kinetically relevant

probe_reagents.name: [D5]-1a parallel reactions
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD = 1.4 (small, secondary); C-H cleavage is a slow step but not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, Co(acac)2, and mixture
probe_reagents.observation: 1a oxidation at 1.47 V; Co(acac)2 at 1.12 and 1.53 V; Co(acac)2+1a shows stronger peak at 1.13 V indicating Co(II) is oxidized first; potential-controlled electrolysis at 1.13 V gave 3aa in 70% yield

probe_reagents.name: Kinetic order studies (variation of [1a], [NaOPiv], [Co], current)
probe_reagents.observation: Rate independent of [1a], [NaOPiv], and [Co]; rate depends on current, indicating electrochemical oxidation is rate-limiting

probe_reagents.name: GC headspace analysis
probe_reagents.observation: Large amount of H2 detected, confirming cathodic proton reduction

10.1038_s41467-018-03246-4.notes: Authors invoke prior EXAFS evidence (ref 31) that anodic oxidation of Co(acac)2 generates Co(III). Pivalate (HOPiv/NaOPiv) acts as CMD base for C-H activation. 8-Aminoquinoline (Q) bidentate directing group is essential. Induction period observed in most runs. Cathodic half-reaction is H2 evolution.

10.1038_s41467-022-32084-8_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electro-oxidative three-component 1,2-amino oxygenation of alkenes with sulfonamides and trifluoroethanol (TFE) to give β-amino alcohol derivatives via N-centered radicals
10.1038_s41467-022-32084-8_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation: DBU is electroactive and shuttles electrons from the sulfonamide anion (deprotonated by DBU) to the anode, generating an N-centered radical (NCR); the benzylic carbon radical formed after addition to alkene is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation that is trapped by TFE
10.1038_s41467-022-32084-8_sup_1.electron_flow: DBU deprotonates the sulfonamide and is oxidized at the anode; electron transfer from sulfonamide anion to oxidized DBU generates an N-centered radical that adds to the alkene; the resulting benzylic radical is anodically oxidized to a cation and trapped by TFE to give the β-amino ether, with H2 evolved at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: Benzylic carbon radical from NCR addition to styrene

probe_reagents.name: Aliphatic sulfonamide 10 (non-benzylic competitive substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard conditions with 3 equiv styrene, intermolecular 1,2-amino oxygenation product 10a formed in 15% while intramolecular pyrrolidine 10b (from 5-exo cyclization of NCR onto pendant alkene) formed in 41%, supporting an N-centered radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: DBU titration (0–2.0 equiv)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 4aa increased monotonically with DBU loading (0→76% at 2.0 equiv), consistent with DBU acting as base and electroactive mediator

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of DBU/1a/2aa
probe_reagents.observation: DBU shows catalytic current that increases with added sulfonamide 1a; 1a alone shows no oxidation peak 0–2.5 V; styrene 2aa is electroactive but EDG-styrenes give low yields, arguing against direct alkene oxidation as the initiation step

10.1038_s41467-022-32084-8_sup_1.notes: Reviewers questioned the rigor of competitive experiments (benzylic C–H of substrate 10 could in principle be oxidized to a cation rather than going through NCR); authors addressed by using an aliphatic substrate where intramolecular 5-exo cyclization (10b, 41%) provides evidence for the NCR pathway. Reviewer #3 proposed an alternative: anodic oxidation of tosylamide anion directly to N-radical without DBU mediation. CV data indicate DBU mediation is operative rather than direct sulfonamide oxidation.

10.1038_s41467-024-47168-w_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical hydrogenation of unactivated alkyl alkenes using catalytic chlorosilanes as a Si-based H-carrier with H2O as the terminal hydrogen source, mediated by Fe (sacrificial anode) and Ni cocatalysis.
10.1038_s41467-024-47168-w_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of Ph3SiCl to a silyl anion (via silyl radical) that abstracts H from H2O to give Ph3SiH; concurrently the sacrificial Fe anode releases Fe(II/III) salts that enter an Fe-H catalytic cycle delivering H to alkene; Ni(II) precatalyst (NiBr2·dtbpy) provides a parallel Ni-H pathway with opposite regioselectivity.
10.1038_s41467-024-47168-w_sup_1.electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ph3SiCl (via Si radical to silyl anion) which is protonated by H2O to give Ph3SiH; Ph3SiH transfers H to an Fe(II)-X species generated from the sacrificial Fe anode, forming Fe-H that inserts into the alkene; hydrolysis of the resulting Fe-alkyl by H2O releases alkane and Fe-OH, which is reduced back into the cycle, while Ph3SiOH (resting Si species) is recycled via fluorination/chlorination back to Ph3SiX.

probe_reagents.name: D2O / CF3CH2OD
probe_reagents.observation: Deuterium incorporation in product confirms H2O/ROH is the H-source; inverse D-ratio between Fe-H and Ni-H regimes supports two parallel metal-hydride cycles.

probe_reagents.name: CF3CH2OH (replacing H2O)
probe_reagents.observation: Ph3SiOCH2CF3 detected by 19F NMR, confirming silanol/silyl-ether intermediates; reaction still proceeds in 90% yield.

probe_reagents.name: (TMS)2O
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows E1/2 = -3.17 V vs Ag/Ag+; under electrolysis converts to TMSOCH2CF3, supporting Si-O cleavage and re-entry to Si cycle.

probe_reagents.name: GC-MS detection of Ph3SiOH
probe_reagents.observation: Significant Ph3SiOH peak at 15.6 min after 5 h electrolysis, identifying silanol as the stable Si resting species.

probe_reagents.name: PhMe2SiCl (1.0-2.0 equiv)
probe_reagents.observation: Disilane Ph(Me)2Si-Si(Me)2Ph isolated (56-58%) alongside hydrogenation product (15-20%), evidencing silyl-anion intermediate via nucleophilic Si-Si coupling.

probe_reagents.name: Fe(BF4)2·6H2O / FeCl3 (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Stoichiometric mixing with Ph3SiH and alkene gave 0% alkane (98% recovered SM), showing that electrochemistry is indispensable for generating the active Fe species.

probe_reagents.name: H2O loading screen (0, 1, 2, 3, 5 equiv)
probe_reagents.observation: Sharply non-monotonic: 0 equiv 92% (H from DMF), 1/2/5 equiv 0%, 3 equiv 92%; consistent with a narrow optimum balancing H-transfer vs. silane hydrolysis.

10.1038_s41467-024-47168-w_sup_1.notes: Authors favor a homogeneous dual Fe/Ni-hydride cycle with a Si-shuttle (Ph3SiCl -> Ph3Si- -> Ph3SiH -> Ph3SiOH -> back to Ph3SiX). Reviewers raised serious alternatives: (i) heterogeneous Fe/Ni nanoparticle hydrogenation from electrodeposited Fe on Ni foam, and (ii) hydroxysilane (Ph3SiOH) rather than chlorosilane being the true active mediator. The stoichiometric Fe(BF4)2/FeCl3 + Ph3SiH controls gave no alkane, which the authors attribute to inability to mimic the anodically generated Fe species but reviewers argue is consistent with heterogeneous catalysis. Ni-H and Fe-H proposed to give opposite regioselectivity (branch vs linear), supported by DFT (Figures S2/S3). Disilane formation at high chlorosilane loading is rationalized as off-pathway Si-Si coupling of silyl anion with chlorosilane.

10.1038_s41467-019-13024-5.rxn_class_summary: Diastereoselective electrocatalytic 1,2-diamination of di- and tri-substituted alkenes with sulfamides using a triarylamine redox mediator, producing cyclic sulfamides with H2 evolution at the cathode
10.1038_s41467-019-13024-5.electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes tris(2,4-dibromophenyl)amine mediator to its aminium radical cation, which performs outer-sphere SET oxidation of the alkene to a radical cation; cathode reduces protons to H2
10.1038_s41467-019-13024-5.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes triarylamine 1 to NAr3•+, which performs SET oxidation of the alkene to a radical cation; the sulfamide N–H adds to give a β-amino C-radical, which is oxidized by a second equivalent of NAr3•+ to a carbocation that cyclizes diastereoselectively to the 5-membered cyclic sulfamide. Protons released are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: β-amino carbon radical (IV) from sulfamide addition to alkene radical cation
β-amino carbocation (V) after second SET oxidation

probe_reagents.name: triarylamine redox catalysts of varying Ep/2 (1, 6, 7, 8)
probe_reagents.observation: Higher-potential mediator 1 (Ep/2 = 1.48 V vs SCE) gave product; lower-potential analogs 6–8 (1.06–1.33 V) were less effective, consistent with need to oxidize alkene (Ep/2 = 1.66–1.80 V)

probe_reagents.name: pure E- vs Z-isomer of alkene 2
probe_reagents.observation: Both isomers converged to the same trans diastereomer 5 (>20:1 dr; 65% and 69% yield) supporting a stepwise radical-cation pathway with stereochemistry set during cyclization

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1, 2, 3 (and mixtures)
probe_reagents.observation: Catalytic current enhancement upon mixing 1 with alkenes 2 and 3 confirms mediator-based electrocatalytic oxidation of the alkenes

10.1038_s41467-019-13024-5.notes: Stereoselectivity is rationalized by cyclization of cationic intermediate V placing R1/R2 trans on the nascent 5-membered ring; no radical traps (TEMPO/BHT), KIE, or DFT are explicitly shown on these pages. Alternative direct anodic oxidation of alkene is disfavored because of self-dimerization and electrode-surface reactivity, motivating the indirect (mediated) electrolysis.

10.1038_s41467-024-52248-y.rxn_class_summary: Enantioselective photoelectrocatalytic three-component alkylarylation of olefins with aliphatic C(sp3)-H bonds and aryl halides via paired Fe/Ni dual catalysis in an undivided cell
10.1038_s41467-024-52248-y.electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes Fe(II) to Fe(III) generating [FeIIICl4]- which under light undergoes LMCT to release a chlorine radical for HAT on the alkane; cathode reduces Ni(II) precatalyst to Ni(I) to initiate the Ni cross-coupling cycle and reduce Ni(III) back to Ni(I)
10.1038_s41467-024-52248-y.electron_flow: At the anode Fe(II) is oxidized to Fe(III) ([FeIIICl4]-), which upon photoexcitation undergoes LMCT to release Cl• that abstracts H from the alkane to give an alkyl radical; this radical adds to the acrylate to form a secondary C-radical that is captured by an aryl-Ni(II) (formed from cathodic reduction of Ni(II) to Ni(I) followed by oxidative addition with ArX) to give Ni(III)(aryl)(alkyl), which reductively eliminates the product and the Ni(I) is regenerated via further cathodic reduction.
10.1038_s41467-024-52248-y.rate_determining_step: Electron transfer at the electrodes (first-order dependence of rate on current)

probe_reagents.name: 1-methylnaphthalene (no Ni catalyst)
probe_reagents.observation: 1-chloromethylnaphthalene 34 isolated in 27% yield, confirming an electrophilic Cl radical generated via LMCT activates C(sp3)-H by HAT

probe_reagents.name: chlorocycloheptane + cyclohexane co-addition
probe_reagents.observation: Only product 1 (from cyclohexane) was observed; product 17 from alkyl chloride not detected, excluding alkyl chlorides as intermediates

probe_reagents.name: stoichiometric Ar-Ni(II)-Br complex (Ac-C6H4-Ni(II)-Br with L1)
probe_reagents.observation: Reacted with cyclohexane and tert-butyl acrylate under FeCl3/light to give product 1 in 31% yield, 91% ee — supports aryl-Ni(II) as a productive intermediate

probe_reagents.name: electrode potential monitoring
probe_reagents.observation: Anode held at ~+0.28 V vs Ag/AgCl (Fe(II)/Fe(III)); cathode at ~-0.83 V vs Ag/AgCl, sufficient to reduce ArNi(III) to ArNi(II) but not ArNi(II) to ArNi(I)

probe_reagents.name: nonlinear effects with scalemic L1
probe_reagents.observation: Nonlinear relationship between ee of product 1 and ee of L1, suggesting ≥2 chiral BiIm ligands involved in the enantio-determining step

probe_reagents.name: ee vs time monitoring
probe_reagents.observation: Product ee constant throughout reaction — rules out product deracemization, stereoconvergent mechanism

probe_reagents.name: current-dependence kinetics
probe_reagents.observation: First-order dependence of rate on current (y=0.192x+3.06, R2=0.98), indicating electron transfer at electrodes is rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: control without FeCl3 or Ni(NO3)2
probe_reagents.observation: No product detected — both catalysts required

probe_reagents.name: control without electricity or light
probe_reagents.observation: No product detected — both inputs required

10.1038_s41467-024-52248-y.notes: Paired electrolysis: anodic Fe(II)/Fe(III) photo-LMCT cycle generates Cl• for HAT while cathodic Ni(II)→Ni(I) initiates Ni cross-coupling; chiral BiIm ligand L1 (R=sBu, Ar=3,5-tBu2-C6H3) on Ni controls enantioselectivity. Nonlinear effects suggest two ligands operative in the stereo-determining step. Aryl chlorides also viable when cathode potential is tuned (-2.0 V vs Ag/AgCl, constant-potential electrolysis with KBr additive).

10.1038_s41467-023-42566-y_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical halogen-atom transfer (e-XAT) alkylation of alkyl iodides with electron-deficient alkenes, mediated by anodically generated α-aminoalkyl radicals from Et3N
10.1038_s41467-023-42566-y_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic one-electron oxidation of trialkylamine (Et3N) to its aminium radical cation, which deprotonates to an α-aminoalkyl radical that performs XAT on alkyl iodide; cathode reduces the alkene/arene radical acceptor or protons in the productive cycle
10.1038_s41467-023-42566-y_sup_1.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Et3N by one electron to its radical cation, which loses an α-H to give an α-aminoalkyl radical; this radical performs halogen-atom transfer with R-I to release an alkyl radical that adds to the electron-deficient alkene, and the resulting α-acceptor radical is reduced at the cathode and protonated by H2O to give the alkylation product.
10.1038_s41467-023-42566-y_sup_1.rate_determining_step: Anodic one-electron oxidation/deprotonation of the alkylamine to generate the α-aminoalkyl radical (XAT step is fast and exothermic per DFT)

key_intermediates.name: iminium cation (V) / enamine-like (VI) from second oxidation

probe_reagents.name: TMSCN (iminium trap)
probe_reagents.observation: No cyanation product observed with Et3N, i-Pr2NEt, or 1-methylpiperidine in absence of R-I — argues against free iminium V

probe_reagents.name: CH3OH (iminium trap)
probe_reagents.observation: No α-methoxyamine product observed — argues against iminium intermediate V

probe_reagents.name: acrylonitrile (radical acceptor without R-I)
probe_reagents.observation: No α-aminoalkyl radical addition product; ascribed to inefficient cathodic reduction/protonation of adduct

probe_reagents.name: Boc-protected dehydroalanine
probe_reagents.observation: No trapping product

probe_reagents.name: 1,4-dicyanobenzene
probe_reagents.observation: Without H2O and without R-I: α-aminoalkyl radical from Et3N/i-Pr2NEt is captured by cathodically generated radical anion of 1,4-DCB to give cross-coupled products 92–94 (72–86%); with R-I added, alkyl-DCB product 95 (42%) is obtained and 92 is not observed, indicating XAT outcompetes direct radical trapping

probe_reagents.name: Et3N omission control
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely abolished without Et3N across all anode materials

probe_reagents.name: Amines lacking α-H (Ph2N-PMP, 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine)
probe_reagents.observation: No reactivity — confirms α-H/α-aminoalkyl radical is essential

probe_reagents.name: 2,2,6,6-tetramethyl-N-methylpiperidine (only primary α-H)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave product in 32% yield, supporting α-aminoalkyl radical pathway and selectivity for secondary α-H

10.1038_s41467-023-42566-y_sup_1.notes: Alternative mechanism considered and disfavored: classical Shono-type two-electron oxidation to iminium V then nucleophilic addition. Control trapping with TMSCN/CH3OH gave no addition products, and dedicated radical-trapping with 1,4-dicyanobenzene confirmed α-aminoalkyl radical generation and rapid XAT with alkyl iodides. RVC anode is optimal but not essential (other carbon anodes also work), so the amine—not the electrode material—is the key XAT mediator.

10.1038_s41467-018-06020-8.rxn_class_summary: Triarylamine-mediated electrochemical [4+2] annulation of alkenes with diols to afford 1,4-dioxanes and 1,4-dioxepanes
10.1038_s41467-018-06020-8.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of triarylamine catalyst 3 to its radical cation I, which acts as a homogeneous single-electron oxidant (mediator) to oxidize the alkene; protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2
10.1038_s41467-018-06020-8.electron_flow: The anode oxidizes triarylamine 3 to radical cation I, which performs SET on the alkene to give radical cation II; ethylene glycol adds to II and loses a proton to form C-radical III, which is further oxidized by another equivalent of I to carbocation IV; intramolecular cyclization of the pendant hydroxyl onto IV yields the 1,4-dioxane 4, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: triarylamine radical cation I (oxidized mediator, Ar3N•+)
β-hydroxyethoxy carbon-centered radical III (after diol addition and deprotonation)
carbocation IV (after second SET oxidation of III)

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 3, 1, and mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Catalytic current observed when 1 is added to 3 (Ep/2(3)=1.48 V, Ep/2(1)=1.68 V vs SCE), supporting mediated oxidation of alkene by 3•+

probe_reagents.name: less-oxidizing triarylamine mediators 3a, 3b and imidazole 3c
probe_reagents.observation: Mediators with lower Ep/2 than 3 gave drastically lower yields (5–44%), consistent with thermodynamic requirement for SET to alkene

probe_reagents.name: electrolysis of product 4 with catalyst 3
probe_reagents.observation: 4 was recovered in 96% yield; no decomposition to 5/6, showing 3 suppresses overoxidation of 4

probe_reagents.name: electrolysis of product 4 without catalyst 3
probe_reagents.observation: 32% of 4 decomposed, giving 1,3-dioxolane 5 (16%) and benzophenone 6 (7%), confirming direct anodic overoxidation pathway in absence of mediator

probe_reagents.name: iPrCO2H additive omission / acid screen (AcOH, EtCO2H, CF3CO2H, TsOH)
probe_reagents.observation: Removing iPrCO2H or substituting other acids decreased yield, suggesting acid promotes H2 evolution and protects I, solvent and alkene from cathodic reduction

10.1038_s41467-018-06020-8.notes: Authors invoke two sequential SET events from the same mediator I (radical-polar crossover via radical cation II then cation IV). No radical traps (TEMPO/BHT), no KIE, and no DFT were reported; mechanism is supported primarily by CV (catalytic current) and by control electrolysis of isolated 4 demonstrating that 3 protects the product from anodic decomposition to dioxolane 5 and benzophenone 6.

10.1038_s41467-024-47168-w.rxn_class_summary: Electroreductive hydrogenation/deuteration of unactivated alkyl alkenes using catalytic Ph3SiCl as an H/D-carrier and H2O/D2O as the H/D source, with sacrificial Fe anode and Ni-bipyridine co-catalyst
10.1038_s41467-024-47168-w.electrochemical_role: Cathodic two-electron reduction of Ph3SiCl (silicon mediator) generates a silyl anion that is protonated by H2O to give Ph3SiH; the sacrificial Fe anode releases Fe(n+) salts that, upon further reduction, form the active [Fe]-H species via sigma-bond metathesis with Ph3SiH
10.1038_s41467-024-47168-w.electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ph3SiCl by two sequential SETs to silyl radical then silyl anion, which is protonated by H2O to Ph3SiH; sacrificial Fe anode dissolves to give Fe(n+), which after reduction undergoes sigma-bond metathesis with Ph3SiH to form [Fe]-H. [Fe]-H performs migratory insertion across the alkene, and the resulting alkyl-Fe is protonated by H2O to release the hydrogenated product and Fe-OH, closing both Si and Fe cycles.

key_intermediates.name: linear alkyl-iron Fe-INT3A (major after migratory insertion)
silanol Ph3SiOH (D, regenerates Ph3SiX via fluorination)

probe_reagents.name: Ph3SiH (instead of Ph3SiCl)
probe_reagents.observation: Affords product in 73% yield, supporting silane as the operative H-carrier intermediate

probe_reagents.name: alkyl-SiMe3 (substrate 75)
probe_reagents.observation: No hydrogenation product; 86% substrate recovered, showing Si-Cl bond (not Si-C) is the activated bond

probe_reagents.name: PhMe2SiD / D2O
probe_reagents.observation: α and β positions deuterated (up to 85/79% D-inc.); two H atoms come from silane and from water; w/o NiBr2·dtbpy α-D drops (56/85), supporting Ni-assisted insertion

probe_reagents.name: ((1R,2S)-2-vinylcyclopropyl)benzene (76)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 77 (20%) and unopened hydrogenation product 78 (33%) detected, consistent with β-carbon elimination from a metal-alkyl intermediate (not free radical)

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: >99% D-incorporation at terminal alkene carbons; chain-walking gives multi-site D-labeling in internal alkenes

probe_reagents.name: TMSCl / TMSBr (19F NMR monitoring)
probe_reagents.observation: Rapid conversion to TMSF via reaction with TBABF4, evidencing fluorosilane as a viable mediator state

probe_reagents.name: no electricity control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed (>99% substrate recovered), confirming electrochemical activation is essential

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of Ph3SiCl and Ni/L
probe_reagents.observation: Ph3SiCl reduces at Ep/2 = -2.4 V; Ni(II)/Ni(I) at -1.82 V and Ni(I)/Ni(0) at -2.55 V; addition of Ph3SiCl enhances Ni(0) generation

10.1038_s41467-024-47168-w.notes: Two interconnected catalytic cycles are proposed: a Si-mediator cycle (Ph3SiCl → Ph3Si• → Ph3Si- → Ph3SiH → Ph3SiX) and an Fe-hydride cycle that performs migratory insertion/protonation on the alkene. A minor parallel Ni-catalyzed cycle is also invoked (supported by ring-opening of vinylcyclopropane and Ni CV data) and detailed in SI. Heterogeneous hydrogenation on Fe-coated Ni foam contributes as well, since Zn(+) or Mg(+) anodes give product only when the cathode is pre-coated with Fe particles. Authors favor the [Fe]-H pathway over a free-radical pathway based on the radical-clock outcome (β-carbon elimination from alkyl-Fe rather than radical ring-opening).

10.1038_s41467-023-36000-6_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical synthesis of a DABCO-derived bromine complex (PTC1-Br2) from a benzyl-DABCO chloride salt via anodic oxidation of bromide in an undivided cell with HBr additive
10.1038_s41467-023-36000-6_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Br- (from NaBr) at the Pt anode generates Br2, which is sequestered by the DABCO nitrogen of PTC1 to form the PTC1-Br2 charge-transfer/N-Br2 complex; HBr additive is required to enable formation of the isolable yellow solid
10.1038_s41467-023-36000-6_sup_1.electron_flow: At the Pt anode, Br- is oxidized to Br2; the tertiary amine of the DABCO-based PTC1 binds Br2 to give the PTC1-Br2 complex, with HBr additive protonating/balancing the system to enable productive complex isolation (without it only trace product forms).

probe_reagents.name: HBr additive (control: no additive vs 2 equiv HBr)
probe_reagents.observation: Without additive only trace PTC1-Br2 was obtained; with 2 equiv HBr the complex was isolated in 81% yield as a yellow solid

10.1038_s41467-023-36000-6_sup_1.notes: This page documents the stoichiometric electrochemical preparation and isolation/characterization (1H, 13C NMR) of the proposed bromenium/Br2-DABCO intermediate (PTC1-Br2) implicated as the active brominating species in the parent catalytic reaction; HBr appears essential to stabilize/form the isolable complex.

10.1038_s41467-022-31453-7.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative NHC organocatalysis of enals/aldehydes via iodide-mediated single-electron-transfer oxidation of the Breslow intermediate, enabling α-, β-, γ-, δ- and carbonyl-carbon functionalizations (cyclization, benzannulation, DKR).
10.1038_s41467-022-31453-7.electrochemical_role: Anode performs two SET events: (A) one-electron oxidation of the Breslow intermediate I to the NHC-bound ketyl radical cation II, and (B) one-electron oxidation of iodide to iodine radical; radical-radical coupling near the anode then forms an α-iodo intermediate III which loses I- to give the acyl azolium IV.
10.1038_s41467-022-31453-7.electron_flow: NHC adds to aldehyde to give Breslow intermediate I, which is anodically oxidized (event A) to the NHC ketyl radical cation II; concurrently iodide is anodically oxidized (event B) to I•. The two radicals couple at the anode surface to give III, which eliminates iodide to give acyl azolium IV; IV is then trapped by the nucleophilic partner (hydrazone, 1,3-dicarbonyl, ketimine, etc.) to deliver the chiral product and regenerate NHC.
10.1038_s41467-022-31453-7.rate_determining_step: Anodic SET oxidation of the Breslow intermediate to the ketyl radical cation II is implicated as the key (and likely turnover-limiting) electrochemical step; direct oxidation of enal without NHC requires >2 V vs SCE while Breslow intermediate is oxidized below -1.0 V.

key_intermediates.name: Breslow intermediate I (NHC-bound enol from aldehyde + NHC)
NHC-attached ketyl radical cation II (radical intermediate after anodic SET)
α-Iodo NHC adduct III from radical-radical coupling
Acyl azolium intermediate IV (after I- elimination)
Iodinated NHC adduct D/E (poisoned catalyst, off-cycle)

probe_reagents.name: cis-2-phenylcyclopropane-1-carbaldehyde (radical clock, cis-20)
probe_reagents.observation: Under electrochemical NHC/I- conditions gave ring-opened/isomerized trans-21 (15%), consistent with a radical intermediate; under chemical DQ oxidation only non-isomerized cis-21 was obtained (92%), indicating a two-electron pathway in DQ chemistry vs SET pathway electrochemically.

probe_reagents.name: I2 (stoichiometric chemical oxidant test)
probe_reagents.observation: 50 mol% I2 fully suppressed the [4+2] annulation (0% 3a); 10 mol% I2 added to standard reaction gave 53% yield, showing I2 poisons NHC catalyst by forming iodinated adducts D/E.

probe_reagents.name: Iodinated NHC adducts D and E (isolated poisoned catalysts)
probe_reagents.observation: Isolated in 48% (D) and 52% (E); when used as catalysts under standard conditions gave 0% product, confirming they are catalytically inert off-pathway species.

probe_reagents.name: Control without NHC (γ-iodination test)
probe_reagents.observation: No γ-iodinated enal 19 formed (0%), excluding an enal γ-iodination pathway.

probe_reagents.name: α-epimerization control on cis-20
probe_reagents.observation: cis-20 subjected to basic standard conditions showed no trans-20, ruling out α-racemization as the source of isomerization.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, NHC A, DBU, TBAI
probe_reagents.observation: Breslow intermediate oxidation potential <-1.0 V vs SCE (vs >2 V for free enal); mixing Breslow intermediate with TBAI causes disappearance of I-/I2 redox waves and irreversible coupling features, supporting radical-radical coupling between I• and ketyl radical II.

10.1038_s41467-022-31453-7.notes: Authors explicitly contrast their SET radical-coupling mechanism with the conventional two-electron (DET) oxidative NHC catalysis using DQ; the radical clock gives different products between the two regimes. An alternative γ-iodination/SN2-like pathway was considered but excluded by the no-NHC control. Iodide functions as a redox mediator that is normally a catalyst poison; the non-uniform concentration of the electrolysis system (high local [NHC] at electrode) is invoked to explain why radical coupling outcompetes catalyst iodination.

10.26434_chemrxiv.12639215.v2_si2.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical halogen-atom transfer (e-Shuttle) that transfers Br2 or Cl2 from a vicinal dihaloalkane donor to an alkene acceptor, regenerating an alkene from the donor and producing a 1,2-dihalide on the acceptor.
10.26434_chemrxiv.12639215.v2_si2.electrochemical_role: Paired electrolysis: cathodic 2e- reduction of the vicinal dihaloalkane donor releases 2 X- and the alkene byproduct; the halide is then oxidized back to X+ equivalent at the anode (directly for Br2; via a Mn2+/Mn3+ mediator for Cl2) which halogenates the acceptor alkene.
10.26434_chemrxiv.12639215.v2_si2.electron_flow: At the cathode, the vicinal dihaloalkane donor accepts 2 electrons and fragments to an alkene plus 2 X- (E2-type reductive dehalogenation). For Br2 shuttle, Br- is oxidized at the anode to a Br+ equivalent (bromiranium) that adds across the acceptor alkene to give the 1,2-dibromide; for Cl2 shuttle, Cl- is captured by Mn2+ and the Mn2+-Cl is anodically oxidized to Mn3+-Cl, which transfers Cl2 to the alkene to give the 1,2-dichloride.

key_intermediates.name: bromide anion released by cathodic reduction
electrophilic Br+ equivalent generated at anode
Mn3+-Cl (oxidized Mn(III) chloride mediator that delivers Cl to alkene)

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-dibromoethane (donor)
probe_reagents.observation: Used as Br2 donor; absence of HBr elimination products supports a 2e- reductive debromination releasing ethylene rather than HX elimination.

probe_reagents.name: 2,3-dibromobutane (donor)
probe_reagents.observation: Donor releases 2-butene cleanly, again ruling out HX elimination pathway.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1,1,2-tetrachloroethane (donor)
probe_reagents.observation: Acts as Cl2 donor without producing HCl-elimination byproducts.

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-dichloroethane (DCE, donor)
probe_reagents.observation: Serves as Cl2 donor, releasing ethylene; consistent with reductive dechlorination.

probe_reagents.name: Lindane (gamma-hexachlorocyclohexane, donor)
probe_reagents.observation: Stepwise Cl2 transfer ultimately yields benzene, confirmed by crude NMR and GC, supporting sequential reductive dechlorination releasing alkene intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of Br2 shuttle components
probe_reagents.observation: CV demonstrates the redox couple responsible for Br- oxidation / dibromide reduction supporting the proposed paired electrochemistry.

probe_reagents.name: Representative intramolecular Br2 transfer experiment
probe_reagents.observation: An intramolecular variant confirms net Br2 transfer from donor to acceptor alkene.

10.26434_chemrxiv.12639215.v2_si2.notes: Authors explicitly rule out an HX-elimination pathway (Section 6) by showing donors lacking acidic beta-H or known to favor elimination still cleanly release alkenes and transfer X2. The Cl2 cycle requires a Mn2+/Mn3+ mediator at the anode because chloride oxidation is otherwise too anodic, whereas the Br2 cycle proceeds without a metal mediator via direct anodic Br- oxidation. Lindane remediation is demonstrated as a practical application of sequential Cl2 transfer to benzene.

10.26434_chemrxiv.12639215.v2.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical halogen (Br2/Cl2) shuttle transferring vicinal dihalides from a saturated donor (e.g., vicinal dibromide or polychloroalkane) to an alkene acceptor to furnish a new vicinal dihalide and an alkene byproduct via a paired-electrolysis e-Shuttle process.
10.26434_chemrxiv.12639215.v2.electrochemical_role: Paired electrolysis: cathode reduces the vicinal dihalide donor (2 e-) releasing halide and an alkene; halide is oxidized at the anode (directly for Br-, or via Mn2+/Mn3+ mediator for Cl-) to generate an electrophilic halonium/X+ that adds across the acceptor alkene to give the vicinal dihalide product.
10.26434_chemrxiv.12639215.v2.electron_flow: At the cathode, two electrons reduce the vicinal dihalide donor, cleaving both C-X bonds to give an alkene and 2 X-. The X- migrates to the anode where it is oxidized (directly to Br+ for bromides, or via a Mn2+/Mn3+ redox mediator for chlorides) and the resulting electrophilic halogen adds across the acceptor alkene to deliver the vicinal dihalide product.

key_intermediates.name: halide anion (Br- or Cl-) released by cathodic cleavage of donor C-X bond
alkene byproduct from reductive dehalogenation of donor
bromonium / Br+ electrophile generated at anode
Mn(III)-Cl oxidant generated by anodic oxidation (Cl2 cycle)
vicinal dihalide product from X+ addition to acceptor alkene

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-dibromoethane (donor probe for HX elimination)
probe_reagents.observation: Used as Br2 donor; absence of HBr-elimination products supports a 2e- dehalogenation pathway rather than E2/HX elimination.

probe_reagents.name: 2,3-dibromobutane
probe_reagents.observation: Donor probe; ethylene/butene byproduct profile consistent with reductive vicinal dehalogenation, ruling out HX elimination.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1,1,2-tetrachloroethane
probe_reagents.observation: Cl2 donor; product distribution rules out HCl elimination pathway.

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-dichloroethane (DCE)
probe_reagents.observation: Cl2 donor; consistent with reductive dechlorination releasing ethylene + 2 Cl-.

probe_reagents.name: Lindane (γ-HCH)
probe_reagents.observation: Cl2 donor; formation of benzene (confirmed by crude NMR and GC) supports stepwise reductive dechlorination rather than HCl elimination.

10.26434_chemrxiv.12639215.v2.notes: Authors explicitly designed Section 6 experiments to exclude an HX-elimination pathway from the donor; the favored mechanism is 2e- cathodic vicinal dehalogenation + anodic halide oxidation (direct for Br, Mn-mediated for Cl). A representative intramolecular Br2 transfer (Section 8) and CV studies (Section 7) further support discrete halide/halonium intermediates. Mn2+/Mn3+ is essential as an anodic mediator for the Cl2 cycle because direct Cl- oxidation is too anodic.

10.26434_chemrxiv-2025-8zr6h_si2.rxn_class_summary: Cobalt-catalyzed enantioselective migratory insertion of an alkene into a Co-aryl bond generating stereoisomeric cobaltacyclic intermediates studied by DFT
10.26434_chemrxiv-2025-8zr6h_si2.electron_flow: Within an inner-sphere Co(III) coordination sphere bearing a pyridyl-imine and an oxazoline (iPr) ligand, the coordinated acenaphthylene undergoes concerted migratory insertion into the Co-aryl bond, producing diastereomeric cobaltacyclic alkyl intermediates Int2A/Int2B via TS(1-2)A/B.
10.26434_chemrxiv-2025-8zr6h_si2.rate_determining_step: Migratory insertion of the alkene into the Co-aryl bond (TS(1-2))

key_intermediates.name: Co(III)-aryl alkene complex Int1A (pro-R coordination, acenaphthylene bound)

10.26434_chemrxiv-2025-8zr6h_si2.notes: Computational protocol: Gaussian 16, TPSS-D3(BJ)/def2-SVP geometry optimizations in gas phase; PW6B95-D4/def2-TZVPP single points with SMD implicit solvation (acetone ε=20.493 and ethanol ε=24.852, modeling 1:1 acetone/EtOH mixture). Two diastereomeric insertion pathways (A vs B) are compared to rationalize stereoisomer generation; the favored pathway determines product enantio/diastereoselectivity but relative energies are not given on this page.

10.26434_chemrxiv-2024-vz46b.rxn_class_summary: Enantioselective three-component alkylarylation of acrylates with aliphatic C(sp3)-H (alkanes) and aryl halides via asymmetric paired oxidative (Fe/LMCT photocatalysis) and reductive (chiral Ni) electrocatalysis in an undivided cell
10.26434_chemrxiv-2024-vz46b.electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes Fe(II) to Fe(III) to regenerate [FeCl4]- which under light undergoes LMCT to give Cl radical (HAT agent); cathode reduces Ni(II) precatalyst to Ni(I) to initiate the Ni cross-coupling cycle and reduces Ni(III)-alkyl back to Ni(I) after reductive elimination
10.26434_chemrxiv-2024-vz46b.electron_flow: At the anode, Fe(II) is oxidized to Fe(III) generating [FeCl4]- which is photoexcited and undergoes LMCT to release a Cl radical that abstracts H from the alkane to give an alkyl radical; this radical adds to the acrylate, and the resulting alpha-ester radical is captured by a chiral aryl-Ni(II) species (formed by cathodic reduction of Ni(II) and oxidative addition of Ar-X to Ni(I)) to give Ni(III)(aryl)(alkyl), which reductively eliminates the product and the Ni(I) catalyst is regenerated/refreshed by cathodic reduction.
10.26434_chemrxiv-2024-vz46b.rate_determining_step: Electron transfer at the electrodes (reaction is first-order in current)

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical from HAT on alkane (species E, e.g. cyclohexyl)
alpha-ester carbon radical after Giese addition to acrylate (species F)
LnNi(III)-Ar (species H) from oxidative addition of Ar-X
LnNi(III)(aryl)(alkyl) species J after radical capture

probe_reagents.name: 1-methylnaphthalene (no Ni, radical trap by chlorination)
probe_reagents.observation: 1-(chloromethyl)naphthalene 29 isolated in 27% yield, indicating Cl-radical mediated HAT generates a benzylic radical that is chlorinated

probe_reagents.name: chlorocyclohexane (replacing alkane)
probe_reagents.observation: no desired product formed, excluding alkyl chloride as a key intermediate

probe_reagents.name: chlorocycloheptane + cyclohexane competition
probe_reagents.observation: only cyclohexane-derived product 1 observed, alkyl chloride is not an intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Ac-C6H4-Ni(II)-Br stoichiometric complex
probe_reagents.observation: with cyclohexane, tert-butyl acrylate and FeCl3 under light gave product 1 in 31% yield, 91% ee — supports aryl-Ni(II) as productive intermediate

probe_reagents.name: L1 with varying ee (nonlinear effect study)
probe_reagents.observation: positive nonlinear effect — at least two chiral BiIm ligands involved in the enantio-determining step

probe_reagents.name: ee vs time monitoring
probe_reagents.observation: ee of product 1 constant throughout reaction — stereo-convergent, not deracemization

probe_reagents.name: current variation kinetics
probe_reagents.observation: first-order dependence of rate on current (R^2=0.985), confirming electron transfer is rate-limiting

probe_reagents.name: electrode potential monitoring
probe_reagents.observation: anode held ~+0.28 V vs Ag/AgCl (Fe(II)/Fe(III)); cathode ~-0.83 V vs Ag/AgCl, sufficient to reduce ArNi(III) to ArNi(II) but not ArNi(II) to ArNi(I)

10.26434_chemrxiv-2024-vz46b.notes: Mechanism is a dual Fe/Ni paired electrocatalytic cycle: anodic Fe-LMCT photocatalysis activates the C(sp3)-H bond of the alkane (HAT by Cl•), while cathodic chiral Ni catalysis controls enantioselective C(sp3)-C(sp2) bond formation. Positive NLE suggests aggregation of >=2 chiral BiIm ligands on Ni in the stereo-determining step. Alternative pathway via alkyl chloride intermediates was explicitly ruled out by competition and substitution experiments. Single-crystal X-ray confirmed absolute configuration of product 7.

10.26434_chemrxiv-2023-dzw3d.rxn_class_summary: Cobalt-catalyzed enantioselective C–H functionalization / spiroannulation of 8-aminoquinoline benzamides with maleimides, with a chiral phosphoric acid (CPA) co-catalyst controlling enantioselectivity
10.26434_chemrxiv-2023-dzw3d.electron_flow: Co(II) coordinates the 8-aminoquinoline amide and undergoes pivalate-assisted concerted metalation–deprotonation (CMD) of the ortho C–H bond; oxidation to Co(III)/Co(IV) and migratory insertion into the maleimide C=C, followed by C–N bond-forming reductive cyclization, delivers the spirocyclic product. The chiral phosphoric acid binds to the Co center and enforces enantioselectivity in the C–C/C–N forming step.
10.26434_chemrxiv-2023-dzw3d.rate_determining_step: Product-forming step after C–H metalation (likely migratory insertion / C–N reductive elimination); C–H cleavage is not rate-limiting since KIE ≈ 1.0–1.2

key_intermediates.name: Co(II)–pivalate–amide complex (benzamide bound to Co with pivalate ligand)
Bis(amidate) Co(II) complex (two benzamide ligands on Co)
Spirocyclic succinimide–isoindolinone product 3 bound to quinoline (released product)
Co(IV)–maleimide insertion intermediate ligated by CPA phosphate (m/z 839.1210)

probe_reagents.name: [D]5-1 (pentadeuterated benzamide substrate) – parallel KIE with CPA1
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 1.2 from parallel reactions with CPA1, indicating C–H cleavage is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: [D]5-1 – parallel KIE without CPA1
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 1.0 without CPA1, confirming C–H activation is not rate-limiting in either regime

probe_reagents.name: HRMS aliquot analysis during catalysis
probe_reagents.observation: Detected masses consistent with Co(II)–amide–pivalate (m/z 408.0873), bis(amidate)Co (554.1142), Co(III)–insertion/cyclometalated species (739.1608), CPA-bound Co complexes (695.1004, 756.1447, 902.1693) and a Co(IV) maleimide-insertion species (839.1210)

probe_reagents.name: DFT computation of product racemization barrier
probe_reagents.observation: ΔG‡ = 48.3 kcal/mol for 36-(S) → 36-(R) rotation, t1/2 ≈ 3.2×10^18 yr, confirming configurational stability of the atropisomeric product

10.26434_chemrxiv-2023-dzw3d.notes: HRMS detects multiple plausible on-cycle species, including CPA-ligated Co complexes, supporting direct coordination of the chiral phosphate to cobalt rather than a purely outer-sphere Brønsted-acid role. Near-unity KIE (1.0 without CPA, 1.2 with CPA) rules out turnover-limiting C–H metalation; CPA likely modulates a later enantiodetermining migratory insertion or C–N bond-forming step. Product atropisomerism is configurationally locked (DFT).

10.1002_asia.202101033.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative selenocyclization of cinnamyl thioether benzimidazoles with diaryl diselenides to give trans-dihydro-benzoimidazo[1,3]thiazines via an anodic ionic (seleniranium) pathway
10.1002_asia.202101033.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of diphenyl diselenide (PhSeSePh) to its radical cation, which fragments into PhSe+ (electrophile) and PhSe•; cathode reduces protons to H2 to balance charge
10.1002_asia.202101033.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh by one electron to a radical cation that fragments to PhSe+ and PhSe•; PhSe+ adds electrophilically to the cinnamyl alkene of 1a to form a bridged seleniranium ion, which undergoes 6-endo nucleophilic ring closure by the benzimidazole nitrogen through a loose SN1-like transition state; deprotonation gives 3aa while cathodic H+ reduction to H2 balances the charge.
10.1002_asia.202101033.rate_determining_step: 6-endo nucleophilic annulation of the benzimidazole nitrogen onto the seleniranium ion via a loose benzylic-cation-like transition state

key_intermediates.name: bridged seleniranium ion intermediate (F) on cinnamyl thioether
benzylic radical (G) from PhSe• addition to alkene
benzylic cation (H) from SET oxidation of G

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: 3aa still isolated in 85% yield under standard conditions with 2 equiv TEMPO, indicating radical pathway is not the major contributor

probe_reagents.name: Divided cell (anolyte 1a+2a)
probe_reagents.observation: Exclusive formation of 3aa (82%) when reactants kept only in anodic chamber, showing cathodic reduction is not required

probe_reagents.name: Divided cell (catholyte 1a+2a)
probe_reagents.observation: 3aa not detected, ruling out a purely cathodic initiation

probe_reagents.name: H2O2 oxidative deselenation of 3aa
probe_reagents.observation: Syn-elimination to vinyl thiazine 6 (61%) supports trans-relative geometry of substituents in 3aa

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a and mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Ep/2(1a)=1.26 V, Ep/2(2a)=1.29 V individually; mixture shows waves at 1.28 and 1.66 V; reduction of 2a at -1.18 V; supports anodic oxidation of 2a initiating the reaction

probe_reagents.name: Thiopropargyl benzimidazole 4
probe_reagents.observation: Annulation gives benzoimidazo[1,3]thiazine 5 in 92%, demonstrating generality of the selenocyclization

10.1002_asia.202101033.notes: Authors explicitly disfavor two alternative pathways: (i) a radical pathway via PhSe• addition giving benzyl radical G, which would require SET oxidation to cation H and is ruled out by divided-cell experiments and TEMPO insensitivity; (ii) initial anodic oxidation of 1a to radical cation K followed by 5-exo radical annulation and selenylation, which would give the undetected 5-exo regioisomer 3aa'. Exclusive 6-endo trans-diastereoselectivity is rationalized by a loose, SN1-like benzylic cation transition state retaining stereospecificity of the seleniranium opening.

10.1002_asia.202100121.rxn_class_summary: Manganese-catalyzed electrochemical oxidative azidation-annulation cascade of N-arylacrylamides and N-arylcinnamamides with NaN3 to give azidated oxindoles and quinolinones.
10.1002_asia.202100121.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of a Mn(II)-N3 complex to a high-valent Mn(III)-N3 species which transfers azide to the alkene; the resulting cyclohexadienyl radical is further anodically oxidized to a cation that rearomatizes; H2 is evolved at the Pt cathode.
10.1002_asia.202100121.electron_flow: NaN3 binds Mn(II) to give Mn(II)-N3 (A), which is anodically oxidized by one electron to Mn(III)-N3 (B); B delivers an azidyl group to the alkene of the N-arylacrylamide/cinnamamide producing a C-centered radical (C/C'), which undergoes intramolecular radical annulation onto the N-aryl arene (D/D'); a second anodic SET gives cationic E/E', and deprotonative rearomatization yields the azidated oxindole or quinolinone, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

key_intermediates.name: tertiary C-radical from azide addition to acrylamide (C)
benzylic/resonance-stabilized radical from azide addition to cinnamamide (C')
cyclohexadienyl radical after ipso radical annulation onto N-aryl ring (D/D')
Wheland-type cation after anodic SET oxidation (E/E')

probe_reagents.name: N-arylcinnamamide / N-arylacrylamide (substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows oxidation up to ~2.5 V (1.87 V peak), substrate not directly oxidized under operating conditions.

probe_reagents.name: NaN3
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows oxidation peak at 1.03 V.

probe_reagents.name: MnBr2
probe_reagents.observation: Two quasi-reversible oxidation peaks at 0.75 and 0.86 V assigned to Mn(II)/Mn(III) and free bromide oxidation.

probe_reagents.name: NaN3 + MnBr2 mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Azide oxidation peak shifts to 0.43 V, consistent with formation of Mn(II)-N3 / Mn(III)-N3 adduct that is more easily oxidized.

10.1002_asia.202100121.notes: An 'additional pathway' is depicted in Scheme 5 in which free azidyl radical (generated by direct anodic oxidation of N3-) adds to the alkene, offering an alternative to the favored Mn(III)-N3 inner-sphere azide transfer. Regioselectivity is rationalized by stability of the resulting radical (3° tertiary radical C for acrylamides, benzylic radical C' for cinnamamides). No radical trapping (TEMPO/BHT), KIE, or DFT experiments are reported in the shown pages.

10.1007_s11426-022-1410-6.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical synthesis of Z-selective 2-alkylidene-tetrahydrofurans via sequential paired electrolysis coupling activated organochlorides (trichloro esters/amides) with olefins, enabled by halogen-bond (XB) activation using dtbbpy as the XB acceptor
10.1007_s11426-022-1410-6.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of the noncovalent XB complex (dtbbpy···Cl-CCl2-) lowers the SET barrier, generating a C-centered radical after C-Cl fragmentation; the resulting alkyl radical (after addition to olefin) is then oxidized at the anode to a cation (radical-polar crossover)
10.1007_s11426-022-1410-6.electron_flow: dtbbpy halogen-bonds to a C-Cl of the trichloro substrate forming complex IV, which is cathodically reduced and fragments to Cl- and α,α-dichloro radical V; V adds to the alkene to give radical VI, which migrates to the anode and is oxidized to cation VII; the pendant carbonyl tautomerizes to an enol and cyclizes intramolecularly to deliver Z-2-alkylidene-tetrahydrofuran III.
10.1007_s11426-022-1410-6.rate_determining_step: Cathodic SET reduction of the C-Cl bond, whose activation barrier is lowered by XB complexation with dtbbpy (reduction potential shifted positively by 220 mV)

key_intermediates.name: Noncovalent halogen-bond complex IV between dtbbpy (N lone pair) and the C-Cl bond of trichloro ester/amide I
α,α-Dichloro carbon radical V generated after cathodic SET and Cl- loss from complex IV
Alkyl radical VI from addition of V to alkene II
Cation VII formed by anodic oxidation of radical VI (radical-polar crossover)
2-Alkylidene-tetrahydrofuran III formed by keto-enol tautomerization and intramolecular O-cyclization onto cation VII

probe_reagents.name: dtbbpy (4,4'-di-tert-butyl-2,2'-bipyridine) as XB acceptor
probe_reagents.observation: CV showed +220 mV positive shift of the C-Cl reduction potential upon adding dtbbpy, evidencing XB-mediated activation

probe_reagents.name: UV-vis titration of dtbbpy with trichloro substrate
probe_reagents.observation: UV-vis changes consistent with formation of noncovalent XB complex IV

probe_reagents.name: NiCl2(dtbbpy) control catalyst
probe_reagents.observation: Use of 20 mol% NiCl2(dtbbpy) with Pt cathode gave only 9% of 3, excluding Ni-catalyzed C-Cl activation from Ni-leached species

probe_reagents.name: Less polar solvent screen (PhCN, 1,2-DCE)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace or much lower yields in solvents known to disrupt noncovalent interactions, supporting XB being essential

probe_reagents.name: Alternative XB-acceptor analogs (bpy, 1,10-phen derivatives, 1,4-dicyanobenzene, tetrathiafulvalene)
probe_reagents.observation: All gave decreased or trace yield, highlighting unique reactivity of dtbbpy as XB acceptor

10.1007_s11426-022-1410-6.notes: Alternative mechanism considered and excluded: in situ formation of a Ni-dtbbpy complex from the nickel foam cathode acting as an inner-sphere catalyst for C-Cl activation; control with NiCl2(dtbbpy)/Pt gave only 9% product, ruling this out. Key challenges noted by the authors: (1) low stability of weakly bound XB complex IV (XB-donor strength F<<Cl<<Br<I makes Cl weak); (2) potential 2e- overreduction of electron-deficient radicals V and VI at the cathode; (3) lifetime/diffusion of V and VI must be sufficient for sequential paired electrolysis (anode trip for radical-polar crossover). Exclusive Z-selectivity of the alkylidene tetrahydrofuran is a notable stereochemical outcome but its origin is not mechanistically dissected on these pages.

10.1007_s11426-024-2154-8.rxn_class_summary: Organo-mediated electrochemical three-component arylcarboxylation of styrenes with aryl halides and CO2 to give 2,2-diarylpropionic-type carboxylic acids
10.1007_s11426-024-2154-8.electrochemical_role: Cathodic single-electron reduction of a naphthalene-2,6-dicarboxylate mediator (Med-1) generates a radical anion that acts as an outer-sphere SET mediator, reducing the aryl halide to an aryl radical; Mg sacrificial anode supplies Mg2+ and electrons
10.1007_s11426-024-2154-8.electron_flow: Cathode reduces Med-1 to its radical anion, which performs outer-sphere SET to ArX giving [ArX]•- that fragments to Ar• and X-; Ar• adds anti-Markovnikov to styrene to form a benzyl radical, which is further reduced at the cathode to a benzyl carbanion that nucleophilically attacks CO2. Mg anode is sacrificially oxidized to Mg2+.
10.1007_s11426-024-2154-8.rate_determining_step: Outer-sphere SET from Med-1 radical anion to aryl halide (ΔG‡ ≈ 21.2 kcal/mol by DFT for PhI)

key_intermediates.name: Med-1 radical anion (dimethyl naphthalene-2,6-dicarboxylate radical anion)
benzyl radical from anti-Markovnikov addition to styrene

probe_reagents.name: isobutyraldehyde (electrophile swap)
probe_reagents.observation: Replacing CO2 gave alcohol 60 in 21%, evidencing a benzyl carbanion intermediate

probe_reagents.name: cyclobutanone (electrophile swap)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave tertiary alcohol 61 in 12%, supporting benzyl anion intermediate

probe_reagents.name: H2O
probe_reagents.observation: 20 equiv H2O gave hydrogenation/arylation product 62 in 39% (carbanion protonation)

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: Product 63 isolated in 37% with 92% D incorporation at benzylic position, confirming benzyl carbanion

probe_reagents.name: vinylidenecyclopropane radical clock (64)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened arylcarboxylation product 65 obtained in 37% (Z/E = 1/9), supporting radical addition of Ar• to alkene

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline N-oxide)
probe_reagents.observation: Phenyl-radical DMPO adduct observed by EPR (g=2.0058, AN=14.40 G, AH=21.18 G) and confirmed by ESI-HRMS; no EPR signal without Med-1

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of Med-1 with PhI / styrene
probe_reagents.observation: Med-1 reduction peak at -2.47 V shows increasing catalytic current with added PhI; no change with 4-phenylstyrene, indicating Med-1 mediates SET to ArX and not to styrene

10.1007_s11426-024-2154-8.notes: DFT comparison shows SET from Med-1•- is favorable to PhI (ΔG = -5.6 kcal/mol, ΔG‡ = 21.2) but unfavorable to styrene (ΔG‡ = 27.4) and CO2 (ΔG‡ = 25.3), explaining chemoselectivity. Reactivity tuning: more electron-rich Med-7 used for aryl bromides; Med-9 (methyl benzoate) for electron-rich aryl bromides with more negative potentials. Competing pathways suppressed include hydrogenation, hydrocarboxylation, dicarboxylation, and direct reduction of styrene/ArX.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03940_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical reductive cross-coupling of two vinyl bromides to form unsymmetrical 1,3-dienes using a Ni catalyst with sacrificial-like cathodic SET activation.
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03940_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction generates low-valent Ni species and/or reduces a Ni-vinyl intermediate to enable cross-coupling; vinyl bromide is activated via SET to give a vinyl radical that engages Ni.
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03940_sup_1.electron_flow: At the cathode, Ni(II)/dtbbpy is reduced to a low-valent Ni that oxidatively adds/couples with one vinyl bromide; the second vinyl bromide is activated by SET to a vinyl radical which is captured by Ni, and reductive elimination delivers the 1,3-diene. TMSCl activates the SS anode/scavenges bromide.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a not detected (ND at 6.5 F/mol; trace at 13.0 F/mol); 1a recovered 51%/34% — reaction strongly inhibited, consistent with radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Little effect: 3a still formed in 62%; suggests the radical is not a free, BHT-trappable species (likely metal-bound or short-lived).

probe_reagents.name: (Z)-styryl bromide (1m)
probe_reagents.observation: Cross-coupling with 2a gave 3a (13%) and 3a' (Z-isomer, 14%) — partial loss of olefin geometry, consistent with a vinyl-radical intermediate that loses stereochemistry.

probe_reagents.name: nitrosobenzene
probe_reagents.observation: Used in one-pot tandem (procedure IX) to trap intermediate and give product 6i.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03940_sup_1.notes: TEMPO suppresses but BHT does not — authors interpret this as a Ni-bound/short-lived vinyl radical rather than a freely diffusing one. E/Z scrambling from (Z)-1m supports a vinyl radical intermediate. Pre-electrolysis and CV experiments referenced (sections 5.3 and 6) further support cathodic activation of the Ni catalyst before substrate engagement.

10.1021_acs.orglett.0c00176.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular oxytrifluoromethylation of N-tethered alkenyl alcohols using Langlois reagent (CF3SO2Na) to deliver 2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethyl)morpholines
10.1021_acs.orglett.0c00176.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na (Langlois reagent) generates CF3SO2 radical that extrudes SO2 to release CF3 radical; a second anodic SET oxidizes the alkyl radical adduct to a carbocation that is trapped intramolecularly by the tethered alcohol
10.1021_acs.orglett.0c00176.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2Na by SET to give CF3SO2 radical which loses SO2 to form CF3 radical; CF3 radical adds regioselectively to the terminal alkene to form a secondary C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; the tethered hydroxyl performs intramolecular nucleophilic addition with loss of H+ to give the morpholine, while cathodic reduction of H+/H2O liberates H2.

key_intermediates.name: CF3SO2 radical (from anodic SET on CF3SO2Na)

probe_reagents.name: no-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction in the absence of electric current (Table 1, entry 10), confirming electrochemical initiation

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of Langlois reagent vs substrate 1a
probe_reagents.observation: CF3SO2Na shows irreversible anodic oxidation at 1.48 V (vs Ag/AgCl), substantially lower than substrate 1a at 2.07 V, indicating selective oxidation of Langlois reagent at the anode

10.1021_acs.orglett.0c00176.notes: Mechanism proposed on the basis of control experiments (no current = no reaction), CV data, and analogy to prior reports; no radical trap (e.g., TEMPO/BHT), KIE, or DFT studies are reported. Method also extends to a 7-membered benzoxazepine from an alken-7-ol substrate (Scheme 3).

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03816.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical decarboxylative alkoxy-alkoxycarbonylation of alkenes with potassium oxalate monoesters and alcohols to give β-alkoxy alkanoates
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03816.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of oxalate monoester to oxalate radical, which decarboxylates to an alkoxycarbonyl radical; iodide (from NH4I) acts as a redox mediator shuttling electrons to the anode; H2 evolution at the Pt cathode closes the cell balance
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03816.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the oxalate monoester (assisted by I-/I2 mediation) to an oxalate radical which loses CO2 to give an alkoxycarbonyl radical; this radical adds to the styrene to give a benzyl radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a benzyl cation that is captured by the alcohol nucleophile, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

key_intermediates.name: oxalate radical (RO2C-CO2•) from anodic oxidation of oxalate monoester
benzyl radical from addition to styrene (II)
acyl hypoiodide (RO-C(=O)-OI) alternative precursor to alkoxycarbonyl radical (path b)
iodide / I2 redox mediator from NH4I

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (3a, 0% yield), indicating radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: α-cyclopropylstyrene (13)
probe_reagents.observation: Mainly non-ring-opened β-ethoxy ester 14 (63%) with small amount of ring-opened product 15 (10%), indicating fast anodic oxidation of the benzyl radical to cation before ring opening

probe_reagents.name: NH4I (cyclic voltammetry control)
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows substantial current increase upon addition of NH4I, consistent with iodide acting as a redox mediator

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03816.notes: Authors favor path a (direct anodic oxidation of oxalate to oxalate radical, then decarboxylation). An alternative path b — homolysis of an acyl hypoiodide RO-C(=O)-OI formed from I2 — cannot be ruled out. NH4I enhances current and likely facilitates charge transfer as an iodide mediator. Tethered-nucleophile alkenes (9, 10) give cyclized THF/γ-butyrolactone products (11, 12), consistent with carbocation capture by the pendant OH/CO2H.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03816_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical alkoxycarbonyl-alkoxylation of styrenes with potassium ethyl oxalate and alcohols via a radical-polar crossover pathway
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03816_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (from NH4I mediator) generates I2/I• which oxidatively decarboxylates the oxalate to an ethoxycarbonyl radical; this carbon radical adds to the styrene and the resulting benzylic radical is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation trapped by EtOH
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03816_sup_1.electron_flow: Anodic oxidation of iodide regenerates an I-based mediator that oxidizes potassium ethyl oxalate, releasing CO2 and an ethoxycarbonyl radical; this radical adds to the styrene to give a benzylic radical, which is oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation and trapped by ethanol, while the cathode reduces protons/NH4+ to H2.

key_intermediates.name: ethoxycarbonyl radical (•CO2Et) from decarboxylation of ethyl oxalate
benzylic C-radical after addition to styrene
benzylic carbocation after anodic oxidation of benzyl radical
cyclopropylcarbinyl radical / ring-opened homoallyl radical (from radical clock substrate 13)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of 2 equiv TEMPO completely suppressed formation of product 3a (no 3a detected by GC-MS/1H NMR), consistent with a radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)-4-methylbenzene (radical clock substrate 13)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave both the unopened cyclopropyl product 14 (63%) and the ring-opened homoallylic ether 15 (10%), indicating a carbon-centered radical intermediate that partially undergoes cyclopropylcarbinyl ring opening

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03816_sup_1.notes: Mechanistic studies shown include only TEMPO trapping and a radical clock; the radical-polar crossover (oxidation of benzylic radical to cation, trapped by alcohol) is inferred from the alkoxylation outcome rather than directly probed on these pages. NH4I acts as a redox mediator and Et4NBF4 as supporting electrolyte.

10.1021_acs.orglett.8b04041.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative radical selenylation/1,2-carbon migration of alkenylcyclobutanols with diselenides to give beta-selenyl cyclopentanones, which undergo Dowd-Beckwith ring expansion to cyclohexanones.
10.1021_acs.orglett.8b04041.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diaryl diselenide to a diselenide radical cation that fragments to a selenyl radical (and selenyl cation); a further anodic oxidation converts the carbon-radical adduct to a carbocation that undergoes semipinacol 1,2-alkyl migration. Cathode reduces protons to H2.
10.1021_acs.orglett.8b04041.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh to a radical cation that cleaves to PhSe• and PhSe+; PhSe• adds to the vinyl group of the alkenylcyclobutanol forming a benzylic C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; 1,2-alkyl migration (semipinacol ring expansion of the cyclobutanol) gives the beta-selenyl cyclopentanone, while the cathode reduces H+ to H2.

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical from PhSe addition to alkenylcyclobutanol (IV)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product formation suppressed to trace, supporting a radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield reduced to 62%, consistent with partial radical involvement

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethene
probe_reagents.observation: Yield reduced to 57%, consistent with a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: no current control
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction without applied current, confirming electrochemical initiation

10.1021_acs.orglett.8b04041.notes: Authors propose two competing pathways: favored path a involves PhSe• radical addition to the alkene followed by anodic oxidation to a cation and semipinacol 1,2-migration; alternative path b invokes nucleophilic attack of the alkenylcyclobutanol on PhSe+ (cation III) followed by 1,2-carbon migration, which could not be ruled out. CV showed oxidation peaks of 1a at 1.74 V and PhSeSePh (2a) at 1.33 V, consistent with diselenide being oxidized first. A subsequent radical-initiated Dowd-Beckwith ring expansion (Bu3SnH/AIBN) converts the beta-selenyl cyclopentanones to cyclohexanones.

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04041.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical dichlorinative cyclization of 1,n-enynes catalyzed by 4-iodotoluene via in situ generated hypervalent ArICl2 as the active chlorinating/cyclizing reagent
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04041.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of chloride (from Et3N·HCl) to a chlorine-radical/active [Cl+]ox species (Eox = 1.75 V), which then oxidizes 4-iodotoluene (ArI) to the hypervalent ArICl2 mediator that delivers Cl to the enyne substrate
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04041.electron_flow: At the anode, Cl- is oxidized (Eox 1.75 V) to an active [Cl+]ox species which oxidizes ArI to ArICl2; ArICl2 performs electrophilic cis-chloroiodination of the alkyne to give vinyl-iodonium B, intramolecular syn-migratory insertion onto the alkene gives cation C, and Cl- displaces ArI by nucleophilic substitution to yield the dichloride product while regenerating ArI; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: active oxidized chlorine species [Cl+]ox (chlorine radical / Cl+ equivalent)
chloriranium / cationic alkyne-Ar(I)Cl adduct A from cis-chloroiodination of the alkyne
vinyl-ArI(Cl) intermediate B (cis-chloroiodination product of alkyne)
cyclic carbocation C from syn-migratory insertion across the alkene

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard electrochemical conditions no conversion of 1a; however with pre-formed 4-Me-PhICl2 (no current) BHT did not inhibit product formation, indicating no free radical on the productive ArICl2 pathway

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: No conversion of 1a under standard electrochemical conditions, suggesting a radical step (Cl•) in the anodic mediator-generation phase

probe_reagents.name: no-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction without electric current, confirming current is required to generate the active oxidant

probe_reagents.name: stoichiometric 4-Me-PhICl2 (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: 1.5 equiv 4-Me-PhICl2 without current gave 52% yield of 2a, supporting ArICl2 as the key catalytic intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Selectfluor / CsCl with ArI (no direct current step bypass)
probe_reagents.observation: In situ chemical generation of 4-Me-PhICl2 also delivered the product, corroborating the ArICl2 intermediate

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04041.notes: CV shows Et3N·HCl oxidizes at 1.75 V, below ArI at 1.98 V, and addition of ArI to Et3N·HCl produces a new peak matching 4-Me-C6H4ICl2 while the Et3N·HCl peak disappears — direct CV evidence that Cl- is oxidized first and rapidly transfers Cl to ArI. The authors note BHT inhibition under electrochemical conditions is 'not conclusive' for a free-radical productive pathway; the radical character is assigned only to the anodic Cl-/Cl• step generating ArICl2, after which the cyclization proceeds via cationic (iodonium/carbocation) intermediates, not a free C-radical chain. An asymmetric version is mentioned as ongoing work.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03936_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Ni-catalyzed 1,4-diarylation of 1,3-dienes with aryl iodides to form 1,4-diaryl-2-alkenes
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03936_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of Ni(II) precatalyst to active low-valent Ni(I)/Ni(0); also reduces Ni(III)-aryl intermediate B to Ni(I) C during the cycle
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03936_sup_1.electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ni(II)X2 to Ni(I) (A); A undergoes oxidative addition with ArI to give Ni(III)-Ar (B), which is cathodically reduced to ArNi(I) (C); 1,3-diene migratory insertion gives allyl-Ni(I) (D), a second ArI oxidative addition yields Ni(III) intermediate E, and reductive elimination delivers the 1,4-diaryl product 3 with regeneration of Ni(I) A.

key_intermediates.name: Ni(I)-Ln (active catalyst A in favored cycle)
Ni(I)-allyl/alkyl species D from migratory insertion of 1,3-diene
Ar-Ni(III)(X)-allyl intermediate E (after second oxidative addition)
Ni(0)-Ln (alternative active species in Ni(0)/Ni(II) cycle)

probe_reagents.name: NiBr2·diglyme (CV of catalyst)
probe_reagents.observation: Redox peak at E1/2 = -1.39 V (vs SCE) assigned to Ni(II)/Ni(I)

probe_reagents.name: NiBr2·diglyme + 1a (diene)
probe_reagents.observation: Two peaks at -1.39 V (Ni(II)/Ni(I)) and -2.60 V (free diene); no significant interaction of Ni(I)X with diene

probe_reagents.name: NiBr2·diglyme + 1a + 2aa (PhI)
probe_reagents.observation: Disappearance of -1.39 V peak and appearance of -2.60 V (Ni(I)/Ni(0)) peak, indicating Ni(I)X is the species that undergoes oxidative addition with ArI to form ArNi(III)X

probe_reagents.name: Iodobenzene (2aa) CV
probe_reagents.observation: E1/2 = -2.50 V (vs SCE); more easily reduced than diene

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric reaction without current
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeds, indicating Ni(0)-initiated pathway is also feasible

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03936_sup_1.notes: Authors propose two cycles: (favored) Ni(I)/Ni(III) pathway supported by CV showing Ni(I)X is the species engaging ArI; (alternative) Ni(0)/Ni(II) pathway not ruled out, supported by stoichiometric reaction proceeding without current — Ni(0) A inserts into ArI to form Ni(II)-Ar (B), then cathodic reduction to Ni(I) C, migratory insertion with diene to D, oxidative addition of second ArI to E (Ni(III)), reductive elimination yields product 3 and Ni(I) F, which is cathodically reduced back to Ni(0) A.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c02607.rxn_class_summary: Organoelectrophotocatalytic tandem radical cyclization of N-arylacrylamides with formamides or aldehydes to give acylated 3-CF3-2-oxindoles via carbamoyl/acyl radicals generated by phenanthrenequinone (PQ) under combined electrolysis and visible light
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c02607.electrochemical_role: PQ acts as a photoexcited HAT catalyst that abstracts the formyl C-H of formamide/aldehyde to give a carbamoyl/acyl radical and PQH•; PQH• is reoxidized at the RVC anode (via PQH2) to regenerate PQ, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c02607.electron_flow: Visible light excites PQ to PQ*, which abstracts the formyl H of formamide/aldehyde (2) to give an acyl/carbamoyl radical (45) and PQH•; 45 adds to the acrylamide 1 and the resulting C-radical cyclizes onto the arene to give cyclohexadienyl radical 47, which is rearomatized either by HAT to PQH• (path A) or by anodic oxidation/deprotonation (path B) to product 3; PQH2 is reoxidized at the RVC anode to close the PQ cycle while H+ is reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c02607.rate_determining_step: HAT from the formyl C-H of formamide/aldehyde by PQ* to generate the acyl/carbamoyl radical

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical 47 after addition/cyclization onto N-arylacrylamide arene

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO-carbamoyl adduct 46 detected by HRMS, supporting a free carbamoyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: control without PQ
probe_reagents.observation: No product 3 formed (nd), PQ is essential

probe_reagents.name: control without electricity
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops to 12%, anodic turnover of PQ is needed

probe_reagents.name: control without light
probe_reagents.observation: No product (nd); photoexcitation of PQ is required

probe_reagents.name: control without CH3SO3H
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops to 23%, acid promotes the reaction

probe_reagents.name: reaction under air
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product, radical chain sensitive to O2

probe_reagents.name: alternative catalysts (anthraquinone, naphthalene-1,2- and 1,4-dione)
probe_reagents.observation: Much lower yields (trace, 17%, 26%), highlighting PQ's unique photoelectrocatalytic activity

probe_reagents.name: DMF as formamide substrate
probe_reagents.observation: Inefficient, attributed to competing HAT from N-methyl C-H

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c02607.notes: Two pathways are proposed for rearomatization of cyclohexadienyl radical 47: path A (HAT to PQH•) and path B (anodic oxidation followed by deprotonation); the authors do not distinguish between them. A single-crystal X-ray structure (CCDC 2270962) of product 3 confirms regiochemistry. PQ plays a dual role as photo-HAT catalyst and anodically recyclable mediator, enabling oxidant- and metal-free conditions with H2 as the cathodic byproduct.

10.1021_acs.orglett.8b03682.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical fluorocyclization of N-allylcarboxamides to 5-fluoromethyl-2-oxazolines mediated by anodically generated hypervalent aryl-iodine(III) species using Et3N·5HF as fluoride source
10.1021_acs.orglett.8b03682.electrochemical_role: Anodic 2-electron oxidation of 4-methyliodobenzene (ArI) to a hypervalent ArI(III)/iodonium-type mediator that activates the alkene; HF provides fluoride and the cathode reduces protons
10.1021_acs.orglett.8b03682.electron_flow: ArI is oxidized at the anode (2e-) to a hypervalent I(III) species that electrophilically activates the allyl alkene of the N-allylcarboxamide, forming an iodiranium; intramolecular attack by the amide carbonyl oxygen gives an iodanyl-methyl oxazoline, which undergoes SN2 displacement by fluoride (from Et3N·5HF) to release the fluoromethyl oxazoline and regenerate ArI.

key_intermediates.name: anodically oxidized 4-methyliodobenzene (ArI cation/ArIF2-type hypervalent iodine(III) mediator)
alkene-coordinated iodonium (three-membered iodiranium) intermediate 4a from attack of allyl C=C on ArI(III)
5-(λ3-iodanyl)methyl oxazoline 4b formed by intramolecular ring-opening by the amide carbonyl oxygen
4-methyliodobenzene (ArI) released after SN2 displacement by fluoride

probe_reagents.name: 4-tert-butyliodobenzene mediator
probe_reagents.observation: mediator recovered quantitatively after electrolysis, supporting catalytic ArI/ArI(III) cycle

probe_reagents.name: ex-cell electrolysis protocol
probe_reagents.observation: pre-generated ArI(III) reagent (ex-cell) converted 1h to 2h cleanly, avoiding side reactions seen in-cell — supports a discrete ArI(III) mediator pathway

10.1021_acs.orglett.8b03682.notes: In-cell electrolysis of 2-phenyl substrate 1h gave intramolecular coupling side product; ex-cell (pre-generated ArI(III)) gave cleaner oxazoline 2h, indicating that direct anodic oxidation of substrate is a competing pathway suppressed when the hypervalent iodine mediator is used as a discrete reagent.

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04041_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-dichlorocyclization/dichlorination of enyne substrates using catalytic aryl iodide (p-Tol-I) as a redox mediator generating an aryl-λ3-iodane (ArICl2) that delivers chlorine radicals/atoms under undivided-cell electrolysis with Et3N·HCl as Cl source.
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04041_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of aryl iodide (p-Tol-I) to a hypervalent dichloro(aryl)-λ3-iodane (ArICl2) using chloride from Et3N·HCl; this hypervalent iodine mediator then transfers Cl to the organic substrate. Cathode reduces protons/Et3NH+ to H2.
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04041_sup_1.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes p-Tol-I in the presence of Cl- (from Et3N·HCl) to generate ArICl2; this λ3-iodane homolytically delivers a Cl• to the alkyne of the enyne, producing a vinyl radical that cyclizes 5-exo onto the alkene; the resulting C-radical abstracts/captures a second Cl from ArICl2, regenerating p-Tol-I.
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04041_sup_1.rate_determining_step: Chlorine atom transfer from ArICl2 to the alkyne (Cl• addition generating vinyl radical)

key_intermediates.name: vinyl/alkenyl chloride radical adduct from Cl• addition to alkyne
carbon-centered radical after 5-exo-trig cyclization onto pendant alkene

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction of 1a fully suppressed (2a N.R.); HRMS detected 1-chloro-2,2-diphenylethylene adduct (calc 215.0622, found 215.0626), trapping a Cl• / vinyl-Cl species.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed (N.R.) under standard electrolysis; with stoichiometric ArICl2 + BHT product still forms (45%), consistent with mediator-derived radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric 4-Me-C6H4ICl2 (preformed ArICl2)
probe_reagents.observation: Without current but with 2 equiv ArICl2 the reaction proceeds in 52% yield (entry 5), supporting ArICl2 as the active chlorinating intermediate; isolated in 20% yield, 49% ee in a separate stoichiometric run.

probe_reagents.name: Selectfluor / CsCl (alternative oxidant)
probe_reagents.observation: Without current, replacing Et3N·HCl with Selectfluor + CsCl gives 23% yield, indicating chemical oxidation of ArI to ArICl2 reproduces reactivity.
