probe_reagents.name: No p-Tol-I control
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction still proceeds in 21% yield without mediator, indicating a minor direct/background pathway, but mediator strongly accelerates the reaction.

probe_reagents.name: No Et3N·HCl control
probe_reagents.observation: No detectable product (N.D.), confirming chloride is the Cl source.

probe_reagents.name: No current control
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction without electrolysis, confirming requirement of anodic oxidation to generate ArICl2.

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04041_sup_1.notes: In-situ formation of ArICl2 was directly observed by 1H NMR of the crude electrolysis mixture (p-Tol-I + Et3N·HCl in DCM/HFIP, 10 mA, 2 h). Control experiments collectively support a mediator-based Cl-atom-transfer (radical) mechanism rather than direct anodic oxidation of substrate: (i) radical traps (DPE, BHT) shut down reaction and trap a Cl-containing radical; (ii) preformed ArICl2 substitutes for current; (iii) alternative chemical oxidants (Selectfluor/CsCl) recover reactivity. An alternative ionic pathway via ArICl2-mediated chloriranium ion cannot be fully excluded but is disfavored by radical-trapping outcomes and modest ee (49%).

10.1021_acs.orglett.5c03365.rxn_class_summary: NaBr-mediated electrochemical anti-Markovnikov hydration of aryl alkenes to β-aryl ethanols via an aryl epoxide intermediate
10.1021_acs.orglett.5c03365.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Br- to Br2/[Br]+ which acts as mediator to brominate the alkene; cathodic reduction of H2O generates OH-/H2 and later reduces the aryl epoxide to the anti-Markovnikov alcohol
10.1021_acs.orglett.5c03365.electron_flow: During constant-current electrolysis, anode oxidizes 2 Br- to Br2 which disproportionates with H2O to [Br]+/HBr; [Br]+ adds to the aryl alkene to form a cyclic bromonium that is opened by water to give a β-bromoalcohol, which is converted to an aryl epoxide by OH- (generated at the cathode from water reduction). Under constant-voltage electrolysis, the aryl epoxide is cathodically reduced (1e-, then protonation, then 1e-) to deliver the anti-Markovnikov β-aryl ethanol.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic ketyl/α-oxy radical (after cathodic 1e- reduction of epoxide)

probe_reagents.name: aliphatic alkene (1al) control
probe_reagents.observation: Yielded only the epoxide 4al (60%) with no anti-Markovnikov alcohol, indicating aryl group is required for cathodic epoxide reduction

probe_reagents.name: isolated aryl epoxide intermediate
probe_reagents.observation: Epoxide isolated and shown to be reduced to the β-aryl ethanol product, supporting it as an on-cycle intermediate

10.1021_acs.orglett.5c03365.notes: Mechanism is sequential paired electrolysis carried out in two stages (constant-current then constant-voltage). Alternative direct hydration pathways via benzylic carbocation or radical-polar crossover are ruled out implicitly by the requirement of the aryl epoxide intermediate; aliphatic substrates stop at the epoxide stage because their epoxides are not reducible under the cathodic conditions.

10.1021_acs.orglett.1c02661.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical selenylation of alkenes (three-component carboselenylation with styrenes/arenes) and direct C-H selenylation of activated arenes via Se-Se bond activation of diselenides without external oxidants
10.1021_acs.orglett.1c02661.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of diselenide (RSeSeR) generates a selenium-centered radical (RSe•) and a selenium anion (RSe-); the latter is reoxidized at the anode to RSe•. After radical addition to alkene/arene, the resulting carbon-centered radical is anodically oxidized to a cation, and H2 is evolved at the cathode (paired electrolysis).
10.1021_acs.orglett.1c02661.electron_flow: Cathodic reduction cleaves the Se-Se bond of PhSeSePh into PhSe• and PhSe- (the anion is reoxidized at the anode to PhSe•). PhSe• adds to a styrene to give a benzylic radical, which is anodically oxidized to a benzylic cation, trapped by a thiophene nucleophile, and deprotonated to product; on the arene branch PhSe• adds to the activated arene to give a radical that is anodically oxidized and deprotonated, with H2 evolution at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic carbon radical from PhSe• addition to styrene (intermediate B)
benzylic carbocation after anodic oxidation of B
arenium/thiophenyl radical adduct from PhSe• addition to 2-ethylthiophene (intermediate C)

probe_reagents.name: diphenyl diselenide (1a) - cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at 1.64 V and reduction peak at -0.98 V observed, indicating both oxidation and reduction processes are accessible in the catalytic cycle

probe_reagents.name: styrene (2a) - cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at 2.51 V, too high to be oxidized first under reaction conditions

probe_reagents.name: 2-ethylthiophene (3a) - cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at ~2.54 V, ruling out arene oxidation as the initiating event

probe_reagents.name: ITO electrode substitution
probe_reagents.observation: Replacing carbon anode with ITO gave no reaction; replacing Pt cathode with ITO decreased yield - indicates electrode plays vital role beyond simple electron transfer

probe_reagents.name: kinetic profile vs 3a concentration
probe_reagents.observation: Higher concentration of 3a (0.6 vs 0.3 mmol) increased rate/yield of 4a, consistent with arene trapping of cationic intermediate

probe_reagents.name: time-course of 4a vs 5b
probe_reagents.observation: Three-component product 4a accumulates while diselenylated arene byproduct 5b stays near 0%, indicating alkene pathway dominates when styrene present

10.1021_acs.orglett.1c02661.notes: Authors cannot rule out an alternative initiation in which PhSeSePh is first anodically oxidized to a radical cation, fragmenting into PhSe• and PhSe+; the favored pathway is cathodic reduction of the Se-Se bond followed by anodic reoxidation of PhSe-. Two parallel product channels: radical-polar crossover with styrene (route a, giving aryl-alkyl selenoethers 4) and radical addition/oxidation/deprotonation on activated arenes (route b, giving aryl-aryl selenoethers 5). H2 evolution at the cathode balances the anodic oxidation events (paired electrolysis).

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c03922_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical addition of Meldrum's acid-derived C-radicals to 1,3-dienes (e.g., 2,4-dimethyl-1,3-pentadiene) to give allylic methoxy-functionalized adducts
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c03922_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Meldrum's acid (1A) generates a C-centered radical that adds to the 1,3-diene; the resulting allyl radical is further oxidized to an allyl cation which is trapped by MeOH
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c03922_sup_1.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Meldrum's acid 1A to a C-centered radical (via SET/deprotonation); this radical adds to the 1,3-diene to give an allyl radical, which undergoes a second anodic oxidation to an allyl cation that is trapped by methanol to deliver the methoxylated adduct 3Aa.

key_intermediates.name: Meldrum's acid C-centered radical (after anodic SET/deprotonation of 1A)
BHT-trapped Meldrum's acid radical adduct 9 (C22H32O5, cyclohexadienone-type)
Allyl radical from radical addition to diene 2a
Allyl cation (post second SET) trapped by MeOH

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: With diene + BHT: 3Aa formation completely inhibited (0% yield); BHT-Meldrum's acid radical adduct 9 detected by HRMS (m/z 376.2265, calc 376.2250 for C22H32O5). Without diene + BHT under electrolysis: adduct 9 also detected by HRMS. Without electricity (BHT, 5h30, no current): adduct 9 NOT detected, confirming current is required to generate the radical.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited in presence of TEMPO; product 3Aa not obtained

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c03922_sup_1.notes: Control experiment without electricity but with electrodes immersed showed no BHT adduct, demonstrating that radical generation is electrochemically driven (not from background chemistry). The BHT adduct 9 is a cyclohexadienone arising from radical addition of the Meldrum's acid radical to BHT's aromatic ring.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c01130.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical metal-free 1,1-difunctionalization of gem-difluoroalkenes with two nucleophiles (alcohols/H2O and azoles) to give alpha-difluoro(alkoxyl/azolated) methylated ethers
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c01130.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the aryl gem-difluoroalkene to a radical cation; cathode reduces protons to H2 (no sacrificial oxidant needed)
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c01130.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the gem-difluoroalkene to a radical cation, which is attacked by Nu1-H; loss of a proton and intramolecular electron shift gives a benzyl radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a benzyl carbocation that is trapped by Nu2-H. Protons released are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: alpha-alkoxy/azolyl benzyl radical (II) after Nu1H addition, deprotonation and intramolecular electron shift
alpha-alkoxy/azolyl benzyl carbocation (III) from second anodic SET

probe_reagents.name: controlled-potential electrolysis at sub-oxidation potentials (1.25 V vs SCE, below 1.30 V onset)
probe_reagents.observation: no product at 1.25 V; 15% yield at 1.35 V, confirming need for anodic oxidation of the alkene

probe_reagents.name: 1H-imidazole / O2 trap (radical capture as hydroperoxide)
probe_reagents.observation: alpha-imidazolyl benzyl hydroperoxide/alcohol detected by GC-MS (23%), supporting a benzyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: biphenyl-type homo-coupling product observed
probe_reagents.observation: detection of (alpha-functionalized)-biphenyl species by GC-MS, consistent with benzyl radical II

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c01130.notes: DFT shows HOMO of the aryl gem-difluoroalkene is delocalized over the whole molecule (not just the olefin), rationalizing anodic SET. Mechanism is proposed as radical-cation / radical-polar crossover with two sequential anodic SET events; cathodic H2 evolution removes the need for a chemical oxidant. No KIE or TEMPO-trap experiments reported.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03901_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-difunctionalization (fluoroamination/fluoroalkoxylation) of gem-difluoroalkenes via anodic oxidation and sequential nucleophilic additions of fluoride and a second nucleophile (azole or alcohol)
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03901_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the gem-difluorostyrene to a radical cation; cathode reduces protons to H2 (paired electrolysis)
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03901_sup_1.electron_flow: Graphite anode removes one electron from the gem-difluoroalkene to give radical cation I; a nucleophile (F-, alcohol, or azole, modulated by HFIP H-bonding) adds at the CF2 terminus to give benzylic radical II; II is further oxidized at the anode to benzylic cation III, which is trapped by a second nucleophile to give product 4; at the Pt cathode, protons are reduced to H2.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic radical II/II' after 1st nucleophilic addition of F- (or alcohol/azole) at CF2 terminus
benzylic cation III/III' after second anodic oxidation
aryl trifluoromethyl ketone (oxidation trapping product under O2/MeOH)

probe_reagents.name: MeOH under O2 (radical trapping / oxygenation probe)
probe_reagents.observation: GC-MS detected aryl trifluoromethyl ketone (C14H9F3O, MW 250.22), consistent with a benzylic radical intermediate intercepted by O2

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, CsF, and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: CV in 0.1 M nBu4NBF4 HFIP/DCM (1:4), 0–3.0 V vs SCE, used to establish oxidation potentials of substrate vs product and fluoride

probe_reagents.name: Control: no electricity
probe_reagents.observation: 0% yield, confirming electrochemical initiation is required

probe_reagents.name: Control: under N2 vs air
probe_reagents.observation: 46% under N2 vs 50% under air; reaction not dependent on O2 for productive pathway

probe_reagents.name: Alternative alcohol co-solvents (TFE, tBuOH)
probe_reagents.observation: Lower yields due to competitive nucleophilic addition by the alcohol vs fluoride

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03901_sup_1.notes: Authors propose two regiochemical channels (II/III vs II'/III') leading to 4 or 4'; selectivity attributed to HFIP-mediated H-bonded aggregates that tune nucleophilicity of F-, alcohol, and azole. No DFT, KIE, or TEMPO trapping reported. Attempts to extend to 1,1-difluorodec-1-en-3-yne failed (insoluble anode deposit), and alkoxy-/azofluorination of difluoro-diene 1o was too messy to isolate.

10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00263.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical tandem trifluoromethylation (or sulfonylation)/semipinacol rearrangement of cyclobutanol-substituted allylic alcohols to give α-quaternary β-CF3 (or β-sulfonyl) cyclopentanones via 1,2-alkyl migration.
10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00263.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of CF3SO2Na (Langlois reagent) generates CF3SO2 radical that extrudes SO2 to give CF3 radical; after CF3 addition to the alkene, the resulting benzyl radical is further anodically oxidized to a benzyl carbocation that triggers ring expansion. Cathode reduces H2O/H+ to H2.
10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00263.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3SO2 radical which loses SO2 to give CF3 radical; CF3 radical adds to the styrenyl C=C of the allylic cyclobutanol giving a benzyl radical, which is oxidized again at the anode to a benzyl cation; 1,2-alkyl (cyclobutyl C-C) migration with deprotonation furnishes the α-quaternary cyclopentanone, while the cathode reduces water to H2.

key_intermediates.name: CF3SO2 radical (sulfonyl radical from anodic SET of Langlois reagent)
Benzylic carbon radical 5 (CF3 addition adduct to styrenyl cyclobutanol)
Ring-expanded oxocarbenium / protonated cyclopentanone after 1,2-alkyl migration

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited; no 2a formed; TEMPO-CF3 adduct detected by GC-MS, supporting a free CF3 radical.

probe_reagents.name: Methanol (as cosolvent)
probe_reagents.observation: Methanol-trapped (methoxy-incorporated) species observed, indicating a carbocation intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: CF3SO2Na / 1a cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: 1a oxidizes at Ep/2 = 2.1 V vs SCE while CF3SO2Na oxidizes at 1.4 V vs SCE, so CF3SO2Na is preferentially oxidized first, generating CF3 radical.

10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00263.notes: Selectivity for 1,2-migration over oxygen-trapping or deprotonation byproducts (4aa/4ab) relies on the high migratory aptitude of the cyclobutyl group. Competing arene trifluoromethylation byproduct 4aa is suppressed by lowering CF3SO2Na to 2.0 equiv and increasing H2O:MeCN ratio. The sulfonylation variant is proposed to follow an analogous pathway with ArSO2 radical instead of CF3 radical (no SO2 extrusion).

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04426.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical cyclizative dicarboxylation of o-vinylphenyl isocyanides with CO2 to give 3-substituted 2-oxoindolin-3-acetic acids in an undivided cell with sacrificial Mg anode
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04426.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction: either the o-vinylphenyl isocyanide alkene (path A) or CO2 (path B) is reduced at the cathode to generate a carbon-centered radical anion that initiates carboxylation; Mg anode is sacrificial
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04426.electron_flow: Cathode delivers one electron to either the vinyl arene (path A, favored) or CO2 (path B) to give a radical anion; this radical adds CO2 (or adds to the alkene), then a second cathodic electron transfer and protonation by H2O furnishes a dicarboxylated isocyanide that undergoes intramolecular cyclization onto the isocyanide and a 1,3-O→N acyl transfer to yield the 2-oxoindolin-3-acetic acid, while the Mg anode is oxidized as sacrificial counter-electrode.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic radical anion of o-vinylphenyl isocyanide (1a•-, path A)
alkyl radical 1a' after CO2 addition to alkene
dicarboxylated isocyanide intermediate (bis-carboxylate with pendant isocyanide)
seven-membered cyclic intermediate prior to 1,3-O→N acyl transfer

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; starting material 1a recovered in 86%, indicating a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace amount of product 2a observed; 2a isolated in 22% and a related product 3a in 21%, supporting radical intermediacy

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of CO2 and styrene
probe_reagents.observation: Reduction potentials are similar (·CO2-/CO2 E1/2 = -2.21 V; styrene E1/2 = -2.58 V vs SCE in DMF), so either substrate or CO2 could be reduced first, supporting two plausible pathways

probe_reagents.name: control without CO2
probe_reagents.observation: Reduced (non-carboxylated) product observed, consistent with path A involving initial cathodic reduction of the alkene

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04426.notes: Two mechanistic pathways are proposed; path A (cathodic reduction of the o-vinylphenyl isocyanide alkene first, followed by CO2 addition) is favored because in the absence of CO2 the reduced (non-carboxylated) substrate is observed. Path B (initial cathodic reduction of CO2 to ·CO2-, then addition to the alkene) cannot be ruled out. Regioselectivity for the five-membered oxindole is rationalized by preferential formation of a seven-membered ring intermediate over an eight-membered ring prior to 1,3-O→N acyl transfer.

10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00771.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component 1,2-bromoesterification of alkenes with carboxylic acids and N-bromosuccinimide via TEMPO-mediated anodic oxidation forming a bromonium intermediate.
10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00771.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of TEMPO to TEMPO+ which activates NBS toward Br+ transfer to the alkene; cathode reduces protons to H2.
10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00771.electron_flow: TEMPO is oxidized at the anode to TEMPO+, which activates NBS to deliver Br+ to the alkene, generating a cyclic bromonium ion; carboxylic acid opens the bromonium and deprotonation gives the β-bromo ester, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Used as a 50 mol% redox mediator/initiator; oxidized at the anode to TEMPO+ which activates NBS.

10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00771.notes: Mechanism is proposed without explicit radical trapping, CV, KIE, or DFT studies; TEMPO is invoked as a redox mediator rather than a radical trap. Pathway is cationic (bromonium) rather than radical, despite the electrochemical oxidative conditions.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c00121.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [2+1] cycloaddition of electron-rich alkenes with diazo compounds to give cyclopropanes, and [2+2] cycloaddition with styrenes to give cyclobutanes, both via anodically generated alkene radical cations
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c00121.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of an electron-rich alkene (e.g., trans-anethole) to an electrophilic alkene radical cation; cathodic reduction of the downstream cyclopropyl/cyclobutyl radical cation closes the cycle
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c00121.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the electron-rich alkene A to radical cation B; B is attacked by the nucleophilic diazo compound C to give distonic radical cation D, which extrudes N2 to give cyclopropyl radical cation E, then cathodic reduction yields cyclopropane F. In the [2+2] manifold, B adds to a second styrene G to form cyclobutanyl radical cation H, which is reduced at the cathode (or via chain) to cyclobutane I.

key_intermediates.name: alkene radical cation of electron-rich styrene (e.g., anethole)
cyclopropyl radical cation (E) after N2 extrusion
cyclobutanyl radical cation (H) from radical cation + styrene

probe_reagents.name: E/Z isomeric alkene substrates (stereochemical probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Both E and Z olefins converge to only trans-cyclopropanes, consistent with a stepwise radical-cation intermediate rather than a concerted pathway

probe_reagents.name: electricity on/off control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed without applied current, confirming electrochemical initiation is essential

probe_reagents.name: tetrasubstituted/stilbene substrates with NO2
probe_reagents.observation: Electron-poor stilbene (NO2) still reactive (58%) because electrochemistry accesses oxidation potentials outside photoredox/Fe(III) window; tetrasubstituted olefin unreactive

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c00121.notes: Authors explicitly note they cannot exclude a radical chain pathway in which radical cations E and H are reduced by an incoming neutral alkene (chain propagation) rather than at the cathode. Mechanism is proposed by analogy to prior Bauld/Ferreira/Kang radical-cation cycloadditions; no DFT, EPR, TEMPO trapping, or KIE experiments are reported.

10.1021_acs.orglett.2c03443.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical reductive functionalization of alkenes with CDCl3 as a one-carbon deuteration block, giving either deuterated cyclopropanes or hydrochloromethylation (CDCl2H/CDCl2 addition) products depending on conditions.
10.1021_acs.orglett.2c03443.electrochemical_role: Cathodic single-electron reduction of CDCl3 cleaves a C-Cl bond to generate a CDCl2 radical (preserving the C-D bond); after addition to the alkene, the resulting C-radical is further reduced at the cathode to a carbanion (radical-polar crossover) that either displaces chloride intramolecularly to form a cyclopropane or is protonated to give the hydrochloromethylation product.
10.1021_acs.orglett.2c03443.electron_flow: Cathode reduces CDCl3 by SET with C-Cl cleavage to give Cl- and a CDCl2 radical; this radical adds to the alkene, the resulting carbon radical is reduced by a second cathodic SET to a carbanion, which either expels a chloride intramolecularly to close a cyclopropane or is protonated by the medium to give the hydrochloromethylation product.

key_intermediates.name: CDCl2 radical (dichloromethyl-d radical from cathodic reduction of CDCl3)
β-CDCl2 alkyl radical from radical addition to alkene
β-CDCl2 alkyl carbanion (after second cathodic SET)

probe_reagents.name: deuterochloroform (CDCl3) as D-source
probe_reagents.observation: Deuterium incorporation typically 93-99% in products, indicating selective C-Cl cleavage with retention of C-D bond and no significant H/D scrambling.

10.1021_acs.orglett.2c03443.notes: Base-free conditions are emphasized to avoid the classical dichlorocarbene (CCl2) pathway that would scramble D and lead to dichlorocyclopropanes; the weaker C-D vs C-H BDE in the CDCl2 radical (~84 vs 95 kcal/mol) makes selective C-Cl cleavage non-trivial, and the electrochemical radical/radical-polar-crossover design is invoked to bypass this. Chemoselectivity between cyclopropanation and hydrochloromethylation is governed by the reaction conditions (proton availability vs intramolecular chloride displacement). Full mechanistic details (CV, DFT, radical traps) are likely on later pages not included here.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03149_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular oxidative dearomative spirocyclization of indole-tethered naphthoquinone amines to give a spiroindoline-fused naphthoquinone via radical intermediates
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03149_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the indole/aminonaphthoquinone substrate generates a nitrogen- or carbon-centered radical (and/or radical cation) that undergoes intramolecular C–C bond formation onto the indole C3, with subsequent oxidation/deprotonation to yield the spirocyclic product
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03149_sup_1.electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the indole-tethered aminonaphthoquinone substrate by single-electron transfer to give a delocalized N/C-centered radical; intramolecular radical addition onto the indole forms the spiro C–C bond, and further oxidation/deprotonation (with cathodic H2 evolution) furnishes the spiroindolenine 2.

key_intermediates.name: substrate-derived nitrogen/carbon radical (indolyl or aminyl radical) generated by anodic SET
indole C3 radical adduct / spirocyclic radical intermediate trapped by BHT to give compound 32
BHT-trapped open-chain adduct 33 (phenol coupled to indole C2/C3 position of substrate)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 2 obtained only in trace amount, indicating a radical intermediate is intercepted

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 2 only in trace; two BHT-derived radical adducts 32 (C35H37N2O3+, [M+H]+ found 533.2858) and 33 ([M+Na]+ found 557.2817) detected by HRMS, consistent with trapping of substrate-derived radicals at the indole position

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-Diphenylethylene
probe_reagents.observation: Product 2 still formed in 26% yield; DPE only partially inhibits the reaction, supporting but not strongly suppressing the radical pathway

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03149_sup_1.notes: Mechanistic support is based solely on radical-trapping experiments (TEMPO, BHT, 1,1-diphenylethylene) with HRMS detection of BHT adducts 32 and 33; no DFT, CV, or KIE studies are shown on these pages. The BHT adducts indicate that both an indole-centered carbon radical (32) and possibly an aminyl/aminonaphthoquinone-centered radical (33) are accessible intermediates, suggesting a stepwise radical cyclization pathway rather than a concerted process.

10.1021_acs.orglett.2c02976.rxn_class_summary: electroreductive three-component dicarbofunctionalization of styrenes with aryl iodides and unactivated alkyl iodides via Ni catalysis
10.1021_acs.orglett.2c02976.electrochemical_role: cathodic reduction of Ni(II) precatalyst to Ni(0) initiates the cycle; Ni(II) species (G) generated after alkyl iodide activation is re-reduced cathodically to Ni(0); sacrificial Fe anode provides counter-oxidation (M to M^n+)
10.1021_acs.orglett.2c02976.electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ni(II) to Ni(0) (A), which undergoes oxidative addition with ArI to give Ar-Ni(II) (B); Ni(I) (F) abstracts iodide from alkyl iodide to give alkyl radical (C) and Ni(II) (G); the alkyl radical adds to styrene forming benzylic radical (D) which combines with Ar-Ni(II) (B) to form Ni(III) (E); reductive elimination delivers the product and Ni(I) (F), while Ni(II) (G) is re-reduced at the cathode to Ni(0).

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Multicomponent product 4a abolished (0%); TEMPO-alkyl adduct 7 isolated in 13% from full system and 31% from alkyl iodide 3a alone, confirming alkyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline N-oxide)
probe_reagents.observation: EPR spin-adduct observed (g = 2.00502, αN = 14.54 G, αH = 20.92 G) for 3a, 1a+3a, and 1a+2a+3a; 1a and 2a alone were EPR-inactive, confirming alkyl radical originates from alkyl iodide 3a

10.1021_acs.orglett.2c02976.notes: Tertiary alkyl bromides gave higher yields than tertiary alkyl iodides (63% vs 13%), suggesting halide identity influences radical generation efficiency. The alkyl radical adds preferentially to styrene to give the thermodynamically favored benzylic radical, which dictates regioselectivity of the two C-C bond formations (Csp3-Csp3 and Csp3-Csp2).

10.1021_acs.orglett.5c01237.rxn_class_summary: electrochemical 1,2-bifunctionalization of styrenes (and alpha-alkylation of unactivated alkenes) with 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds via anodic radical-polar crossover, installing a C-C bond and a benzoate ester from in situ oxocarbenium ring-opening
10.1021_acs.orglett.5c01237.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the deprotonated 1,3-dicarbonyl enolate to an alpha-carbon radical; a second anodic oxidation of the benzylic C-radical (after addition to styrene) gives a benzylic cation; cathode reduces water/H+ to H2
10.1021_acs.orglett.5c01237.electron_flow: Base (OH- generated cathodically) deprotonates the 1,3-dicarbonyl; the anode oxidizes the enolate to an alpha-keto radical, which adds to the styrene to give a benzylic radical; a second anodic oxidation produces a benzylic cation that is captured intramolecularly by a carbonyl oxygen to form an oxocarbenium, which is opened by hydroxide/water to deliver the benzoate ester product, while H2 evolves at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic C-radical adduct (III) after radical addition to styrene
benzylic carbocation (IV) after second anodic oxidation
alpha-alkylated alkene product 5aa (from HAT/isomerization with unactivated alkene)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited; TEMPO-adduct of the 1,3-dicarbonyl radical isolated in 55% and characterized by NMR/HRMS, confirming radical II

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited; BHT-adduct (1a'') detected by HRMS, supporting radical intermediacy

probe_reagents.name: divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3aa formed (60%) only when reaction mixture was in the anodic chamber; no reaction in cathodic chamber, proving anodic initiation

probe_reagents.name: D2O (in place of H2O)
probe_reagents.observation: Product obtained with 99% deuterium incorporation (c1), consistent with protonation of enolate intermediate VII by water

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: 18O incorporated into the benzoate oxygen of the product (c2, HRMS), confirming water as the oxygen source

probe_reagents.name: anhydrous CH3CN under N2
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product formed, indicating water is essential

probe_reagents.name: MeOH co-solvent (trap for oxocarbenium)
probe_reagents.observation: Methoxy-trapped cyclic acetal d2 detected by HRMS, supporting oxocarbenium intermediate V

probe_reagents.name: ethyl 3-oxo-3-phenylpropanoate (1a1) as 1,3-dicarbonyl
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclized gamma-butyrolactone product d1 detected by NMR/HRMS, further supporting the oxocarbenium/cyclization pathway

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a vs 2a
probe_reagents.observation: E_ox(1a) < E_ox(2a) = 1.92 V vs Ag/AgCl, confirming the 1,3-dicarbonyl is oxidized first

10.1021_acs.orglett.5c01237.notes: With unactivated aliphatic alkenes the benzylic-cation/oxocarbenium pathway is unavailable, so after radical addition the system instead undergoes HAT/double-bond isomerization to give alpha-alkylated alkene products (5a-5d) rather than 1,2-bifunctionalization; benzoic acid is the sole byproduct from collapse of the dibenzoyl-derived oxocarbenium. Synthetic utility includes NaBH4 reduction to gamma-hydroxy esters and KOtBu-mediated conversion of 3aa to trans-cyclopropane 4aa with benzoate as leaving group.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03026.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative [3+2] cycloaddition of styrenes with 4-N-Boc-aminophenols to construct 5-aminocoumarans (2,3-dihydrobenzofurans) in an undivided cell
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03026.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of 4-N-Boc-aminophenol generates a phenoxyl/aryl-N radical (delocalized); cathode reduces protons to H2
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03026.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes 4-N-Boc-aminophenol (2a) by SET (with H+ loss) to a phenoxyl radical I that delocalizes to ortho-C radical II; II adds to styrene 1a to give benzylic radical III, which undergoes a second anodic SET to cation IV; intramolecular O-attack closes the dihydrobenzofuran (loss of H+), while H+ is reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: Delocalized carbon-centered (ortho) radical / quinone-imine-like radical II
Benzylic radical adduct III after addition to styrene 1a
Benzylic carbocation IV after second SET oxidation

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully inhibited; HRMS adducts 4a/4b corresponding to intermediates II and III detected

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully inhibited; HRMS adducts 5a/5b corresponding to intermediates II and III detected

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Onset oxidation potentials ~+1.15 V (1a) vs ~+0.55 V (2a); 2a is preferentially oxidized at the anode

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03026.notes: Unsuccessful substrates (electron-poor vinylarenes 1A-1D, 2-halo aminophenols 2A/2B, N-unprotected/NMe2/NHAc aminophenols 2C-2E, and p-methoxyphenol 2F) support the requirement for an easily oxidized N-protected aminophenol that generates a stabilized delocalized radical. No catalyst, oxidant, or sacrificial reagent needed; H2 is the only byproduct.

10.1021_acs.orglett.2c03774.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative sulfoximido-oxygenation of alkenes (styrenes) using NH-sulfoximines and alcohols (MeOH) to give β-alkoxy sulfoximides without metal catalysts or external oxidants
10.1021_acs.orglett.2c03774.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation: NH-sulfoximine (1a) is deprotonated to 1a' then anodically oxidized to an N-centered sulfoximidoyl radical (A); MeOH is also anodically oxidized in the presence of base to a methoxy radical. Cathode reduces 2 H+ to H2.
10.1021_acs.orglett.2c03774.electron_flow: At the anode, deprotonated NH-sulfoximine (1a') is oxidized to an N-centered sulfoximidoyl radical A, which adds to the styrene to give benzyl radical B; B is further oxidized at the anode to benzyl cation C, which is trapped by MeOH and deprotonated to give product 3a. At the cathode, protons are reduced to H2.

key_intermediates.name: benzyl/carbon radical adduct after radical addition to styrene (B)
benzyl cation intermediate (C) from further anodic oxidation of B

probe_reagents.name: radical clock (cyclopropyl-substituted alkene 7)
probe_reagents.observation: Standard product 8 (21%) and ring-opened product 9 (64%) isolated, confirming a benzyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: control experiment detecting sulfoximidoyl dimer 10 and radical addition adducts 11/12
probe_reagents.observation: Dimer 10 from N-centered sulfoximidoyl radical and adducts 11/12 of methoxy radical detected by HRMS, supporting both N-radical and MeO radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of styrene 2a, MeOH/K3PO4, and 1a
probe_reagents.observation: Ep(2a)=2.07 V vs SCE; Ep(MeOH)=1.29 V; oxidation peaks of MeOH and 1a overlap and are lower than styrene, indicating anodic oxidation of 1a/MeOH occurs preferentially (reaction onset ~0.8 V)

probe_reagents.name: constant potential electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction initiates at ~0.8 V (vs SCE), below styrene oxidation potential, indicating initiation by oxidation of 1a/MeOH rather than styrene

10.1021_acs.orglett.2c03774.notes: A minor pathway is proposed in which an anodically generated methoxy radical performs HAT on 1a to furnish the N-centered sulfoximidoyl radical A (Scheme 5, 'minor' arrow); the major pathway goes through direct anodic oxidation of deprotonated 1a'. Initiation by alkene oxidation is ruled out based on CV (styrene oxidation potential is higher than the operating potential).

10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00444.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical fluoroalkylation-distal heteroaryl/vinyl/alkynyl migration of unactivated alkenes bearing tertiary alcohols, affording fluorinated (hetero)aryl ketones
10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00444.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of fluoroalkanesulfinate (NaSO2RF) to generate RF radical (loss of SO2 and e-); a later carbon radical intermediate is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; cathode reduces protons to H2
10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00444.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes NaSO2CF3 (losing SO2) to give CF3 radical, which adds to the terminal alkene to form C-radical A; A undergoes intramolecular addition to the tethered heteroarene (thiazole/benzothiazole) to give N-centered radical B; β-cleavage/ring-opening gives stabilized C-radical C, which is oxidized at the anode to cation D; deprotonation yields the heteroaryl-migrated fluoroalkyl ketone, with H+ reduced at the cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: CF3 (or RF) radical from anodic oxidation of NaSO2CF3 with loss of SO2
Carbon-centered radical A from RF addition to terminal alkene of homoallylic tertiary alcohol
Cyclic nitrogen-centered (aminyl/iminyl) radical B from intramolecular ipso-cyclization onto thiazole/benzothiazole
Ring-opened C-centered radical C stabilized by the alkoxide/alcohol carbon (β-scission product)
Carbocation D after anodic oxidation of radical C

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of 3 equiv TEMPO under standard conditions completely suppressed formation of product 3y (0% yield), indicating radical intermediacy

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-Diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: CF3-DPE adduct 10 detected by GC-MS ([M+H]+ = 248.1), 21% by 19F NMR; alcohol adduct 11 isolated 18%; only 4% of 3y formed — supports CF3 radical addition

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of NaSO2RF reagents
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peaks: NaSO2CF2H at 0.590 V, NaSO2C6F13 at 0.742 V, NaSO2CF3 at 0.814 V (vs SCE), explaining the relative ease of anodic oxidation and reactivity ordering

probe_reagents.name: Derivatization of 3y (Me3OBF4 / NaBH4 / AgNO3)
probe_reagents.observation: 3y converted to aldehyde 12 in 68% over 3 steps, demonstrating utility of the aryl-migrated product

10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00444.notes: The 1,4-heteroaryl migration proceeds via ipso-radical cyclization onto the heteroarene (thiazole/benzothiazole) followed by β-C-C scission (Smiles-type/neophyl-like rearrangement). The final C-radical to cation step is proposed to be anodic (radical-polar crossover), with deprotonation generating the ketone. Lower reactivity of CF2H despite the lowest oxidation potential is attributed to rapid decomposition of CF2H radical on the electrode. Cathode role is H2 evolution from H+ reduction.

10.1021_acs.orglett.1c03284.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical cross-coupling of α,β-unsaturated carboxylic acids (or heteroarenes) with 2-fluoromalonates to give monofluoroalkyl-substituted alkenes/(hetero)arenes via Cp2Fe-mediated radical decarboxylative fluoroalkylation
10.1021_acs.orglett.1c03284.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+ which then oxidizes the fluoromalonate carbanion (51) to the monofluoroalkyl radical (52); cathode reduces MeOH to MeO- and 1/2 H2
10.1021_acs.orglett.1c03284.electron_flow: MeOH is reduced at the Pt cathode to MeO- and H2; MeO- deprotonates 2-fluoromalonate 2 to carbanion 51 and α,β-unsaturated acid 1 to carboxylate 53. At the RVC anode, Cp2Fe is oxidized to Cp2Fe+ (Ep/2 = 0.46 V), which oxidizes 51 (Ep/2 = 0.45 V) by SET to give fluoroalkyl radical 52; 52 adds to 53 to form benzylic radical 54, which undergoes anodic SET oxidation and loss of CO2 to give the E-alkene product 3.

key_intermediates.name: fluoromalonate carbanion (51) from deprotonation of dimethyl 2-fluoromalonate by MeO-
monofluoroalkyl radical (52) from SET oxidation of 51
cinnamate carboxylate (53) from deprotonation of cinnamic acid
benzylic radical adduct (54) bearing carboxylate, from radical addition of 52 to 53
E-monofluoroalkylated styrene product (3) after SET oxidation and decarboxylation of 54

probe_reagents.name: potassium cinnamate (46)
probe_reagents.observation: replacing cinnamic acid 1 with K-cinnamate 46 still afforded product 3 in 65% yield, supporting the carboxylate as the active intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE, 47)
probe_reagents.observation: difunctionalized methoxy adduct 48 isolated in 61% yield (radical addition of 52 followed by MeOH addition after SET), confirming fluoroalkyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: N-aryl acrylamide (49)
probe_reagents.observation: oxidative radical cascade cyclization gave fluoroalkyl oxindole 50 in 80% yield, supporting radical addition/cyclization pathway

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of Cp2Fe, 2, and 1
probe_reagents.observation: Ep/2(Cp2Fe)=0.46 V, Ep/2(2)=1.21 V, Ep/2(53)=1.55 V, Ep/2(51)=0.45 V vs Ag/AgCl — indicates Cp2Fe+ can oxidize 51 but not 2 or 53 directly, supporting mediator role

10.1021_acs.orglett.1c03284.notes: Cp2Fe acts as a redox mediator allowing selective anodic SET to the fluoromalonate carbanion while avoiding direct oxidation of the cinnamate (which is harder to oxidize). The catalyst is regenerated at the electrode without sacrificial oxidant. Allyl chloride analog (45) could not be obtained, suggesting limits when EWG cannot stabilize the radical/anion. Decarboxylation step occurs after SET oxidation of benzylic radical 54 to a cation.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03936.rxn_class_summary: Nickel-catalyzed electrochemical 1,2-diarylation of 1,3-dienes with aryl iodides under mild conditions without chemical reductants
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03936.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction regenerates low-valent Ni(I) species from Ni(III) (and Ni(II)) intermediates in the Ni(I)/Ni(III) catalytic cycle, replacing the need for a stoichiometric chemical reductant
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03936.electron_flow: Ni(II) precatalyst is cathodically reduced to Ni(I) (A); A undergoes oxidative addition with ArI to give Ni(III) (B), which is cathodically reduced to Ar-Ni(I) (C); migratory insertion of the 1,3-diene gives allyl-Ni(I) (D), a second oxidative addition with Ar'I gives Ni(III) intermediate E, and reductive elimination delivers the 1,2-diarylated product while regenerating Ni(I) for the next cycle.

key_intermediates.name: Ni(I)X-Ln (species A, active catalyst generated in situ from Ni(II))
Ar-Ni(I)-Ln (species C, after cathodic reduction of B)
allyl/homoallyl Ar-substituted Ni(I) species D (after migratory insertion of 1,3-diene into Ar-Ni bond)
Ar-Ni(III)(Ar')-Ln species E (second oxidative addition with aryl iodide)

probe_reagents.name: chiral PyOx/BiOx ligands (L1-L7) for asymmetric control
probe_reagents.observation: Up to 56% ee for diarylation (L6); ligand L7 switches chemoselectivity from diarylation to hydroarylation (5a, 35%, 1% ee), implicating divergent Ni-allyl reactivity

probe_reagents.name: solvent (DMA) as putative H-atom source in hydroarylation
probe_reagents.observation: Authors state the hydrogen of hydroarylation product may originate from the solvent (no explicit D-labeling reported on this page)

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03936.notes: Mechanism is proposed based on literature precedent and ligand-controlled chemoselectivity (diarylation vs hydroarylation) rather than direct mechanistic probes on these pages. Authors explicitly do not rule out an alternative Ni(0)/Ni(II) pathway (cycle through A', B', C') shown in Scheme 5. Regioselectivity is exclusively 1,2- (>20:1 vs 1,4-) and E/Z >20:1 for diarylation products.

10.1021_acs.orglett.8b03274.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative amino- and oxyselenation of styrenes with diphenyl diselenide and amine or alcohol nucleophiles to give vicinal aminoselenides or oxyselenides under undivided-cell, metal- and oxidant-free conditions
10.1021_acs.orglett.8b03274.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of PhSeSePh generates PhSe• and PhSe-; anodic oxidation regenerates PhSe• from PhSe- (and/or oxidizes the styrene/benzylic radical to a benzylic cation); a parallel anodic path oxidizes PhSeSePh to a seleniranium-type intermediate that is attacked by NuH
10.1021_acs.orglett.8b03274.electron_flow: At the cathode, PhSeSePh is reduced to PhSe• and PhSe- (with concomitant H2 evolution from ROH); PhSe• adds regioselectively to the styrene to give a benzylic radical B, which is further oxidized at the anode to benzylic cation C. Nucleophile (amine or alcohol) attacks C and deprotonation gives the 1,2-difunctionalized product 4; alternatively PhSeSePh is directly oxidized at the anode to a seleniranium ion E that is opened by NuH.

key_intermediates.name: β-(phenylseleno)benzylic radical B (from PhSe• addition to styrene)
β-(phenylseleno)benzylic cation C (anodic oxidation of B)
cyclic seleniranium ion E (from PhSeSePh anodic oxidation)
ammonium/oxocarbenium adduct D (NuH attack on C)

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl-substituted styrene (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: ring-opened/retained product 8f formed (73%), consistent with a carbon-radical intermediate from PhSe• addition

probe_reagents.name: diphenyl diselenide (PhSeSePh) - CV
probe_reagents.observation: oxidation peak at 1.88 V and reduction peak at -1.74 V; indicates both anodic and cathodic involvement

probe_reagents.name: styrene 1a - CV
probe_reagents.observation: oxidation peak observed at 2.25 V

probe_reagents.name: benzotriazole 5a - CV
probe_reagents.observation: no obvious oxidation peak, ruling out direct anodic oxidation of the N-nucleophile

10.1021_acs.orglett.8b03274.notes: Two parallel anodic pathways are proposed: (i) anodic oxidation of the β-selenyl benzylic radical B to cation C followed by NuH addition (favored), and (ii) anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh to a cyclic seleniranium intermediate E that is opened by NuH. H2 evolution from ROH at the cathode is part of the paired electrolysis. No TEMPO/BHT trapping or KIE was reported.

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c03922.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical regio- and stereoselective 1,4-carboalkoxylation of 1,3-dienes with Meldrum's acid-type 1,3-dioxan-4,6-diones and alcohols to give (E)-polyfunctionalized allyl ethers
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c03922.electrochemical_role: Paired electrolysis in undivided cell: cathodic reduction of R5OH generates alkoxide (with H2 release); anodic oxidation of deprotonated Meldrum's acid enolate (Eox = 0.44 V vs SCE in DMF) generates an electron-poor C-radical that adds to the diene, followed by a second anodic oxidation of the resulting allyl radical to an allyl cation
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c03922.electron_flow: Cathode reduces the alcohol to alkoxide + H2; the alkoxide deprotonates Meldrum's acid to give enolate 10, which is anodically oxidized to electron-poor radical 11. Radical 11 adds to the less hindered terminus of the 1,3-diene to give allyl radical 12, which is further oxidized at the anode to allyl cation 13; the alkoxide traps 13 at the 1,4-position to deliver the (E)-allyl ether product.

key_intermediates.name: Electron-poor C-centered radical from Meldrum's acid (11)
Alkoxide (R5O-) from cathodic reduction of alcohol

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited, consistent with radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited; BHT-Meldrum's adduct 9 detected by HRMS only when current was applied (with or without diene), supporting anodic generation of radical 11

probe_reagents.name: On/off current control
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction without power supply, even in the presence of MeONa; current is required

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(enolate-MA) = 0.44 V vs SCE; Eox(diene 2a) = 1.81 V and Eox(diene 2d) = 1.16 V vs SCE in DMF — enolate is far easier to oxidize than the dienes, supporting initial anodic oxidation of the enolate rather than the diene

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c03922.notes: 1,4-regioselectivity (vs the more common 1,2-mode in radical carbooxygenations) is rationalized by an interplay between the EWG character and steric hindrance of the 1,3-dioxan-4,6-dione moiety disfavoring addition at C2, plus a proposed H-bonding directing event from the boat conformation of Meldrum's acid steering alcohol delivery to C4 of cation 11/13. Authors explicitly state further mechanistic studies are underway.

10.1021_acs.orglett.1c00704.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-dichlorination of aromatic compounds and alpha,beta-unsaturated substrates using Cl3CCN as the chlorine source via paired electrolysis
10.1021_acs.orglett.1c00704.electrochemical_role: Paired electrolysis: cathodic reduction of Cl3CCN releases a chloride anion and a neutral radical A, which is further reduced to dichloroacetonitrile anion B; at the anode, chloride (from cathode and/or from TEAC supporting electrolyte) is oxidized to molecular Cl2, which then chlorinates the substrate
10.1021_acs.orglett.1c00704.electron_flow: At the cathode, Cl3CCN accepts an electron to release Cl- and radical A, which gains a second electron to form the dichloroacetonitrile anion B; simultaneously, at the anode, chloride (from B-derived release and from TEAC) is oxidized to Cl2, which then electrophilically chlorinates the arene or alpha,beta-unsaturated substrate to give the 1,2-dichloride product.

key_intermediates.name: molecular chlorine (Cl2) generated at the anode
chloronium-like / electrophilic chlorination intermediate on aromatic (1 -> 2)

probe_reagents.name: N-allyl-N-(2-propenyl)-p-toluenesulfonamide probe 5 (chloride vs chloride-radical probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Tetrachlorinated open-chain product 6 isolated in 76% yield (consistent with Cl2/Cl+); the cyclized pyrrolidine product 7 (diagnostic of Cl radical) was not detected, supporting chlorine (Cl2) rather than Cl* as the active species

probe_reagents.name: KI-starch test (atmosphere on 1 mol scale of 1b)
probe_reagents.observation: Positive KI-starch response confirmed the presence of Cl2 in the reaction

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of reaction mixtures (acetyl aniline 1b and cinnamide 3a with Et4NCl / Cl3CCN)
probe_reagents.observation: Both mixtures share peaks at ca. -1.7 V vs SCE (reduction of Cl3CCN) and +1.8 V vs SCE (oxidation of chloride), and scans starting from either polarity give identical patterns, indicating a single dominant pathway involving cathodic reduction of Cl3CCN and anodic oxidation of Cl-

10.1021_acs.orglett.1c00704.notes: Authors explicitly distinguish chlorine (Cl2) from chlorine radical using the diallylsulfonamide probe 5: trapping a Cl* would have given cyclized pyrrolidine 7, but only the open tetrachloride 6 formed, arguing against a radical-chain Cl* pathway. For alpha,beta-unsaturated substrates, 1 equiv TEAC is required because no proton is generated to recycle Cl-; the Et4N+ pairs with anion B. The chlorination step itself is described as independent of the electrochemical process (electrophilic addition of in situ Cl2), so the role of electrolysis is on-demand Cl2 generation from Cl3CCN.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03901.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical fluorination-difunctionalization of gem-difluoroalkenes with CsF and nucleophiles (alcohols, azoles) to deliver trifluoromethylated/fluoroazolation products
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03901.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the gem-difluoroalkene substrate (oxidation onset 0.80 V vs SCE) initiates the transformation; fluoride/nucleophile addition and further oxidation generate cationic intermediates trapped by F- and nucleophile
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03901.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the gem-difluoroalkene (1a) to a radical cation; fluoride (from CsF) adds to give a benzylic radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation that is trapped by the nucleophile (alcohol or azole) to install the second functional group.

key_intermediates.name: gem-difluoroalkene radical cation (from anodic SET on 1a)
benzyl radical (after fluoride addition to radical cation)
benzyl cation (after second anodic oxidation) trapped by nucleophile
oxidative gem-difluoroolefin intermediate (probed but not required)

probe_reagents.name: 4-vinylbiphenyl (5, normal alkene control)
probe_reagents.observation: No difluorination product 6 formed under standard conditions (2.0-3.5 V), indicating non-fluorinated alkenes are unreactive and the gem-difluoroalkene functionality is essential

probe_reagents.name: constant anode potential control (0.70 V and 0.90 V vs SCE on 1a)
probe_reagents.observation: At 0.70 V no product 2a formed; at 0.90 V 12% yield of 2a, supporting that anodic oxidation of the gem-difluoroalkene is required to initiate the reaction

probe_reagents.name: O2 (radical trapping under O2 atmosphere)
probe_reagents.observation: Ketone 7 (MW 250.2, ArC(O)CF3) detected by GC-MS, indicating a benzyl radical intermediate from 1a is involved

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03901.notes: Authors also probed whether an oxidative gem-difluoroolefin (overoxidized alkene) intermediate forms (Scheme 2b) — control experiments instead supported direct anodic oxidation of the gem-difluoroalkene. Full proposed pathway is given in Figure S3 (SI). A radiofluorination variant with K[18F]F-kryptofix was demonstrated (5% RCY), consistent with the same mechanism.

10.1021_acs.orglett.0c01470.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical iodosulfonylation/spirocyclization of 1,5-enyne-containing para-quinone methides with arylsulfonyl hydrazides and KI to give iodosulfonated spirocyclohexadienone-containing (E)-indenes.
10.1021_acs.orglett.0c01470.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (I-) to iodonium (I+) via loss of two electrons; I+ acts as redox mediator that oxidizes TsNHNH2 to generate arylsulfonyl iodide (Ts-I) which homolyzes to a sulfonyl radical and an iodine radical (re-oxidized to I+ at the anode). Cathode reduces phenoxyl radical intermediate B' to phenolate anion C and reduces protons to H2.
10.1021_acs.orglett.0c01470.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes I- to I+ (2 e-), which oxidizes TsNHNH2 to Ts-I; homolysis gives Ts• that adds 1,6 to the p-QM alkene to form cyclohexadienone radical B, which tautomerizes to phenoxyl radical B'; the cathode reduces B' to phenolate C, then I+ activates the tethered alkyne and intramolecular 5-exo-dig ipso-cyclization furnishes the iodo-spiroindene 3a, with concomitant cathodic reduction of protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: iodonium cation (I+) generated by anodic oxidation of I-
cyclohexadienone carbon radical B (sulfonyl 1,6-addition adduct to p-QM)
phenol-type aromatic radical B' (zwitterionic resonance form of B)
phenolate anion C (cathodic reduction of B')
vinyl/alkenyl iodonium-alkyne adduct D (I+ added to alkyne of C)
diarylmethyl sulfone 4 (proposed key intermediate isolated by control)

probe_reagents.name: no current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product 3a formed without electric current, confirming electrochemistry is essential.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Complete inhibition of reaction (0% 3a), supporting a radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: diarylmethyl sulfone 4 (isolated intermediate) + KI under standard conditions
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3a (28%) plus recovered p-QM 1a (47%), implicating 4 as an on-pathway intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: intermediate 4 + TsNHNH2 + KI
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3a in 80% yield, confirming 4 as a key intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: intermediate 4 + NIS / NaOH (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3a in 71% yield, indicating that I+ (electrogenerated) activates the alkyne for nucleophilic cyclization.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of KI, 1a, 2a and mixtures
probe_reagents.observation: Only KI-containing solutions show an oxidation peak at 1.85 V vs Ag/AgCl; 1a and 2a are not oxidized in 0-3.0 V window, so KI is the species oxidized at the anode.

10.1021_acs.orglett.0c01470.notes: KI plays a triple role: electrolyte, redox mediator/catalyst (I-/I+), and iodine source for product C-I bond. Cathodic half-reaction is proton reduction to H2 (paired electrolysis). The (E)-selectivity arises from a 5-exo-dig ipso-cyclization of intermediate D. n-Butyl and cyclopropyl alkynyl substrates (1q, 1r) failed, suggesting aryl stabilization of the vinyl cation/radical at the alkyne terminus is required.

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c00756.rxn_class_summary: Metal-free electrocatalytic iodide-mediated cascade bicyclization of N-allyl enamine carboxylates to give [3.1.0]-azabicycles and γ-butyrolactone-fused 1-pyrrolines
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c00756.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide to a hypervalent/cationic I+ species which acts as the active electrophilic mediator; cathodic reduction of protons to H2 maintains charge balance
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c00756.electron_flow: At the graphite anode, I- is oxidized (-2e-) to I+, which activates the pendant allyl alkene of the N-allyl enamine to trigger 5-exo-tet iodocyclization to a C-I pyrroline; tautomerization then enables either intramolecular SN2 cyclopropanation (regenerating I-) to give the [3.1.0]-azabicycle, or, for tetrasubstituted enamines, ester O-attack/dealkylation to give the γ-butyrolactone-fused pyrroline. Protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c00756.rate_determining_step: Anodic electro-oxidation of iodide to I+ (CV shows oxidation peaks at 0.86, 1.20, and 1.43 V required for catalyst turnover)

key_intermediates.name: I+ (cationic iodide / hypervalent iodine species) generated by anodic oxidation of I-
iodonium-activated alkene (iodiranium on pendant allyl of enamine)
iodocyclized pyrroline intermediate B (5-exo-tet cyclized C-I pyrroline)
tautomerized pyrroline intermediate C bearing CH2I and enamine ester

probe_reagents.name: molecular I2 (catalytic or stoichiometric)
probe_reagents.observation: No formation of 2a with I2 alone (cat. or stoich.), confirming that electrochemical oxidation to I+ is required and that I2 itself is not the active species

probe_reagents.name: dry MeCN (no water)
probe_reagents.observation: Diminished yield of 2a in absence of water; water aids KI solubility, stabilizes charged intermediates, and facilitates proton mobility

probe_reagents.name: O2 / Ar atmosphere control
probe_reagents.observation: Yields under air (84%), O2 (85%), and Ar (81%) are essentially identical, ruling out aerobic oxidation and supporting a non-radical (ionic) pathway

probe_reagents.name: isolated iodocyclized intermediate 5a' (cis)
probe_reagents.observation: Isolated with 1 equiv KI at shorter time; under standard conditions cis-5a' lactonizes to 4a while trans-5a is recovered, confirming the iodocyclized pyrroline as a productive intermediate

probe_reagents.name: putative alcohol 6a
probe_reagents.observation: Not detected, excluded as an intermediate in lactone 4 formation

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c00756.notes: Atmosphere-independent yields and isolation of iodocyclized pyrrolines argue against a radical pathway and support an ionic iodonium/5-exo-tet mechanism. Divergence between [3.1.0]-azabicycle (R2 = H, cyclopropanation) and γ-butyrolactone-fused pyrroline (R2 = alkyl, ester annulation/dealkylation) is rationalized by substrate substitution. Borneol ester gave only modest dr (1:1.27), suggesting weak stereocontrol in the cyclization step.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03026_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [3+2] oxidative annulation of 4-methoxystyrene with N-Boc-4-hydroxyaniline to form a 2,3-dihydrobenzofuran via radical intermediates
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03026_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the para-hydroxyaniline (and/or styrene) to generate phenoxyl/aryl radical and quinone-imine type intermediates that couple with the styrene
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03026_sup_1.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes N-Boc-4-hydroxyaniline 2a to a phenoxyl/N-centered radical (II); this radical adds to the terminal carbon of 4-methoxystyrene 1a, generating a stabilized benzylic radical (III); further oxidation and intramolecular O-attack closes the 2,3-dihydrobenzofuran ring of 3a, with H2 evolution at the Pt cathode.

key_intermediates.name: phenoxyl/aminyl radical from N-Boc-4-hydroxyaniline (species II, oxidized aryloxy radical with N-Boc)
benzylic C-radical adduct from styrene addition to phenoxyl radical (species III)
ortho-quinone-imine/methide-like electrophile leading to cyclization to dihydrobenzofuran 3a

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (only trace 3); BHT adducts 4a (with aryloxy/aminyl radical, HRMS [M+Na]+ 450.2617) and 4b (with benzylic radical from styrene, HRMS [M+Na]+ 584.3335) detected

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (only trace 3); TEMPO adducts 5a (HRMS [M+Na]+ 387.2271) and 5b (HRMS [M+H]+ 499.3166) detected, supporting both aryloxy radical and benzylic radical intermediates

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03026_sup_1.notes: Current screening (Table S6) identified 10 mA as optimal (93% yield, 2 h). Mechanism proposal is supported only by radical trapping with HRMS detection of adducts; no KIE, CV, or DFT data shown on these pages.

10.1021_acs.orglett.2c03443_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical deuteration/functionalization of alkenes using CDCl3 as a deuterated dichloromethyl radical source
10.1021_acs.orglett.2c03443_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic/cathodic generation of dichloromethyl-d radical (·CDCl2) from CDCl3, which adds to the alkene as the first step
10.1021_acs.orglett.2c03443_sup_1.electron_flow: Electrolysis homolyzes CDCl3 to generate a ·CDCl2 radical which adds to the styrene C=C; the resulting benzylic radical either cyclizes/closes to give cyclopropane 2ag or dimerizes to S1, and in fluorinated substrate S2 defluorination delivers S3, confirming radical addition as the initiating step.

probe_reagents.name: styrene (radical acceptor probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction with CDCl3 under standard conditions gave cyclopropane 2ag and isolable dimer S1, indicating ·CDCl2 addition to alkene

probe_reagents.name: fluorinated substrate S2
probe_reagents.observation: Defluorination product S3 was obtained, consistent with radical addition of ·CDCl2 followed by β-F loss

10.1021_acs.orglett.2c03443_sup_1.notes: Mechanism supported by isolation of dimer S1 (radical–radical coupling byproduct) and defluorinated product S3 from a fluorinated probe; both implicate ·CDCl2 radical as the chain-initiating species. Comparison table also shows electrochemistry gives higher D-incorporation (96–98%) at low CDCl3 loading vs. non-electrochemical conditions.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03940.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical reductive cross-coupling of α-aryl vinyl bromides with β-alkenyl bromides to construct 1,3-dienes using a sacrificial stainless steel anode that releases Ni catalyst in situ
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03940.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of stainless steel anode releases Ni ions into solution; cathodic reduction generates active Ni(0) catalyst B and reduces Ni(II)/Ni(III) intermediates throughout the catalytic cycle
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03940.electron_flow: Stainless steel anode is oxidized to release Ni(II) (A), which is cathodically reduced to Ni(0) (B); B performs oxidative addition with β-alkenyl bromide to give Ni(II) (C), then cathodic reduction yields Ni(I) (D); a second oxidative addition with α-alkenyl bromide gives Ni(III) (E), which reductively eliminates the 1,3-diene and Ni(I)-Br (F), which is reduced back to B.

key_intermediates.name: Ni(II) precatalyst A (from anodic dissolution of stainless steel)
Ni(II)-alkenyl bromide complex C (oxidative addition of β-alkenyl bromide)
Ni(I)-alkenyl species D (after cathodic reduction of C)
Ni(III)-bis(alkenyl) bromide species E (after 2nd oxidative addition of α-alkenyl bromide)
Ni(I)-Br species F (after reductive elimination releasing 1,3-diene)

probe_reagents.name: (Z)-(2-bromovinyl)benzene (1m)
probe_reagents.observation: 1:1 mixture of E/Z diene isomers obtained, indicating loss of alkene geometry during the coupling

probe_reagents.name: alkenyl chloride / iodide variants
probe_reagents.observation: Lower yields of 1,3-diene 3d with more homocoupling side products when halide is changed from Br

probe_reagents.name: Pre-electrolysis with SS anode/Ni cathode (no Ni catalyst added)
probe_reagents.observation: ICP-OES detected Fe (552.6 mg/L), Cr (187.1 mg/L), Ni (63.9 mg/L) in solution; subsequent addition of substrates under Gong's protocol gave product 3a in 28% yield, confirming in situ generation of Ni catalyst from electrodes

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Supported proposed redox events of Ni species (details in SI)

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c03940.notes: Notable that no exogenous metal catalyst or chemical reductant is required: Ni is generated in situ from the stainless steel anode (which also contains Fe and Cr by ICP-OES). The Z/E scrambling with 1m suggests a radical or planar alkenyl-Ni intermediate rather than fully stereoretentive oxidative addition. TMSCl additive likely activates the electrode surface or Ni species.

10.1021_acs.orglett.5c00193.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular bromocyclization of alpha-allenols to enantioenriched spirocyclic 2,5-dihydrofuran derivatives using KBr as the bromine source
10.1021_acs.orglett.5c00193.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Br- to Br radical, which dimerizes to Br2 (and Br3-) as the active electrophilic brominating species; cathode concurrently reduces water to H2 and OH-
10.1021_acs.orglett.5c00193.electron_flow: At the anode, Br- is oxidized (two sequential peaks at ~1.0 and 1.4 V vs SCE) to Br•, which dimerizes to Br2 and equilibrates with Br3-; Br2 then electrophilically activates the allene of (R)-1h to a bromonium ion, which is opened by intramolecular anti-attack of the pendant hydroxyl followed by deprotonation to give the dihydrofuran. At the cathode, water is reduced to H2 + OH-.
10.1021_acs.orglett.5c00193.rate_determining_step: Slow electrochemical release of Br2 (controlling Br2 concentration is responsible for the high yield vs direct Br2 addition)

key_intermediates.name: bromonium ion intermediate (int. I) from allenol

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of KBr and substrate 1e
probe_reagents.observation: Two anodic peaks at ~1.0 and 1.4 V (Br-/Br• and Br3-/Br2); oxidative current increases when KBr + 1e are combined, indicating chemical reaction between electrogenerated Br2 and the allenol

probe_reagents.name: molecular Br2 (stoichiometric, direct or dropwise)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave product 2a in only 28% (direct) and 25% (dropwise) yield, much lower than the electrochemical conditions; KBr/Br2 combination gave 47%, supporting slow in situ Br2 release

probe_reagents.name: enantiopure (R)-1h and (S)-1h substrates
probe_reagents.observation: Chirality fully transferred (98% ee retained), consistent with a stereospecific bromonium-ion/anti-cyclization pathway rather than a free radical/racemizing route

10.1021_acs.orglett.5c00193.notes: Chirality transfer with high ee retention argues for an ionic bromonium-ion mechanism (cation-initiated electrophilic cyclization) rather than a free Br-radical addition pathway; the role of electrochemistry is to slowly generate Br2/Br3- in situ rather than to perform direct SET on the substrate.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c00310.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical difluoromethylation of electron-rich olefins (enamides and styrenes) using HCF2SO2Na as the CF2H radical source in an undivided cell, giving (E)-difluoromethylated enamides or β-hydroxy-difluoromethylated styrene products.
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c00310.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF2HSO2Na (sodium difluoromethanesulfinate) generates the CF2H radical via loss of SO2; a second anodic oxidation of the alkyl radical adduct gives a benzylic/α-aminocarbocation; cathode reduces H+ to H2.
10.1021_acs.orglett.3c00310.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF2HSO2Na (1e-) to release SO2 and a CF2H radical, which adds to the electron-rich olefin to give a stabilized α-amino or benzylic radical I; I is further oxidized at the anode to carbocation II, which either undergoes E1 deprotonation (enamides, route A) or is trapped by water (styrenes, route B); 2H+ are reduced at the cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: CF2H radical (from anodic oxidation of HCF2SO2- and loss of SO2)
α-amino/benzylic C-radical I from CF2H addition to electron-rich alkene
benzylic/α-amino carbocation II (from anodic oxidation of I)
β-hydroxy-CF2H styrene adduct (route B, H2O addition to cation)

probe_reagents.name: cyclohexene (unactivated/non-stabilizing alkene control)
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction, indicating that a stabilized (α-amino or benzylic) radical I is required.

probe_reagents.name: alkyl-substituted (non-aryl, non-enamide) alkenes
probe_reagents.observation: Poorly reactive (<20% yield), supporting the need for radical stabilization.

probe_reagents.name: CF2HSO2Na (CV redox probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Shows an irreversible oxidation wave; enamide and styrene show no oxidation/reduction up to ~1.5 V, and mixtures with CF2HSO2Na give unchanged profiles, ruling out substrate oxidation and supporting sulfinate as the species oxidized at the anode.

10.1021_acs.orglett.3c00310.notes: Two product-forming branches share a common cation II: route A (E1 elimination) for enamides gives a single (E)-vinyl-CF2H diastereomer; route B (nucleophilic addition of H2O) for styrenes gives β-hydroxy-CF2H products. No radical trapping (e.g., TEMPO/BHT) or DFT was reported; the mechanism is supported mainly by CV and control experiments with non-stabilizing alkenes.

10.1021_acs.orglett.5c01237_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component coupling of 1,3-dicarbonyls (e.g., dibenzoylmethane) with styrenes and water to give β-functionalized γ-acyloxy ketones via radical-polar crossover and an oxocarbenium intermediate
10.1021_acs.orglett.5c01237_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the deprotonated 1,3-dicarbonyl enolate to a C-centered radical that adds to styrene; subsequent anodic oxidation of the benzylic radical to a benzylic cation. Cathode reduces H2O to OH- + H2, providing base and the oxygen nucleophile.
10.1021_acs.orglett.5c01237_sup_1.electron_flow: Cathode reduces H2O to OH- + H2; OH- deprotonates the 1,3-dicarbonyl, the resulting enolate is oxidized at the anode to a C-radical that adds to styrene; the benzylic radical is oxidized again at the anode to a benzylic cation, which is trapped intramolecularly by a carbonyl oxygen to give an oxocarbenium that is opened by water/OH- to deliver the γ-acyloxy ketone.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic radical adduct after styrene addition (III)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO-adduct of the 1,3-dicarbonyl radical isolated in 55%; product 3aa formed only in trace amounts, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: BHT-adduct 1a'' detected by HRMS; product 3aa not observed, supporting radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: Product formed with 99% deuterium incorporation, confirming moisture (H2O) is the proton/O-H source

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: 18O incorporation into the benzoate oxygen of product detected by HRMS (m/z 369.1369, calcd 369.1353 for C23H20-16O2-18O1Na), confirming water as the oxygen source

probe_reagents.name: ethyl benzoylacetate 1a1 + α-methylstyrene 2p (H2O)
probe_reagents.observation: γ-butyrolactone d1 (C18H17O3, [M+H]+ 281.1172) detected by NMR/HRMS, consistent with OH- opening of the proposed oxocarbenium

probe_reagents.name: MeOH (in place of H2O) with 1a + 2p
probe_reagents.observation: Methyl ether adduct d2 (C25H25O3, [M+H]+ 372.1784) detected by HRMS, confirming nucleophilic trapping of the oxocarbenium intermediate

probe_reagents.name: divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Case 1 gave 3aa in 60%; Case 2 gave no product, indicating both anodic and cathodic processes are required

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: CV of 1a and 2a in CH3CN:H2O with Bu4PF6 used to establish oxidation potentials and order of electron transfer

10.1021_acs.orglett.5c01237_sup_1.notes: An alternative pathway shown in the proposed cycle leads to allylic/olefinic product 5aa via HAT/elimination from intermediate III' (formed when the dicarbonyl radical adds to a terminal alkene 4a instead of styrene), suggesting competing oxocarbenium ring-closure (V') vs β-H elimination depending on substrate. The favored pathway for styrenes goes through benzylic cation IV → cyclic oxocarbenium V → lactol VI → ring-opened benzoate VII → product 3aa.

10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00361.rxn_class_summary: Catalyst-free direct electrochemical tri- and difluoroalkylation/cyclization of N-aryl acrylamides with sodium sulfinates (CF3SO2Na, CF2HSO2Na) to access fluoroalkylated oxindoles and 3,4-dihydroquinolin-2(1H)-ones.
10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00361.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the sulfinate anion (CF3SO2- or CF2HSO2-) generates a sulfonyl radical that fragments (loss of SO2) to the fluoroalkyl radical; concurrent cathodic reduction of protons releases H2.
10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00361.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the sulfinate to a sulfonyl radical that loses SO2 to give CF3• (or CF2H•); the fluoroalkyl radical adds to the acrylamide alkene, the resulting C-radical cyclizes intramolecularly onto the N-aryl ring to a cyclohexadienyl radical, which is further oxidized at the anode and deprotonated to rearomatize, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: CF3SO2• (sulfonyl radical from anodic oxidation of triflinate)
CF3• fluoroalkyl radical (after desulfurative loss of SO2)
Carbon-centered radical 9 from CF3• addition to acrylamide alkene
Cyclohexadienyl (aryl) radical 10 after intramolecular ipso-cyclization onto arene
Oxindole product 3 after anodic oxidation/rearomatization of 10 and deprotonation

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction of 1a fully suppressed with only trace product; BHT–CF3 adduct 8 isolated and fully characterized, supporting CF3• and the radical intermediate 9.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, 2b, and BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Triflinate 2a shows lower oxidation potential than difluoromethanesulfinate 2b; acrylamide 1a is harder to oxidize than the sulfinates, consistent with anodic oxidation of sulfinate as the initiating SET step.

10.1021_acs.orglett.9b00361.notes: Authors explicitly favor a SET-based radical manifold initiated by anodic oxidation of the sulfinate over any direct alkene oxidation, supported by CV showing sulfinates are oxidized at lower potential than the acrylamide substrate. The same mechanism is invoked for both CF3 and CF2H series and extends to N-arylcinnamamides giving dihydroquinolinones (no rearomatization step needed in that case).

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04426_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical carboxylative cyclization of 2-isocyano-styrenes with CO2 using a sacrificial Mg anode to give 2-oxindole-3-acetic acids.
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04426_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction generates a CO2 radical anion (and/or substrate-derived radical) while the Mg anode is sacrificially oxidized to Mg(II); the CO2 radical anion adds to the vinyl isocyanide, triggering radical cyclization onto the isocyano carbon.
10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04426_sup_1.electron_flow: At the Fe cathode, CO2 is reduced to CO2 radical anion which adds to the terminal alkene of the 2-isocyanostyrene; the resulting benzylic radical cyclizes onto the isocyanide to form an imidoyl radical, which is further reduced and protonated/formylated, while the Mg anode is consumed to balance charge.

key_intermediates.name: alpha-carboxyl benzyl radical from CO2 addition to vinyl group
imidoyl radical from cyclization onto isocyanide carbon
imidoyl anion / Mg-amide after further reduction

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed; starting material 1a recovered in 86% yield, indicating a radical intermediate is intercepted.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction partially proceeds, giving the desired acid 2a (22%) and the deformyl/desaturated byproduct 3a (3,5-dimethyl-2-oxo-3-phenylindoline-1-carbaldehyde, 21%), consistent with a radical pathway not fully quenched by BHT.

10.1021_acs.orglett.4c04426_sup_1.notes: Only the radical trapping experiments and a cyclic voltammetry section are visible on this page; CV data details and any DFT support are not shown. The formation of byproduct 3a under BHT conditions hints at a competing pathway lacking the CO2-derived carboxymethyl group, suggesting CO2 incorporation occurs after initial radical generation.

10.1021_acs.orglett.8b03191.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intermolecular sulfonylation/heteroarylation of alkenes (benzothiazole-substituted tertiary homoallylic alcohols) with sulfinic acids via distal heteroaryl ipso-migration, forming new C–C and C–S bonds.
10.1021_acs.orglett.8b03191.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sulfinate anion (from deprotonation of sulfinic acid) to a sulfonyl radical; cathode reduces protons to H2; a second anodic SET oxidizes a ketyl radical intermediate to a carbocation.
10.1021_acs.orglett.8b03191.electron_flow: Sulfinic acid is deprotonated and the sulfinate is anodically oxidized (SET) to a sulfonyl radical, which adds across the alkene to give a C-radical; intramolecular ipso-addition onto the benzothiazole forms a spiro N-radical that undergoes C–C bond cleavage/ring-opening to a ketyl radical, which is anodically oxidized to a cation, then deprotonated to give the migrated ketone product. Protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: S-centered sulfonyl radical (III), resonance form of II
carbon radical from sulfonyl addition to alkene (IV)
spiro N-radical (V) from intramolecular ipso-cyclization onto benzothiazole
carbocation intermediate (VII) after anodic SET oxidation of VI

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (3a 0%), starting material 1a almost fully recovered — supports radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Sodium p-tolylsulfinate (4)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3a in 65% yield, similar to sulfinic acid, supporting deprotonation/sulfinate as the active species.

probe_reagents.name: p-toluenesulfinic acid alone (no tertiary alcohol)
probe_reagents.observation: Homocoupling to 4,4'-dimethyldiphenyl disulfone (5) in 70% yield, indicating sulfonyl radical generation under electrolysis.

probe_reagents.name: No-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product 3a formed, confirming the transformation is electrochemically driven.

probe_reagents.name: Chain-length variation (1u–1x, n=0,1,3,4)
probe_reagents.observation: Only n=2 (1,4-mig.) and n=3 (1,5-mig.) gave product; 3-, 4-, and 7-membered cyclic TS were ineffective, supporting 5-/6-membered cyclic intramolecular migration via spirocyclic N-radical.

10.1021_acs.orglett.8b03191.notes: X-ray structure of 3a (CCDC 1867118) confirms regiochemistry. Sulfonyl radicals II (O-centered) and III (S-centered) are described as resonance structures; S-centered form adds to alkene. Mechanism is intramolecular heteroaryl ipso-migration (Truce–Smiles-type) rather than intermolecular; supported by chain-length scan showing strict preference for 5-/6-membered spirocyclization.

10.1021_acs.orglett.1c03461_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical thiol-ene type hydrothiolation/coupling of styrenes with arylthiols mediated by PhB(OH)2 proceeding through thiyl radical addition to the alkene.
10.1021_acs.orglett.1c03461_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of arylthiolate/thiol to generate an aryl thiyl radical (ArS•) that adds to the styrene; PhB(OH)2 acts as a hydroxyl/proton shuttle generating a HO-B(Ph)(NMe2)-type byproduct.
10.1021_acs.orglett.1c03461_sup_1.electron_flow: At the anode, ArSH (or ArS-) is oxidized to a thiyl radical ArS•, which adds to the styrene to form a benzylic C-radical; in the productive cycle this radical is reduced/protonated to give the hydrothiolation product, while in the TEMPO probe experiment it is trapped as adduct 3n'.
