probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 5a trace; CF3-DPE adduct detected by HRMS ([M]+ found 248.0807, calc 248.0812), confirming CF3 radical

probe_reagents.name: CF3SO3Na (sodium triflinate/triflate) as alternative CF3 source
probe_reagents.observation: No 5a obtained, indicating CF3 radical does not arise from triflate fragment

probe_reagents.name: Benzimidazolium BF4 salt 1b (instead of OTf counterion)
probe_reagents.observation: 5a obtained in 63% yield, confirming that the CF3 radical originates from the N-triflyl benzimidazolium cation portion of reagent 1

10.1039_D1CC03389A_sup_1.notes: Counterion-swap experiment (OTf- vs BF4-) combined with CF3SO3Na control elegantly localizes the CF3 source to the N-Tf group on the benzimidazolium cation rather than the triflate counterion. Solvent screen identifies MeCN as optimal (44%); concentration has little effect (42-44% over 0.025-0.1 M). Subsequent anodizing verification experiment (section 3.3) likely supports anodic initiation but details not shown on these pages.

10.1039_D5CC04608D.rxn_class_summary: Catalyst-free electrochemical bromochlorination of alkenes using bromide as Br source and hexachloroethane (C2Cl6) as Cl source via paired electrolysis
10.1039_D5CC04608D.electrochemical_role: Anodic two-step oxidation of Br- to Br+ cation; simultaneous cathodic reduction of C2Cl6 to Cl- anion and C2Cl4
10.1039_D5CC04608D.electron_flow: At the anode, Br- undergoes two-step oxidation to Br+, which adds to the alkene to form a bromonium ion M; chloride, generated at the cathode by reduction of C2Cl6 (yielding Cl- and C2Cl4), opens M to give the bromochloride product 2 (Path I). In a parallel Path II, Br- opens M to dibromide 5, which is then converted to 2 by Cl-.

key_intermediates.name: chloride anion (from cathodic reduction of C2Cl6)
dibromide product 5 (from Br- attack on bromonium)

probe_reagents.name: MeOH
probe_reagents.observation: Bromoalkoxylation product 6a formed in 21% yield, supporting bromonium intermediate

probe_reagents.name: tBuOH
probe_reagents.observation: Bulky alcohol gave only trace bromoalkoxylation (6b) and bromochloride 2a in 56%, consistent with bromonium intermediate

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Significant yield decrease of 2a, but no BHT adducts detected by HRMS

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Significant yield decrease of 2a, no TEMPO adducts observed

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)benzene 1p
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 7p obtained in 50% yield, suggesting involvement of a Br radical or cation

probe_reagents.name: bromochloride 2a (as substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Converts to dichloride 4a (up to 18% with nBu4NCl); 8% without electricity, confirming interconversion

probe_reagents.name: dibromide 5a (as substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Converts to bromochloride 2a (up to 53% with nBu4NCl) and small amounts of 4a, confirming 5a is on-path intermediate

10.1039_D5CC04608D.notes: CV showed Br- oxidation (E1/2 = 0.98 V and 1.4 V) easier than styrene (2.06 V), indicating Br- is oxidized first rather than direct alkene oxidation. CV also confirmed C2Cl6 reduction at -1.46 V as the Cl source. Kinetics show dibromide 5a forms first, then converts to bromochloride 2a as Cl- accumulates; dichloride 4a forms only later from over-chlorination. Radical trap experiments suppress reaction but give no adducts, while cyclopropyl clock opens — the authors favor a cationic bromonium pathway but acknowledge possible Br radical/cation character.

10.1039_D3CC05770D_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical aerobic oxidative C=C bond cleavage/oxyfunctionalization of 1,1,2-triaryl alkenes to α,α-diaryl ketones using O2 as the oxygen source
10.1039_D3CC05770D_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the triaryl alkene to a vinyl/alkene radical cation (Ep,a ~0.918 V observed by CV under O2), which is then trapped by molecular oxygen
10.1039_D3CC05770D_sup_1.electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the triaryl alkene to its radical cation; molecular O2 (incorporated as shown by 18O2 labeling) adds to the radical cation to give a peroxy/dioxetane species that fragments/rearranges to the α,α-diaryl ketone. Water is not the oxygen source (no reaction under Ar with H2 18O).

key_intermediates.name: peroxy/dioxetane intermediate from O2 addition to radical cation

probe_reagents.name: 18O2 (isotopically labeled dioxygen)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 2a shows 18O incorporation by HRMS (found 275.1322 for C20H17 18O), confirming O2 as the oxygen source

probe_reagents.name: H2 18O (isotopically labeled water)
probe_reagents.observation: Under Ar with various equivalents of H2 18O (0.5–2.0 equiv, or 4:1 CH3CN/H2O), no reaction (N.R.) — water is not the O source

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry under N2 vs O2
probe_reagents.observation: An additional oxidation feature at ~0.918 V appears under O2 atmosphere, consistent with an O2-involved oxidation event

10.1039_D3CC05770D_sup_1.notes: The 18O2 vs H2 18O double-labeling cleanly distinguishes O2 (operative) from H2O (inoperative) as the oxygen source. CV under O2 suggests interaction between O2 and the oxidized substrate/electrode. No radical traps (TEMPO/BHT) or DFT reported on these pages, so the exact peroxy intermediate (open peroxy vs 1,2-dioxetane) is not directly distinguished.

10.1039_D2CC06246A.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical anti-Markovnikov azidoacetamidation of styrenes with TMSN3 and MeCN to give vicinal azidoacetamides via a β-azido Ritter intermediate
10.1039_D2CC06246A.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the styrene to a radical cation, followed by a second anodic oxidation of the β-azido benzylic radical to a benzyl cation
10.1039_D2CC06246A.electron_flow: The anode oxidizes styrene to a radical cation, which is trapped by TMSN3 to give a β-azido benzylic radical; a second anodic oxidation generates a benzyl cation that is intercepted by MeCN to form a Ritter nitrilium, and hydrolysis delivers the vicinal azidoacetamide. Protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: β-azido benzyl alcohol from water trapping (E)

probe_reagents.name: vinylcyclopropane radical clock (substrate 30)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened allyl-azide/acetamide product 31 obtained in 17% (4.4:1), consistent with a carbon radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: chemical oxidants (NFSI, TBHP, CAN, m-CPBA, DDQ, PhI(OAc)2, AgNO3)
probe_reagents.observation: None reproduced the anodic reaction (product n.d. except trace); only the anode gave 56% of 10, supporting a specifically electrochemical oxidation pathway

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of styrene, TMSN3, and product 3
probe_reagents.observation: Styrene Ep/2 = 1.92 V is oxidized preferentially over TMSN3 (no oxidation peak); product 3 (Ep/2 = 0.95 V) is more easily oxidized than starting material, explaining overoxidation/lower yields

10.1039_D2CC06246A.notes: A competing pathway diverts the benzyl cation (C) to a β-azido alcohol (E) via water trapping instead of MeCN; this is the main off-pathway side product. The CV result (alkene oxidized, not azide) contradicts the authors' earlier HFIP-mediated azidotetrazolation work where TMSN3 was oxidized first, indicating a switch from azide-radical addition to alkene-radical-cation initiation under these conditions.

10.1039_d4cc05092d.rxn_class_summary: Asymmetric electrocatalytic cyclopropanation of alpha,beta-unsaturated aldehydes with malonates via iminium ion organocatalysis and anodically generated electrophilic iodine
10.1039_d4cc05092d.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (Et4NI) to electrophilic iodine species (I+/I2) which iodinates the enamine intermediate; cathode reduces protons (from HFIP) to H2 as counter half-reaction
10.1039_d4cc05092d.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes iodide (2 e-) to an electrophilic I+ species; the enamine (formed by Michael addition of malonate onto the chiral iminium) is iodinated to give an alpha-iodo iminium that undergoes intramolecular malonate alkylation (ring closure) releasing I-; hydrolysis of the resulting iminium gives the cyclopropane and regenerates the aminocatalyst. Cathode reduces protons (from HFIP) to H2.

key_intermediates.name: iminium ion from cinnamaldehyde and diarylprolinol-TMS catalyst I (i)
enamine after Michael addition of malonate to iminium (ii)
Michael adduct aldehyde 4a (hydrolysis product of enamine ii)
electrophilic iodine species (I+ / I2) generated at anode

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only minor suppression of 3a (36% yield); TEMPO-adduct 6 observed (25% NMR yield); ambiguous since TEMPO can be oxidized to N-oxoammonium and react in polar fashion

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclopropane 3a still formed (38% NMR), only 4% of 4a; no quenching of putative enamine radical cation -> argues for polar pathway

probe_reagents.name: pre-synthesized Michael adduct 4a
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard electrolysis 4a gave 43% 3a + 17% 5a; without aminocatalyst 4a gave 23% 3a (94% ee) confirming ring closure can proceed via enamine/iodination pathway

probe_reagents.name: molecular I2 / NIS (no electricity)
probe_reagents.observation: No conversion and catalyst I decomposed, showing that controlled electrocatalytic generation of I+ is essential to protect the aminocatalyst from Grob fragmentation

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of components
probe_reagents.observation: Iodide oxidizes at +0.19 and +0.38 V; enamine (II from 4a + cat I) oxidizes at +0.27 V; iodide is the lowest-potential and preferred species oxidized at the anode

10.1039_d4cc05092d.notes: Authors favor a polar (iminium/enamine + electrophilic iodination) mechanism over a SET/radical cation pathway; an alternative enamine radical-cation route was considered (since enamine Eox = +0.27 V is below iodide's second oxidation) but BHT trapping and ring closure from isolated 4a without catalyst support the polar pathway. A radical pathway via TEMPO oxidation to N-oxoammonium adding to the enamine is mentioned in ESI Scheme S2. Side product 5a arises from base-induced retro-Michael of iminium iv.

10.1039_D4CC05092D.rxn_class_summary: Asymmetric electrochemical cyclopropanation of α,β-unsaturated aldehydes with malonates via iminium-ion organocatalysis and anodically generated electrophilic iodine species (Michael-initiated ring closure)
10.1039_D4CC05092D.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (Et4NI) to electrophilic iodine species (I+/I2), which iodinates the enamine intermediate; cathode reduces protons (from HFIP) to H2 as the counter half-reaction
10.1039_D4CC05092D.electron_flow: Organocatalyst condenses with enal to form an iminium ion that accepts malonate (Michael addition) giving an enamine; the anode oxidizes iodide to electrophilic iodine, which α-iodinates the enamine to an α-iodo iminium; intramolecular enolate displacement of iodide closes the cyclopropane and hydrolysis releases the product and regenerates catalyst, while H2 evolves at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: iminium ion from condensation of diarylprolinol-OTMS catalyst I with cinnamaldehyde (i)
enamine after Michael addition of dimethyl malonate to iminium (ii)
Michael adduct aldehyde 4a (hydrolysis product of enamine ii)
α-iodo iminium ion (iii) from iodination of enamine by electrophilic I+
cyclopropane-bound iminium ion (iv) after intramolecular SN2 ring closure
electrophilic iodine species (I+ / I2) generated at anode

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only minor suppression of 3a (36% NMR yield); TEMPO-adduct 6 isolated (25%); result deemed ambiguous because TEMPO can be oxidized to N-oxoammonium and react with enamine in polar fashion

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclopropane 3a still formed in 38% yield with only 4% of 4a, indicating no quenching of putative enamine radical cation — supports a polar pathway

probe_reagents.name: pre-synthesized intermediate 4a (stoichiometric)
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard electrochemical conditions 4a gave 43% 3a (and 17% 5a); without catalyst still gave 23% 3a — confirms 4a/enamine is on-cycle and ring closure is electrochemically driven

probe_reagents.name: I2 / NIS (stoichiometric, no current)
probe_reagents.observation: No conversion of starting materials and catalyst I decomposed — controlled electrocatalytic generation of I+ is essential to protect catalyst from Grob-type fragmentation

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of components
probe_reagents.observation: Iodide oxidizes at +0.19/+0.38 V; enamine derived from 4a+I oxidizes at +0.27 V (between I- peaks); other components have higher potentials — iodide is selectively oxidized first

probe_reagents.name: no-electricity control
probe_reagents.observation: Only Michael adduct 4a formed (49%), no cyclopropane — electricity required for ring closure

probe_reagents.name: no-organocatalyst control
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction — iminium activation required

10.1039_D4CC05092D.notes: Authors favor a fully polar mechanism (iminium/enamine + electrophilic I+ ionic alkylation), supported by BHT insensitivity, CV showing iodide as the lowest-potential oxidizable species, and ring closure from isolated 4a. An alternative SET pathway via enamine radical cation (oxidation at +0.27 V) is considered possible but disfavored. Side pathways: base-induced retro-Michael of iminium iv gives byproduct 5a; free catalyst can decompose via Grob-type fragmentation when excess electrophilic iodine is present, hence the need for catalytic, electrochemically controlled I+ generation. The reaction comprises two coupled cycles (organocatalytic, temperature-controlled; electrocatalytic, current-controlled) whose rates must be balanced.

10.1039_D2CC00761D_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [3+2] cycloaddition of N-sulfonyl cyclopropylamines with alkenes/alkynes (cinnamates) to give aminocyclopentane/cyclopentene products via ring-opening of an aminyl radical, mediated by ferrocene.
10.1039_D2CC00761D_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+ which oxidizes the deprotonated sulfonamide anion (A) to an N-centered aminyl radical (B); the distal alkyl radical adduct is reduced at the cathode to a carbanion that cyclizes/protonates.
10.1039_D2CC00761D_sup_1.electron_flow: K2CO3 deprotonates the N-H sulfonamide; ferrocene is oxidized at the anode to Cp2Fe+ which single-electron oxidizes the sulfonamide anion to an N-centered radical that undergoes cyclopropane ring-opening to a distal C-radical; this radical adds to the cinnamate, the resulting α-ester radical is reduced at the cathode to a carbanion which undergoes 5-exo cyclization onto the N-sulfonyl imine and protonation to give the trans-aminocyclopentane.

key_intermediates.name: Sulfonamide anion (A) from K2CO3 deprotonation of N-sulfonylcyclopropylamine
Ring-opened distal carbon radical / N-sulfonyl iminyl-tethered alkyl radical (C)
Alkene/alkyne radical adduct (D) after addition to cinnamate (Michael acceptor)
5-exo cyclized cyclopentene carbanion (F/G) bearing N-Ts iminyl/amide
Cyclic N-sulfonyl imine intermediate III (detected by HRMS)
Hydrolysis product IV: tosylsulfonamide + aldehyde V (confirmed by 1H NMR)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Complete shutdown of reaction, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Complete shutdown of reaction, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: N-acetyl cyclopropylamine (vs N-tosyl)
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction; tosyl (electron-withdrawing/stabilizing) group required, acetyl detrimental

probe_reagents.name: Divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: ~70% starting material recovered; indicates no chain propagation

probe_reagents.name: (3-bromoprop-1-en-2-yl)benzene (2o) intermediate trap
probe_reagents.observation: HRMS detects cyclic N-sulfonyl imine III; hydrolysis gives tosylsulfonamide IV (NMR) + aldehyde V, consistent with an anionic 5-exo cyclization pathway

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry (Cp2Fe, 1a, 2a, K2CO3 combinations)
probe_reagents.observation: Establishes Cp2Fe as the redox mediator oxidizing the sulfonamide anion rather than direct substrate oxidation

probe_reagents.name: Time-based electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: Monitoring substrate/product evolution over time

10.1039_D2CC00761D_sup_1.notes: Authors propose two mechanisms: (favored, H) anionic 5-exo cyclization pathway via cathodic reduction of the radical adduct to a carbanion that cyclizes onto the N-sulfonyl imine (supported by HRMS detection of imine III and isolation of IV/V); (alternate, I) all-radical pathway where the alkene radical adduct cyclizes to a C-centered radical (J) which is cathodically reduced to a carbanion (K) and protonated. NOESY confirms trans diastereoselectivity, rationalized by steric repulsion between N-sulfonyl imine and ester in the chair-like transition state (Aggarwal model). Divided cell shows no radical chain propagation, consistent with stoichiometric mediator turnover.

10.1039_d5cc00744e.rxn_class_summary: Electroreductive dual-radical tri-/difluoromethylarylation of alkenes coupling cyanoarenes with (di/tri)fluoromethylsulfones using a sacrificial Zn anode and a naphthalene-type mediator (Med-1)
10.1039_d5cc00744e.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction generates two radicals: cyanoarene (2) is reduced to its radical anion (I), which then mediates SET to the fluoroalkylsulfone (3aa) to release CF3 radical; in parallel, Med-1 is cathodically reduced to its radical anion which provides an alternative SET pathway to 3aa. Zn sacrificial anode supplies electrons by oxidation.
10.1039_d5cc00744e.electron_flow: At the cathode, cyanoarene 2 (and/or Med-1) is reduced by one electron to a persistent radical anion, which performs SET to the fluoroalkylsulfone 3aa to fragment it into ArSO2- and CF3•; CF3• adds to alkene 1 forming a benzyl radical (III) that undergoes radical/radical-anion cross-coupling with the cyanoarene radical anion (I), expelling CN- to give the arylated product. Electrons originate from oxidation of the sacrificial Zn anode.

key_intermediates.name: benzyl radical adduct from CF3• + alkene (III)
cyanide anion released upon radical/radical-anion coupling

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Products 4aa and 7aa were not obtained in its presence, consistent with radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: HRMS detection of CF2H adduct
probe_reagents.observation: Difluoromethyl radical captured and detected by HRMS; aryl radical and CF3 radical not observed directly

probe_reagents.name: radical clock substrate
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened products 10 and 11 formed in 56% and 43% yield, supporting a dual-radical mechanism

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1aa/2aa/3aa/Med-1
probe_reagents.observation: 1aa not reduced in window; 2aa reversible Ered = -1.86 V; 3aa irreversible Ered = -2.05 V; Med-1 reversible Ered = -2.86 V; addition of 3aa enhances catalytic current of 2aa and suppresses oxidation, indicating SET from 2aa•- to 3aa; 3aa/Med-1 mixture shows decreased oxidation and increased catalytic current of Med-1, confirming SET; 2aa charge consumption 6.9 F/mol indicates multi-step SET chain

10.1039_d5cc00744e.notes: Two cathodic SET pathways operate in parallel: (a) direct reduction of cyanoarene 2 to its radical anion, which reduces sulfone 3aa to CF3•; (b) Med-1-mediated pathway in which the naphthalene mediator radical anion reduces 3aa. Multi-step SET chain mechanism is invoked given charge consumption far exceeds 1 F/mol. Final C–C bond formation is proposed as radical/radical-anion cross-coupling with extrusion of CN-, rather than radical addition followed by rearomatization.

10.1039_d2cc00761d.rxn_class_summary: External-oxidant-free electrochemical [3+2] cycloaddition of N-sulfonylcyclopropanes with olefins via N-centered radical intermediates to access di- and tri-substituted cyclopentane analogs.
10.1039_d2cc00761d.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of ferrocene to ferrocenium (Cp2Fe+) which acts as a single-electron mediator to oxidize the sulfonamide anion (generated by K2CO3 deprotonation of N-H sulfonamide) to an N-centered radical; cathode reduces a downstream distonic/imine radical intermediate to a carbanion that undergoes 5-exo cyclization.
10.1039_d2cc00761d.electron_flow: At the anode Cp2Fe is oxidized to Cp2Fe+, which performs outer-sphere SET on the K2CO3-derived sulfonamide anion to generate an N-centered radical; this NCR triggers β-scission of the cyclopropane to a primary C-radical that adds to the alkene, and the resulting radical is reduced at the cathode to a carbanion that undergoes 5-exo cyclization onto the N-sulfonyl imine followed by protonation to deliver the cyclopentane.

key_intermediates.name: Ring-opened distonic carbon radical / N-sulfonyl imine with pendant C-radical (C)
Protonation by-product imine F (off-pathway, detected by HRMS [M+H]+ 298.1113)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Complete shutdown of the reaction at 2.0 equiv, supporting a radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: BHT (butylated hydroxytoluene)
probe_reagents.observation: Complete shutdown of the reaction at 2.0 equiv, consistent with radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: N-cyclopropylacetamide (4, R=COCH3 instead of tosyl)
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction, showing that the N-sulfonyl group is essential (acetyl cannot generate the requisite aza-anion / NCR).

probe_reagents.name: Divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product after 1 h with starting materials largely recovered; rules out a chain-propagation mechanism and confirms continuous electron input is required.

probe_reagents.name: (3-Bromoprop-1-en-2-yl)benzene (allyl bromide radical clock/trap, 2o)
probe_reagents.observation: HRMS detection of trapped intermediate F ([M+H]+ 328.1371); hydrolysis liberated p-toluenesulfonamide confirmed by 1H NMR, supporting the proposed anionic/imine intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a with/without Cp2Fe and K2CO3
probe_reagents.observation: Ep(1a) = 2.08 V vs Ag/AgCl shifts slightly to 2.24 V with ferrocene, and a marked enhancement in catalytic current is observed with Cp2Fe and K2CO3, confirming electrocatalytic mediation by ferrocene.

10.1039_d2cc00761d.notes: An alternative closure is proposed in which radical D adds directly to the N-sulfonyl imine and the resulting N-centered radical is cathodically reduced and protonated to give 3; the carbanion E can also be quenched by a proton to give acyclic imine F (HRMS-detected), accounting for moderate yields. SCXRD of 3ag (CCDC 2115609) confirms product connectivity/stereochemistry.

10.1039_d5cc03082j_si2.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Ni-catalyzed α-allylation/alkylation of β-ketoesters with alkenes (e.g., 1,1-diphenylethylene or allyl-TMS) via radical-polar crossover at the anode.
10.1039_d5cc03082j_si2.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of a Ni(II)-enolate complex generates a carbon-centered radical/cation that adds to the alkene; cathode reduces protons to H2 (and TMS-related byproducts), closing the paired electrolysis.
10.1039_d5cc03082j_si2.electron_flow: Ni(II) coordinates the β-ketoester enolate (A'); single-electron anodic oxidation gives an α-carbon radical (B') that adds to the alkene to form alkyl–Ni intermediate C'; a second anodic SET produces β-silyl cation D', which loses TMS+ (as TMSOH/TMS2O) to give product 31, while the cathode evolves H2.

key_intermediates.name: C-C bond formed Ni(II) alkyl intermediate C' (after alkene addition with allyl-TMS 2k)
β-silyl carbocation D' (after second SET oxidation)

probe_reagents.name: no current (control)
probe_reagents.observation: No product 3 formed, confirming the reaction is electrochemically driven.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3 not detected; TEMPO-adduct 50 isolated in 82% yield (HRMS [M+Na]+ 368.1831), supporting a C-centered radical at the α-position of 1a.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3 not detected; BHT-adduct 51 isolated in 75% yield (HRMS [M+Na]+ 431.2183), corroborating the radical intermediate.

10.1039_d5cc03082j_si2.notes: Mechanism proposed (Scheme S3) is for the allyl-TMS variant giving product 31; the α-radical is generated via anodic oxidation of a Ni(II)-enolate rather than direct substrate oxidation. Cathodic chemistry includes H2 evolution and degradation of TMS group (TMSOH, TMS2O). No DFT or CV reported in the provided pages.

10.1039_D4CC03432E_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical chalcogenation/cyclization of N-tosyl alkenyl amines with diaryl dichalcogenides (PhXXPh, X = S, Se, Te) to give 2-(chalcogenylmethyl)pyrrolidines
10.1039_D4CC03432E_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diaryl dichalcogenide PhXXPh to generate PhX• radical (and/or PhX+); the chalcogenyl radical adds to the terminal alkene, and the resulting carbon radical undergoes intramolecular cyclization with the tethered N-tosyl amine
10.1039_D4CC03432E_sup_1.electron_flow: At the anode (GF) the diaryl dichalcogenide is oxidized to give PhX•, which adds to the terminal alkene of the N-tosyl pent-4-enylamine; the resulting secondary C-radical cyclizes onto the N-tosylamide nitrogen (with loss of H+, reduced at the GP cathode to H2) to deliver the 2-substituted pyrrolidine.

key_intermediates.name: phenylchalcogenyl radical PhX• (X = S, Se, Te)
carbon-centered radical from anti-Markovnikov addition of PhX• to terminal alkene of N-tosyl pent-4-enylamine

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: With 2 equiv TEMPO, formation of S-product 4 was suppressed to trace, while Se (32, 70%) and Te (47, 47%) products still formed, indicating partial radical involvement (chalcogen-dependent)

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: DPE intercepted PhX• radicals giving (2,2-diphenylvinyl)(phenyl)chalcogenides I (S, 30%), II (Se, 10%), III (Te, trace), while cyclized pyrrolidines were still obtained (4, 15%; 32, 65%; 47, 49%), supporting a PhX• radical intermediate

10.1039_D4CC03432E_sup_1.notes: Radical trapping behavior is chalcogen-dependent: the S pathway is most sensitive to TEMPO/DPE (strong radical character), whereas Se and Te reactions are only partially inhibited, possibly implying a competing two-electron/ionic (PhX+) pathway for the heavier chalcogens. Cyclic voltammetry (HFIP/TBABF4, GC working electrode) was used to probe redox potentials of PhXXPh; CV of 1,2-diphenylditellane was measured in TFE due to poor HFIP solubility.

10.1039_D4CC02543A.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical difluoromethylation/Smiles rearrangement of N-arylsulfonyl acrylamides with CF2HSO2Na to afford β-difluoromethylamides via radical addition, aryl migration, and desulfonylation
10.1039_D4CC02543A.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF2HSO2Na (sodium difluoromethanesulfinate) to generate CF2H radical with loss of SO2 and electron; cathode reduces protons to H2
10.1039_D4CC02543A.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF2HSO2Na (Eox ~1.40 V) to release SO2 and CF2H radical, which adds to the acrylamide alkene of 1a forming carbon radical A; intramolecular ipso-attack on the N-aryl ring gives spirocyclic radical B, which loses SO2 to form nitrogen radical C; C is reduced/abstracts H (proton from trace water) to give product 3a while the cathode evolves H2.
10.1039_D4CC02543A.rate_determining_step: Anodic oxidation of CF2HSO2Na to generate CF2H radical (irreversible oxidation peak at 1.40 V vs Ag/AgCl)

key_intermediates.name: CF2H radical generated from anodic oxidation of CF2HSO2Na
Intermediate A: tertiary C-radical from CF2H addition to acrylamide alkene
Intermediate B: spirocyclic cyclohexadienyl radical from ipso-attack on N-aryl ring (Smiles cyclization)
Intermediate C: N-centered (aminyl) radical after extrusion of SO2

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3g formed; TEMPO-CF2H adduct detected by HRMS (M+H+ 321.3189), confirming CF2H radical

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3g; BHT-CF2H adduct detected by HRMS (M+H+ 271.1848)

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: In ultradry CH3CN with 10 equiv D2O, 43% D incorporation on N-H of product (39% yield), showing N-H hydrogen originates from water

probe_reagents.name: CD3CN/DCE (no D2O)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% D incorporation (34% yield), excluding solvent CH3CN as H source

probe_reagents.name: Divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a obtained in 65% yield in separated electrolytic cell, ruling out paired-electrolysis coupling requirement

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: CF2HSO2Na shows irreversible oxidation at 1.40 V; 1a oxidizes at 1.90 V (irreversible) — sulfinate is preferentially oxidized at the anode

10.1039_D4CC02543A.notes: Smiles-type 1,4-aryl migration from sulfonyl-N to carbon with extrusion of SO2 is invoked. Control with divided cell shows pairing of anodic/cathodic half-reactions is not required for productivity. The N-H proton was shown to derive from adventitious/added water rather than acetonitrile solvent.

10.1021_jo402623r.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular cyclization of unsaturated amides via anodically generated amidyl radicals that add to electron-rich olefins (vinyl sulfides, enol ethers, ketene dithioacetals) to form five- and six-membered lactams with a tetrasubstituted carbon
10.1021_jo402623r.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation: the deprotonated amide (hydroxamate/sulfonamide anion) is oxidized at the RVC anode to generate a nitrogen-centered amidyl radical; after cyclization a second anodic oxidation of the cyclized C-radical gives a cation trapped by MeOH
10.1021_jo402623r.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the amide/hydroxamate anion (after deprotonation by LiOMe) to an N-centered amidyl radical, which cyclizes onto the tethered electron-rich olefin to give a stabilized C-radical; this radical undergoes a second anodic SET to a cation that is trapped by MeOH, while Pt cathode reduces MeOH to H2.
10.1021_jo402623r.rate_determining_step: Oxidation of the cyclized radical (second SET) is critical; cyclization step itself must be exergonic enough to keep [cyclic radical] > [acyclic amidyl radical] so that productive oxidation outcompetes radical–radical dimerization

key_intermediates.name: amidyl (N-centered) radical from anodic oxidation of amide anion
cyclized C-centered radical (alpha to heteroatom-stabilized carbon)
cyclized carbocation after second SET oxidation
distal-olefin-localized radical cation (intramolecular ET form, intermediate 25)

probe_reagents.name: competition substrate 23 (amide with both ketene dithioacetal and pendant alcohol)
probe_reagents.observation: Designed to distinguish direct radical cyclization (via 26) from intramolecular SET to give olefin radical cation 25 trapped by tethered OH to give 27

probe_reagents.name: N-phenyl substrate 9d (six-membered cyclization)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave complex mixture, no lactam; DFT shows competing 1,5-HAT has lower barrier than 6-exo cyclization for N-phenyl amidyl radical

probe_reagents.name: Substrate 9c (seven-membered ring attempt)
probe_reagents.observation: No cyclization; only methyl ester 12c (74%) from amidyl radical dimerization to hydrazide 14 and decomposition, indicating 7-membered cyclization too slow to compete with dimerization

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 5a, 9a, 9b, 9c, ketene dithioacetal
probe_reagents.observation: E_p/2 = 0.40–0.52 V (Ag/AgCl) for hydroxamates vs 1.06 V for ketene dithioacetal and 0.90 V for sulfonamide; consistent with amide-anion oxidation as initiation

10.1021_jo402623r.notes: Two mechanistic possibilities are considered for the cyclization: (favored) direct N-radical addition to the olefin to give cyclized C-radical 26, vs. (alternative) intramolecular SET from olefin to amidyl radical giving olefin radical cation 25 that is then attacked by nitrogen — competition substrate 23 was designed to distinguish these. DFT (UB3LYP/6-31G(d,p)) IRC analysis shows the spin density remains localized on N up to the TS, supporting a radical (not radical-cation/polar) cyclization. Success of the reaction depends on (i) sufficiently electron-rich olefin to lower oxidation potential of the cyclized radical, and (ii) cyclization being exergonic so the cyclic radical accumulates over the acyclic amidyl radical, suppressing dimerization to hydrazide 14 (which decomposes with loss of N2 to methyl ester 12 + BnOH). N-phenyl amidyl radicals fail in 6-exo cyclization due to competing 1,5-HAT.

10.1021_acs.joc.2c02966.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical transition-metal-free sulfonylative cycloetherification/cycloesterification of unsaturated alcohols (and acids) with sulfonyl hydrazides to give saturated sulfonyl-substituted 5- to 8-membered O-heterocycles
10.1021_acs.joc.2c02966.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of sulfonyl hydrazide to its radical cation, which loses H+/N2/H2 to generate the sulfonyl radical; the resulting alkyl radical adduct is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation that is trapped intramolecularly by the tethered hydroxyl; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode
10.1021_acs.joc.2c02966.electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the sulfonyl hydrazide to a radical cation that loses H+, N2, and H2 to give a sulfonyl radical; this radical adds to the alkene of the unsaturated alcohol to give a benzylic C-radical, which is oxidized again at the anode to a carbocation that is captured by the pendant OH (with loss of H+) to form the cyclic ether; cathode reduces H+ to H2.

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered alkyl radical from sulfonyl radical addition to alkene (C)
alkyl carbocation after second anodic oxidation (D)

probe_reagents.name: N-phenyl tosylhydrazide (2d-1)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3ad in 62% yield, showing the terminal N-H is not essential

probe_reagents.name: N-morpholinyl tosylhydrazide (2d-2)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3ad in 54% yield, confirming N-H bonds in hydrazide are non-essential

probe_reagents.name: N-benzoyl-N-morpholinyl tosylhydrazide (2d-3)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3ad in 35% yield, further supporting non-essential N-H

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3ad not detected; TEMPO-alkyl adduct detected by HRMS, indicating an alkyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: 3ad only in trace; BHT-sulfonyl adduct 7e isolated in 27%, supporting sulfonyl radical

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene
probe_reagents.observation: 3ad in trace; Ts-CH2-C(Ph)=CHPh adduct detected by GC-MS, supporting sulfonyl radical generation

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak of 2a at 1.67 V and of 1a at 2.52 V (vs Ag/AgCl) shows the sulfonyl hydrazide is preferentially oxidized at the anode

10.1021_acs.joc.2c02966.notes: Alternative pathway where the C-radical C is intercepted by another oxidant rather than oxidized at the anode to a cation cannot be fully excluded, but CV and trapping experiments favor the radical/cation (radical-polar crossover) sequence depicted in Scheme 4. Method is catalyst- and oxidant-free, undivided cell, n-Bu4NClO4 electrolyte, carbon fiber anode / Pt cathode, 10 mA, MeCN, 70 °C.

10.1021_acs.joc.1c02069.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [3+2] cycloaddition between N-benzyl-N,N-bis(trimethylsilylmethyl)amine precursors and electron-poor alkenes to form 3- and 3,4-disubstituted pyrrolidines via in situ anodic generation of a nonstabilized azomethine ylide.
10.1021_acs.joc.1c02069.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the bis(silylmethyl)amine 1a to its aminium radical cation, followed by a second SET after desilylation to give an iminium cation; cathode reduces water to H2 and hydroxide, which traps the TMS+ cation as silanol.
10.1021_acs.joc.1c02069.electron_flow: At the anode, amine 1a undergoes SET to give aminium radical cation A; loss of TMS+ yields neutral α-amino radical that is further oxidized (second SET) to iminium cation B; a second desilylation generates the nonstabilized azomethine ylide C, which undergoes concerted [3+2] cycloaddition with the electron-poor alkene 2a to give pyrrolidine 3aa. At the cathode, water is reduced to H2 and HO–, which quenches TMS+ to silanol.
10.1021_acs.joc.1c02069.rate_determining_step: Initial anodic SET oxidation of the amine 1a (E_ox = 0.75 V vs Ag/AgCl), as 2a shows no oxidation peak.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, and 1a+2a
probe_reagents.observation: 1a oxidizes at 0.75 V vs Ag/AgCl; 2a shows no oxidation peak, supporting selective anodic oxidation of the amine precursor.

10.1021_acs.joc.1c02069.notes: Mechanism is proposed based on CV studies and literature precedent; no radical traps, KIE, or DFT reported. The TMS+ byproduct is quenched by cathodically generated hydroxide to give trimethylsilanol, consistent with a paired-electrolysis scenario. Gram-scale synthesis (38 h, 70%) supports robustness but is not mechanistic evidence.

10.1021_acs.joc.3c02186.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [4+1] cycloaddition of arylidene/alkylidene chromane-2,4-diones with NaI as mediator/reactant to form spiro[benzofuran-2,2'-furan]-3-ones
10.1021_acs.joc.3c02186.electrochemical_role: Cathode reduces the α,β-unsaturated chromane-2,4-dione 1a to a radical anion (1a'); iodide is oxidized at the anode to I2 which iodinates the substrate; cathode also reduces water generating hydroxide that hydrolyzes/opens the ring; the final iodo-α-elimination yields a carbene that undergoes [4+1] with another molecule of 1a.
10.1021_acs.joc.3c02186.electron_flow: Cathode injects an electron into 1a giving radical anion 1a', which combines with anodically generated I2 to form α-iodo radical 1a''; further anodic oxidation plus hydroxide addition gives 1a''', which ring-opens with water to 1b. Base-promoted cyclization, decarboxylation, and a second iodination give 1c', then loss of PhCHO with α-elimination of iodide generates carbene 1c'' that undergoes intermolecular [4+1] cycloaddition with 1a to give spirocycle 2a, while cathodic water reduction produces H2 and OH- (paired electrolysis).

key_intermediates.name: α-iodo benzofuran-3(2H)-one 1c' (after decarboxylation and iodination of enolate)
benzofuran-3(2H)-one-derived carbene 1c'' (after PhCHO release and α-elimination of I-)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed (Table 1, entry 30), supporting a radical intermediate

10.1021_acs.joc.3c02186.notes: X-ray structure (CCDC 2262815) of product 2j confirms the spirocyclic connectivity and diastereochemistry. GC-MS detection of free benzaldehyde supports the proposed extrusion step from 1c' en route to carbene 1c''. CV shows a reduction peak of 1a near -1.28 V vs Ag/AgCl distinct from solvent; 1a is not anodically oxidizable, consistent with cathodic initiation. Gram-scale electrolysis (67%) demonstrates scalability.

10.1021_acs.joc.3c02001.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical difluoromethylation-carboxylation and -deuteration of alkenes using difluoromethyltriphenylphosphonium bromide as a CF2H radical precursor with CO2 or D2O as electrophile
10.1021_acs.joc.3c02001.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of [Ph3PCF2H]+Br- generates a CF2H radical; the benzylic radical adduct is further cathodically reduced to a benzyl anion which traps CO2 or D2O; Al anode is sacrificially oxidized to Al3+
10.1021_acs.joc.3c02001.electron_flow: Cathode reduces [Ph3PCF2H]+ to give CF2H radical (extruding PPh3); the CF2H radical adds to the alkene to form a benzylic radical, which is reduced again at the cathode to a benzyl anion that attacks CO2 (then esterified with TMSCHN2) or D2O. Al anode is oxidized sacrificially.

key_intermediates.name: CF2H radical from cathodic reduction of phosphonium salt
Benzylic radical A from CF2H addition to alkene
Benzyl anion B from further cathodic reduction of A

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)benzene radical clock with D2O
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened HF2C-CH=CH-CH2-CH(Ph)-D product detected by HRMS, supporting CF2H radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)benzene radical clock with CO2
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product with CF2H and COOH groups detected by HRMS, supporting radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-Diphenylethylene with D2O
probe_reagents.observation: Product formed in 63% yield with 86% D incorporation, consistent with benzyl anion intermediate trapped by D2O

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of phosphonium salt and DPE
probe_reagents.observation: [Ph3PCF2H]+Br- is more easily reduced (-1.640 V) than 1,1-diphenylethylene (-2.562 V); mixture shows new wave suggesting radical anion of alkene possible at more negative potentials

10.1021_acs.joc.3c02001.notes: Authors propose a minor pathway where the alkene is reduced to its radical anion C, which couples with CF2H radical to give anion B (radical-radical coupling); the major pathway is sequential CF2H radical addition followed by second cathodic reduction to the benzyl anion. X-ray structure (CCDC 2281142) of carboxylation product A-1 confirms regiochemistry. Monosubstituted alkenes give low yields due to instability of intermediate relative to diphenyl-stabilized anions.

10.1021_acs.joc.4c00722.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical fluorosulfonylation of styrenes with sodium arylsulfinates and Et3N·3HF to give β-fluorosulfones via radical-polar crossover
10.1021_acs.joc.4c00722.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sodium arylsulfinate (2a) to a sulfonyl radical; subsequent anodic oxidation of the benzylic radical adduct to a benzylic cation; cathodic reduction of protons generates H2 (obviating chemical oxidant)
10.1021_acs.joc.4c00722.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes ArSO2Na (2a) to sulfonyl radical I, which adds to the styrene to give benzylic radical II; a second anodic oxidation forms benzylic cation III, which is trapped by fluoride from Et3N·3HF to deliver the β-fluorosulfone 3. Protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic radical from sulfonyl addition to styrene (II)

10.1021_acs.joc.4c00722.notes: Mechanism is proposed primarily on the basis of CV showing low oxidation potential of sulfinate 2a (oxidized first) and analogy to literature precedents; no radical-trap (TEMPO/BHT), KIE, or DFT experiments are reported on this page. The same paradigm is extended to fluorothiocyanation (with NH4SCN) and to vinylsulfonylation (with stainless-steel cathode).

10.1021_acs.joc.4c01548.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical sulfonylation of olefins with sodium sulfinates to access vinyl, alkyl, and allyl sulfones using NaI as redox mediator
10.1021_acs.joc.4c01548.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide to I2 (and/or direct oxidation of sulfinate) generates sulfonyl radical A; cathode reduces protons to H2 and, for activated olefins, reduces the C-centered radical B to a carbanion
10.1021_acs.joc.4c01548.electron_flow: At the anode, iodide is oxidized to I2 which oxidizes ArSO2Na to sulfonyl radical A (direct anodic oxidation of sulfinate also possible); A adds to the olefin giving C-radical B. Path I: B is trapped by I2 to give β-iodo sulfone C, which undergoes base-promoted HI elimination to the vinyl sulfone. Path II (EWG/cyclohexenyl olefins): B is reduced at the cathode to anion D, which is reoxidized by I2 or protonated/eliminated to give alkyl or allyl sulfone; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: C-centered carbon radical B (β-sulfonyl alkyl radical after addition to olefin)
β-sulfonyl carbanion D (Path II, after SET reduction of B)
I2 generated by anodic oxidation of I-

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of 4.0 equiv TEMPO fully suppressed formation of vinyl sulfone 1b, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: N,N-diallyl-4-methylbenzenesulfonamide (radical cyclization probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Afforded cyclized pyrrolidine product 5d (iodomethyl-tosylmethyl pyrrolidine) in 35%, indicating a radical addition/cyclization process

probe_reagents.name: Standard reaction (isolation of iodinated intermediate)
probe_reagents.observation: β-iodo sulfone byproduct 1f isolated in 15% under standard conditions, supporting iodosulfone C as intermediate of Path I

10.1021_acs.joc.4c01548.notes: Two mechanistic pathways are proposed depending on olefin electronics: Path I (favored for simple/aliphatic olefins) goes through β-iodo sulfone C with base-mediated HI elimination to give vinyl sulfone; Path II operates for electron-poor olefins (EWG) or cyclohexene-type substrates where cathodic SET reduces radical B to a carbanion giving alkyl or allyl sulfones. The reaction also proceeds in absence of NaI (lower yield), indicating direct anodic oxidation of sulfinate is a viable parallel route to radical A.

10.1021_jo500559r.rxn_class_summary: Anodic oxidation of 4,4'-dimethoxystilbene and substituted stilbenes to give tetraaryltetrahydrofurans, dehydrotetralins, aldehydes, indanyl acetamides, and pallidol/ampelopsin F-type products via radical cation dimerization
10.1021_jo500559r.electrochemical_role: Anodic one-electron oxidation of the stilbene alkene to a radical cation, which dimerizes to a stable dication; subsequent nucleophilic capture (H2O, MeOH, AcOH, AcO-, MeCN) and intramolecular cyclization steps are non-electrochemical
10.1021_jo500559r.electron_flow: The anode removes one electron from the stilbene to give a radical cation (30), which dimerizes through C-C bond formation at the β-carbons to a stable dication (31); nucleophiles (H2O, MeOH, AcO-, MeCN) then attack 31 or its rearranged spirocyclic cations (33, 35, 36) to deliver tetrahydrofurans, acetals, aldehydes, dehydrotetralins, or acetamides after deprotonation.
10.1021_jo500559r.rate_determining_step: Radical cation dimerization of 30 to dication 31 (per Steckhan's kinetic studies)

key_intermediates.name: open-chain dication 31 from radical cation dimerization (threo 31a and meso 31b)
hydroxylated benzylic cation 32 (from H2O attack on 31)
spirocyclic carbocation 33 (ring-closed onto OH; precursor to aldehydes 1e/1f)
spirocyclic carbocation 35 (dearomatized; precursor to dehydrotetralins via 1,2-shift)
methoxylated spirocyclic cation 36 (MeOH analog of 33; precursor to acetals 1g/1h)
open-chain cation 37 (alkyl stilbene dication for stilbene 17)
indanyl cation (from cation 35-type cyclization in alkyl stilbenes)

probe_reagents.name: added water (1% H2O in MeCN/LiClO4)
probe_reagents.observation: Traps dication 31 to give tetrahydrofurans 1a/1b as major products, confirming dication is the primary electrolysis product

probe_reagents.name: nonaqueous workup (SiO2/CH2Cl2)
probe_reagents.observation: Markedly reduced tetrahydrofuran yield and increased dehydrotetralin yield, showing THFs arise from H2O attack during workup

probe_reagents.name: methanol (Steckhan's conditions, 25% MeOH/CH2Cl2)
probe_reagents.observation: Gives dimethyl acetals 1g/1h as major products via methoxylated spirocation 36, supporting spirocyclic intermediate

probe_reagents.name: sodium acetate / AcOH (Eberson's conditions)
probe_reagents.observation: AcO- captures the radical cation 30 before dimerization, giving acetates 1i/1j (62%)

probe_reagents.name: Br2/CH2Cl2 on dehydrotetralin 1d
probe_reagents.observation: Gives 1,6-dibromo-7-methoxy-2,3,4-tris(4-methoxyphenyl)naphthalene confirming 1d skeleton

probe_reagents.name: differentially disubstituted stilbenes (4-OMe vs 4'-CF3, 4'-NMe2 etc.)
probe_reagents.observation: Aryl migration in unsymmetrical stilbenes rules out open-chain carbocation; supports spirocyclic intermediate. Stronger donor placement determines regiochemistry of THF and dication coupling, supporting radical cation dimerization model

probe_reagents.name: X-ray diffraction on 1a, 1b, 1c, 1h, 1i, 1j, 2d, 6d, 14k, 15k, 16k, 20r, 21p, 22p, 22s, 23p, 23s, 25p
probe_reagents.observation: Unambiguous configurational assignment of products supporting the proposed pathways

probe_reagents.name: acetonitrile solvent (Ritter-type capture)
probe_reagents.observation: Traps indanyl cation in alkyl-substituted stilbenes 17-20 to give indanyl acetamides and tetralinyl acetamides (20q/20r), supporting cationic cyclization pathway

10.1021_jo500559r.notes: Favored mechanism: one-electron anodic oxidation -> radical cation 30 -> radical cation dimerization to dication 31 -> nucleophile/intramolecular trapping. Alternative pathway via 1,2-aryl shifts in an open-chain carbocation 32 (Scheme 2, path a) was initially proposed for aldehyde 1e but ruled out by results on unsymmetrically substituted stilbenes, which require a spirocyclic cation (33/35/36) for the observed aryl migrations. Two cation-radical coupling modes (threo 31a, C2; meso 31b) explain the two THF diastereomers 1a/1b. Two ring-expansion pathways from spirocation 35 (1,2-p-methoxybenzyl shift -> 1c; 1,2-p-methoxystyryl shift -> 1d) compete, with the styryl shift preferred ~5:1 for stilbene 1. Competing radical cation capture (vs. dimerization) operates in the presence of strong nucleophiles (NaOAc). For alkyl/EWG-substituted stilbenes, open-chain dication and acetonitrile (Ritter) trapping become competitive.

10.1021_acs.joc.2c02579.rxn_class_summary: Metal-free electrochemical oxidative difluoroethylation/cyclization of N-arylacrylamides and N-methacryloyl-N-alkylbenzamides with sodium difluoroalkylsulfinates (RCF2SO2Na) to construct difluoroalkylated oxindoles and isoquinoline-1,3-diones.
10.1021_acs.joc.2c02579.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (SET) of the sulfinate anion MeCF2SO2- to a sulfonyl radical, which extrudes SO2 to give the difluoroethyl radical; a second anodic oxidation aromatizes the cyclohexadienyl radical intermediate; cathode reduces protons to H2.
10.1021_acs.joc.2c02579.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes MeCF2SO2- by SET to sulfonyl radical A, which loses SO2 to give •CF2Me; this carbon radical adds to the acrylamide alkene to form radical B, which cyclizes onto the aryl ring to give cyclohexadienyl radical C; C is oxidized at the anode (−e-, −H+) to rearomatize to product, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.
10.1021_acs.joc.2c02579.rate_determining_step: Anodic SET oxidation of MeCF2SO2Na to generate the difluoroethyl radical (MeCF2SO2Na oxidized at 0.72 V, more easily than the acrylamide at 1.35 V).

key_intermediates.name: tertiary alkyl radical B (after addition to acrylamide)

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction greatly inhibited; only trace product formed.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully suppressed; DPE-CF2Me adduct 36 isolated in 42%, confirming free difluoroethyl radical.

probe_reagents.name: Hu's cathodic-reduction conditions (benzothiazolyl difluoroethyl sulfone, TBAOAc)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% of oxindole 1 — cathodic-reduction protocol does not deliver the cyclized product, supporting an anodic-oxidation pathway.

probe_reagents.name: difluoroethyl sulfone (replacing MeCF2SO2Na) under standard conditions
probe_reagents.observation: 0% product, confirming that the difluoroethyl radical is produced by SET oxidation of the sulfinate, not from the sulfone.

probe_reagents.name: controlled potential electrolysis at 0.73 V vs Ag/AgNO3
probe_reagents.observation: Product 1 obtained in 64% yield, confirming sulfinate oxidation at the anode initiates the reaction.

10.1021_acs.joc.2c02579.notes: CV shows MeCF2SO2Na oxidation peak at 0.72 V vs N-methyl-N-phenylmethacrylamide at 1.35 V, indicating sulfinate is preferentially oxidized at the anode. The method also extends to cyclopropyldifluoromethylsulfinate. No KIE or DFT was reported; mechanism is supported by radical trapping, CV, and controlled-potential electrolysis.

10.1021_acs.joc.2c02844.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical four-component 1,2-difunctionalization of alkenes with diaryl diselenides, CS2, and secondary amines to give beta-selenyl dithiocarbamates via simultaneous C-Se and C-S bond formation
10.1021_acs.joc.2c02844.electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh to a radical cation that cleaves to PhSe radical (II) and PhSe cation (III); anode also oxidizes the in-situ generated dithiocarbamate anion (VI) to dithiocarbamate cation (VII), which couples to disulfide 3g. Cathode reduces PhSe+ back to 1/2 PhSeSePh and reduces protons to H2.
10.1021_acs.joc.2c02844.electron_flow: At the anode, PhSeSePh is oxidized (-1e) to a radical cation that fragments into PhSe• and PhSe+; PhSe• adds to styrene to give a benzylic C-radical (IV) that is either trapped by 3g (path a) or further anodically oxidized to benzylic cation VIII which is captured by dithiocarbamate anion VI (path b), while PhSe+ alternatively forms seleniranium V that opens with VI (path c). In parallel, CS2 + amine form dithiocarbamate VI, which is anodically oxidized (-2e) to cation VII coupling to give disulfide 3g; the cathode reduces protons to H2 and PhSe+ back to PhSeSePh.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of 3.0 equiv TEMPO completely suppressed formation of 4a (0% yield), indicating a radical pathway involving PhSe• and/or benzylic C-radical

probe_reagents.name: PhSeCl (1d) without electricity
probe_reagents.observation: Stoichiometric PhSeCl with CS2/amine/K2CO3 and no current gave 4a in 18% yield, supporting involvement of an electrophilic Se / seleniranium intermediate

probe_reagents.name: bis(dithiocarbamoyl) disulfide 3g
probe_reagents.observation: Using preformed 3g instead of CS2 + amine under electrolysis gave 4a in 71% yield, supporting 3g as a key intermediate

probe_reagents.name: sodium diethyldithiocarbamate (3h)
probe_reagents.observation: Using preformed dithiocarbamate salt 3h gave 4a in 42% yield, consistent with dithiocarbamate anion VI as an on-cycle species

10.1021_acs.joc.2c02844.notes: Three competing product-forming pathways are proposed: (path a) radical IV + disulfide 3g; (path b) anodic oxidation of IV to benzylic cation VIII trapped by dithiocarbamate VI; (path c) PhSe+ forms seleniranium V which is opened by VI. The authors do not definitively distinguish among them. CV data (Ep = 0.09 and 1.13 V for dithiocarbamate; 1.90 and 1.64 V for PhSeSePh vs Ag/AgCl) support the order of anodic oxidations.

10.1021_acs.joc.4c02530.rxn_class_summary: Electrophotocatalytic thiocyanation/sulfonylation cyclization of unactivated alkenes (pyrazole-tethered styrenes) using NaSCN or RSO2Na as radical sources and TPPT as photocatalyst under anodic electrolysis.
10.1021_acs.joc.4c02530.electrochemical_role: Anodic regeneration of the photocatalyst: photoexcited TPPT* oxidizes NaSCN (or MeSO2SNa) to •SCN (or •SO2Me); reduced TPPT•– is reoxidized to TPPT at the graphite anode; protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
10.1021_acs.joc.4c02530.electron_flow: Blue-light excitation of TPPT gives TPPT*, which oxidizes NaSCN or MeSO2SNa (lower Eox than 1a) to the corresponding S- or N-centered radical; this radical adds to the alkene of 1a to give alkyl radical A, which undergoes intramolecular ipso-cyclization onto the pendant aryl to give cyclohexadienyl radical B; a second SET from B to TPPT* furnishes arenium cation C, which deprotonates to product 3a/5a, while TPPT•– is reoxidized to TPPT at the anode with concomitant cathodic H2 evolution.

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical A from •SCN/•SO2Me addition to alkene
cyclohexadienyl radical B (intramolecular ipso/aryl radical trap)
Wheland-type arenium cation C (after SET oxidation of B by TPPT*)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully suppressed (0% yield of 3a).

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: <1% yield of 3a; reaction inhibited.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: <1% of 3a; SCN–DPE adduct 6 isolated in 75%, confirming free •SCN radical.

probe_reagents.name: p-dinitrobenzene (pDNB)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% of 3a; consistent with SET pathway being blocked.

probe_reagents.name: CuCl2
probe_reagents.observation: 0% yield; single-electron pathway intercepted.

probe_reagents.name: CCl4
probe_reagents.observation: 50% of 3a (partial inhibition).

probe_reagents.name: (SCN)2 (thiocyanogen, via NCS-SCN)
probe_reagents.observation: Reacts with 1a to give 3a in 64% (with electricity) but only trace without electricity, suggesting (SCN)2 is competent but not the principal/most productive intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: on/off light and current
probe_reagents.observation: Product formation requires both continuous light and current; switching either off halts conversion and H2 evolution, indicating concurrent photoredox + electrolysis.

probe_reagents.name: TPPT fluorescence quenching with 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: 2a (NaSCN) and 4a (MeSO2SNa) strongly quench TPPT* fluorescence; 1a less so, consistent with PC* oxidizing the SCN/sulfinate anion first.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, 4a
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(2a)=1.31 V, Eox(4a)=1.14 V vs SCE, both lower than 1a (1.95 V), supporting selective oxidation of SCN–/sulfinate by TPPT*.

10.1021_acs.joc.4c02530.notes: Authors explicitly consider an alternative pathway where electrooxidation of NaSCN gives (SCN)2 as the active S-electrophile/radical source. Although (SCN)2 does deliver 3a, it is less reactive than NaSCN under standard conditions and gives only trace product without electricity, so the favored mechanism proceeds via direct photocatalytic SET oxidation of SCN–/sulfinate to •SCN/•SO2Me, with (SCN)2 deemed an unnecessary intermediate. H2 detection at the cathode confirms paired electrochemistry (anodic PC turnover / cathodic proton reduction).

10.1021_acs.joc.5c01984_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component thiol-ene/perfluoroalkylation of styrenes with arylthiols and perfluoroalkyl iodides to give β-thio-perfluoroalkylated products via a radical pathway
10.1021_acs.joc.5c01984_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction generates the perfluoroalkyl radical (nC4F9•) from nC4F9I; the perfluoroalkyl radical adds to the styrene to form a benzylic C-radical that is trapped by the arylthiol/thiyl species
10.1021_acs.joc.5c01984_sup_1.electron_flow: Cathode reduces nC4F9I to give nC4F9• which adds to the styrene producing a benzylic radical; this carbon radical is captured by the arylthiol (or its thiyl radical) to deliver the ArS group, completing the three-component coupling.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic C-radical from nC4F9• addition to 4-vinylbiphenyl

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 4aa only trace; TEMPO-benzylic adduct B detected by LC-MS ([M+H]+ 556.4, calc 556.57)

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: 4aa still formed in 70% yield (incomplete suppression)

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: 4aa obtained in 42% yield (partial suppression)

probe_reagents.name: diaryl disulfide 2a' (in place of thiol 2a)
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 4aa formed, indicating disulfide is not a competent intermediate under the standard conditions

10.1021_acs.joc.5c01984_sup_1.notes: Incomplete suppression by BHT (70%) and DPE (42%) suggests the benzylic radical is short-lived/efficiently trapped before scavenger interception; only TEMPO fully suppresses and gives a detectable adduct. Control with the disulfide 2a' giving only trace product argues against a disulfide-derived thiyl radical pathway under the standard electrochemical conditions.

10.1021_acs.joc.3c00015.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular amino- and oxysulfonylation of alkenes with sodium sulfinates affording sulfonylated O- and N-heterocycles (THFs, THPs, oxepanes, pyrrolidines, piperidines, δ-valerolactones)
10.1021_acs.joc.3c00015.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sodium sulfinate (RSO2Na) at the carbon anode generates a sulfonyl radical; subsequent anodic oxidation of the benzylic radical intermediate generates a benzylic carbocation; cathode reduces protons to H2
10.1021_acs.joc.3c00015.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes sulfinate to sulfonyl radical, which adds to the alkene to give a benzylic radical; the anode further oxidizes this radical to a benzylic cation, which is trapped intramolecularly by the pendant O- or N-nucleophile; protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic carbon-centered radical after sulfonyl addition to alkene
benzylic carbocation after second anodic oxidation

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction of S1 strongly inhibited; P1 formed in only 30% yield and a DPE-sulfonyl adduct (P70) detected by GC-MS, supporting sulfonyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of S1, S47, S63 vs p-tolylsulfinate
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peaks of unsaturated alcohol S1 (~1.60 V), N-tosyl amine S47 (~1.65 V), and carboxylic acid S63 (~1.50 V) are higher than p-tolylsulfinate (~1.2 V), indicating sulfinate is oxidized first under electrolysis

10.1021_acs.joc.3c00015.notes: DPE was used as a radical trap (analogous role to TEMPO/BHT) rather than a typical persistent radical scavenger. Mechanism is supported by CV ordering of oxidation potentials and trapping evidence; no DFT, KIE, or isotope labeling reported. The benzylic cation pathway (radical–polar crossover) is favored over a direct radical cyclization.

10.1021_acs.joc.3c00790_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical radical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-aryl-N-benzyl methacrylamides with CF3SO2Na to afford 3-CF3-substituted pyrrolin-2-ones (3-CF3 oxindole/pyrrolinone-type products)
10.1021_acs.joc.3c00790_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (assisted by photoexcited Mes-Acr+ photocatalyst under 455 nm irradiation) generates CF3• from CF3SO2Na via SET/desulfonylation; the Pt cathode reduces protons to H2 to close the redox cycle
10.1021_acs.joc.3c00790_sup_1.electron_flow: Under 455 nm light, Mes-Acr+* oxidizes CF3SO2Na to generate CF3•, which adds to the methacrylamide alkene; the resulting C-radical cyclizes onto the pendant enamide and is further oxidized (by Mes-Acr+* or at the anode) to a cation that loses a proton to give the pyrrolinone product, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical from desulfonylation of CF3SO2Na
Carbon-centered radical from CF3 addition to methacrylamide alkene
Cyclized carbocation/enamide-derived intermediate after intramolecular addition onto the N-vinyl group
Mes-Acr• (reduced acridine radical), reoxidized at the anode

10.1021_acs.joc.3c00790_sup_1.notes: Mechanistic section (per TOC) includes electricity on/off experiments (demonstrating need for continuous current/light), cyclic voltammetry studies (to assign oxidation events), and H2 detection tests at the cathode confirming proton reduction as the counter-reaction. PC screening (Table S7) shows Mes-Acr+ClO4- is optimal (81%) and that an added supporting electrolyte (Bu4NBF4, LiClO4) actually lowers yield, suggesting the photocatalyst plus KPF6/Cs2CO3 alone suffice for SET. Detailed radical-trap experiments (TEMPO/BHT) are not visible in the provided pages.

10.1021_acs.joc.1c01946.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular fluoroarylation of allyl aryl ethers to 3-fluorochromanes via an anodically generated difluoro-aryl-λ3-iodane mediator (ex-cell protocol)
10.1021_acs.joc.1c01946.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (2e-) of aryl iodide (tolyl iodide, 2) in HF:amine/CH2Cl2 generates difluoro-λ3-aryl iodane 4; cathode reduces protons to H2. The iodane (generated ex-cell) then activates the pendant alkene of substrate 1 toward intramolecular Friedel-Crafts-type cyclization with fluoride delivery.
10.1021_acs.joc.1c01946.electron_flow: At the anode, ArI (2) is oxidized by 2e- (losing nothing initially; ligand exchange with fluoride from HF:amine) to give ArIF2 (4); protons are reduced at the cathode to H2. ArIF2 then electrophilically activates the alkene of 1 to form iodonium intermediate 5, which undergoes intramolecular SEAr by the tethered aryl ether to give arenium 6; rearomatization with loss of H+ and fluoride delivery (with extrusion of ArI) furnishes 3-fluorochromane 3.
10.1021_acs.joc.1c01946.rate_determining_step: Intramolecular nucleophilic attack of the aromatic ring onto the iodane-activated alkene (intermediate 5); this step is outcompeted by fluoride attack (giving difluorination 7) when the arene is electron-poor (σp+m > 0.75) and is bypassed by direct SET on the arene when σp+m ≤ 0.

key_intermediates.name: iodonium-activated alkene adduct (5) from alkene + ArIF2
arene radical cation (from electron-rich substrate 1r, off-pathway SET)

probe_reagents.name: iodotoluene (2)
probe_reagents.observation: CV in 5.6 HF:amine/DCM shows two oxidation features (peak currents linear with v^0.5) assigned to sequential 1e- oxidations at iodine to form ArIF2 (4); peaks merge at low [HF]

probe_reagents.name: electron-rich substrate 1r (no EWG on arene)
probe_reagents.observation: Solution turns deep blue on addition of 4; UV/vis red-shift matches that produced by NOBF4, indicating SET to give an arene radical cation; oxidation peak of 1r disappears upon addition of NOBF4, PIFA, or 4 — consistent with SET deactivating the arene

probe_reagents.name: NOBF4
probe_reagents.observation: Single-electron oxidant that reproduces the UV/vis red shift seen with 4, supporting SET pathway for electron-rich arenes

probe_reagents.name: PIFA (PhI(OCOCF3)2)
probe_reagents.observation: Consumes the oxidation feature of 1r in CV, confirming arene oxidation as the competing pathway

probe_reagents.name: Hammett series (substrate scope with varied σp+m)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of fluoroarylation 3 is high for 0 < σp+m < 0.75; difluorination 7 dominates for σp+m > 0.75; SET/no reaction for σp+m ≤ 0 — correlates product distribution with Epa/2 (1.2–1.8 V optimal)

probe_reagents.name: internal alkene substrate 1p
probe_reagents.observation: Largely unreactive, consistent with iodane activation of terminal/β-substituted alkenes

probe_reagents.name: homoallyl substrate 1q
probe_reagents.observation: Gives 6-exo product preferentially over 7-endo, consistent with cation/iodonium-controlled cyclization regiochemistry

10.1021_acs.joc.1c01946.notes: Two competing off-pathway regimes are explicitly mapped: (i) for very electron-poor arenes (σp+m > 0.75), intermolecular fluoride attack on iodonium 5 outcompetes intramolecular SEAr, giving 1,2-difluoride 7; (ii) for very electron-rich arenes (σp+m ≤ 0), direct SET from the arene to 4 generates an unreactive arene radical cation. An 'ex-cell' protocol (pre-generate 4, then add substrate) is essential — in-cell and divided-cell variants give <5% product, attributed to substrate/intermediate decomposition under direct electrolysis. No KIE, radical trap (TEMPO/BHT), or DFT data reported on these pages.

10.1021_jo981231f.rxn_class_summary: Electroreductive hydrocoupling of chiral 3-trans-cinnamoyloxazolidinones to give cyclized hydrodimers (trans-3,4-diaryl cyclopentanones) with diastereo- and enantiocontrol governed by supporting electrolyte.
10.1021_jo981231f.electrochemical_role: Cathodic one-electron reduction of the α,β-unsaturated cinnamoyloxazolidinone to a Z-enolate-type anion radical, which dimerizes; choice of supporting electrolyte (Et4NOTs vs LiClO4) determines whether the naked or Li-chelated radical anion couples.
10.1021_jo981231f.electron_flow: Cathode delivers one electron to the cinnamoyloxazolidinone giving a Z-enolate-type β-carbon radical anion; two such radical anions couple C–C at their β-carbons (Si/Si from naked conformer A → R,R; Re/Re from Li-chelated conformer B → S,S), and the resulting bis-enolate undergoes intramolecular Dieckmann condensation to the cyclopentanone hydrodimer.

key_intermediates.name: syn-Z enolate-type anion radical (conformer A, naked)
anti-Z Li-chelated enolate-type anion radical (conformer B)
s-cis anion radical from cis-cinnamoyl substrate 8

probe_reagents.name: cis-cinnamoyloxazolidinone 8 (geometric isomer probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave the same R,R-selective dimer 2a as trans-1a, indicating fast s-cis→s-trans isomerization of the radical anion before coupling.

probe_reagents.name: 3-phenylpropioloyloxazolidinone 9 (alkyne analog)
probe_reagents.observation: Also afforded cyclized dimer 2a (45%, same de) plus non-cyclized diene 10 (15%), supporting in situ generation of 1a-type radical anion.

probe_reagents.name: LiClO4 vs Et4NOTs (counterion probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Et4NOTs gives R,R-dimer (87:13); LiClO4 inverts to S,S-dimer (up to 11:89), consistent with Li-chelated conformation B coupling on the Re face.

probe_reagents.name: ortho/meta/para aryl-substituted 1 (steric/electronic probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Ortho substitution hinders coupling and erodes de (esp. α-naphthyl 1j), consistent with steric control at the coupling face.

probe_reagents.name: cinnamoyloxazolidinethione 11a / thiazolidinethione 11b
probe_reagents.observation: Gave trans-3,4-diphenylcyclopentanone 5 directly via hydrolysis/decarboxylation under electrolysis with similar stereoselectivity.

10.1021_jo981231f.notes: Authors previously invoked an E-enolate-type anion radical (A') but here revise to Z-enolate conformers A (naked, syn-Z) and B (Li-chelated, anti-Z) based on PM3/AM1 + COSMO semiempirical calculations (Table 3, Scheme 4): naked anion radical favors C (syn-Z, A) while lithiated favors F (anti-Z, B), explaining electrolyte-dependent inversion of stereochemistry. Alternative rationales for diastereoselectivity in the Dieckmann step (cathode-surface templating or water-templating between two radical anions, ref 9) are noted but not adopted here; steric interaction between oxazolidinone substituents is preferred.

10.1021_jo026183k.rxn_class_summary: Electroreductive stereoselective hydrocoupling (homocoupling) of aryl-substituted methyl cinnamates to all-trans cyclized hydrodimers (cyclopentanone-carboxylates) via Dieckmann condensation, enabling asymmetric synthesis of dl-3,4-diarylhydrodimers.
10.1021_jo026183k.electrochemical_role: Cathodic one-electron reduction of the cinnamate C=C to a substrate-centered anion radical (radical anion), which undergoes homocoupling; performed in an undivided cell at constant current with Pb cathode/Pt anode in MeCN/Et4NOTs.
10.1021_jo026183k.electron_flow: Cathode delivers one electron to the cinnamate to give a β-aryl-stabilized ester anion radical; two such radical anions homocouple C-β to C-β to form a dl-favored 3,4-diaryladipate dianion, which undergoes intramolecular Dieckmann condensation to the all-trans cyclopentanone-carboxylate hydrodimer.
10.1021_jo026183k.rate_determining_step: Stereodetermining step is the C–C homocoupling of two anion radicals; the relative energies of the two diastereomeric coupling transition states (dl vs meso) set the high dl-selectivity.

key_intermediates.name: cinnamate anion radical (one-electron reduced methyl cinnamate)
meso-3,4-diaryladipate diester (meso-3, minor open-chain product not cyclized by base)

probe_reagents.name: methyl cis-cinnamate (cis-1a)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave same dl/meso = 92/8 stereoselectivity as trans-1a, indicating a common (planar) anion-radical intermediate independent of olefin geometry.

probe_reagents.name: cumarin (coumarin, rigid cyclic cinnamate analogue)
probe_reagents.observation: Electroreduction gave hydrodimer 9 in 66% with reversed (meso-preferred, 50% de) selectivity, attributed to the rigid cyclic constraint; supports that acyclic conformational freedom of the anion radical controls dl-selectivity.

probe_reagents.name: chiral auxiliary [(1R)-exo]-3-exo-(diphenylmethyl)borneol cinnamate (14a)
probe_reagents.observation: DFT/semiempirical optimization (UHF/AM1, UB3LYP/3-21G*, UB3LYP/6-31G*) of its anion radical shows conformer A more stable than B by 3.1–6.0 kcal/mol; Re face is shielded by the CHPh2 group, so homocoupling at Si face gives (R,R)-15a, rationalizing the observed 87–95% ee.

10.1021_jo026183k.notes: Authors explicitly reject earlier hypotheses invoking unusual interactions between anion radicals and the cathode surface or templating by water (Utley, Nielsen); they argue PM3/AM1 transition-state calculations on the bare anion-radical homocoupling already reproduce dl-preference for cinnamates and meso-preference for coumarin, so no special surface/water templating is required. Dieckmann cyclization to 2 requires sufficiently basic electrolyte conditions; under undivided-cell constant current, meso open-chain diester (meso-3) is not cyclized, accounting for the small (<10%) meso byproduct. Over-reduction to methyl phenylpropionate (4a) is a minor competing pathway.

10.1021_acs.joc.2c03020_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical halo-acyloxylation of vinylarenes with carboxylic acids and tetrabutylammonium halides (nBu4NX) as halogen source to give 1,2-halo-benzoate products via halogen-radical addition.
10.1021_acs.joc.2c03020_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of halide (I-, Br-, Cl- from nBu4NX) to halogen radical/X2; halide is preferentially oxidized over the styrene substrate (Eox of nBu4NI 0.373 V, nBu4NBr 0.686 V, nBu4NCl 0.815 V vs SCE, all below 1a at 1.413 V).
10.1021_acs.joc.2c03020_sup_1.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes halide (X-) to X•; X• adds to the vinylarene to give a benzylic C-radical (trapped by DMPO; confirmed by cyclopropyl radical clock giving ring-opened products with Br/Cl); this radical is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation that is captured by the carboxylate to deliver the β-halo benzoate.

key_intermediates.name: halogen radical (I•, Br•, Cl•) generated by anodic oxidation of halide
benzylic carbon radical from X• addition to vinylarene (cyclopropyl radical clock substrate 2a gives intact cyclopropyl-CR2-X adduct Int-1/Int-2/Int-3)
ring-opened homoallyl/allyl halide radical-clock product (e.g., 56, 58) indicating radical intermediate that partly ring-opens with Br/Cl
benzylic cation after further oxidation of benzylic radical
benzoate (PhCO2-) from deprotonation of benzoic acid 1b, traps cation to form ester product

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)benzene (2a, radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: With nBu4NI: only non-ring-opened product 54 (56%) via Int-1, indicating fast trapping of benzylic radical by I. With nBu4NBr: 55 (12%) non-opened plus 56 (40%) ring-opened homoallyl bromide, showing radical intermediate (Int-2). With nBu4NCl: 57 (20%) plus ring-opened 58 (32%), consistent with longer-lived radical (Int-3).

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (spin trap)
probe_reagents.observation: EPR signals observed for nBu4NI alone, nBu4NBr alone, and for 1a+1b+nBu4NX systems under electrolysis (Figure S4 A–D), confirming radical intermediates.

10.1021_acs.joc.2c03020_sup_1.notes: Halide identity controls radical lifetime: I gives clean closed-ring product (fast trap), while Br and Cl give increasing amounts of ring-opened radical-clock products, indicating slower radical capture and more pronounced free-radical character with Br/Cl. CV shows halides are oxidized preferentially over the styrene, supporting a halide-mediator pathway rather than direct alkene oxidation.

10.1021_acs.joc.1c01267.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical selenylation/cyclization of N-aryl acrylamides (and related alkenes) with diphenyl diselenide to access SePh-functionalized oxindoles and isobenzofuran-imines
10.1021_acs.joc.1c01267.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh (two sequential SET events) generates phenylselenium cation (PhSe+) and phenylselenium radical (PhSe•); cathode reduces protons to H2
10.1021_acs.joc.1c01267.electron_flow: PhSeSePh is oxidized at the anode in two SET steps to PhSe• and PhSe+; PhSe+ adds across the alkene of the N-aryl acrylamide to form a seleniranium ion, which is opened by the pendant carbonyl/amide oxygen (intramolecular nucleophilic cyclization) to give a cationic intermediate; deprotonation furnishes the product while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: seleniranium ion C (three-membered Se+ adduct on alkene)
benzylic/tertiary carbocation D after ring-opening with SePh on CH2
Wheland-type oxocarbenium/iminium intermediate E from intramolecular nucleophilic attack by carbonyl/amide oxygen

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Selenylation product 3a still obtained in excellent yield, suggesting the major pathway is not radical

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Did not suppress formation of 3a, consistent with a non-radical (ionic) pathway

probe_reagents.name: PhSeCl
probe_reagents.observation: Provided 3a in 43% yield under non-electrochemical conditions, supporting involvement of an electrophilic PhSe+ species (but does not fully exclude a radical pathway)

probe_reagents.name: PhSSPh
probe_reagents.observation: Analogous sulfurized product 9 formed in 45% yield under standard electrochemical conditions, indicating generality of the electrophilic chalcogen pathway

10.1021_acs.joc.1c01267.notes: CV showed PhSeSePh oxidizes at 1.416 V and 1.757 V (vs Ag/AgCl), both below substrate 1a (2.101 V), supporting preferential anodic oxidation of the diselenide rather than the alkene. The authors favor an ionic (seleniranium) pathway initiated by anodically generated PhSe+, but explicitly note that a radical pathway involving PhSe• addition cannot be ruled out for other substrate classes (e.g., acrylamides 4 leading to oxindoles 5).

10.1021_acs.joc.3c00790.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical (or photoelectrochemical) polyfluoroalkylative cyclization of 3-aza-1,5-dienes with sodium fluoroalkanesulfinates to give 3-polyfluoroalkyl-4-pyrrolin-2-ones
10.1021_acs.joc.3c00790.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na (2a, Eox = 1.20 V vs Ag/AgCl) generates CF3 radical with loss of SO2; a second anodic oxidation converts the cyclized C-radical B into cation C; cathode reduces protons to H2. Under photoelectrochemical conditions, Mes-Acr+ is anodically oxidized to Mes-Acr•, whose excited state (Ered* = 1.78 V) oxidizes 2a and intermediate B.
10.1021_acs.joc.3c00790.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3• (+ SO2); CF3• regioselectively adds to the acryloyl alkene of the 3-aza-1,5-diene giving C-radical A, which undergoes 5-exo intramolecular addition onto the enaminic C=C to give cyclized radical B; B is oxidized at the anode (or by Mes-Acr•*) to cation C, which loses a proton to furnish the 4-pyrrolin-2-one; cathode reduces H+ to 1/2 H2.

key_intermediates.name: intermediate A: alpha-amido carbon radical after CF3 addition to acryloyl moiety
intermediate B: ring-closed C-centered radical after intramolecular addition onto enaminic C=C
intermediate C: cationic pyrrolinone after anodic oxidation of B

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction shut down (0% yield of 3a), supporting radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction shut down (0% yield)
