10.1039_c9sc05729c.notes: Mechanism (Scheme 9) is initiated by anodic SET on aniline 1a rather than on alkene 2a (which is not electroactive in the operating window). DDQ is proposed as an auxiliary oxidant for intermediate III (parallel to direct anodic oxidation). AcOH serves primarily as proton source for cathodic H2 evolution. No reaction occurs at the electrode surface for DDQ-mediated step (heterogeneous vs homogeneous oxidation of III remains ambiguous).

10.1039_D2SC04027A.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical alkene sulfonation with inorganic sulfites and alcohols to form alkoxysulfonyl-functionalized products (sulfonate esters) via anodic generation of alkoxysulfonyl radicals
10.1039_D2SC04027A.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of inorganic sulfite (SO3^2- / HSO3-) to sulfite radical anion (SO3•-), which is trapped by alcohol to give alkoxysulfonyl radical ROSO2•; subsequent anodic oxidation of the C-radical adduct to a carbocation enables polar trapping by alcohol
10.1039_D2SC04027A.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes inorganic sulfite to SO3•-, which is captured by ROH to give an alkoxysulfonyl radical (ROSO2•). This radical adds to the alkene to give a C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; alcohol (or deprotonation) then delivers the sulfonate ester product, while the cathode reduces protons/ROH to H2.

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical B from addition to styrene
benzylic carbocation C (after anodic SET of B)
β-hydroxy carbon radical D (from addition to homoallyl/allyl alcohol 4)
migrated radical E (after radical functional group migration)
cation F (after anodic oxidation of E)

probe_reagents.name: methacryloyl-N-methylbenzamide (6) radical trap/cyclization probe
probe_reagents.observation: cyclized sulfonate ester 7 obtained in 18% yield, indicating an alkoxysulfonyl radical adds to the alkene and the resulting C-radical cyclizes

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl-substituted styrene (1bi) radical clock
probe_reagents.observation: ring-opened product 8 (15%) formed alongside difunctionalized 3bi (24%), supporting a radical addition intermediate

probe_reagents.name: trifluoromethyl-substituted styrene (1bj)
probe_reagents.observation: gave difunctionalized product 3bj (37%) and elimination alkene 9 (14%), consistent with cationic intermediate undergoing proton elimination

probe_reagents.name: enamide 1bk
probe_reagents.observation: afforded coupling sulfonate 10 in 84% yield, supporting cationic intermediate formed by anodic oxidation of radical adduct

probe_reagents.name: SO2 gas (CV probe)
probe_reagents.observation: no oxidation peak in MeCN/MeOH, excluding oxidation of SO2 / alkyl sulfite as radical source

probe_reagents.name: NaHSO3 (CV probe)
probe_reagents.observation: oxidation peak at 1.23 V vs SCE, lower than styrene 1a (1.80 V), indicating preferential anodic oxidation of inorganic sulfite

probe_reagents.name: Na2S2O5 (CV probe)
probe_reagents.observation: oxidation peak at 1.15 V vs SCE; alkene 4a showed no oxidation peak, supporting sulfite oxidation as initiation

10.1039_D2SC04027A.notes: For homoallyl/allyl alcohol substrates 4, a radical functional group migration pathway (D → E → F → 5) is proposed in parallel to the direct radical–polar crossover route used for styrenes. CV experiments rule out direct SO2 or alkyl sulfite oxidation as the radical source.

10.1039_d0sc05665k.rxn_class_summary: Catalyst-free electrochemical [3+2] annulation of N-cyclopropylanilines with alkenes (styrenes) via a radical cation chain mechanism
10.1039_d0sc05665k.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of N-cyclopropylaniline (1a) to its aminium radical cation (1a+•) initiates a chain; cathode reduces the product radical cation (3a+•) only in initiation; propagation is via SET between 3a+• and 1a
10.1039_d0sc05665k.electron_flow: The anode oxidizes 1a to the aminium radical cation 1a+•, which undergoes cyclopropane ring-opening and addition to styrene 2a to give the cyclized radical cation 3a+•; 3a+• is then reduced by another molecule of 1a (chain propagation), regenerating 1a+• and releasing neutral product 3a, so only sub-stoichiometric current is needed.

key_intermediates.name: ring-opened distonic radical cation (β-iminium carbon radical)

probe_reagents.name: divided H-cell (frit-separated anode/cathode)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a (23% NMR yield) and [3a+H]+ detected only in the anode chamber, none in the cathode chamber — rules out cathodic reduction as the productive reduction step and supports the chain mechanism

probe_reagents.name: online EC/MS (sonic spray ionization)
probe_reagents.observation: Direct detection of 1a+• (m/z 161) upon applying +3.0 V, confirming anodic generation of the aminium radical cation

probe_reagents.name: catalytic current / Faradaic analysis
probe_reagents.observation: Only ~0.34 equiv of electrons consumed relative to 1a (32.4 C vs 96.5 C required for stoichiometric oxidation), consistent with a radical chain rather than stoichiometric electrolysis

10.1039_d0sc05665k.notes: Authors explicitly translate a known photoredox (Ru(bpz)3) [3+2] annulation into a catalyst-free electrochemical version; alternative non-chain pathway (anodic oxidation + cathodic reduction of 3a+•) is considered but disfavored by the divided-cell experiment and the sub-stoichiometric charge consumption. Conditions: undivided RVC(+)/Pt(–) cell, LiOTf/MeCN, 1 mA constant current.

10.1039_D2SC07045F_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical sulfonylation/cyclization of alkenes with sodium sulfinates proceeding via sulfonyl radical addition under undivided-cell conditions.
10.1039_D2SC07045F_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sulfinate (ArSO2Na) to a sulfonyl radical (ArSO2•), which adds to the alkene; cathode reduces protons to H2.
10.1039_D2SC07045F_sup_1.electron_flow: Sulfinate is oxidized at the anode by SET to generate a sulfonyl radical that adds to the alkene; the resulting carbon radical is further oxidized/deprotonated to the vinyl sulfone, while protons are reduced at the Pt cathode.

key_intermediates.name: tosyl radical (ArSO2•) from anodic oxidation of sodium p-toluenesulfinate
benzylic/cyclopropylcarbinyl C-radical from sulfonyl radical addition to 1-cyclopropyl-1-phenylethylene
vinyl sulfone product 9 (sulfonyl-trapped alkene, no ring-opening observed)

probe_reagents.name: 1-cyclopropyl-1-phenylethylene (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction with sodium p-toluenesulfinate under electrolysis (GF anode, Pt cathode, Et4NClO4, HFIP/H2O 20:1, 4 mA, 8 h) gave the vinyl sulfone 9 without cyclopropane ring-opening rearrangement, indicating fast trapping of the sulfonyl-adduct radical or a non-radical pathway at that stage.

10.1039_D2SC07045F_sup_1.notes: Page also contains characterization data for Suzuki cross-coupling product 6 (Pd(OAc)2/PPh3/K2CO3, i-PrOH, 90 °C) of bromo-thiazonine 4l with (3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl)boronic acid — this is product diversification, not mechanistic. Only the radical trapping/clock experiment at the bottom is mechanism-relevant.

10.1039_d2sc07045f.rxn_class_summary: Electrooxidative radical cascade annulation of 1,6-enynes with sulfinates to access 7- and 9-membered sulfonamide-containing bridged/fused heterocycles via sulfonyl radical addition.
10.1039_d2sc07045f.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of p-toluenesulfinate (TolSO2Na, 1a) generates the sulfonyl radical (Ts·); a subsequent anodic SET oxidation of the cyclized C-radical intermediate (C or F) followed by deprotonation furnishes the aromatic product.
10.1039_d2sc07045f.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes sulfinate 1a to sulfonyl radical Ts·, which chemoselectively adds to the alkyne (Path B, R=Ph) or alkene (Path A, R=H) of the 1,6-enyne; the resulting C-radical undergoes intramolecular radical cyclization (6-exo-trig or 5-exo-dig) and then aromatic cyclization, after which a second anodic SET oxidation and deprotonation yield the bridged (4a) or fused (3aa) sulfonamide product.

key_intermediates.name: vinyl radical A from Ts· addition to alkyne of 1,6-enyne (R=Ph)
aryl/spiro radical C (after intramolecular cyclization to bridged system)
alkyl radical D from Ts· addition to alkene of 2 (R=H)
cyclohexadienyl radical F (after intramolecular aromatic cyclization)

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)benzene
probe_reagents.observation: Trapped Ts· to give adduct 9 in 60% yield; benzyl radical formed was oxidized without cyclopropyl ring-opening, supporting Ts· generation and fast SET oxidation of the benzyl radical.

probe_reagents.name: Preformed medium-sized ring substrate 5
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard conditions with 1a, no 4k was formed (0%), indicating that Ts· addition does not occur after formation of the medium-sized ring; Ts· must add before cyclization.

10.1039_d2sc07045f.notes: Two divergent pathways are proposed depending on alkyne substitution: Path B (R=Ph, internal alkyne) — Ts· adds to the alkyne, 6-exo-trig cyclization, then intramolecular cyclization to give bridged tricyclic sulfonamide 4a (6-7-5 system not formed here, instead bridged); Path A (R=H, terminal alkyne) — Ts· adds chemoselectively to the alkene, 5-exo-dig cyclization gives vinyl radical E, intramolecular aromatic cyclization to F, SET oxidation/deprotonation affords fused 6-7-5 product 3aa. Selectivity is rationalized by different activation barriers for Ts· addition to C=C vs C≡C depending on alkyne substitution. CV was referenced (ref 19) to support sulfinate oxidation potential.

10.1039_d0sc01078b.rxn_class_summary: Electrooxidative [3+2] annulation of phenols with electron-deficient alkenes (cinnamates) to give C3-functionalized 2-aryl-2,3-dihydrobenzofurans under undivided-cell conditions with cathodic H2 evolution.
10.1039_d0sc01078b.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the electron-rich phenol generates a phenoxyl/aryl radical (I); a later anodic oxidation of the post-addition benzylic radical to a stabilized benzylic cation enables intramolecular O-cyclization; cathode reduces protons to H2.
10.1039_d0sc01078b.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes phenol 1 by SET to give aryl/phenoxyl radical I; I adds regioselectively to the α-carbon of cinnamate 2a yielding a benzylic C-radical (Int-a-I→Int-a-II after rearomatization); a second anodic SET oxidizes this radical to a benzylic cation that is trapped intramolecularly by the phenolic OH to close the 2,3-dihydrobenzofuran ring, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.
10.1039_d0sc01078b.rate_determining_step: Regioselective α-addition of aryl radical I to the electron-deficient alkene (TS-a-I, ΔG‡ = +24.0 kcal/mol; β-addition TS-b-I = +30.4 kcal/mol).

key_intermediates.name: phenoxyl/aryl C-radical from anodic oxidation of phenol 1 (radical I, drawn as ortho-C-radical / phenoxyl resonance form)
Int-a-I: adduct radical from α-addition of aryl radical I to cinnamate 2a (favored)
Int-a-II: dienone/keto tautomer after rearomatization, still C-radical α to ester
benzylic carbocation α to CO2Et formed by SET oxidation of Int-a-II (stabilized by p-methoxyphenyl)
Dimer 5 from radical I self-coupling (observed when 2a is absent or with electron-rich 1o)

probe_reagents.name: electron-rich phenol 1o (control substrate, no alkene partner / outcompetes addition)
probe_reagents.observation: In absence of efficient trapping by 2a, 1o dimerizes to give 5 in 87% yield with >95% recovery of 2a, evidencing anodic generation of aryl radical I.

probe_reagents.name: DFT (U)M06-2X(D3)/6-311+G(d,p)//(U)B3LYP-D3/6-31G(d) in CF3CH2OH/CH2Cl2
probe_reagents.observation: α-addition TS (+24.0) lower than β (+30.4); Int-a-II (-0.6) more stable than Int-b-II (+3.8), rationalizing exclusive α-regioselectivity.

probe_reagents.name: X-ray crystallography of 3ac
probe_reagents.observation: Confirms 2,3-trans stereochemistry of the dihydrobenzofuran product.

10.1039_d0sc01078b.notes: Authors explicitly compare α- vs β-radical-addition pathways with DFT; α-pathway is both kinetically and thermodynamically favored due to stabilization of the resulting radical/cation by the p-methoxyphenyl group of the cinnamate. The dimerization experiment with 1o supports anodic generation of a phenol-derived radical but does not directly rule out a phenoxenium (2e) pathway; no TEMPO/BHT trapping or KIE reported. HFIP cosolvent likely stabilizes cationic intermediates in the radical-polar crossover step.

10.1039_d4sc07923j.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemically driven allylation of unactivated primary/secondary/tertiary alkyl bromides with allylic carbonates to form C(sp3)–C(sp3) bonds via radical–polar crossover, without transition-metal catalysts
10.1039_d4sc07923j.electrochemical_role: Cathodic single-electron reduction of alkyl bromide generates alkyl radical; a second cathodic reduction of the benzylic radical intermediate generates a carbanion that undergoes β-elimination of carbonate
10.1039_d4sc07923j.electron_flow: Cathode reduces alkyl bromide to an alkyl radical, which adds to the allylic carbonate's alkene to give a stabilized tertiary benzylic radical; this radical is further reduced at the cathode to a carbanion that undergoes β-elimination of the ethyl carbonate to deliver the allylated product.

key_intermediates.name: tertiary benzylic radical from addition to allylic carbonate
benzylic carbanion after second cathodic reduction
allylated alkane product after β-elimination of OCOOEt

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: DPE-trapped alkyl radical adducts 8 (36%), 9 (47%), 10 (61%) isolated, confirming alkyl radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO–alkyl adducts 11 (21%) and 12 (18%) observed; 13 (tBu) not detected; reaction suppressed

probe_reagents.name: (bromomethyl)cyclopropane (2d)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened allylated product 14 obtained in 55%, indicating primary alkyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: ethyl 2-bromo-2-cyclopropylacetate (2e)
probe_reagents.observation: Desired product 15 in trace; ring-opened by-product 15a in 45%, supporting secondary alkyl radical formation

probe_reagents.name: allyl-tethered bromotetrahydrofuran substrates (2f, 2g)
probe_reagents.observation: 5-exo radical cyclization products 16 (67%) and 17 (31%) isolated, confirming alkyl radical intermediacy

probe_reagents.name: competition between 1°, 2°, 3° alkyl bromides
probe_reagents.observation: Tertiary (3a, 59%) > secondary (5a, 17%) > primary (4a, 8%), consistent with ease of C–Br reduction / radical stability

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of alkyl bromides and allylic carbonate
probe_reagents.observation: Ep/2: tBu-Br −2.52 V, iPr-Br −2.66 V, 3-PhPrBr −2.70 V; allylic carbonate 1a −2.40, −2.74 V vs SCE, showing alkyl bromides are reduced under conditions

10.1039_d4sc07923j.notes: Authors explicitly ruled out cathodic reduction of the allylic carbonate to release an allyl radical (not observed). FeXn is shown at the anode in the proposed scheme, suggesting sacrificial Fe anode chemistry, though it is not emphasized mechanistically. TBAB serves as supporting electrolyte in DMA at 4 mA, r.t.

10.1039_D2SC04027A_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical methoxysulfonylation/cyclization of N-methacryloyl-N-methylbenzamide with NaHSO3 and MeOH to form a methylsulfonyl-functionalized isoquinolinedione
10.1039_D2SC04027A_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bisulfite (HSO3-) to a sulfonyl radical (and/or oxidation of methanol-derived species), which adds to the methacrylamide alkene to trigger radical cyclization
10.1039_D2SC04027A_sup_1.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes bisulfite to a sulfonyl radical that adds to the terminal alkene of the methacrylamide; the resulting tertiary C-radical undergoes intramolecular cyclization onto the pendant aryl ring, followed by rearomatization (further oxidation/loss of H+) with MeOH incorporation to give the methoxy/methylsulfonyl-functionalized isoquinolinedione product.

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical from sulfonyl radical addition to methacrylamide alkene
aryl/cyclohexadienyl radical after intramolecular ipso/ortho cyclization onto benzamide arene
methoxy-substituted alpha-aryl-beta-methylsulfonyl ethanol model compound (NMR D-labeling study)

probe_reagents.name: deuterated MeOH (CD3OD / H/D labeling)
probe_reagents.observation: 1H NMR integrations on model methoxysulfonyl product show partial D incorporation (0.24D and 0.65D at OMe/SO2OMe positions), consistent with MeOH being the source of the methoxy group

probe_reagents.name: N-methacryloyl-N-methylbenzamide (radical cyclization substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis with MeOH/NaHSO3 gives the cyclized isoquinoline-1,3-dione bearing a CH2SO2OMe group (product 7, 18%), supporting a radical addition/cyclization pathway

10.1039_D2SC04027A_sup_1.notes: The radical-cyclization probe (substrate 6 → isoquinolinedione 7) functions as a radical clock, indicating a sulfonyl-radical addition followed by intramolecular aryl cyclization. H/D labeling on a model sulfonate ester suggests MeOH is the methoxy source. No TEMPO/BHT trapping or DFT was shown on this page.

10.1039_d2sc05423j.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical multicomponent C-H tetrazolation of alkyl arenes and vicinal azidotetrazolation of alkenes using TMSN3 and acetonitrile via radical-cation/carbocation Ritter-type pathway
10.1039_d2sc05423j.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation: for alkyl arenes, the arene is oxidized to a radical cation that loses a proton to give a benzyl radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a benzyl carbocation; for alkenes, anodic oxidation generates an azido radical from N3- (from TMSN3) that adds to the alkene, and the resulting C-radical is then oxidized to a β-azido carbocation
10.1039_d2sc05423j.electron_flow: For C-H tetrazolation: anode oxidizes the alkyl arene to a radical cation, which deprotonates to a benzyl radical and is oxidized again at the anode to a benzyl carbocation; MeCN traps the cation to form a nitrilium ion, which is attacked by azide to deliver the tetrazole, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2. For alkenes: anodically generated azido radical adds to the alkene; the resulting β-azido C-radical is further oxidized at the anode to a β-azido carbocation, trapped by MeCN then azide to give the vicinal azidotetrazole.
10.1039_d2sc05423j.rate_determining_step: Formation of the benzyl cation via stepwise electron-transfer/proton-transfer (ET/PT), as indicated by Hammett ρ = -2.97 (positive charge buildup in the transition state)

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropyl-1-phenyl)ethylene (radical clock 54)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 55 isolated (21%, 7.5:1), confirming involvement of an azido radical adding to the alkene

probe_reagents.name: p-substituted ethylbenzenes (Hammett series)
probe_reagents.observation: Hammett ρ = -2.97 (r²=0.97), much more negative than typical HAT (ρ > -1), indicating positive charge buildup, inconsistent with HAT and consistent with radical-cation/ET-PT mechanism

probe_reagents.name: BDE-matched substrate 21'
probe_reagents.observation: Substrates with similar benzylic BDEs (88 vs 83.3 kcal/mol) but different electronics show distinct reactivity, disproving one-step HAT

probe_reagents.name: benzyltrimethylsilane (56)
probe_reagents.observation: Cleaves C-Si bond to afford tetrazole 7 exclusively (34%), supporting radical-cation intermediate (desilylation) rather than HAT

probe_reagents.name: TMSN3 omission control (Fig. 2G, with nBu4NHSO4)
probe_reagents.observation: In absence of TMSN3, Ritter-type acetamide product 57 forms (27%), substantiating involvement of benzyl carbocation/nitrilium intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry (CV) of ethylbenzene, TMSN3, styrene
probe_reagents.observation: TMSN3 Ep/2 = 0.92 V; ethylbenzene Ep/2 = 2.43 V (drops to 2.01 V with HFIP); styrene Ep/2 = 2.21 V — HFIP stabilizes radical cations and enables substrate oxidation; for alkenes TMSN3 is oxidized first

10.1039_d2sc05423j.notes: Authors explicitly disfavor a HAT pathway for benzyl radical generation (based on Hammett ρ, BDE comparisons, and benzylsilane experiment), favoring stepwise ET/PT via the arene radical cation. For alkenes, two alternative pathways to the diazide byproduct were considered: radical-radical cross-coupling (path b, disfavored due to transient radicals) and polar trapping of carbocation II by N3- (path c, mitigated by large excess of MeCN outcompeting low-concentration TMSN3 on the anode surface). HFIP is critical as co-solvent for stabilizing radical-cation intermediates; electrolyte choice (nBu4NClO4 vs nBu4NBF4 vs nBu4NHSO4) strongly tunes selectivity between azidotetrazolation and competing azidoacetamidation.

10.1039_D2SC05423J.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical multicomponent reaction for C-H tetrazolation of alkyl arenes and vicinal azidotetrazolation of alkenes using acetonitrile and TMSN3
10.1039_D2SC05423J.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation: for alkyl arenes, anode oxidizes the arene to a radical cation that loses a proton to form a benzyl radical, which is further oxidized to a benzyl cation; for alkenes, anode oxidizes azide (from TMSN3) to azido radical which adds to alkene, then anodic oxidation gives β-azido carbocation. Cathode reduces protons to H2.
10.1039_D2SC05423J.electron_flow: For alkyl arenes: anode oxidizes the arene (SET) to a radical cation, deprotonation gives a benzyl radical, a second anodic SET forms the benzyl cation, MeCN traps it as a Ritter nitrilium ion, and azide closes the ring to give the tetrazole; protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode. For alkenes: anodic oxidation of TMSN3-derived azide gives N3•, which adds to the alkene, the resulting C-radical is further oxidized to a β-azido cation, trapped by MeCN/azide to form the vicinal azidotetrazole.
10.1039_D2SC05423J.rate_determining_step: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the alkyl arene (transition state bears positive charge build-up, ρ = -2.97)

key_intermediates.name: ethylbenzene radical cation (phenyl radical cation intermediate)

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropyl-1-phenyl)ethylene (radical clock 54)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened azidotetrazole 55 obtained (21%, 7.5:1), confirming involvement of an azido radical that adds to the alkene

probe_reagents.name: p-substituted ethylbenzenes (Hammett series)
probe_reagents.observation: Hammett ρ = -2.97 (vs σp+), much more negative than typical HAT (ρ > -1), indicating positive charge build-up in TS, consistent with radical cation/SET mechanism rather than HAT

probe_reagents.name: selectivity probe 21' (two benzylic C-H of different BDE: 88 vs 83.3 kcal/mol)
probe_reagents.observation: Sites of similar BDE but different electronic properties were well distinguished, disproving HAT pathway

probe_reagents.name: benzyltrimethylsilane (56)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave tetrazole 7 (34%) exclusively via C-Si cleavage of the radical cation, supporting radical cation (not HAT) mechanism

probe_reagents.name: omission of TMSN3 (control)
probe_reagents.observation: Ritter-type acetamide product 57 isolated in 27%, confirming benzyl carbocation/nitrilium intermediate

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of substrates and TMSN3
probe_reagents.observation: Ep/2 (ethylbenzene) = 2.43 V; with HFIP drops to 2.01 V; TMSN3 Ep/2 = 0.92 V; styrene Ep/2 = 2.21 V — for alkene case TMSN3 is oxidized preferentially, generating N3•

10.1039_D2SC05423J.notes: Authors explicitly disfavor a HAT pathway (via electrogenerated azido radical abstracting benzylic C-H) based on Hammett ρ = -2.97, BDE-insensitive selectivity in 21', and selective C-Si cleavage of benzyl-TMS 56 — favoring instead stepwise ET/PT (radical cation → deprotonation → benzyl radical → second SET → cation). For the alkene azidotetrazolation, an alternative diazide pathway (III) via radical-radical coupling (path b) or polar TMSN3 trapping of cation (path c) is considered disfavored because excess MeCN outcompetes low-concentration TMSN3 at the anode. HFIP is crucial as a radical-cation-stabilizing co-solvent that lowers the substrate oxidation potential.

10.1039_d3sc05946d.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical four-component domino [1+2+1+1] cycloaddition of aldehydes, styrenes, tert-butyl nitrite, and H2O to give 3,5-disubstituted isoxazoles
10.1039_d3sc05946d.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide (from n-Bu4NBr electrolyte) to Br radical, which serves as a HAT mediator to abstract the aldehyde C-H and generate an acyl radical; cathode reduces protons to H2
10.1039_d3sc05946d.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Br- to Br•, which abstracts the aldehyde C-H to form an acyl radical that adds to styrene; the resulting C-radical combines with an N=O radical (from TBN homolysis and/or TBN+H2O→HNO2→N=O) to give a β-carbonyl nitroso/oxime intermediate that cyclodehydrates (TFA-assisted) to the isoxazole, while the cathode evolves H2.
10.1039_d3sc05946d.rate_determining_step: Step involving alkene 2 and TBN 3 (first-order in [alkene] and [TBN], zero-order in aldehyde), consistent with radical addition/N=O trapping being rate-limiting

key_intermediates.name: bromine radical (Br•) from anodic oxidation of Br-
acyl radical A from aldehyde HAT
tert-butoxy radical II from TBN homolysis
N=O radical III from TBN homolysis
N=O radical IV generated in situ from TBN + H2O via HNO2
carbon-centered radical B from acyl radical addition to styrene

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops to 3%, indicating radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed (trace); BHT-N=O adduct 6 isolated and confirmed by HR-MS, supporting N=O radical

probe_reagents.name: D2O (replacing H2O)
probe_reagents.observation: Product m/z shifts 246→247; 88% D incorporation at C4 of isoxazole, showing H at C4 comes from water

probe_reagents.name: DMF-d7
probe_reagents.observation: No D incorporation (m/z 246), excluding DMF as H source

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: m/z 246→248 (100%), showing isoxazole O partly originates from H2O (in addition to TBN)

probe_reagents.name: 1,3-diketone 10 (presumed intermediate)
probe_reagents.observation: Subjected to standard conditions: no isoxazole formed, excluding 1,3-dicarbonyl pathway

probe_reagents.name: β-carbonyl ketoxime 11
probe_reagents.observation: Gave isoxazole 7 in 91% yield, supporting ketoxime D as on-cycle intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Substituted benzaldehyde competition (1a/1b, 1a/1c)
probe_reagents.observation: Electron-deficient aldehydes react faster (Hammett-type trend)

10.1039_d3sc05946d.notes: Two parallel sources of N=O are proposed: direct TBN homolysis (pathway III) and TBN + H2O → HNO2 → N=O (pathway IV); 18O-labeling shows both TBN and water contribute the ring O. CV shows that mixing 1a+2a+3 lowers the oxidation potential of 1a from 0.99 V to 0.73 V vs SCE, supporting anodic involvement. α-Methylstyrene fails (steric effect); no reaction without current.

10.1039_d1sc00760b.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical syn-diformyloxylation (protected syn-1,2-diol formation) of vinylarenes using DMF as the oxygen/formyl source and TFA as a co-nucleophile under undivided-cell anodic oxidation.
10.1039_d1sc00760b.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the vinylarene to a benzylic-stabilized alkene radical cation (Eox ~1.15-1.75 V vs SCE); a second anodic SET oxidizes the β-DMF-adduct radical to a benzylic carbocation. TFA/TFA- couple at the cathode produces H2 and regenerates TFA.
10.1039_d1sc00760b.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the vinylarene to a radical cation; the carbonyl oxygen of DMF adds to the benzylic position giving an α-iminium-oxy carbon radical, which is oxidized again at the anode to a benzylic cation. Trifluoroacetate captures this cation (major path) to give an isolable α-OCOCF3 iminium that undergoes SN1-like ionization and stereoinverted nucleophilic attack by a second DMF, followed by hydrolysis with H2(18)O to deliver the syn-diformate; TFA is regenerated and protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
10.1039_d1sc00760b.rate_determining_step: Initial anodic single-electron oxidation of the vinylarene to its radical cation (alkene Eox 1.15–1.75 V; thermodynamically more accessible than DMF oxidation at 2.1 V).

key_intermediates.name: β-(N,N-dimethyl-iminium-oxy) benzylic radical (C) from nucleophilic addition of DMF oxygen to radical cation
benzylic carbocation (D) formed by second anodic SET oxidation of C
trifluoroacetoxy-iminium adduct (E/F, isolable) — major path via TFA- capture of D
stereoinverted carbocation/iminium intermediate (G) leading to syn-product via DMF attack with stereoinversion
anti-product H' (minor path, direct DMF attack on D)

probe_reagents.name: DMF-d7
probe_reagents.observation: Both formyl groups in the product are >99% deuterated, confirming that both O-CHO units originate from DMF, not from formic acid or water.

probe_reagents.name: formic acid-d2 (DCOOD)
probe_reagents.observation: No deuterium incorporation in product formyl groups, ruling out hydrolytically generated HCOOH as the formyl source.

probe_reagents.name: H2(18)O
probe_reagents.observation: Doubly 18O-labeled diformate (>69%) is obtained; hydrolysis to diol removes the 18O label, showing the formyloxy oxygens (and thus the diol oxygens) originate from DMF and are only delivered via hydrolytic workup of the iminium intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: (2-phenylcyclopropyl)-substituted vinyl (radical clock, 30)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened diformyloxylation product 31 (49%) is obtained, implicating a benzylic radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: cis- vs trans-β-methylstyrene (35)
probe_reagents.observation: Both isomers give predominantly syn-diformate but with different dr (trans-35: 10:1; cis-35: 1:3 favoring 19'), showing partial memory of olefin geometry and supporting a stepwise cation/iminium pathway with stereoinversion at the second nucleophilic attack.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of styrene 1 ± TFA ± DMF
probe_reagents.observation: Styrene oxidation feature at Ep/2 ≈ 1.7 V vs SCE; DMF oxidation only at 2.1 V; no anodic shift with TFA/DMF additives, confirming direct anodic oxidation of the alkene rather than mediation through DMF.

10.1039_d1sc00760b.notes: Two competing pathways from the benzylic cation D are proposed: a minor 'direct' path where DMF attacks D to give the anti-product H', and a major path where trifluoroacetate first traps D to form isolable α-OCOCF3 iminium F, which then ionizes and is attacked by DMF with stereoinversion to give the syn-product H. The unusual syn-selectivity (and its partial dependence on starting alkene geometry) is rationalized by this TFA-mediated SN1-with-inversion sequence rather than by a concerted cyclic intermediate as in Woodward/Prévost/OsO4 chemistry. With a 1,3-diene (32), mono-oxidation gives a 3-OCOCF3 allylic intermediate (33) which can be converted to the diformate 34 by treatment with DMF/H2O, further supporting F-type intermediates.

molecules-29-00973-v2.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical 1,2-difluoroalkylation/cyclization of N-methacryloyl-2-arylbenzimidazoles (and 2-arylindoles) with sodium difluoroalkylsulfinates to give CF2Me-substituted benzimidazo/indolo[2,1-a]isoquinolinones.
molecules-29-00973-v2.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of MeCF2SO2Na to a sulfonyl radical (which loses SO2 to give the MeCF2 radical) initiates the cascade; the anode also oxidizes the post-cyclization aryl/cyclohexadienyl radical B to aryl cation C. Cathode reduces protons to H2.
molecules-29-00973-v2.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes MeCF2SO2– to its sulfonyl radical, which loses SO2 to give the MeCF2 radical; this adds to the acrylamide C=C to form C-radical A, which cyclizes intramolecularly onto the 2-aryl ring to give aryl radical B; B is oxidized at the anode to aryl cation C, which deprotonates (rearomatizes) to product 3. Cathodic reduction of H+ gives H2.

key_intermediates.name: MeCF2SO2 sulfonyl radical (from anodic SET of sulfinate)
Carbon-centered radical A (after MeCF2 addition to acrylamide alkene)
Cyclohexadienyl/aryl radical B (after intramolecular cyclization onto arene)
Aryl cation C (after second anodic oxidation of B)

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Product formation fully suppressed; BHT–CF2Me adduct detected by HRMS, indicating a free MeCF2 radical.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Product yield dropped to 16%; DPE–CF2Me radical adduct detected by HRMS, supporting a radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Substrates 1a and 2a (CV experiment)
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peaks at 1.47 V (1a) and 0.67 V (2a) vs Ag/AgNO3, indicating sulfinate 2a is more easily oxidized than 1a, consistent with anodic initiation at the sulfinate.

probe_reagents.name: No electricity control
probe_reagents.observation: No product detected, confirming the reaction is electrochemically driven.

molecules-29-00973-v2.notes: X-ray structures of 3m (CCDC 2303467) and 5i (CCDC 2307007) confirm product connectivity. Mechanism is proposed as a radical cyclization with terminal anodic oxidation/deprotonation (radical–cation crossover at the arene) rather than HAT-based rearomatization; cathode serves only for proton reduction. Scope tolerates halides, esters, nitriles, methoxy, naphthyl and thienyl arenes, and extends to cyclopropyl-CF2 sulfinate.

10.1246_bcsj.77.1545.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical one-electron cathodic reduction of accumulated N-acyliminium ion 'cation pools' to generate carbon free radicals that homo-couple or add to activated olefins (e.g., methyl acrylate)
10.1246_bcsj.77.1545.electrochemical_role: Two-step paired sequence: anodic two-electron oxidation of N-(methoxycarbonyl)pyrrolidine (or silyl-substituted precursor) at -78 °C generates and accumulates the N-acyliminium cation pool; subsequent cathodic one-electron reduction (Ep ≈ -0.85 to -0.90 V vs Ag/Ag+) of the cation gives the α-amino C-radical
10.1246_bcsj.77.1545.electron_flow: Anode performs 2e- oxidation of carbamate precursor (with loss of H+ or SiMe3+) to accumulate N-acyliminium cation; cathode then delivers 1e- to the cation giving an α-amino carbon radical, which either homo-couples (possibly via radical-radical or radical-cation dimer reduction) or adds to methyl acrylate, after which the resulting electron-poor radical is reduced once more and protonated to close a formal C-H/C=C addition.

key_intermediates.name: α-amino carbon radical (3) from one-electron reduction of 2
α-amino carbanion (9) from two-electron reduction of 2
dimeric radical cation (8) from radical-cation coupling
adduct radical (20) from addition of 3 to methyl acrylate
2:1 coupling byproduct (22) from reaction of 21 with 2
simply reduced pyrrolidine (1) via protonation of 9

probe_reagents.name: Zn powder (chemical 2e- reductant)
probe_reagents.observation: Predominantly gives simply reduced 1 (54%) with only 16% dimer 4, indicating 2e- pathway via carbanion 9 dominates chemically

probe_reagents.name: SmI2 (chemical 1e- reductant)
probe_reagents.observation: Gives mainly 1 (10%) with little 4 (4%); supports 2e- pathway in chemical reduction vs 1e- in electrochemistry

probe_reagents.name: Methyl acrylate (radical acceptor)
probe_reagents.observation: Trapping gives adduct 19 (up to 88% with 50 equiv TfOH), supporting intermediacy of carbon radical 3

probe_reagents.name: Trifluoromethanesulfonic acid (proton source)
probe_reagents.observation: Suppresses 2:1 byproduct 22 by protonating carbanion 21; increases 19 to 88%

probe_reagents.name: Mixed cation pool (2 + 7) crossover
probe_reagents.observation: Non-selective formation of three coupling products (4, 16, 18) consistent with radical coupling but also compatible with radical-cation or anion-cation coupling

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of cation pools 2 and 7
probe_reagents.observation: Ep = -0.85 to -0.90 V for 2 and -0.75 V for 7; positive shift vs N,N-dialkyliminium (-1.95 V) attributed to electron-withdrawing CO2Me

10.1246_bcsj.77.1545.notes: Authors favor the radical pathway (path A: 3 adds to methyl acrylate → 20 → 21 → 19) over the carbanion pathway (path B: 2e- reduction of 2 to carbanion 9, then conjugate addition). For homo-coupling, radical-radical dimerization of 3 and radical-cation coupling (3 + 2 → 8, then reduction) cannot be experimentally distinguished. Electrode choice matters: Pt cathode favors 1e- reduction (selective dimer 4, up to 75%), while C cathode allows more 2e- over-reduction to 1. Chemical reductants (Zn, SmI2) instead favor the 2e- carbanion pathway, possibly via metal-ion stabilization of 9.

10.1002_slct.202503086.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxyselenation of carbamate- and benzoate-tethered terminal alkenes with diphenyl diselenide to deliver β-hydroxyselenides via an anodically generated selenium electrophile and intramolecular cyclic seleniranium intermediate opened by water.
10.1002_slct.202503086.electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of PhSeSePh to a selenonium radical cation (A) which fragments to PhSe• (B) and PhSe+ (C); the cathode reduces protons to H2.
10.1002_slct.202503086.electron_flow: PhSeSePh is oxidized at the graphite anode to a radical cation that splits into PhSe• and PhSe+; the electrophilic PhSe+ adds to the tethered alkene with anchimeric assistance from the carbamate/ester oxygen to form a six-membered cyclic selenonium ion D, which is opened by water/hydroxide to give the β-hydroxyselenide while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

key_intermediates.name: Six-membered seleniranium/cyclic selenonium intermediate (D) from intramolecular carbamate-O assistance

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a still formed in 65% yield, indicating the productive pathway is not radical.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a still obtained in 49% yield, supporting a non-radical (cationic) pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclohexene (6)
probe_reagents.observation: No β-hydroxyselenide product 8 formed; non-tethered simple alkenes are unreactive, supporting the need for an intramolecular nucleophile to form intermediate D.

probe_reagents.name: Allylbenzene (7)
probe_reagents.observation: No desired product 9, again indicating tether-assisted seleniranium opening is required.

10.1002_slct.202503086.notes: Substrate-scope failures (cyano, nitro, dimethylcarbamate, cinnamyl) are rationalized by competing cathodic reduction of EWGs or anodic oxidation of N-methyl groups rather than by a change in mechanism. X-ray structure of 3j (CCDC 2408983) confirms the β-hydroxyselenide regiochemistry consistent with seleniranium-opening pathway. A radical chain involving PhSe• cannot be fully excluded but is disfavored by TEMPO/BHT results.

10.1016_j.jcat.2025.116252.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical epoxidation of styrene catalyzed by a Mn-based MOF (FICN-24) using bromide as a redox mediator in aqueous acetonitrile.
10.1016_j.jcat.2025.116252.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation cycles Mn(III)→Mn(IV)→Mn(V)=O at the FICN-24 MOF; bromide is co-oxidized to Br radical/Br2 which assists in forming a Mn(V)=O(Br) active species that transfers oxygen to the alkene.
10.1016_j.jcat.2025.116252.electron_flow: At the anode, L-Mn(III)-OH is oxidized stepwise (−e−/−H+) to L-Mn(IV)=O and then to L-Mn(V)=O; concurrently Br− is anodically oxidized to Br•/Br2, which binds Mn to give an L-Mn(V)=O(Br) species that delivers oxygen to styrene, releasing the epoxide and regenerating L-Mn(III)-Br/OH with H2O.
10.1016_j.jcat.2025.116252.rate_determining_step: Oxygen-atom transfer from L-Mn(V)=O(Br) to styrene C=C

probe_reagents.name: No-catalyst control
probe_reagents.observation: With NaBr but no FICN-24, 45% yield/45% selectivity — bromide mediation alone gives partial conversion, confirming MOF accelerates and improves selectivity.

probe_reagents.name: FICN-24-l (alternate catalyst)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 75% yield, supporting Mn-MOF involvement.

probe_reagents.name: TBAPF6 (non-bromide electrolyte)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% yield — bromide mediator is essential.

probe_reagents.name: NaClO4 / TBAClO4 / TBACl
probe_reagents.observation: <1–18% yield; only Br− supports the catalytic cycle, ruling out generic anion effects.

probe_reagents.name: Anhydrous MeCN (no water)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% epoxide — water is required as the oxygen source for Mn=O regeneration.

10.1016_j.jcat.2025.116252.notes: Mechanism is supported by CV showing Mn(III/IV) and Mn(IV/V) redox waves, in-situ Raman detecting Br-Br stretches (175, 269 cm−1) indicating Br2/polybromide formation, and in-situ FT-IR detecting an 880 cm−1 Mn-O-OH band plus C-O-C epoxide bands (1067, 1230 cm−1). Direct O-atom transfer from Mn(V)=O to alkene (via a putative Mn-oxo-alkene or radical-rebound) is proposed; an alternative purely Br•/Br2 (bromohydrin then base-closure) pathway is implicitly excluded because non-Mn controls give much lower selectivity.

10.1016_j.mencom.2019.07.010.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical synthesis of functionalized cyclopropanes (tetracyanocyclopropanes and dimethyl 2,2-dicyano-3-arylcyclopropane-1,1-dicarboxylates) from alkylidenemalononitriles and CH-acids using pyridinium bromide (PyHBr) as a redox mediator
10.1016_j.mencom.2019.07.010.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide (from PyHBr) to Br2 acts as the mediator that brominates the CH-acid anion; cathodic reduction of pyridinium cation (PyH+) generates H2 and regenerates free pyridine that deprotonates the CH-acid
10.1016_j.mencom.2019.07.010.electron_flow: At the anode, Br- is oxidized to Br2; at the cathode, PyH+ is reduced to free pyridine and H2. Pyridine deprotonates the CH-acid, the resulting carbanion is brominated by Br2 to give an alpha-bromo CH-acid (A); pyridine displaces bromide to form pyridinium ylide B, which performs Michael addition to the alkylidenemalononitrile followed by intramolecular SN2 expelling pyridine to close the cyclopropane.

key_intermediates.name: free pyridine (from cathodic reduction of PyH+)
pyridinium ylide B (Py+-C(-)(X)(X) from intermediate A and pyridine)
Michael adduct of ylide B with alkylidenemalononitrile leading to cyclopropane via intramolecular displacement of pyridine

10.1016_j.mencom.2019.07.010.notes: Mechanism proposed by analogy with prior chemical synthesis of cyclopropanes from pyridinium ylides and benzylidenemalononitriles (ref 13). No radical traps, KIE, CV, or DFT reported; supporting observations are bromine coloration at the anode and H2 evolution at the cathode. PyHBr acts as both bromide source and proton shuttle/mediator, and the reaction is run in undivided cell with graphite anode/iron cathode in MeCN.

10.1002_chem.202005368.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical tri-/difluoromethylation-triggered radical cyclization of N-substituted 2-aryl indole acrylamides coupled with oxidative cleavage of the indole C(2)=C(3) bond to deliver fluoroalkylated 2-(2-acetylphenyl)isoquinoline-1,3-diones.
10.1002_chem.202005368.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na (or Mn(II)/CF2HSO2Na for CF2H) generates the fluoroalkyl radical that adds to the acrylamide alkene; subsequent anodic oxidations advance the cyclized intermediate to a radical cation and then to an iminium, while the cathode reduces water/H+ to H2 generating hydroxide that delivers the two new C=O oxygens.
10.1002_chem.202005368.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- (or Mn(II) for CF2H) to generate a fluoroalkyl radical that adds to the acrylamide alkene and cyclizes onto the indole; a second anodic oxidation/deprotonation gives the closed indoloisoquinoline 6, which is further oxidized at the anode to a radical cation, then sequentially attacked by cathodically generated hydroxide at C3 and C2 to give a trans-diol; deprotonation and C2–C3 bond cleavage afford the diketone product while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical from anodic oxidation of CF3SO2- (loss of SO2)
Carbon radical A from CF3 addition to acrylamide alkene
Cyclohexadienyl-type radical B after radical cyclization onto indole C2
Radical cation D from anodic oxidation of 6
C3-hydroxylated intermediate E (iminium after OH addition)
trans-2,3-dihydroxy intermediate F after second OH addition at C2
Alkoxide/diol G prior to C-C cleavage
Ring-opened intermediate H after C2-C3 bond cleavage

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely quenched; TEMPO–CF3 and TEMPO–CF2H adducts detected, supporting fluoroalkyl radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: Isolated cyclization intermediate 6
probe_reagents.observation: 6 isolated in 65% after 2 h electrolysis and converted to product 3a in 93% under standard conditions, establishing 6 as a key on-cycle intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: N-Boc 2-aryl indole 1r (no acrylamide tether)
probe_reagents.observation: Without Langlois reagent gave indole oxidative cleavage product 7 in 71%, showing the oxidative cleavage step is independent of fluoroalkylation.

probe_reagents.name: H2O-18 isotope labeling
probe_reagents.observation: Doubly 18O-labeled product 3f detected by HRMS, confirming both new carbonyl oxygens originate from water.

probe_reagents.name: N2 atmosphere control
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction under N2 still gave 3a in 76%, ruling out O2 as the oxygen source.

probe_reagents.name: Anhydrous conditions
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product formed without water (Table 1 entry 11), consistent with H2O as O source.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, 2b
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(CF3SO2Na)=1.1 V and Eox(CF2HSO2Na)=0.8 V vs Ag/AgCl, both well below Eox(1a)=1.58 V, so fluoroalkyl precursors are oxidized first.

10.1002_chem.202005368.notes: For the CF2H variant a MnCl2·4H2O co-catalyst is required: anodic oxidation of Mn(II) to a Mn(III)-CF2H species delivers the CF2H radical, while the CF3 pathway is metal-free. Authors explicitly establish the sequence as fluoroalkylation → cyclization → indole C(2)=C(3) oxidative cleavage (not the reverse order).

10.1002_chem.202103728.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical synthesis of substituted isoxazolines via mediator-assisted (chloride/HFIP) coupling of aldoximes with electron-deficient alkenes, proceeding through a stepwise radical pathway rather than a concerted 1,3-dipolar [3+2] cycloaddition.
10.1002_chem.202103728.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of chloride to chlorine radical (mediator) which performs HAT on the aldoxime O-H/C-H to generate a hydroxyimoyl/oximyl radical; a secondary pathway involves direct anodic oxidation of the oxime to its radical cation (HFIP-stabilised), followed by Cl-mediated HAT; cathode reduces an anionic cyclic intermediate (and H+ to H2) to deliver the isoxazoline.
10.1002_chem.202103728.electron_flow: At the anode, chloride is oxidised to Cl• (rate-determining), which abstracts an H atom from the aldoxime to give a hydroxyimoyl radical; this radical adds (SRN2-like) to the Michael acceptor forming a C–C bond, then undergoes 5-exo-tet cyclisation onto the oxime O, and finally cathodic reduction (with H+ → ½H2) furnishes the neutral isoxazoline. A parallel minor pathway: direct anodic oxidation of the oxime to a (HFIP-stabilised) radical cation, then Cl-mediated HAT, addition, cyclisation and cathodic reduction.
10.1002_chem.202103728.rate_determining_step: Anodic oxidation of chloride to chlorine radical (slow, surface-limited; rationalises absence of observable KIE despite a calculated HAT KIE ~5).

key_intermediates.name: chlorine radical (Cl•) from anodic oxidation of Cl-
C-centered radical adduct after addition to acrylate (β-radical α to ester)
anionic cyclised intermediate prior to cathodic reduction
free nitrile oxide (R-C≡N+-O-) — proposed in original hypothesis but disfavoured by DFT (~182 kcal/mol N-chlorination barrier)

probe_reagents.name: deuterated aldoxime [D1]15a
probe_reagents.observation: Small/negligible KIE 1.0–1.5 by independent rates; [D1]15a gave 17a in 70% vs 78% for 15a — consistent with C–H cleavage not being globally rate-limiting (RDS is Cl- oxidation).

probe_reagents.name: dimethyl fumarate (E-alkene)
probe_reagents.observation: Stereoselective (not stereospecific) formation of anti-isoxazoline; anti forms first, syn appears later — supports stepwise (not concerted [3+2]) mechanism.

probe_reagents.name: dimethyl maleate (Z-alkene)
probe_reagents.observation: Also gives predominantly anti-diastereomer; loss of stereospecificity rules out concerted cycloaddition.

probe_reagents.name: Et4NOTs (halide-free electrolyte, no Cl-)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction still proceeds but yield drops from 78% to 24%, implicating direct oxime oxidation as a minor parallel pathway and Cl- as a key mediator.

probe_reagents.name: HFIP omission control
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops to 36% (vs 78%); HFIP stabilises the radical cation intermediate (supported by DFT).

probe_reagents.name: ferrocene (Fc/Fc+ internal reference)
probe_reagents.observation: Used to reference CV; Et4NCl gives reversible couple E1/2 = 0.734 V; oxime shows three irreversible single-electron oxidations; acrylate inactive in window.

probe_reagents.name: stirrer-speed variation
probe_reagents.observation: Rate independent of stirring above 200 rpm — consistent with diffusion-limited but not mass-transport-limited at electrode.

10.1002_chem.202103728.notes: Authors initially hypothesised a classical pathway via electrophilic chlorination of oxime → nitrile oxide → concerted [3+2] cycloaddition (Scheme 2, route B / Scheme 8 path B). Combined CV, KIE, diastereochemistry (loss of stereospecificity from E/Z alkenes), kinetic time-course, COPASI microkinetic modelling, and DFT (M06-2X/Def2TZVP//6-31+G(d,p), SMD MeCN) instead support a stepwise open-shell radical (SRN2-like) mechanism: barriers for [3+2] are ~2.5× higher, and N-chlorination/E2 to nitrile oxide is prohibitively high (~182 kcal/mol). Regioselectivity for 3,5- over 3,4-isoxazoline is governed by distortion (Boys-Bernardi counterpoise) at the SRN2 TS. The syn-diastereomer arises via post-cyclisation epimerisation of the kinetic anti product. Limitation noted by authors: O–H proton-transfer steps were not modelled by DFT.

10.1002_chem.201802167.rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic radical chlorotrifluoromethylative ene-yne cyclization of 1,6-enynes to chlorotrifluoromethylated pyrrolidines using anodically coupled electrolysis with Mn catalysis
10.1002_chem.201802167.electrochemical_role: Two parallel anodic oxidations on the carbon anode: (A) single-electron oxidation of CF3SO2- (from CF3SO2Na) with loss of SO2 to generate CF3 radical, and (B) single-electron oxidation of [Mn(II)]-Cl (formed from Mn(OTf)2 + MgCl2) to a persistent [Mn(III)]-Cl species that serves as a Cl-atom transfer reagent
10.1002_chem.201802167.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- (-SO2) to CF3 radical, which adds regioselectively to the alkene of the 1,6-enyne to give a tertiary C-radical; this radical undergoes 5-exo-dig cyclization onto the tethered alkyne to give an alkenyl radical that is captured by anodically generated [Mn(III)]-Cl via Cl atom transfer, releasing [Mn(II)]-Cl which is re-oxidized at the anode; protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
10.1002_chem.201802167.rate_determining_step: Cl-atom transfer from [Mn(III)]-Cl to the alkenyl radical (stereodetermining; under Curtin-Hammett control for alpha-alkyl alkenyl radicals)

key_intermediates.name: sp3 carbon-centered radical I after CF3 addition to alkene of 1,6-enyne
alkenyl (vinyl) radical II/IV after 5-exo-dig cyclization onto alkyne

probe_reagents.name: no Mn catalyst control
probe_reagents.observation: no desired pyrrolidine formed (entry 5/6), demonstrating essential role of Mn

probe_reagents.name: no electrical current control
probe_reagents.observation: <5% product, confirming electrochemical oxidation required

probe_reagents.name: 2,2'-bipyridine ligand
probe_reagents.observation: adding 20 mol% bpy increased Z/E from 5.1:1 to >19:1, supporting steric control at Mn during Cl transfer

probe_reagents.name: KBr substitution for MgCl2
probe_reagents.observation: bromotrifluoromethylated pyrrolidine 19 obtained as single geometric isomer, consistent with analogous [Mn]-Br atom transfer

probe_reagents.name: X-ray of product 7
probe_reagents.observation: confirmed Z-configuration of alkene, supporting stereochemical model

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of CF3SO2Na and Mn(OTf)2/MgCl2
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(CF3SO2-) = 0.81 V and Eox([Mn(II)]-Cl) = 0.75 V vs Fc+/0; [Mn(III)/Mn(II)]-Cl couple reversible, indicating persistence of Mn-Cl species

10.1002_chem.201802167.notes: Stereoselectivity rationalized by Curtin-Hammett model: for alpha-aryl alkenyl radicals (sp-hybridized due to conjugation), steric shielding by quaternary C directs [Mn(III)]-Cl approach from less congested face, giving Z-selectivity; for alpha-alkyl alkenyl radicals (sp2), pro-(Z) and pro-(E) rotamers interconvert and competing kinetics/thermodynamics erode selectivity. Side pathways noted for substrates 20 (direct alkene/alkyne difunctionalization) and 21 (labile alpha-O C-H abstraction by CF3 radical giving CF3H). 1,7-enyne extension to piperidine 12 shows lower E/Z selectivity due to ring conformational flexibility during Cl transfer.

10.1002_chem.202400280_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical radical selenocyclization of N-alkenylacrylamides with diselenides (PhSeSePh) catalyzed by 4CzIPN to form selenylated pyrrolinones (e.g., 3a).
10.1002_chem.202400280_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation regenerates/oxidizes the photocatalyst (4CzIPN) or directly oxidizes selenide to a selenyl radical; cathode reduces protons to H2 (hydrogen evolution detected), enabling net oxidative selenocyclization under combined photo/electrochemical conditions.
10.1002_chem.202400280_sup_1.electron_flow: Visible light excites 4CzIPN; the excited photocatalyst oxidizes PhSeSePh to generate a PhSe• radical that adds to the acrylamide alkene of 1a, initiating 5-exo-trig radical cyclization onto the pendant alkene. The resulting alkyl radical is oxidized (radical-polar crossover) to a cation that undergoes deprotonation/aromatization to give 3a; the anode regenerates the photocatalyst while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical from PhSe• addition to acrylamide alkene of 1a
cyclized alkyl radical after intramolecular addition onto pendant styrenyl alkene
carbocation after oxidation of cyclized radical (radical-polar crossover)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield partially suppressed (60% A / 40% B / 37% C); radical pathway implicated but not fully shut down.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (0% under all conditions), strongly supporting radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Partial suppression (41/32/46%), consistent with radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: azulene
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction shut down (trace), suggesting triplet-state involvement.

probe_reagents.name: ferrocene (Fc)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace yield; triplet quenching suppresses reaction.

probe_reagents.name: biisobutenyl (2,5-dimethyl-2,4-hexadiene)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace under A, 31% under B; partial triplet quenching.

probe_reagents.name: trans-stilbene/parbenate
probe_reagents.observation: 16% (A), 18% (B); significant triplet quenching.

probe_reagents.name: benzil
probe_reagents.observation: 15% (A), 46% (B); triplet quencher partially suppresses.

10.1002_chem.202400280_sup_1.notes: Mechanistic SI includes quenching experiments (radical and triplet scavengers), reaction profiles, on-off light/current experiments, CV, UV-Vis, fluorescence, H2 detection, Se-scrambling crossover experiments with MeSeSeMe under 395 nm, excited-state redox potential determinations for 4CzIPN, 1a, and 3a in DCE/HFIP (3:1), and DFT calculations. Control experiments confirm 1a/3a do not act as 395-nm photooxidants or energy-transfer photocatalysts, supporting 4CzIPN as the active photocatalyst. Triplet-state involvement supported by quenching with azulene, Fc, benzil, etc.

10.1002_chem.202005368_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-aryl acrylamides bearing 2-aryl indoles followed by oxidative indole cleavage to give CF3-containing isoquinolinediones
10.1002_chem.202005368_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2Na (Langlois reagent) to generate CF3 radical (loss of SO2, e-); subsequent intermediates are further oxidized anodically through several SET steps, with cathode reducing H+/H2O to H2 and regenerating mediator
10.1002_chem.202005368_sup_1.electron_flow: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na releases CF3 radical, which adds to the acrylamide alkene; the resulting C-radical cyclizes onto the 2-aryl indole to give a spiro indolinyl radical, which undergoes sequential anodic SET oxidations and H2O additions to install two oxygens at the indole C2-C3 bond, ultimately cleaving the indole ring to the isoquinolinedione product. The cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical from anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na
alkyl radical A' after CF3 addition to acrylamide alkene
spirocyclic indolyl radical B' after radical cyclization onto indole C3
indolenium cation C' after anodic oxidation of B'
radical cation D' from further anodic SET
hydroxylated adduct E' from H2O addition to radical cation
dihydroxy intermediate F'/G' en route to C-C cleavage
ring-opened diketo/ortho-acyl amide product 5 (3a) after oxidative C-C cleavage of indole

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a formed in 0%; TEMPO-CF3 adduct detected by HRMS ([M+H]+ calc 226.1408, found 226.1427), supporting CF3 radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: HRMS of product from reaction in H2-18O shows incorporation of one and two 18O atoms (m/z 468.0851 mono-18O and 470.0893 di-18O alongside 466.0818 unlabeled), confirming both carbonyl oxygens of cleavage product derive from water

probe_reagents.name: Time-course / reaction profile of 1a
probe_reagents.observation: 3aa' accumulates early (up to ~47% at 4 h) then decays as 3a grows, indicating 3aa' is an on-pathway intermediate that is further oxidized/cleaved to 3a

10.1002_chem.202005368_sup_1.notes: Mechanism proposed for difluoroalkylation variant in Figure S6 is drawn analogously (CF2H instead of CF3) and invokes a Mn(II)/Mn(III) mediator in one cycle branch; alternative would be direct anodic SET without mediator. Spiro intermediate 3aa' is competent and converts to 3a, supporting an indole oxidative C-C cleavage by sequential water addition/SET rather than a single dioxygenation event.

10.1002_chem.202400280.rxn_class_summary: Electrophotocatalytic and electrochemical cascade selenylation/cyclization of 3-aza-1,5-dienes with diaryl diselenides to give 3-selenomethyl-4-pyrrolin-2-ones under three protocols (4CzIPN photoelectrocatalysis, PC-free direct photolysis, or NHPI-mediated electrolysis).
10.1002_chem.202400280.electrochemical_role: Method A: anode oxidizes the cyclized C-radical II to cation III (with EnT from 4CzIPN* generating PhSe• from (PhSe)2); Method B: anode oxidizes radical II while 395 nm light homolyzes (PhSe)2 directly; Method C: anode oxidizes NHPI to NHPI•+ which oxidizes (PhSe)2 to PhSe• and PhSe+; cathode reduces H+ to H2 in all cases.
10.1002_chem.202400280.electron_flow: In method A, 4CzIPN* transfers triplet energy to (PhSe)2 giving 2a* which homolyzes to PhSe•; PhSe• adds to the acrylamide alkene of 1a, the resulting C-radical cyclizes onto the enamide to give radical II, which is oxidized to cation III by 4CzIPN•+/anode/NHPI•+ depending on method, followed by deprotonation to product and cathodic H+→H2. A parallel ionic pathway proceeds via PhSe+ + alkene → seleniranium IV → cation III.

key_intermediates.name: β-selenyl carbon radical I (from PhSe• addition to acrylamide alkene)
Cyclized α-amido C-centered radical II (pyrrolinone radical)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction not fully suppressed (yields 60/40/37% under A/B/C), indicating radical pathway is operative but not exclusive.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a completely suppressed under all three conditions.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-Diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Partial inhibition; phenylselanyl-DPE adduct 10 isolated in 9–15%, confirming PhSe• generation.

probe_reagents.name: 4-Iodoanisole
probe_reagents.observation: PhSe-anisole adduct detected by GC-MS upon 395 nm irradiation of 2a, confirming PhSe• under PC-free photolysis.

probe_reagents.name: Azulene
probe_reagents.observation: Triplet quencher; suppresses reaction under A and B, supporting EnT/triplet involvement.

probe_reagents.name: Ferrocene (Fc)
probe_reagents.observation: Triplet quencher; trace product under A and B.

probe_reagents.name: Biisobutenyl
probe_reagents.observation: Triplet quencher; trace under A, 31% under B.

probe_reagents.name: Parbenate
probe_reagents.observation: Triplet quencher; 16% (A), 18% (B), partial inhibition.

probe_reagents.name: Benzil
probe_reagents.observation: Triplet quencher; 15% (A), 46% (B).

probe_reagents.name: PhSeCl
probe_reagents.observation: Reacts with 1a in absence of light/electricity to give 3a in 88%, supporting PhSe+ / seleniranium ionic pathway.

probe_reagents.name: PhSeH
probe_reagents.observation: Affords 3a under A/B/C in 24/60/8% over 9 h, supporting selenolate/radical pathways.

probe_reagents.name: Diselenide scrambling ((PhSe)2 + (MeSe)2)
probe_reagents.observation: Greatly accelerated under 395 nm vs dark, supporting photohomolysis of Se–Se bond.

10.1002_chem.202400280.notes: Three mechanisms proposed in parallel for methods A/B/C. Favored pathway under A is triplet–triplet energy transfer from 4CzIPN* to (PhSe)2 rather than SET (SET from 2a to 4CzIPN* is thermodynamically unfavorable: E_red*(4CzIPN*) = 1.51 V vs Ep/2(2a) = 1.52 V). DFT triplet energies (4CzIPN 46.1, 2a 32.7 kcal/mol) support exergonic EnT. Both radical (PhSe• addition/cyclization/oxidation) and ionic (PhSe+/seleniranium) pathways may operate concurrently, as TEMPO does not fully suppress reaction and PhSeCl directly gives product. NHPI in method C functions as anodic electron shuttle (not cathodic) based on CV. Cathodic H2 evolution detected by gas detector closes the redox balance.

10.1002_chem.201404078.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular aminooxygenation of unactivated alkenes using tethered carbamates/amides as nitrogen sources and TEMPO as the oxygen trap, giving trans-1,2-aminoalkoxyamines.
10.1002_chem.201404078.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation: TEMPO is oxidized at the RVC anode to the oxoammonium cation, which oxidizes the carbamate/amide N-H to generate a nitrogen-centered radical (with H2O/base accepting the proton); cathode reduces water/H+ to H2.
10.1002_chem.201404078.electron_flow: At the RVC anode, TEMPO is oxidized to its oxoammonium salt, which abstracts/oxidizes the carbamate or amide N-H (with base/H2O removing the proton) to give a nitrogen-centered radical; this N-radical undergoes 5-exo-trig cyclization onto the tethered unactivated alkene to give a carbon-centered radical, which is trapped by a second equivalent of TEMPO to afford the trans-aminooxygenation product. The cathode evolves H2.

key_intermediates.name: TEMPO oxoammonium cation (mediator, generated at anode)
carbon-centered radical after 5-exo-trig cyclization onto tethered alkene

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Trapped the cyclized carbon radical, delivering the alkoxyamine product as a single trans-diastereomer (e.g. 2a in 70% yield).

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl/allyl radical-clock substrate 9 (cinnamyl-type carbamate)
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis of 9 gave ring-opened/rearranged TEMPO adduct 10 (52%), consistent with a discrete C-centered radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: preformed TEMPO oxoammonium tetrafluoroborate (11)
probe_reagents.observation: With Cs2CO3 as base and 11 (2 equiv) the aminooxygenation product 2a was obtained in 62% yield without electrolysis, supporting oxoammonium as the active oxidant/mediator; no reaction without base or with Na2CO3.

10.1002_chem.201404078.notes: X-ray structures of 2h and 2q confirm trans-diastereoselectivity of the cyclization. The N-radical generation is proposed to proceed via the TEMPO/oxoammonium redox couple acting as an anodic mediator rather than direct SET oxidation of the carbamate N-H; the stoichiometric experiment with oxoammonium salt 11 supports this mediator pathway. Direct anodic oxidation of the N-H substrate cannot be fully excluded as a minor pathway.

10.1016_S1872-2067(23)64510-3_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative coupling of dimethyl 2-(4-methoxybenzyl)malonate with alkenes/alkynes proceeding via an anodically generated benzylic/malonyl radical that undergoes radical-polar crossover and ipso-cyclization onto the p-methoxyaryl ring with water as the oxygen source
10.1016_S1872-2067(23)64510-3_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the malonate substrate (mediated by ferrocene/ferrocenium as a redox mediator) generates a tertiary C-centered (malonyl/benzylic) radical; subsequent oxidation of a cyclohexadienyl radical intermediate to a cation enables nucleophilic trapping by H2O
10.1016_S1872-2067(23)64510-3_sup_1.electron_flow: Ferrocene is oxidized at the anode to Fc+, which oxidizes the malonate 1a to a tertiary C-radical; this radical adds to the alkene/alkyne, the resulting radical undergoes ipso-cyclization onto the p-methoxyaryl ring, and the cyclohexadienyl radical is further oxidized to a cation that is trapped by H2(18)O to deliver the spirocyclohexadienone with 18O incorporation; H2 is evolved at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: no radical trap (homo-coupling control)
probe_reagents.observation: Homo-coupled dimer of 1a isolated in 33% yield, supporting a C-radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 2-methylthiophene
probe_reagents.observation: Radical addition/coupling product with thiophene isolated in 39% yield, consistent with a free C-radical

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Desired cyclization product not detected (n.d.); reaction suppressed, indicating a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Desired product not detected (n.d.); radical intermediates are intercepted

probe_reagents.name: H2(18)O
probe_reagents.observation: Product shows 18O:16O > 20:1 incorporation in the cyclohexadienone carbonyl, demonstrating water as the O source

10.1016_S1872-2067(23)64510-3_sup_1.notes: Mechanism supported by (i) isolation of homo-coupling dimer, (ii) thiophene radical trapping, (iii) complete suppression with TEMPO and BHT, and (iv) >20:1 18O incorporation from H2(18)O establishing water as the oxygen source in the spirocyclohexadienone product. Ferrocene functions as a single-electron-transfer mediator at the anode.

10.1016_S1872-2067(23)64510-3.rxn_class_summary: Cp2Fe-mediated electrochemical regioselective ortho-(4+2) cyclization and ipso-(3+2) spirocyclization of benzylic malonates with alkenes and alkynes to give tetrahydronaphthalenes and spirocarbocycles
10.1016_S1872-2067(23)64510-3.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+ mediator, which oxidizes the deprotonated malonate anion via single-electron transfer to generate a malonyl C-radical; cathode reduces MeOH to MeO- and H2
10.1016_S1872-2067(23)64510-3.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+ while cathode reduces MeOH to MeO- + H2; MeO- deprotonates the benzylic malonate, and Cp2Fe+ oxidizes the anion by SET to a malonyl radical that adds to alkene/alkyne. The resulting alkyl radical undergoes ortho-(4+2) cyclization (giving cyclohexadienyl radical, then anodic oxidation/deprotonation to product), whereas the vinyl radical undergoes ipso-(3+2) spirocyclization followed by SET oxidation to a cation that is captured by MeO-/H2O to give the dienone.
10.1016_S1872-2067(23)64510-3.rate_determining_step: SET oxidation/radical addition step: TS-1 (18.8 kcal/mol) for alkene pathway and TS-4 (17.1 kcal/mol) for alkyne pathway are the highest barriers; cyclization regiochemistry controlled by TS-3 vs TS-2 (alkyl, ortho favored by 2.0 kcal/mol) and TS-5 vs TS-6 (vinyl, ipso favored by 5.7 kcal/mol)

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical adduct II (from addition to alkene)
vinyl radical adduct III (from addition to alkyne)
cyclohexadienyl radical IV (after ortho-(4+2) cyclization onto aryl)
spirocyclic cyclohexadienyl radical V (after ipso-(3+2) cyclization)
spirocyclic cation VI (after SET oxidation of V)
dimethoxy spirocyclic intermediate VII (MeO- trapping of VI)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed with 2.0 equiv, supporting radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed with 2.0 equiv

probe_reagents.name: 2-methylthiophene
probe_reagents.observation: Captured the malonyl radical giving 5-thiophenyl adduct in 39% yield, confirming carbon radical

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: 18O exclusively incorporated into carbonyl of spiro product 22 (18O:16O > 20:1), indicating carbonyl O comes from water, not methoxy/MeOH

probe_reagents.name: Cp2FePF6 (stoichiometric)
probe_reagents.observation: As stoichiometric chemical oxidant, gave spiro 22 in 15% yield, supporting Cp2Fe+ as active oxidant

probe_reagents.name: no electricity control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed without current

probe_reagents.name: no Cp2Fe control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed without ferrocene mediator

probe_reagents.name: absence of alkene/alkyne
probe_reagents.observation: Homo-coupling dimer of malonate formed in 33% yield, consistent with malonyl radical

10.1016_S1872-2067(23)64510-3.notes: Regiodivergence is substrate-controlled: alkyl (sp3) radical from alkene addition prefers ortho-(4+2) cyclization (interaction energy dominant, ΔΔEint = -10.2), while vinyl (sp2) radical from alkyne addition prefers ipso-(3+2) spirocyclization (distortion energy dominant). 7-Cl-dimethoxy intermediate (product 28) was isolated rather than the oxo-product, providing evidence for the cation VI / dimethoxy VII pathway prior to hydrolysis to dienone. Cp2Fe acts as outer-sphere SET mediator, confirmed by CV showing catalytic current only with Cp2Fe + 1a + NaOMe.

10.3390_catal13030631.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical thiocyanation/cyclization cascade of N-(2-alkenylaryl)amides with NH4SCN to give 4-thiocyanatomethyl-4H-benzo[d][1,3]oxazines
10.3390_catal13030631.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of thiocyanate anion to thiocyanate radical (via (SCN)2), which adds to the alkene; the resulting C-radical is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; cathode reduces protons to H2
10.3390_catal13030631.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes SCN- to SCN• (through (SCN)2); SCN• adds to the alkene of 1a to give C-radical I, which is further oxidized at the anode (SET) to carbocation II; intramolecular nucleophilic attack of the amide oxygen and deprotonation deliver oxazine 3a, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

key_intermediates.name: C-centered benzylic radical from SCN• addition to alkene (intermediate I)
benzylic carbocation after SET oxidation (intermediate II)
protonated oxazine intermediate after intramolecular O-attack (intermediate III)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3a formed, indicating a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene
probe_reagents.observation: SCN-DPE adduct 3a' detected by GC-MS, confirming SCN radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a and NH4SCN with/without H2SO4
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(2) = 1.48 V vs Eox(1a) = 2.27 V; SCN- is preferentially oxidized; with acid, secondary oxidation of SCN species (to (SCN)3-/(SCN)2-) is observed

10.3390_catal13030631.notes: Acid (H2SO4) is essential — without it CV shows no 1a oxidation and yield drops sharply; acid promotes secondary oxidation of SCN species, analogous to halide (SCN-/(SCN)3-/(SCN)2-) behavior. Mechanism is the only one proposed; no DFT or KIE reported.

science.abn1395.rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic decarboxylative sp2-sp3 cross-coupling of NHPI redox-active esters with vinyl iodides using Ni catalysis on Ag-nanoparticle-functionalized cathode for modular terpene synthesis
science.abn1395.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction at Ag-nanoparticle-modified RVC cathode reduces Ni catalyst and NHPI redox-active ester (RAE) to generate alkyl radical via decarboxylation; sacrificial Mg anode is oxidized; Ag-NP layer lowers overpotential to prevent Ni catalyst overreduction and electrode passivation
science.abn1395.electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ni(II)(bpy) to low-valent Ni(I); Ni(I) undergoes oxidative addition with vinyl iodide; cathode also reduces NHPI-RAE leading to decarboxylative fragmentation to alkyl radical, which is captured by Ni(II)-alkenyl to form Ni(III)-alkenyl-alkyl that reductively eliminates the sp2-sp3 product; Mg anode supplies electrons sacrificially. The Ag-NP layer modulates electrode potential to avoid overreduction and passivation.
science.abn1395.rate_determining_step: diffusion/reduction of RAE at the electrode surface (modulated by Ag-NP layer); induction period corresponds to silver electrodeposition prior to productive coupling

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical (R2•) from decarboxylation of RAE
Ni(II)-alkenyl (from oxidative addition of vinyl iodide)
Ni(I)-alkenyl intermediate (from reduction of Ni(II/III) alkenyl)
passivated/deactivated Ni species on electrode surface
Ag nanoparticles on RVC cathode (electrodeposited from AgNO3 + halide)

probe_reagents.name: AgNO3 additive (omission control)
probe_reagents.observation: Without AgNO3, yield drops from 52% to 20%; cathode potential shifts from -1.15 V to -1.66 V vs Ag/AgCl (510 mV more negative), indicating Ag-NP lowers overpotential

probe_reagents.name: LiCl (halide source control)
probe_reagents.observation: Halide required to form photosensitive AgCl that decomposes to Ag-NP; without halide source nanoparticle formation is poor; prolonged stir before electrolysis causes photodecomposition and lowers yield to 17%

probe_reagents.name: Methyl ester vinyl iodide (40) vs free acid vinyl iodide (5)
probe_reagents.observation: RDE shows passivation by Ni-alkenyl with 40 but minimal passivation with 5; with halo-acid 5 + RAE 7, second reduction wave at -1.48 V persists, attributed to Ni(II/III)-alkenyl to Ni(I)-alkenyl reduction (deactivation pathway)

probe_reagents.name: Benzyl chloride (BnCl) reduction probe
probe_reagents.observation: 370 mV positive shift in reduction potential on Ag-NP-modified electrode vs bare GC, confirming Ag-NP lowers overpotential for analyte reduction

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric Mg reductant (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Without current, chemical reduction with Mg gives notable improvement only with Ag added, confirming electrochemistry is critical

science.abn1395.notes: Ag-NP layer plays four distinct roles per proposed mechanism (Fig. 4A): (I) prevents Ni catalyst adsorption/decomposition on electrode, (II) lowers overpotential below catalyst decomposition threshold, (III) slows diffusion/mass transport of RAE to electrode (Levich plot shows diffusion coefficient decreased by factor of 2), and (IV) prevents sequential overreduction of Ni(II/III)-alkenyl to deactivated Ni(I)-alkenyl. SEM/TEM/EDS confirm 10-100 nm Ag nanoparticles deposited in situ from AgNO3 + halide via AgCl intermediate. Comproportionation of Ni species also invoked in cycle. Electrode surface characterization (SEM, TEM, anodic stripping voltammetry) provides structural evidence; RDE voltammetry across multiple components provides kinetic/mechanistic basis.

10.1016_j.jorganchem.2017.02.002.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical hydrogenation and reductive coupling of alkenes and alkynes catalyzed by a B12 model complex (heptamethyl cobyrinate) in the presence of TFA
10.1016_j.jorganchem.2017.02.002.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of Co(II)/Co(III) B12 model complex to Co(I), which is protonated by TFA to form a Co(III)-H species that delivers a hydrogen radical to the alkene/alkyne substrate
10.1016_j.jorganchem.2017.02.002.electron_flow: Cathode reduces the Co(II/III) B12 model to Co(I), which is protonated by TFA to give a Co(III)-H with H• character; H• transfers to the beta-position of the alkene/alkyne to generate an alpha-benzylic radical that then dimerizes, disproportionates, abstracts H•, or (for electron-poor substrates) is further reduced to a carbanion that is protonated.

key_intermediates.name: Co(I) species of B12 model complex (heptamethyl cobyrinate)
Co(III)-H hydride intermediate with H-radical character
benzylic carbanion (for R = electron-withdrawing group such as CO2Et or Br)
carbene from alpha-bromo benzylic carbanion after Br- elimination

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline-N-oxide)
probe_reagents.observation: ESR detection of DMPO-H adduct (g=2.009, AN=14.9 G, AH=19.7 G) and DMPO-C carbon-centered radical adduct (g=2.008, AN=15.0 G, AH=21.4 G) during electrolysis of styrene

probe_reagents.name: TFA-d1 (deuterated trifluoroacetic acid)
probe_reagents.observation: DMPO-H ESR signal disappeared when TFA-d1 was used in place of TFA, confirming the trapped H originates from the acid via the Co-H intermediate

probe_reagents.name: control without acid
probe_reagents.observation: No reduced product formed in the absence of TFA for all alkene/alkyne substrates, confirming Co-H requires protonation

10.1016_j.jorganchem.2017.02.002.notes: For styrene derivatives with electron-withdrawing alpha-substituents (R = CO2Et, Br), a second cathodic reduction of the benzylic radical to a carbanion is invoked; with R = Br, elimination of Br- gives a transient carbene that couples with another benzylic carbanion to deliver E/Z 2,3-diphenylbutene. Three competing pathways for ethylbenzene formation from the benzylic radical are proposed: disproportionation, H• abstraction, and reaction with a second Co-H. Phenylacetylene is proposed to be reduced first to styrene, then further to 2,3-diphenylbutanes (racemic/meso) and ethylbenzene at -1.2 V vs Ag/AgCl.

10.1007_s11172-006-0008-6.rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic cyclopropanation of arylidene- or alkylidenemalononitriles with dialkyl malonates in alcoholic NaBr/NaI to give 3-substituted 2,2-dicyanocyclopropane-1,1-dicarboxylates.
10.1007_s11172-006-0008-6.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of halide (Br- or I-) to molecular halogen (Hal2) which serves as the chemical mediator; cathodic reduction of alcohol generates alkoxide base that deprotonates the malonate CH-acid. Substrate itself is not directly oxidized at the electrode.
10.1007_s11172-006-0008-6.electron_flow: At the anode, halide is oxidized to Hal2 (2 Hal- - 2e -> Hal2); at the cathode, alcohol is reduced to alkoxide and H2.
