Alkoxide deprotonates dialkyl malonate to its carbanion, which undergoes Michael addition to the arylidene/alkylidenemalononitrile to give anion A; A is halogenated by Hal2 and then closes the cyclopropane ring via intramolecular SN2 with displacement of Hal- on action of alkoxide.
10.1007_s11172-006-0008-6.rate_determining_step: Deprotonation of the CH-acid and/or Michael addition of the malonate anion to the activated olefin (NaBr preferred over NaI because bromomalonate is a stronger CH-acid and adds faster).

key_intermediates.name: molecular halogen Hal2 (Br2 or I2) generated at anode
alkoxide ion (MeO- or EtO-) generated at cathode
intermediate anion B from malononitrile + arylidenemalonate (Scheme 7)
cyclic tetraester 6 (side product from malonate + arylidenemalonate)
dimethyl 3,3-dicyano-2-phenylpropane-1,1-dicarboxylate (4, open-chain Michael adduct detected at low charge)

probe_reagents.name: reduced charge (0.7 F mol-1) electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: Open-chain Michael adduct 4 (dimethyl 3,3-dicyano-2-phenylpropane-1,1-dicarboxylate) detected by 1H NMR in 25% along with cyclopropane 3a (32%), supporting stepwise Michael then halogenation/ring-closure.

probe_reagents.name: NaI vs NaBr mediator comparison
probe_reagents.observation: NaBr gives higher yields than NaI for malonate substrates (e.g. 75 vs 63%), opposite to tetracarboxylate ring closure; attributed to faster bromomalonate formation/deprotonation and faster Michael addition.

probe_reagents.name: temperature variation (-20 to +20 C)
probe_reagents.observation: 0 C optimal for malonate + alkylidenemalononitrile; raising T causes oligomerization and lower yields.

10.1007_s11172-006-0008-6.notes: An alternative/parallel route (Scheme 5) is proposed in which malonate is first brominated at the anode to bromomalonate, deprotonated to bromomalonate anion, then adds to the activated olefin followed by base-induced ring closure; this rationalizes why NaBr outperforms NaI here (opposite trend to the tetracarboxylate cyclization, ref 18). A side pathway via malononitrile-anion route (Schemes 6-8) gives lower yields owing to oligomerization of malononitrile and formation of cyclic tetraester 6 from CH(COOMe)2- + arylidenemalonate.

10.1126_science.adg2866.rxn_class_summary: Enantioselective electrochemical cobalt-catalyzed aryl C-H activation/annulation with alkenes, alkynes, and alcohols coupled to the hydrogen evolution reaction, accessing C-stereogenic, atropoisomeric, and P-stereogenic products.
10.1126_science.adg2866.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Co(II) to higher-valent Co(III)/Co(IV) species enables C-H metalation and oxidation-induced reductive elimination; cathodic reduction of protons generates H2 as the only stoichiometric byproduct (HER replaces chemical oxidant).
10.1126_science.adg2866.electron_flow: Co(II)/CPA1 coordinates substrate and undergoes inner-sphere C-H cobaltation to give Co(III)-C1; anodic single-electron oxidation produces a higher-valent Co(IV) species that, after alkene/alkyne migratory insertion, undergoes oxidation-induced reductive elimination to form the C-C/C-N/C-O bond and release Co(II); protons are reduced at the cathode to evolve H2.
10.1126_science.adg2866.rate_determining_step: Electron transfer (anodic oxidation of the cobaltacycle) is rate-determining; C-H cleavage and migratory insertion are not rate-limiting (kH/kD ≈ 1.0; no order in maleimide on insertion step).

key_intermediates.name: Cyclometallated Co(III) C-H-activated complex C1 (isolated and characterized)
Co(II)-CPA1 chiral phosphate-coordinated catalyst resting state
Higher-valent Co(IV) (or Co(III)/Co(IV)/Co(II) manifold) intermediate generated by anodic oxidation
Migratory insertion alkyl-Co intermediate (post alkene insertion)

probe_reagents.name: [D]5-benzamide (deuterated substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Parallel-reaction kH/kD ≈ 1.0, indicating C-H cleavage is not rate-determining.

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric Co(III)-C1 cyclometallated complex
probe_reagents.observation: Isolated and fully characterized under galvanostatic conditions; required electricity for efficient productive turnover, supporting oxidation-induced reductive elimination.

probe_reagents.name: Current on/off (galvanostatic vs no electricity)
probe_reagents.observation: No product formation without electricity; product formation correlates directly with current, identifying electron transfer as rate-determining.

probe_reagents.name: Variable-concentration kinetics (maleimide)
probe_reagents.observation: Logarithmic plot of initial rates vs maleimide concentration showed no marked correlation; migratory insertion is fast and not rate-limiting.

probe_reagents.name: Nonlinear effect study (varying ee of CPA1)
probe_reagents.observation: Linear relationship between CPA1 ee and product ee; rules out self-association of acid or multiligand catalysts.

probe_reagents.name: Ag2CO3 as chemical oxidant (replacing electricity)
probe_reagents.observation: Very low enantioselectivity obtained, highlighting that electrochemical conditions are essential for high ee.

10.1126_science.adg2866.notes: High-resolution MS and UV-vis spectroelectrochemistry support a Co(III)/Co(IV)/Co(II) manifold with direct coordination of CPA1 at the cobalt center (inner-sphere enantioinduction) rather than outer-sphere chiral induction. HRMS and S2-S4 figures identify cobalt-CPA1 intermediates. Decagram-scale reaction proceeded with 89% Faradaic efficiency. Stainless steel cathode also viable. The paper does not explicitly distinguish Co(III)/Co(I) vs Co(III)/Co(IV)/Co(II); the latter is favored by the authors.

10.1126_science.abn1395.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical decarboxylative sp2-sp3 cross-coupling of redox-active esters (NHPI esters of carboxylic acids) with vinyl iodides using nickel/bipyridine catalysis on Ag-nanoparticle functionalized reticulated vitreous carbon (RVC) cathode with sacrificial Mg anode to assemble polyene/terpene precursors.
10.1126_science.abn1395.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction at Ag-NP-functionalized RVC: (i) initial cathodic reduction of Ag(I) (from AgNO3 + halide) deposits Ag nanoparticles on the RVC surface; (ii) cathodic reduction of Ni(II)(bpy) to lower-valent Ni species which engages the NHPI redox-active ester to generate an alkyl radical via fragmentation, and reduces Ni(III)-alkenyl intermediates to Ni(I)-alkenyl; sacrificial Mg anode is oxidized to Mg(2+).
10.1126_science.abn1395.electron_flow: AgNO3 + halide generates AgCl which is cathodically reduced (0–0.3 F/mol) to deposit Ag nanoparticles on the RVC; thereafter (0.3–2.3 F/mol) the cathode reduces Ni(II)(bpy) to Ni(I), which undergoes oxidative addition with the vinyl iodide and captures the alkyl radical released by single-electron reduction/fragmentation of the NHPI ester, then reductive elimination from Ni(III) delivers the sp2–sp3 product while Mg is oxidized at the anode.

key_intermediates.name: NHPI redox-active ester (RAE) of carboxylic acid
alkyl radical (R•) from decarboxylative fragmentation of RAE
Ni(II)(bpy)-alkenyl (from oxidative addition of vinyl iodide)
Ag(0) nanoparticles on RVC cathode (electrode modifier)

probe_reagents.name: AgNO3 omission (control)
probe_reagents.observation: Without AgNO3 yield drops to 20% (vs 52% with 0.3 equiv AgNO3); induction period correlates with AgNO3 loading, indicating Ag(I) reduction precedes coupling.

probe_reagents.name: Current off / stoichiometric Mg reductant
probe_reagents.observation: No product without current using only Mg as reductant unless Ag is added; confirms electrochemistry is essential.

probe_reagents.name: Pure Ag electrode / Ag-plated electrode (no AgNP)
probe_reagents.observation: Did not work well, whereas Ag-nanoparticles drop-cast on RVC reproduced high yield; identifies nanoparticulate Ag (not bulk Ag) as the active electrode modifier.

probe_reagents.name: LiCl omission / halide source variation
probe_reagents.observation: Halide required to form photosensitive AgCl that decomposes to Ag NPs; pre-stirring AgNO3+LiCl in light before electrolysis gave only 17% yield (photodecomposition diminishes NP formation).

probe_reagents.name: Methyl ester vinyl iodide 40 vs free-acid vinyl iodide 5 (RDE)
probe_reagents.observation: Vinyl iodide 40 + NiCl2(bpy) shows passivation on cycling; with free-acid 5, minimal passivation but a second reduction wave at –1.48 V vs Ag/AgCl appears, assigned to Ni(II/III)-alkenyl → Ni(I)-alkenyl over-reduction (deactivation).

probe_reagents.name: Benzyl chloride reduction on AgNP-GC
probe_reagents.observation: 370 mV positive shift in BnCl reduction potential on AgNP-modified electrode, confirming Ag-NP lowers overpotential of electron-transfer events at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: Doubled catalyst loading (NiCl2/bpy 20 mol%) without AgNO3
probe_reagents.observation: Only 13% yield improvement vs 32% improvement with AgNO3, showing Ag effect is not simply more catalyst turnover.

probe_reagents.name: Constant-potential electrolysis at –1.15 V vs Ag/AgCl without AgNO3
probe_reagents.observation: 10% increase in yield, supporting that limiting cathode potential (as Ag does) avoids over-reduction/passivation.

probe_reagents.name: Intermittent +1.5 V oxidative excursions
probe_reagents.observation: Restored catalytic current and gave 11% yield boost, consistent with stripping of passivating Ni deposits.

10.1126_science.abn1395.notes: The authors articulate four distinct effects of the Ag-NP layer on the cathode (Fig. 4A): (I) adsorption of NiCl2(bpy) catalyst onto Ag-NPs slows electrode passivation; (II) Ag-NPs lower the operative cathode overpotential (–1.15 V vs –1.66 V Ag/AgCl, a 510 mV shift) keeping potential above catalyst-decomposition threshold; (III) Ag-NP film slows diffusion/mass transport of RAE to the electrode (Levich diffusion coefficient halved); (IV) lower overpotential prevents a second, deleterious reduction (Ni(II/III)-alkenyl → Ni(I)-alkenyl) that causes catalyst deactivation via dimerization/aggregation of Ni(I) species. The homogeneous Ni cycle follows literature radical-chain decarboxylative cross-coupling precedent (Ni(I)/Ni(III) or Ni(0)/Ni(II)/Ni(III)); the novel mechanistic contribution is the role of in-situ-formed Ag nanoparticles as an electrode functionalization that engenders selectivity analogous to electroanalytical sensors. Surface characterization (SEM, TEM, EDS, anodic stripping voltammetry) confirms Ag-NP formation; control with pre-photodecomposed AgCl gives no NPs and low yield.

10.1126_science.adg2866_1.rxn_class_summary: Enantioselective electrochemical cobalt-catalyzed aryl C-H activation enabling C-C, C-N, and C-O annulations of carboxylic amides and P-stereogenic phosphinamides with alkenes/alkynes, coupled to cathodic hydrogen evolution (HER).
10.1126_science.adg2866_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of cyclometalated Co(III) species (Co(III)-CPA1 intermediate, complex C1) to higher oxidation states (Co(IV)/Co(III)/Co(II) manifold), enabling oxidation-induced reductive elimination; cathode performs HER releasing H2 as the sole stoichiometric byproduct.
10.1126_science.adg2866_1.electron_flow: Co(II)/Co(III) precatalyst with chiral phosphoric acid CPA1 performs enantioselective C-H metalation of the amide substrate to give cyclometalated Co(III)-C1; the anode oxidizes this Co(III) intermediate to Co(IV) (after alkene migratory insertion), triggering oxidation-induced C-N/C-O/C-C reductive elimination and releasing Co(II); protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
10.1126_science.adg2866_1.rate_determining_step: Electron transfer (anodic oxidation of the Co intermediate) is rate-limiting; C-H cleavage is not rate-determining (kH/kD ≈ 1.0) and migratory insertion is relatively fast (no order dependence on maleimide).

key_intermediates.name: Cyclometalated Co(III) aryl complex C1 (C-H-activated Co(III)-CPA1)
Co(III)-CPA1 chiral phosphate-bound cobalt species (inner-sphere enantio-determining)
Higher-valent Co(IV) intermediate from anodic oxidation
Alkene-inserted Co alkyl intermediate (post migratory insertion)
Co(II) species (post reductive elimination, re-oxidized at anode)

probe_reagents.name: Deuterated benzamide [D]5-1
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 1.0 from parallel reactions, indicating C-H cleavage is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: Isolated Co(III) complex C1
probe_reagents.observation: Stoichiometrically prepared, isolated, and fully characterized; electricity required for productive turnover to product, supporting oxidation-induced reductive elimination

probe_reagents.name: Variable maleimide concentration (kinetic order)
probe_reagents.observation: Logarithmic plot of initial rates showed no marked correlation with maleimide concentration; migratory insertion considered relatively fast

probe_reagents.name: Variable current (product formation monitoring)
probe_reagents.observation: Direct correlation between current and product formation rate; electron transfer identified as rate-determining factor

probe_reagents.name: Nonlinear effect study with scalemic CPA1
probe_reagents.observation: Linear relationship between ee of CPA1 and ee of product 3, ruling out self-association/multiligand catalysts; single chiral acid coordinates Co

probe_reagents.name: Silver carbonate (chemical oxidant control)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave very low enantioselectivity, highlighting electrochemistry's role in enantiocontrol

probe_reagents.name: No-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formation in absence of electricity, confirming electrochemical requirement

probe_reagents.name: Headspace GC analysis
probe_reagents.observation: Molecular H2 detected, confirming HER as the cathodic half-reaction

10.1126_science.adg2866_1.notes: Authors favor an inner-sphere mechanism in which CPA1 directly coordinates to cobalt (rather than outer-sphere enantioinduction), supported by linear NLE and HRMS/UV-vis detection of cobalt-CPA1 intermediates traversing a Co(III)/Co(IV)/Co(II) manifold. Cathode material can be replaced with stainless steel without loss of efficiency. Faradaic efficiency up to 89% on decagram scale. Natural sunlight can power the cell. DFT used primarily for atropostability (rotational barrier of axially chiral product 36) rather than core mechanism.

10.1126_science.abf2974.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical halogen-shuttle (e-shuttle) reaction that transfers Br2 or Cl2 from vicinal dihaloalkane donors (e.g., 1,2-dibromoethane, polychloroalkanes including Lindane) to alkene acceptors to give 1,2-dihalogenated products with extrusion of ethylene.
10.1126_science.abf2974.electrochemical_role: Paired electrolysis: cathodic 2e- reduction of the vicinal dihaloalkane donor cleaves two C-X bonds releasing 2 X- and ethylene; anodic 2e- oxidation regenerates X+ (as bromonium or, for Cl2, as Mn(III)-Cl from a Mn(II) mediator) which halogenates the alkene acceptor.
10.1126_science.abf2974.electron_flow: Cathode delivers 2 e- to the vicinal dihaloalkane donor, cleaving two C-X bonds to release 2 X- and ethylene; the X- is oxidized at the anode (directly for Br-, or via a Mn(II)/Mn(III) mediator for Cl-) to an X+ equivalent that adds across the alkene acceptor, with a second X- closing the addition to give the 1,2-dihalide product.
10.1126_science.abf2974.rate_determining_step: Second cathodic electron transfer to the dihaloalkane donor (irreversible CV wave at ca. -2.7 V vs Fc, coupled to extrusion of ethylene + bromide)

key_intermediates.name: bromonium ion / Br+ equivalent on alkene

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-dibromoethane (donor probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Acts as Br2 donor; no HBr elimination products observed, supporting C-Br homolytic/2e- cleavage with ethylene extrusion rather than E2.

probe_reagents.name: 2,3-dibromobutane (donor probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Delivers Br2 to alkene acceptor; absence of HX elimination products confirms shuttle (not elimination) pathway.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1,1,2-tetrachloroethane
probe_reagents.observation: Functions as Cl2 donor without HX elimination.

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-dichloroethane (DCE)
probe_reagents.observation: Cl2 donor in Mn-mediated e-shuttle; absence of HCl elimination supports proposed mechanism.

probe_reagents.name: Lindane (γ-HCH)
probe_reagents.observation: Serves as Cl2 donor; benzene confirmed as co-product by crude NMR and GC, consistent with sequential dechlorination/extrusion.

10.1126_science.abf2974.notes: Br2 shuttle proceeds without a transition-metal mediator (direct anodic oxidation of Br- to Br+/bromonium). Cl2 shuttle requires a Mn(II)/Mn(III) redox mediator because direct anodic oxidation of Cl- is too high in potential. The irreversibility of the second cathodic reduction is attributed to fast chemical follow-up (loss of ethylene + bromide), and N2 sparging removes ethylene to prevent reverse reaction. A representative intramolecular Br2 transfer experiment supports the proposed two-electron shuttle picture; HX elimination pathway was explicitly ruled out across multiple donors.

10.1126_science.abn1395_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical decarboxylative sp2-sp3 cross-coupling of NHPI redox-active esters with vinyl iodides using a Ni/bpy catalyst on Ag-nanoparticle-functionalized RVC cathode with sacrificial Mg anode
10.1126_science.abn1395_1.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of Ag(I) (from AgNO3 + halide) deposits Ag nanoparticles on RVC; the Ag-NP-modified cathode then reduces Ni(II)(bpy) to lower-valent Ni species that activates the NHPI redox-active ester (RAE) to an alkyl radical (with loss of CO2 and phthalimide), which is captured by a Ni-alkenyl species formed from oxidative addition of the vinyl iodide; sacrificial Mg anode is oxidized to Mg(2+)
10.1126_science.abn1395_1.electron_flow: Initial cathodic charge (0–0.3 F/mol) reduces Ag(I) (generated in situ from AgNO3 + halide) to Ag(0) nanoparticles on the RVC cathode; subsequently the Ag-NP cathode reduces Ni(II)(bpy) to Ni(I), which fragments the NHPI redox-active ester into an alkyl radical, CO2, and phthalimide, while a separate Ni(II)-alkenyl (from oxidative addition of vinyl iodide) intercepts the radical and undergoes reductive elimination to forge the sp2-sp3 bond; Mg(0) anode supplies the balancing oxidation.
10.1126_science.abn1395_1.rate_determining_step: Not explicitly identified; induction period is governed by Ag(I) reduction/deposition prior to productive cross-coupling

key_intermediates.name: Ag nanoparticles on RVC cathode (functionalized electrode)
Ni(III)(bpy)(alkenyl)(alkyl) intermediate prior to reductive elimination
Ni(II)(bpy)(alkenyl) halide (after oxidative addition of vinyl iodide)
Over-reduced Ni(0) / aggregated Ni(I) (catalyst deactivation, passivating film)

probe_reagents.name: AgNO3 omission control
probe_reagents.observation: Without AgNO3, yield drops to 20%; with 0.3 equiv AgNO3 yield rises to 52%, confirming Ag-NP role

probe_reagents.name: LiCl additive / halide source control
probe_reagents.observation: Halide required to form photosensitive AgCl; without LiCl, NiCl2 can serve as halide donor; prolonged stir before electrolysis decomposes AgCl photochemically and lowers yield to 17%

probe_reagents.name: Pure Ag electrode / Ag-plated electrode substitution
probe_reagents.observation: Bulk Ag electrodes performed poorly; Ag-NP on RVC was essential, indicating nanoparticulate Ag (not bulk) is the active surface

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric Mg reductant (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction does not proceed efficiently without electrolysis even with Mg reductant; electrochemistry is essential

probe_reagents.name: Benzyl chloride redox probe at Ag-NP vs bare GC electrode
probe_reagents.observation: 370 mV positive shift of BnCl reduction on Ag-NP-modified electrode, confirming Ag-NP lowers overpotential

probe_reagents.name: Methyl ester vinyl iodide 40 vs free-acid vinyl iodide 5 (RDE comparison)
probe_reagents.observation: ECcat current observed with 40 (no passivation in presence of RAE); with acid 5, a second reduction wave at -1.48 V persists even with RAE, attributed to Ni(II/III)-alkenyl reduction to Ni(I)-alkenyl that deactivates via dimerization

10.1126_science.abn1395_1.notes: Authors propose four distinct roles for the Ag-NP layer: (I) prevents catalyst adsorption/passivation, (II) lowers cathode overpotential (-1.15 V vs -1.66 V without AgNO3, a 510 mV shift) keeping it above Ni-catalyst decomposition potential, (III) slows diffusion of RAE to the electrode (Levich analysis shows 2x lower diffusion coefficient on Ag-NP electrode), and (IV) prevents sequential over-reduction of Ni(II/III)-alkenyl to deactivated Ni(I)-alkenyl. Electrode surface characterized by SEM/TEM/EDS showing 10–100 nm Ag nanoparticles. Alternative role of bulk silver was ruled out by control with pure Ag electrodes. A comproportionation pathway between Ni(III) and Ni(I) species is also drawn in the proposed cycle.

10.1126_sciadv.add7134.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical asymmetric Ni-catalyzed three-component alkoxy-alkylation of alkenes with 2-acylimidazoles and methanol, forming β-methoxy ketones with a quaternary stereocenter via radical addition.
10.1126_sciadv.add7134.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the Ni(II)-bound enolate (derived from 2-acylimidazole 1a deprotonated by quinuclidine/2,6-di-tert-butylpyridine) generates a Ni-stabilized α-carbonyl radical that adds to the alkene; the resulting benzylic radical/cation is trapped by methanol.
10.1126_sciadv.add7134.electron_flow: The Ni(II)/L8 complex binds the deprotonated 2-acylimidazole; anodic oxidation of this Ni-enolate produces a Ni-stabilized α-carbonyl radical that adds to the alkene to give a benzylic radical, which is further oxidized to a benzylic cation and trapped by MeOH, while protons are reduced at the Pt cathode.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic radical from radical addition to 1,1-diphenylethylene (2a)
ring-opened homoallyl/allyl-carbinyl radical from cyclopropane probe 19

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a not formed; TEMPO-adduct of the α-carbonyl carbon isolated in 99% yield, supporting an α-carbonyl radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO+ BF4- (oxoammonium)
probe_reagents.observation: In the absence of current, TEMPO+ alone yields the same TEMPO-adduct (76%, 59% ee), indicating that TEMPO+ generated in situ by anodic oxidation could also be the trapping species; cannot rule out TEMPO oxidation pathway.

probe_reagents.name: (1-phenyl-2-phenylcyclopropyl)ethylene 19 (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened products 20 (13%, d.r. 1:1) and 21 (10%, d.r. 1:1) observed, consistent with a carbon-centered radical that opens the cyclopropane (intermediate [20-A] → [20-B]).

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of NiCl2-L8 + 1a vs TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Onset potential of the Ni-bound enolate is ~+0.50 V vs SCE, similar to TEMPO, supporting feasibility of anodic generation of the Ni-enolate radical.

10.1126_sciadv.add7134.notes: Constant-current (2-6 mA) and constant-potential (+1.0 V vs SCE) experiments both give 3a with consistently high ee (92-93%), indicating the stereo-determining step is Ni/L8-controlled radical addition rather than potential-dependent. Authors explicitly acknowledge an alternative pathway in which TEMPO is first oxidized to TEMPO+ which then traps the radical/enolate; this cannot be excluded based on the CV and stoichiometric TEMPO+ experiment.

10.1016_j.checat.2021.08.004.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical (3+2) cyclization between amides and olefins using DCE as a chlorine source to give 2-oxazolines via chloronium-ion intermediates
10.1016_j.checat.2021.08.004.electrochemical_role: Consecutive paired electrolysis: DCE is reduced at the cathode to release chloride (with H2 evolution), then chloride is oxidized at the anode to a chlorine radical/Cl+ equivalent that forms a chloronium ion with the alkene; the chloride anion functions as a redox mediator generated in situ
10.1016_j.checat.2021.08.004.electron_flow: At the cathode, DCE accepts 2 e- to release 2 Cl- (and ethylene/H2 byproducts); at the anode, Cl- is oxidized (-2e-) to Cl+/Cl•, which adds to the olefin to form a chloronium intermediate that is opened by the amide oxygen, followed by deprotonation and cyclization (intramolecular N attack with loss of HCl) to give the oxazoline.
10.1016_j.checat.2021.08.004.rate_determining_step: Electrochemical redox step (first-order in current; zero-order in both indene and benzamide), consistent with anodic Cl- oxidation being rate-limiting

key_intermediates.name: chloride anion (from cathodic reduction of DCE)
chlorine radical / Cl+ (anodic oxidation of Cl-)
chloronium ion of olefin (3-membered cyclic chloriranium)

probe_reagents.name: AgNO3 (chloride detection)
probe_reagents.observation: White AgCl precipitate formed from electrolyzed solution, confirming Cl- generation; solution pH = 3.19 (acidic)

probe_reagents.name: Styrene (as model olefin without base)
probe_reagents.observation: Isolated β-chloroamides 5bc (40%) and 5bc' (41%); subsequent treatment with NaOAc converted them to oxazolines 4bc/4bc' (35%/33%), supporting them as on-cycle intermediates

probe_reagents.name: HCl additive (10 mol%) in THF
probe_reagents.observation: In THF (no DCE chloride source) yield dropped to 11%; adding 10 mol% HCl restored 51% yield with r.r. = 10:1, confirming the essential role of chloride

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of DCE, 1a, 2a, TBACl
probe_reagents.observation: DCE reduces at -1.5 V vs Ag/AgCl; Cl- oxidation at 1.5 V is lower than 1a (1.8 V) or 2a, so Cl- is preferentially oxidized at the anode

10.1016_j.checat.2021.08.004.notes: Authors note an induction period in time-monitored conversion (consistent with Cl- buildup before productive turnover). An alternative pathway in which the indene is first anodically oxidized to a radical cation cannot be excluded (see SI Figure S9); the favored pathway proceeds via chloronium ion formed from anodically generated Cl+/Cl•.

10.1016_j.checat.2021.08.004_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical (3+2) cyclization between primary amides and olefins to give 2-oxazolines, using 1,2-dichloroethane (DCE) as both solvent and chloride source via paired electrolysis
10.1016_j.checat.2021.08.004_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of DCE generates chloride anion (with ethylene + Cl- released); chloride is then oxidized at the anode to a Cl+ equivalent (chlorine radical/cation) that activates the alkene as a chloronium intermediate; protons are reduced at the cathode to H2
10.1016_j.checat.2021.08.004_sup_1.electron_flow: At the cathode, DCE is reduced (2 e-) to release ethylene and 2 Cl-; at the anode, Cl- is oxidized to Cl+/Cl• which forms a chloronium with the alkene. The amide nitrogen opens the chloronium to give a beta-chloro amide, and intramolecular O-attack with loss of HCl furnishes the oxazoline; H+ is reduced at the cathode to H2.
10.1016_j.checat.2021.08.004_sup_1.rate_determining_step: Electrochemical redox step (first-order dependence on current; zero-order in indene and benzamide)

key_intermediates.name: chloride anion (from cathodic reduction of DCE)
chlorine radical / Cl+ equivalent (anodic oxidation of Cl-)
chloronium ion (3-membered chloriranium from alkene + Cl+)

probe_reagents.name: AgNO3 (chloride detection)
probe_reagents.observation: White AgCl precipitate formed from the electrolyzed solution, confirming Cl- generation; solution pH dropped to 3.19

probe_reagents.name: styrene (alkene without intramolecular bias)
probe_reagents.observation: Base-free electrolysis gave beta-chloro amides 5bc (40%) and 5bc' (41%); resubjection with CH3COONa converted them to oxazolines 4bc/4bc' (35%/33%), supporting them as on-cycle intermediates

probe_reagents.name: HCl (alternative chloride source)
probe_reagents.observation: In THF (no DCE), 10 mol% HCl raised yield of 3aa from 11% to 51% and improved site selectivity (r.r. 10:1), confirming the essential role of Cl-

probe_reagents.name: current variation
probe_reagents.observation: Initial rate is first-order in current; zero-order in indene and benzamide -> electrochemical step is rate-controlling

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: DCE reduction at -1.5 V vs Ag/AgCl; Cl- oxidation at 1.5 V, below 1a (1.8 V) and 2a, so Cl- is preferentially oxidized at the anode

10.1016_j.checat.2021.08.004_sup_1.notes: An alternative mechanism (Figure S9) cannot be excluded: direct anodic oxidation of the alkene to a radical cation A, trapped by the amide to give benzyl radical B, further oxidized to benzyl cation C, then cyclization/deprotonation to the oxazoline. An induction period was observed in the time-monitored conversion. Thiophene-, furan-, 4-vinylpyridine-, cyclohexene-, cyclohexenone-, p-nitro/p-cyanostyrene-, and urea-type substrates were unsuitable (N.D.).

10.1021_acsorginorgau.4c00008.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical selenocyclization of N-allyl benzamides and unsaturated oximes with diphenyl diselenide to give selenofunctionalized oxazolines and isoxazolines under continuous flow
10.1021_acsorginorgau.4c00008.electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of PhSeSePh generates PhSe radical (A) and PhSe anion (B); the selenide is then anodically oxidized to a phenylselenyl cation (PhSe+) that acts as the electrophile toward the alkene
10.1021_acsorginorgau.4c00008.electron_flow: Cathode reduces PhSeSePh to a PhSe radical and PhSe anion; the anion is anodically oxidized to PhSe+, which adds to the alkene of the N-allyl benzamide to form a cationic intermediate C; intramolecular nucleophilic cyclization by the amide oxygen and loss of H+ furnishes the oxazoline, while H+ is reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: seleniranium / β-selenyl carbocation intermediate from alkene addition (C)

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl-substituted substrate 5y (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: No ring-opening products observed, excluding involvement of a free phenylselenyl radical in productive C–Se bond formation and supporting an ionic (PhSe+) pathway

10.1021_acsorginorgau.4c00008.notes: Mechanism is proposed by analogy to prior electrochemical selenylations (refs 26, 27). Although PhSe radicals are necessarily generated at the electrode, the cyclopropyl probe excludes their direct involvement, favoring an electrophilic PhSe+ mechanism. Sulfur version (Ph2S2 → SPh-oxazoline 9a) is demonstrated and presumably follows an analogous pathway.

catalysts-13-00631-v2.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical thiocyanation/cyclization cascade of N-(2-alkenylaryl)benzamides with NH4SCN to form 4-(thiocyanatomethyl)-4H-benzo[d][1,3]oxazines via radical-polar crossover.
catalysts-13-00631-v2.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of thiocyanate anion to thiocyanate radical (via (SCN)2 / (SCN)3-); subsequent anodic SET oxidation of the C-centered radical adduct to a carbocation; cathodic reduction of protons to H2.
catalysts-13-00631-v2.electron_flow: At the anode, SCN- is oxidized to SCN• (via (SCN)2), which adds to the alkene of 1a to give C-radical I; I undergoes a second anodic SET to carbocation II, which is trapped by intramolecular nucleophilic attack of the amide oxygen and deprotonated to give 3a, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

key_intermediates.name: C-centered benzylic radical from SCN addition to alkene (intermediate I)
benzylic carbocation after second SET (intermediate II)
protonated oxazine after intramolecular O-attack (intermediate III)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited (only trace 3a), supporting a radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene
probe_reagents.observation: Trapping product 3a' (Ph2C=CH-SCN type adduct) detected by GC-MS, confirming SCN radical.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, NH4SCN, H2SO4
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak of 2 (NH4SCN) at 1.48 V is lower than 1a at 2.27 V; with acid, a second oxidation peak of SCN- appears, indicating preferential and stepwise oxidation of thiocyanate.

catalysts-13-00631-v2.notes: Role of H2SO4 is critical: without acid, 1a is not oxidized and SCN- shows only one oxidation event; acid enables secondary oxidation of thiocyanate (analogous to halide (X3-)/(X2-) behavior). The amide oxygen (not nitrogen) acts as the intramolecular nucleophile, giving the 1,3-oxazine regiochemistry.

10.1039_d4qo01068j.rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical oxyhalocyclization of 2-vinyl anilides with nBu4NX to give 4-halomethyl 4H-3,1-benzoxazines using 4CzIPN as photocatalyst under 455 nm irradiation with Pt electrodes.
10.1039_d4qo01068j.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of halide anion (Cl-) to molecular halogen (Cl2); the photoexcited 4CzIPN* further activates Cl2 (via oxidative quenching or energy transfer) to generate chlorine radicals, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.
10.1039_d4qo01068j.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Cl- to Cl2; in path A Cl2 adds to the alkene as an onium cation that is intramolecularly trapped by the amide oxygen to deliver the benzoxazine; in path B 4CzIPN* (via oxidative quenching or EnT) homolyzes Cl2 to Cl•, which adds to the alkene to give a benzyl radical that is further oxidized to a benzyl cation prior to cyclization. Protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: molecular chlorine (Cl2) generated by anodic oxidation of Cl-
chloronium/onium cation from Cl2 addition to vinyl anilide (Int-I)
oxidized intermediate Int-III prior to deprotonation/aromatization
chlorine radical (Cl•) from photoactivation of Cl2
benzyl cation formed by oxidation of benzyl radical (alternative path)

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield decreased (to 61/31/33/30% with 2.0–5.0 equiv BHT) but reaction not fully suppressed, indicating involvement of a radical pathway alongside a non-radical (ionic) pathway.

10.1039_d4qo01068j.notes: Two parallel mechanisms are proposed: (favored/primary) ionic path through Cl2 addition giving an onium cation that is cyclized by the carbonyl oxygen; (alternative) photoredox/EnT radical path via Cl• addition and oxidation of the benzyl radical to a cation. BHT does not fully inhibit the reaction, consistent with both ionic and radical contributions. Gram-scale and late-stage (dehydrocholic acid-derived 1r) reactions validate practicality but do not provide mechanistic data.

10.1039_d3qo01241g.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component vicinal sulfenyltetrazolation of unactivated alkenes using thiols, TMSN3, and acetonitrile via a Ritter-type intermediate that enables 1,3-dipole cycloaddition.
10.1039_d3qo01241g.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of triarylamine mediator (Ar3N) to its radical cation, which performs SET on thiol to generate a thiyl radical; cathode reduces protons to H2.
10.1039_d3qo01241g.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Ar3N to Ar3N•+, which oxidizes/deprotonates thiol to thiyl radical; thiyl adds to alkene giving a C-radical that is further oxidized to an episulfonium cation, intercepted by MeCN to form a Ritter-type nitrilium that undergoes [3+2] with azide from TMSN3 to give the tetrazole; cathode reduces H+ to H2.

probe_reagents.name: disulfide (RSSR, 30)
probe_reagents.observation: Use of disulfide instead of thiol also gave vicinal sulfenyltetrazole 4 in comparable yield, supporting involvement of RS•/RSSR equilibrium.

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed (n.d.); BHT-SR adduct detected by GC-MS, indicating thiyl radical involvement.

probe_reagents.name: 1-(2-phenylcyclopropyl)-vinyl)benzene (31)
probe_reagents.observation: Afforded ring-opened product 32 in 20% yield (7.6:1 dr), consistent with a carbon-centered radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of Ar3N
probe_reagents.observation: Catalytic current and disappearance of Ar3N•+ reduction peak upon adding thiol; further enhanced with alkene, implicating Ar3N in two oxidation steps.

10.1039_d3qo01241g.notes: Two pathways to the episulfonium ion (C) are proposed: path a via oxidation of β-thio C-radical (B); path b via reaction of alkene with bis(arylthio)sulfonium ion (G) derived from disulfide (F). Competing nucleophilic addition of TMSN3 to C would give the off-pathway vicinal azidosulfide (H). Phosphate additive (NaPF6/Bu4NClO4 with phosphate) prolongs Ar3N•+ lifetime.

10.1039_c8qo00803e.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical ferrocene-mediated cascade 5-exo-trig/7-endo-trig radical cyclization of unsaturated carbamates and ureas to give functionalized 7-membered carbocycles (cycloheptane-fused oxazolidinones/imidazolones)
10.1039_c8qo00803e.electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+, which performs single-electron transfer (SET) oxidation of the deprotonated carbamate/urea N-anion to generate a nitrogen-centered radical; cathode reduces MeOH to methoxide and H2, with methoxide deprotonating the N-H substrate.
10.1039_c8qo00803e.electron_flow: Cp2Fe is oxidized at the anode to Cp2Fe+ while MeOH is reduced at the cathode to MeO- + 1/2 H2; methoxide deprotonates the N-H substrate to give anion A, which Cp2Fe+ oxidizes by SET to nitrogen-centered radical B; B undergoes stereoselective 5-exo-trig cyclization to C-radical C, then regioselective 7-endo-trig cyclization to D, which abstracts H from 1,4-CHD to deliver the 7-membered product 2.
10.1039_c8qo00803e.rate_determining_step: 7-endo-trig cyclization of carbon radical C (TS1, ΔG‡ = 10.1 kcal/mol; competing TS2 to 3,4-trans diastereomer at 11.7 kcal/mol)

key_intermediates.name: N-anion of carbamate/urea substrate (conjugate base A)
carbon-centered radical C after 5-exo-trig cyclization (5-membered ring with trans-disposition of radical and pendant alkene)
monocyclized H-atom-transfer product 3 (from C trapping H from 1,4-CHD)

probe_reagents.name: 1,4-cyclohexadiene (1,4-CHD)
probe_reagents.observation: H-atom donor; required for efficient turnover — omitting it drops yield of 2 from 60% to 37% and increases monocyclized HAT product 3 to 18%, consistent with H-atom transfer reduction of bicyclic radical D

probe_reagents.name: substrate with two internal alkenes (gives 18)
probe_reagents.observation: Only monocyclized product 18 isolated (62%), showing the second (7-endo) cyclization is sensitive to steric hindrance at the distal alkene

probe_reagents.name: X-ray crystallography of product 12 (CCDC 1823050)
probe_reagents.observation: Confirms cis ring-fusion stereochemistry of the bicyclic 7-membered product

10.1039_c8qo00803e.notes: DFT (UB3LYP/6-31G*) supports kinetic and thermodynamic preference for 3,4-cis intermediate D over 3,4-trans D' (ΔΔG‡ ≈1.6 kcal/mol; D at -16.0 vs D' at -14.9 kcal/mol relative to C). Origin of selectivity attributed to steric repulsion between PMP and nBu groups in TS2, forcing PMP rotation out of conjugation (dihedral ∠C2-N1-C12-C17 = 61.3° in TS2 vs 35.0° in TS1). Alternative 6-exo-trig pathway from radical C is proposed to be inhibited by ring strain of the trans-fused bicyclic system; the trans disposition of radical and alkene in C is the key stereoelectronic feature that channels cyclization to 7-endo rather than the usual 6-exo for 6-heptenyl radicals.

10.1039_d2qo01947g.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical tandem trifluoromethylation–fluorination of styrenes using CF3SO2Na as CF3 source and Et3N·3HF as fluoride source to give β-fluoro-α-trifluoromethyl products
10.1039_d2qo01947g.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na at carbon rod anode generates CF3 radical (with loss of SO2); the benzylic radical adduct is further anodically oxidized to a benzylic carbocation; protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2
10.1039_d2qo01947g.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2Na with loss of SO2 to give CF3 radical, which adds to the styrene C=C to form benzylic radical I; I is further oxidized at the anode to benzylic cation II, which is trapped by fluoride from Et3N·3HF; protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical generated from anodic oxidation/desulfonylation of CF3SO2Na
Benzylic carbon radical I from CF3 addition to styrene C=C
Benzylic carbocation II from anodic oxidation of radical I

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely shut down, but no TEMPO–CF3 adduct detected by GC-MS; attributed to TEMPO being oxidized to TEMPO+ at lower potential than CF3SO2Na

probe_reagents.name: BHT (butylated hydroxytoluene)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction shut down; BHT–CF3 adduct 3a detected by GC-MS, supporting CF3 radical

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Both addition product 3b and coupling product 3c detected by GC-MS, corroborating radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: CF3SO2Na (CV probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at 1.14 V vs Ag/AgNO3 confirms CF3SO2Na is anodically oxidized first; pink trace with 1a shows catalytic current

probe_reagents.name: TBACl (CV control)
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation of Cl- at 1.44 V matches 1.37 V peak, assigned to chloride from DCE substitution

10.1039_d2qo01947g.notes: Authors propose radical–polar crossover: radical addition followed by anodic oxidation to cation, then F- nucleophilic addition. TEMPO failed as trap due to its lower oxidation potential than CF3SO2Na (ineffective TEMPO/TEMPO+ conversion); BHT and DPE successfully trapped the CF3 radical. CV also revealed a side oxidation peak at 1.37 V assigned to chloride generated by nucleophilic substitution of DCE solvent by CF3SO2Na.

10.1039_c8qo00645h.rxn_class_summary: Bromide-mediated electrochemical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-arylacrylamides with CF3SO2Na (Langlois' reagent) to give CF3-substituted oxindoles.
10.1039_c8qo00645h.electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes Br- to Br2 (mediator, easier to oxidize than CF3SO2Na); cathode reduces the in situ formed CF3SO2Br (4) / CF3SO2OBr (5) to regenerate Br- and release a CF3SO2 radical that fragments to CF3 radical.
10.1039_c8qo00645h.electron_flow: Br- is oxidized at the anode to Br2, which reacts with CF3SO2Na to give CF3SO2Br (4)/CF3SO2OBr (5); cathodic single-electron reduction of 4/5 regenerates Br- and produces a CF3SO2 radical that loses SO2 to give CF3 radical. CF3 adds to the acrylamide, the resulting C-radical undergoes intramolecular aryl cyclization, and the cyclohexadienyl intermediate is rearomatized by anodic oxidation/deprotonation to the oxindole.

key_intermediates.name: molecular bromine (Br2) from anodic oxidation of Br-
sulfonyl hypobromite CF3SO2OBr (intermediate 5, in equilibrium with 4)
alkyl radical from CF3 addition to acrylamide (8)

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace of 3a under standard conditions with 3.0 equiv BHT, supporting a radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Br2 (stoichiometric)
probe_reagents.observation: 1a + CF3SO2Na with 1.2 equiv Br2 in CH3CN at 75 °C gave only trace product (no electrolysis), showing Br2 alone is insufficient.

probe_reagents.name: CF3SO2Br (4, isolated/used stoichiometrically)
probe_reagents.observation: Thermal reaction of 1a with 4 in CH3CN/75 °C gave 3a in 44%; under standard electrolysis with 4, 72% was obtained, confirming 4 as a key intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: divided-cell test (anodic compartment)
probe_reagents.observation: Only 10% of 3a in the anodic compartment vs 14 (undivided) showing the cathode is required for CF3 radical formation.

probe_reagents.name: divided-cell test (cathodic compartment with 4)
probe_reagents.observation: 1a + 4 in the cathodic compartment gave 3a in 38%, confirming cathodic reduction of 4 generates CF3 radical.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of n-Bu4NBr, CF3SO2Na, CF3SO2Br
probe_reagents.observation: Br- oxidations at 0.85 and 1.11 V vs Ag/AgNO3 (lower than CF3SO2Na at 1.39 V); catalytic current with CF3SO2Na and a new reduction peak at ~0.48 V matching CF3SO2Br reduction.

10.1039_c8qo00645h.notes: Authors emphasize that bromide mediation keeps the steady-state CF3 radical concentration low, suppressing decomposition. Both the O-centered (6, from 5) and S-centered (7, from 4) CF3SO2 radicals are proposed to converge to CF3 radical via SO2 loss; the two are presented as parallel branches rather than competing mechanisms. N-H acrylamide (3f), N-acetyl (3g), and p-NO2 (3k) substrates failed, suggesting electronic/steric limits on the radical cyclization/oxidation step.

10.1039_d3qo00290j.rxn_class_summary: Electrophotocatalytic trifluoromethylative (or difluoromethylative) radical cyclization of unactivated alkenes using CF3SO2Na and eosin Y under combined visible light and electrochemistry to access fluoromethylated N-heterocycles.
10.1039_d3qo00290j.electrochemical_role: Anode reoxidizes the reduced eosin Y radical anion (PC•−) back to ground-state PC, closing a reductive quenching photocatalytic cycle; cathode reduces protons to H2 (no external oxidant needed).
10.1039_d3qo00290j.electron_flow: Visible light excites eosin Y to PC*, which oxidizes CF3SO2Na (E_ox = 0.89 V) by SET to give SO2 and CF3•; CF3• adds to the alkene to form alkyl radical A that cyclizes onto the pendant aryl to form cyclohexadienyl radical B, which is oxidized by a second PC* (or PC*) to cation C; deprotonation rearomatizes to product 3a, while PC•− is reoxidized to PC at the anode and protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: Excited-state eosin Y (PC*), E_red* = 1.51 V vs Ag/AgCl
CF3 radical generated by SET oxidation of CF3SO2Na and loss of SO2
Alkyl radical A from CF3• addition to the 2-methylallyl alkene of 1a
Cyclohexadienyl radical B from intramolecular radical addition onto the 2-phenyl ring
Cationic Wheland-type intermediate C after SET oxidation of B by PC*

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully suppressed (0% yield of 3a), consistent with radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3a; radical pathway implicated.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace 3a; DPE–CF3 adduct 18 isolated in 36% yield, directly trapping CF3•.

probe_reagents.name: p-dinitrobenzene (pDNB)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% 3a, indicating SET pathway is operative.

probe_reagents.name: CuCl2
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3a, consistent with single-electron transfer step.

probe_reagents.name: CCl4
probe_reagents.observation: Trace 3a, supports SET/radical mechanism.

probe_reagents.name: light on/off and current on/off
probe_reagents.observation: Product formation halts when either light or current is removed; no extended radical chain; under dark periods reaction slows but does not fully stop due to accumulated oxidizing species.

10.1039_d3qo00290j.notes: Authors explicitly exclude an electron-primed photocatalytic mechanism and an oxidative-quenching photoredox cycle because neither ground-state nor photoexcited eosin Y is preferentially oxidized at the anode under the reaction conditions (no new oxidation wave under blue light in CV). Stern–Volmer quenching of EY* by CF3SO2Na and a bathochromic shift in UV–vis upon mixing EY with 2a suggest a non-bonding interaction and SET from 2a to PC*; H2 evolution at the cathode was confirmed by gas detection. A sunlight-driven gram-scale variant also works (86% yield), supporting practicality.

10.1039_D3QO00599B_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical fluoroazotion of gem-difluoroalkenes with pyrazoles and Et3N·3HF to give trifluoromethyl-substituted N-alkylated pyrazoles
10.1039_D3QO00599B_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the gem-difluorostyrene 1a to a radical cation, which is then attacked by fluoride (from Et3N·3HF) and pyrazole nucleophile
10.1039_D3QO00599B_sup_1.electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the gem-difluorostyrene to a radical cation; fluoride from Et3N·3HF adds to give a benzylic radical/cation that is further oxidized and trapped by the pyrazole N-H nucleophile, while protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
10.1039_D3QO00599B_sup_1.rate_determining_step: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the gem-difluorostyrene

key_intermediates.name: benzylic α-fluoro carbocation/radical after F- addition
α-OOH, α-OMe, α-F peroxide adduct (compound 13) from O2/MeOH trapping

probe_reagents.name: MeOH/O2 (radical trapping)
probe_reagents.observation: GC-MS detected peroxide adduct 13 (C15H14F2O3, exact mass 280.0911), consistent with a benzylic radical intermediate trapped by O2 and MeOH

probe_reagents.name: 4-vinylbiphenyl (non-fluorinated styrene control)
probe_reagents.observation: Easily oxidized under the conditions (lower oxidation potential by CV) but gave only messy mixtures with no fluorination product, indicating the gem-difluoro group is essential for selectivity

10.1039_D3QO00599B_sup_1.notes: CV of 1a, Et3N·3HF and product 3a recorded in nBu4NBF4/HFIP-PhCF3 shows 1a is oxidized within the operating window; control with normal styrene (4-vinylbiphenyl) demonstrates that the CF2 group is required to stabilize/direct the radical-cation intermediate. Optimization shows DCM/HFIP or PhCF3/HFIP mixed solvent and graphite(+)/Pt(-) electrodes are critical; no electricity gives 0% yield, confirming electrochemical initiation.

10.1039_d3qo01935g.rxn_class_summary: Electrode-switchable electrochemical regiodivergent β-azidoiodination (Markovnikov vs anti-Markovnikov) of alkenes with TMSN3 and Bu4NI, where the anode material (Pt vs graphite) controls regiochemistry
10.1039_d3qo01935g.electrochemical_role: On graphite anode: I- is oxidized to I2, which forms an iodonium ion with the alkene (polar/cationic Markovnikov path to 2a). On Pt anode: I- is oxidized to hypervalent IO4-, which oxidizes TMSN3 to generate an azide radical that adds to the alkene to give an α-C radical, subsequently trapped by I·/I2 (radical anti-Markovnikov path to 3a).
10.1039_d3qo01935g.electron_flow: On graphite: anode oxidizes I- to I2, which forms an iodonium with the alkene; azide opens it at the more substituted carbon to give Markovnikov β-azido-α-iodo product 2a. On Pt: anode oxidizes I- further to hypervalent IO4-, which oxidizes TMSN3 to azide radical; ·N3 adds to alkene producing α-carbon radical B that is captured by I·/I2 to deliver anti-Markovnikov product 3a.

key_intermediates.name: I2 generated by anodic oxidation of I-
iodonium ion intermediate A from alkene + I2
β-iodo-α-azido cationic intermediate C (Markovnikov, from N3- opening of iodonium)
hypervalent iodine species IO4- formed on Pt anode
azide radical (·N3) from IO4- oxidation of TMSN3
α-carbon radical intermediate B from ·N3 addition to alkene

probe_reagents.name: N-allyl amide substrate 4 (radical clock / cyclization probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Under graphite anode standard conditions afforded iodocyclization product 6 (80%), consistent with I2/iodonium pathway; with Pt anode, 6 was not formed (0%), indicating different active iodine species (IOx- on Pt)

probe_reagents.name: NaIO4 (chemical oxidant surrogate for hypervalent iodine)
probe_reagents.observation: Replacing electrolysis with NaIO4 + styrene + TMSN3 gave 3a in 15% yield; with NaIO4 + I2 + Bu4NI gave 3a in 58%, supporting IO4- as the active oxidant on Pt

probe_reagents.name: NaIO3
probe_reagents.observation: Ineffective; no reaction, indicating IO3- is not the operative oxidant

10.1039_d3qo01935g.notes: CV showed two oxidation peaks at ~0.58 and 0.86 V (I- → I2 stepwise) on carbon, and an additional higher oxidation at ~2.23 V on Pt that intensified upon TMSN3 addition, supporting formation of hypervalent IO4- on Pt. A minor competing pathway on Pt is direct anodic oxidation of TMSN3 to azide radical, but CV did not show this on graphite. Oxidation of B to carbocation C (radical-polar crossover) cannot be ruled out, but control experiments favor radical trapping of B by I·/I2 to give anti-Markovnikov 3a.

10.1039_d3qo00687e.rxn_class_summary: Iodide-mediated electrochemical intramolecular cyclization of N-pentenylamines (and their HCl salts) to 3-chloropiperidines under undivided-cell conditions with TBAI as redox mediator and chloride source
10.1039_d3qo00687e.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (I-) to iodine radical/I2 (mediator); the alkyl radical formed after iodine-radical addition to the alkene is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation that cyclizes; protons are reduced to H2 at the Ni cathode
10.1039_d3qo00687e.electron_flow: At the anode, iodide is oxidized to iodine radicals which add to the pendant alkene of the protonated pentenylamine; the resulting C-radical is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation, which cyclizes via the amine to an iodomethyl-pyrrolidinium/aziridinium that is opened by chloride to deliver the 3-chloropiperidine, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Ni cathode and iodide is regenerated.

key_intermediates.name: beta-iodo carbon-centered alkyl radical from anti-Markovnikov I• addition to the pentenylamine alkene
beta-iodo carbocation (after anode oxidation of the alkyl radical)
3-chloropiperidine product (after Cl- attack and ring expansion of aziridinium)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield dropped from 82% to 23% upon addition of 1 equiv TEMPO, consistent with a radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: I2 / NEt3 (stoichiometric, no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Only 31% yield obtained, ruling out simple electrophilic iodination of the alkene / iodoamine pathway as the operative mechanism

probe_reagents.name: LiClO4 (replacing TBAI as electrolyte)
probe_reagents.observation: No conversion, confirming iodide is required as electrochemical mediator

probe_reagents.name: Exclusion of visible light
probe_reagents.observation: No decrease in yield in the dark (82%), excluding a TBAI3 photolysis pathway

probe_reagents.name: No-current control
probe_reagents.observation: 0% conversion without applied potential, confirming the transformation is electrochemical

probe_reagents.name: Cyclopropyl-bearing substrate (2n)
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclopropyl group tolerated (69% yield, no ring opening), indicating the reaction does not proceed via an aminyl (N-centered) radical

10.1039_d3qo00687e.notes: Authors explicitly rule out (i) electrophilic iodination of the alkene followed by cyclization, (ii) direct oxidation of amine to an aminyl radical (supported by cyclopropyl probe and prior CV by Yin), and (iii) a TBAI3 photolysis pathway. Direct anodic oxidation of substrate (without iodide mediation) cannot be fully excluded but is disfavored by the LiClO4 control. Proposed cyclization goes through an iodomethyl-pyrrolidinium → aziridinium → ring expansion by Cl- to give the 3-chloropiperidine.

10.1039_D3QO00731F_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical-radical coupling of N-aryl acrylamides with sodium difluoromethanesulfinate (CF2HSO2Na) to form difluoromethylated benzoxazine/related heterocycles (compound 3) via anodic substrate oxidation and cathodic CF2H radical generation.
10.1039_D3QO00731F_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Paired electrolysis: anodic (Pt) oxidation of the N-aryl acrylamide 1 to a radical cation (Int-1), which cyclizes to a carbon-centered radical (Int-2); CF2HSO2Na is oxidized/decomposed at the carbon cathode (per scheme depiction) to release CF2H radical, with concomitant H+ reduction to H2.
10.1039_D3QO00731F_sup_1.electron_flow: At the anode, substrate 1 is oxidized by single-electron transfer to radical cation Int-1, which undergoes intramolecular cyclization (loss of H+) to give C-radical Int-2; in parallel, CF2HSO2Na delivers a CF2H• radical (with SO2 extrusion) while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode; Int-2 and CF2H• then undergo radical-radical coupling to give product 3.

probe_reagents.name: 1a + 2a + TFA mixture (CV)
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclic voltammetry shows a distinct oxidation wave around ~1.2-1.5 V vs Ag/AgCl for the 1a+2a+TFA combination that is absent or weaker for individual components, supporting anodic oxidation of the substrate under the reaction conditions.

probe_reagents.name: Control electrolysis probing intermediates 7 and 8
probe_reagents.observation: Under the constant-current electrolysis (6 mA, RT, 6.5 h, Pt cathode/graphite anode), neither compound 7 nor compound 8 could be detected by HRMS, ruling out these species as on-pathway intermediates.

10.1039_D3QO00731F_sup_1.notes: Scheme S2 depicts the favored radical-radical coupling pathway. Note that in the drawn scheme CF2HSO2Na appears at the cathode side and substrate 1 at the anode side; mechanistically the more typical assignment is anodic oxidation of the sulfinate to CF2H• and anodic oxidation of 1 to Int-1, with cathodic H+ reduction to H2 — the figure's electrode labeling may be schematic rather than literal. Alternative ionic (Int-2 oxidation to cation then trapped by CF2H-anion) pathway is not explicitly supported and is disfavored relative to the radical-radical coupling depicted.

10.1039_d3qo00731f.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative radical cascade oxydifluoromethylation of N-acyl-(2-ene)anilines (olefinic amides) with sodium difluoromethanesulfinate to give difluoromethylated benzoxazines in an undivided cell.
10.1039_d3qo00731f.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF2HSO2Na (2a) on graphite anode to generate the difluoromethyl radical (with SO2 extrusion); a second anodic oxidation converts the cyclized benzyl radical to a benzyl cation. Cathode (Pt) reduces protons to H2.
10.1039_d3qo00731f.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF2HSO2Na to •CF2H (loss of SO2), which adds to the alkene of the N-acyl olefinic amide to give a benzyl radical (Int-1); a second anodic oxidation generates a benzyl cation (Int-2) that is trapped by intramolecular nucleophilic attack of the amide oxygen to form the benzoxazine, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: benzyl radical Int-1 from CF2H addition to alkene
benzyl cation Int-2 (after anodic oxidation of Int-1)

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3aa formed only in trace at 1.0–3.0 equiv BHT; BHT–CF2H adduct 4 detected by EI-MS, supporting •CF2H formation.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully inhibited at 2.0–3.0 equiv TEMPO; TEMPO–CF2H adduct 5 detected by EI-MS; however putative TEMPO adduct of the benzyl radical (6) was not detected, and self-coupling products 7/8 of Int-1 were not observed.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Olefin 1a not oxidized up to 2.0 V (vs Ag/AgCl); 2a oxidized at ~0.75 V, indicating the cascade is initiated by oxidation of 2a rather than the alkene, and ruling out a radical–radical coupling pathway.

10.1039_d3qo00731f.notes: A radical–radical coupling pathway between •CF2H and an anilide-derived radical was explicitly ruled out by CV (1a is not oxidized in the operating window). Absence of TEMPO-trapped benzyl radical (6) and self-coupling products (7,8) is rationalized by rapid further anodic oxidation of Int-1 to the cation Int-2 (radical–polar crossover) rather than radical termination.

10.1039_D3QO01241G_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component thioether-tetrazole synthesis from TMSN3, vinyl arenes, and arylthiols, proceeding through a thiyl radical addition / radical-polar crossover pathway as probed by a radical clock experiment.
10.1039_D3QO01241G_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation generates a thiyl radical from the arylthiol (ArSH); the thiyl radical adds to the alkene, and subsequent oxidation/azide trapping with the tetrazole-forming step closes the cycle.
10.1039_D3QO01241G_sup_1.electron_flow: ArSH is oxidized (anodically, mediated by Ar3N) to an arylthiyl radical that adds to the vinylarene; the resulting cyclopropylcarbinyl radical undergoes ring-opening (radical clock), and the ring-opened C-radical is further oxidized to a benzylic cation that is trapped by azide/tetrazole nitrogen to give 32.

key_intermediates.name: cyclopropylcarbinyl radical from thiyl addition to 1-(2-phenylcyclopropyl)vinylbenzene
benzylic cation (after oxidation of ring-opened radical)

probe_reagents.name: 1-(2-phenylcyclopropyl)vinylbenzene (cyclopropyl radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 32 isolated in 20% yield (7.6:1 dr), confirming a carbon-radical intermediate that opens the cyclopropane after thiyl addition.

10.1039_D3QO01241G_sup_1.notes: Only the radical clock experiment is shown on this page; other mechanistic probes (if any) are not included here. The ring-opened olefinic thioether-tetrazole product supports a stepwise radical addition followed by radical-polar crossover rather than a concerted pathway.

10.1039_d3qo00692a.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical four-component aminochlorination of alkenes/dienes using benzimidazole as a redox modulator to generate an in situ N-chlorobenzimidazole chlorinating agent
10.1039_d3qo00692a.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Cl- to Cl radical and concurrent PCET oxidation of benzimidazole 1a to an N-centered radical; the two radicals couple to give N-chlorobenzimidazole (INT-1), the active ionic chlorinating mediator. Cathode reduces HFIP/H+ to H2.
10.1039_d3qo00692a.electron_flow: At the anode, Cl- is oxidized by SET to Cl radical while benzimidazole 1a is oxidized by PCET to an N-radical; their cross-coupling (or Cl radical dimerization + coupling with 1a, path B) furnishes ionic N-chlorobenzimidazole INT-1. INT-1 transfers Cl+ to styrene giving a chloriranium ion, which is opened by MeCN (Ritter) to a nitrilium that is trapped by a second benzimidazole 1a to yield product 3a; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: N-centered benzimidazolyl radical (D) from PCET oxidation of 1a
nitrilium ion (F) from MeCN attack on E (Ritter-type)

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a eroded to 22%

probe_reagents.name: triethyl phosphite P(OEt)3
probe_reagents.observation: Yield dropped to 20%

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield reduced to 35%

probe_reagents.name: alpha-phenylsulfonyl acrylate
probe_reagents.observation: Yield 59%; partial suppression

probe_reagents.name: N,N-diallyl tosylamide (8)
probe_reagents.observation: No radical cyclization product observed; linear aminochlorinated product 9 obtained (39%)

probe_reagents.name: vinylcyclopropane dibenzyl malonate (10)
probe_reagents.observation: No ring-opening product observed; cyclopropane retained in product 11 (49%)

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of MgCl2, 1a, styrene 2a, 4-methoxystyrene
probe_reagents.observation: Cl- and 1a oxidize earlier (~1.0 V) than styrene; failed substrate 4-methoxystyrene overlaps with Cl-/1a oxidation, indicating Cl- and 1a oxidation initiate the reaction

probe_reagents.name: anode potential monitoring
probe_reagents.observation: Anode held below styrene oxidation potential (1.68 V) for first 2 h, confirming alkene is not directly oxidized

probe_reagents.name: INT-1 as substrate
probe_reagents.observation: Preliminary reaction with isolated INT-1 supports its intermediacy and Ritter-type ionic pathway

10.1039_d3qo00692a.notes: Authors favor path A (direct cross-coupling of Cl radical C with N-radical D to give INT-1); an alternative path B invokes dimerization of Cl radical to Cl2 followed by reaction with 1a to give the same INT-1. Radical scavengers attenuate but do not fully shut down the reaction, and radical clocks show no cyclization/ring-opening, indicating radicals are formed but rapidly converted to an ionic (Ritter-type) chlorination pathway via N-chlorobenzimidazole.

10.1039_d4qo01354a.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative three-component tandem synthesis of selenyl imidazo[2,1-b]thiazinones from diaryl diselenides, acryloyl chlorides, and 2-mercaptobenzimidazoles/2-mercaptoimidazoles.
10.1039_d4qo01354a.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diaryl diselenide (PhSeSePh) to a selenium-centered cationic radical that fragments into a selenium cation (PhSe+) and a selenium radical (PhSe•); cathode reduces H+ to H2.
10.1039_d4qo01354a.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh to a radical cation that splits into PhSe+ and PhSe•; base-mediated condensation of acryloyl chloride with 2-mercaptobenzimidazole gives acyl-thioether D, which undergoes intramolecular cyclization to carbanion E; E is trapped by PhSe+ to give the selenylated product, while PhSe• auto-couples to regenerate diselenide and H+ is reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeded with only slight yield decrease (80%), suggesting non-radical main pathway.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product still formed in 70% yield; radical pathway not dominant.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Product obtained in 67% yield; radical trapping ineffective at suppressing reaction.

probe_reagents.name: PhSeCl (in place of PhSeSePh, no electricity)
probe_reagents.observation: Afforded 4a in 80% yield without current, supporting in situ generation of selenium cation.

probe_reagents.name: D2O (replacing diselenide)
probe_reagents.observation: Deuterated product 6-d obtained in 95% yield with 96% D-incorporation, supporting a carbanion intermediate E.

10.1039_d4qo01354a.notes: Radical trapping experiments (TEMPO/BHT/DPE) did not suppress the reaction, leading the authors to favor an ionic (cation/anion) pathway over a radical chain; CV showed oxidation of 1a at 1.327 V supporting anodic activation of the diselenide. An alternative full-radical pathway (PhSe• addition) is implicitly disfavored but cannot be entirely excluded.

10.1039_d2qo00406b.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative selenocyclization of olefinic amides with diselenides to give 3-(selenomethyl)iminoisobenzofurans via a radical cascade, constructing C-Se and C-O bonds in one step
10.1039_d2qo00406b.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diphenyl diselenide (PhSeSePh) to a radical cation that fragments into a PhSe• radical and PhSe+ cation; the alkyl radical formed after cyclization is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2 (and PhSe+ is reduced back to PhSe- at cathode).
10.1039_d2qo00406b.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh (Eox ≈ 2.0 V, lower than substrate 1a at 2.2 V) to a radical cation that splits into PhSe• and PhSe+; PhSe• adds across the C=C of the olefinic amide to give an alkyl radical, which cyclizes onto the amide oxygen; the resulting radical is oxidized at the anode to a carbocation that deprotonates to product, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode and PhSe+ is reduced back to PhSe- for turnover.

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered alkyl radical D after SePh addition to alkene
benzylic/tertiary carbocation E after radical cyclization and anodic oxidation

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: With 2.0 equiv TEMPO, no selenocyclization product (3a) was obtained — only trace; indicates radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: DPE-SePh adduct detected by GC-MS; 3a still formed in 45% yield, supporting a PhSe• radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak of PhSeSePh (2a) at 2.0 V vs 1a at 2.2 V, indicating diselenide is preferentially oxidized at the anode

10.1039_d2qo00406b.notes: Authors favor anodic oxidation of the diselenide (not the alkene) based on CV. An alternative would be direct oxidation of the olefinic amide to a radical cation followed by PhSe- attack, but CV data argue against this. Acid hydrolysis of imine product 3a gives the isobenzofuranone 4a; ester and 2-vinylbenzyl alcohol substrates also undergo analogous selenocyclization to give furan/lactone products.

10.1039_d2qo01111e.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical β-chlorosulfoxidation of alkenes using thiols and HCl as limiting reagents with water as the oxygen source
10.1039_d2qo01111e.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of thiophenol generates a thiyl radical; subsequent anodic oxidations convert the β-thioether radical to an episulfonium cation and then oxidize the sulfide to a sulfoxide; cathode reduces protons to H2
10.1039_d2qo01111e.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes ArSH to a thiyl radical (in equilibrium with ArSSAr/disulfide radical cation); the thiyl radical adds to the alkene to give a β-thio C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to an episulfonium cation that is captured by chloride to give a β-chloro sulfide; a second anodic SET on sulfur followed by water attack and deprotonation delivers the β-chlorosulfoxide, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: H2 18O isotope labeling
probe_reagents.observation: Sulfoxide O is 95:5 18O/16O, confirming water as the oxygen source

probe_reagents.name: vinylcyclopropane (radical clock, 56)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 57 obtained (10%, 2:1 dr), consistent with a thiyl/carbon-radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: β-hydroxyl sulfoxide 47
probe_reagents.observation: Submission of 47 under standard conditions gave only trace 3, ruling out β-hydroxy sulfoxide as a competent intermediate

probe_reagents.name: sampling/time-course of electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: Disulfide (Int1), β-functionalized sulfides (Int2–5) and β-hydroxy sulfoxide (Int6) accumulate in the first hour; β-chlorosulfoxide 8 forms only afterward, supporting β-functionalized organosulfur species as on-cycle intermediates

10.1039_d2qo01111e.notes: A parallel path b is proposed where the alkene is first oxidized to a radical cation that is then trapped by ArS•/ArSSAr to converge on the episulfonium ion F. The β-hydroxy sulfoxide pathway (via Int6/47) is explicitly excluded by the control experiment, so chloride capture of the episulfonium ion precedes S-oxidation.

10.1039_D4QO01354A_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component selenocyclization of diphenyl diselenide, methacryloyl chloride, and 2-mercaptobenzimidazole to construct selenium-containing benzimidazole-fused thiazinones.
10.1039_D4QO01354A_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation: TBPB acts as a redox mediator/initiator and PhSeSePh is oxidized at the anode to generate PhSe radical; cathode reduces protons/water to H2 (with D2O giving D-incorporation in the product).
10.1039_D4QO01354A_sup_1.electron_flow: TBPB mediates anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh to give PhSe•, which adds to the methacrylamide-type acceptor formed by N-acylation of 2-mercaptobenzimidazole with methacryloyl chloride; the resulting carbon radical cyclizes onto S and is reduced/protonated (D from D2O) while H2 evolves at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: carbon radical from PhSe• addition to methacryloyl chloride
C-centered radical alpha to carbonyl prior to H/D abstraction

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops from 90% to 80% (0.8 equiv) and 54% (1.2 equiv), indicating radical involvement.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield decreases to 70% (0.8 equiv) and 65% (1.2 equiv).

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield decreases to 67% (0.8 equiv) and 60% (1.2 equiv).

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: 96% D-incorporation at the methyl/CH position of product 6-d (95% yield), indicating proton (deuteron) source from water at the late stage.

10.1039_D4QO01354A_sup_1.notes: CV shows an oxidation feature around 1.327 V when 1a (PhSeSePh) is added to TBPB/Cs2CO3/MeCN, supporting anodic oxidation of the diselenide (likely mediated by TBPB). Radical traps only partially suppress the reaction, suggesting a radical chain or partially caged radical pathway rather than free diffusing radicals exclusively.

10.1039_D2QO01111E_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical chlorosulfinylation of styrenes with arenethiols giving β-chloro sulfoxides via radical/radical-polar crossover and in situ sulfide-to-sulfoxide oxidation
10.1039_D2QO01111E_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of arenethiol/thiolate to thiyl radical (and further to disulfide/sulfide oxidation to sulfoxide); thiyl radical adds to styrene to give a β-thio C-radical that is oxidized to a benzylic cation and trapped by chloride
10.1039_D2QO01111E_sup_1.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes ArSH to ArS• (and ArSSAr); ArS• adds to styrene to give a β-thio benzylic radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation and trapped by Cl- to deliver the β-chlorosulfide; subsequent anodic oxidation of the sulfide to the sulfoxide (with O incorporation from H2O) furnishes the β-chloro sulfoxide product.

probe_reagents.name: H2 18O labeling
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3 shows 18O incorporation (O16/O18 = 95:5 from a 90:10 H2 16O/H2 18O feed), indicating the sulfoxide oxygen originates from water

probe_reagents.name: (1-(2-phenylcyclopropyl)vinyl)benzene (radical clock 56)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 57 obtained in 10% yield (dr 2:1), consistent with a carbon-centered radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: time-course sampling with 19F NMR (4-fluorothiophenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Detection and kinetic profiling of Int1-Int6 (disulfide, β-hydrosulfide, β-hydroxylsulfide, β-chlorosulfide, β-acetoxysulfide, β-hydroxylsulfoxide) as transient intermediates during electrolysis

probe_reagents.name: HCl additive control (sulfide → sulfoxide)
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis of a model sulfide gives sulfoxide in 11% without HCl vs 31% with HCl in 1,4-dioxane, indicating HCl promotes the S→S=O oxidation step

10.1039_D2QO01111E_sup_1.notes: 18O labeling confirms water (not O2 or AcOH) as the sulfoxide oxygen source. Time-resolved 19F NMR sampling reveals a cascade: ArSH → ArSSAr → β-thioether → β-functionalized (OH/Cl/OAc) sulfides → β-hydroxysulfoxide → final β-chloro sulfoxide. Radical clock supports a discrete C-radical, while Cl-/OH/OAc trapping points to a benzylic cation (radical-polar crossover). Cyclic voltammetry (Section 6.6) further supports anodic oxidation of the thiol/sulfide.

10.1039_d5qo00289c.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical anti-Markovnikov hydration of aryl-substituted alkenes to primary/secondary alcohols using chloride as a redox mediator and water as the hydrogen/oxygen source
10.1039_d5qo00289c.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of chloride (from TBAC) to Cl2/Cl+, which acts as a mediator that converts the aryl alkene to a chloriranium/chlorohydrin; cathodic reduction of a benzylic radical/cation occurs in the epoxide ring-opening sequence
10.1039_d5qo00289c.electron_flow: Chloride is anodically oxidized to Cl2/Cl+, which adds to the aryl alkene to form a chloriranium that is opened by H2O to chlorohydrin 5; base (K3PO4) closes 5 to epoxide 6, which is then reduced at the cathode in two sequential SET steps via a benzylic radical and carbanion that is protonated by water to deliver the anti-Markovnikov alcohol.
10.1039_d5qo00289c.rate_determining_step: Cathodic reductive ring-opening of the aryl epoxide to the benzylic radical/carbanion (slow conversion of 6 to 2a observed by kinetics)

key_intermediates.name: chloriranium ion (intermediate I) from aryl alkene + Cl+
benzylic radical (intermediate II) from epoxide ring-opening / SET
benzylic carbanion (intermediate III) after second cathodic SET

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 2a fully suppressed (89% 1a recovered); with epoxide 6 + TEMPO, the TEMPO–benzylic radical adduct was detected by HRMS, confirming a benzylic radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Chlorohydrin 5 (as substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Subjected to standard conditions, gave 2a in 66%, confirming 5 as an on-cycle intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Epoxide 6 (as substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Subjected to standard conditions, gave 2a in 82%, confirming 6 as an on-cycle intermediate

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: D-2a obtained in 60% with 96% d-incorporation at the β-position, showing water is the H source

probe_reagents.name: 4-phenyl-1-butene (alkyl-tethered alkene)
probe_reagents.observation: No anti-Markovnikov hydration product 4i; epoxide 4i' isolated in 46%, indicating phenyl conjugation is required for the epoxide ring-opening/reduction step

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry (Cl- vs 1a)
probe_reagents.observation: Cl- oxidation onset 2.02 V vs SCE, below 1a first oxidation at 2.95 V; without Cl-, no 2a formed — confirming Cl- mediator role

10.1039_d5qo00289c.notes: The mechanism is a chloride-mediated paired-electrolysis-like sequence: anodic generation of Cl+/Cl2 to form chlorohydrin/epoxide, followed by cathodic two-electron reductive ring-opening of the aryl epoxide. Kinetics show 6 builds up early then decays into 2a, supporting 6 as the productive intermediate. Phenyl conjugation is essential to stabilize the benzylic radical/anion formed on cathodic ring-opening.

10.1039_d0qo00300j.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical tandem thiocyanation/cyclization of N-allylthioamides with NH4SCN to afford thiocyanato-substituted thiazolines (and thiazines from cyclic substrates)
10.1039_d0qo00300j.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of SCN- to (SCN)2, which acts as an electrophilic thiocyanating mediator toward the alkene; cathode reduces protons to H2
10.1039_d0qo00300j.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes two SCN- to (SCN)2 with loss of 2 e-; (SCN)2 adds to the alkene of the N-allylthioamide to form a cyclic sulfonium intermediate A bearing a pendant SCN; the thioamide sulfur opens A intramolecularly and loss of a proton gives the thiazoline (or thiazine), while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: thiocyanogen (SCN)2 generated by anodic oxidation of SCN-
thiiranium/sulfonium intermediate A from (SCN)2 addition to alkene of N-allylthioamide
thiazoline product 3 (or thiazine 4 from cyclic alkene) after intramolecular nucleophilic ring-opening by thioamide sulfur and deprotonation

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction not inhibited; 3a still formed in 68% yield

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction not inhibited; 3a obtained in 57% yield

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl-substituted allylthioamide 1ag (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3ag obtained in 72% yield with cyclopropyl group intact, excluding a thiyl radical intermediate on the substrate

10.1039_d0qo00300j.notes: CV showed oxidation peaks for 1a at 1.67 V and for NH4SCN at 1.15 V, indicating SCN- is preferentially oxidized at the anode—supporting a (SCN)2-mediator pathway rather than direct substrate oxidation. The lack of TEMPO/BHT inhibition and the retention of the cyclopropyl ring in the radical-clock experiment argue against a free thiyl/N-centered radical pathway on the substrate, favoring the polar (SCN)2/sulfonium mechanism shown in Scheme 4.

10.1039_d1qo01175h.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative three-component cyclization of allylic alcohols, arylboronic acids, and dichalcogenides (RSeSeR/RSSR) to access chalcogenated cyclic boronic esters (1,3,2-dioxaborinanes) that are hydrolyzed to chalcogenated 1,3-diols.
