10.1039_d1qo01175h.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation generates the active chalcogenating species: in pathway I, Br- is oxidized to Br+ (Br2) which reacts with PhSeSePh to give electrophilic PhSeBr; in pathway II, direct anodic SET oxidation of PhSeSePh gives a diselenide radical cation that fragments to PhSe• + PhSe+. Protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
10.1039_d1qo01175h.electron_flow: Allylic alcohol and ArB(OH)2 condense (TFA) to an allyloxy boronate I; in parallel, the anode oxidizes either Br- (to Br+/Br2, which converts PhSeSePh to PhSeBr) or PhSeSePh directly to a radical cation that cleaves to PhSe• + PhSe+. The electrophilic/radical Se species adds to the tethered alkene of I to give a seleniranium (cationic, pathway I) or a C-radical that is anodically oxidized to a cation (pathway II); intramolecular trapping by the pendant B-OH oxygen closes the six-membered cyclic boronate 4a, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

key_intermediates.name: boronate ester I from condensation of cinnamyl alcohol and PhB(OH)2 (allyloxy-arylboronate)
seleniranium/β-selenocarbocation intermediate II on boronate scaffold (pathway I)
carbon-centered β-selenoalkyl radical III on boronate scaffold (pathway II)
β-selenoalkyl cation IV after anodic oxidation of III (pathway II)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a/4a decreased to 20% with 2 equiv TEMPO, indicating radical involvement.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield dropped to 28% with 2 equiv BHT, consistent with radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: p-benzoquinone (BQ)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield dropped to 22% with 2 equiv BQ, supporting a radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: PhSeBr (stoichiometric, no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction of cinnamyl alcohol + PhB(OH)2 + PhSeBr without electricity gave 4a in 78% yield, supporting PhSeBr as the active electrophile in pathway I.

10.1039_d1qo01175h.notes: Two mechanisms are proposed and presented as co-existing: pathway I (Br+/PhSeBr-mediated electrophilic seleniranium, cation-initiated cyclization) is supported by the control experiment with stoichiometric PhSeBr giving 4a in 78% without current. Pathway II (direct anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh to a radical cation, generating PhSe• + PhSe+, radical addition then anodic oxidation to a cation — a radical-polar crossover) is supported by partial inhibition with TEMPO/BHT/BQ. The authors do not unambiguously favor one pathway over the other.

10.1039_D3QO00290J_sup_1.rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-allyl 2-arylbenzimidazoles with CF3SO2Na to access CF3-bearing benzimidazo-fused tetrahydroisoquinolines
10.1039_D3QO00290J_sup_1.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation regenerates the photoexcited eosin Y catalyst (and/or oxidizes the cyclized radical to a cation); CF3SO2Na is oxidized via SET to release CF3 radical after extrusion of SO2
10.1039_D3QO00290J_sup_1.electron_flow: Photoexcited eosin Y oxidizes CF3SO2- to generate a CF3 radical (after SO2 loss), which adds to the terminal allyl alkene of 1a; the resulting carbon radical cyclizes onto the pendant aryl ring, and the cyclohexadienyl radical is further oxidized (anodically and/or via eosin Y+) to a cation, then deprotonated to give 3a. Protons are reduced at the Pt cathode.

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical after CF3 addition to allyl alkene
cyclohexadienyl radical from intramolecular arene attack
Wheland-type cation (after anodic oxidation of cyclized radical)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: 0% yield of 3a; reaction fully suppressed

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3a

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace 3a; CF3-DPE adduct 18 isolated in 36%, confirming CF3 radical

probe_reagents.name: p-dinitrobenzene (pDNB)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% 3a; SET inhibitor shuts down reaction

probe_reagents.name: CuCl2
probe_reagents.observation: Trace 3a

probe_reagents.name: CCl4
probe_reagents.observation: Trace 3a

probe_reagents.name: on/off control: no PC/no light
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction profile shows greatly diminished/no formation of 3a without photocatalyst and light

probe_reagents.name: on/off control: no electricity
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction profile shows greatly diminished/no formation of 3a without electrolysis, confirming synergy of photo- and electrocatalysis

10.1039_D3QO00290J_sup_1.notes: Trapping experiments collectively support a radical pathway initiated by CF3 radical generated via SET oxidation of CF3SO2Na; isolation of CF3-DPE adduct 18 (36%) provides direct evidence for free CF3 radical. SET inhibitor (pDNB) abolishes reactivity. On/off control reactions (no PC/light or no electricity) demonstrate both photochemical and electrochemical components are required, consistent with a photoelectrochemical radical-polar crossover cyclization.

10.1039_d3qo00599b.rxn_class_summary: Direct electrochemical difluorination and azo-fluorination of gem-difluorostyrenes using Et3N·3HF as fluoride source to access perfluoroalkyl aromatics
10.1039_d3qo00599b.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of gem-difluorostyrene to a radical cation, then a second anodic oxidation of the benzyl radical to a benzyl cation; cathode reduces protons to H2
10.1039_d3qo00599b.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the gem-difluorostyrene to a radical cation (I); fluoride from Et3N·3HF attacks C1 to give a benzylic radical (II); a second anodic oxidation furnishes a benzyl cation (III), which is trapped by a second fluoride (or other nucleophile such as azole/alcohol) to deliver the difunctionalized product; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic radical after F- addition at C1 (II)
trifluoromethyl benzylic product after second F- addition (3)

probe_reagents.name: alkyl-substituted gem-difluoroalkene (11, non-conjugated)
probe_reagents.observation: No product 12 formed, indicating a conjugated aryl group is essential to enable substrate oxidation

probe_reagents.name: controlled-potential electrolysis at 1.05 V vs SCE (below oxidation onset)
probe_reagents.observation: No product 3a at 1.05 V; 9% at 1.30 V, supporting oxidation of the gem-difluorostyrene as the initiating step

probe_reagents.name: O2 (radical trapping under O2 atmosphere)
probe_reagents.observation: Hydroperoxide/methoxy adduct 13 (MW 280.27) detected by GC-MS, consistent with a benzyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 4-vinylbiphenyl (non-fluorinated styrene)
probe_reagents.observation: Easily oxidized but no fluorination occurs, indicating the gem-difluoro motif is required for productive F- capture

probe_reagents.name: TMSCN as competing nucleophile
probe_reagents.observation: Fluorocyanation product 8 detected by GC-MS, consistent with cation interception by CN-

10.1039_d3qo00599b.notes: Mechanism additionally supported by selective cathodic hydrodefluorination/deuteration of the benzyl C-F bond of product 3g (giving 9/10 in 83%/81%), demonstrating reversible electrochemical handling of the C-F bond. No DFT or KIE studies reported.

10.1039_d5qo00353a.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [3+2]/[4+2] radical cascade cyclization between N-methacryloylindoles and α-allyl-substituted methylenes to form indole-fused polycyclics using a Cp2Fe/DMAP mediator system.
10.1039_d5qo00353a.electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes a coordinated Cp2Fe(II)-DMAP complex to Fe(III), which then performs single-electron oxidation of the α-allyl methylene carbanion (generated by HFIP-mediated deprotonation, with H2 evolved at the cathode) to a carbon-centered radical; later anodic/Fe(III) oxidation of a cyclohexadienyl radical to a cation enables rearomatization.
10.1039_d5qo00353a.electron_flow: Cathode reduces HFIP to release H2 and generate an HFIP anion that deprotonates the α-allyl methylene to a carbanion (2-1); concurrently the anode oxidizes the Cp2Fe(II)-DMAP complex to Fe(III), which SET-oxidizes 2-1 to a C-radical (2-2). This radical adds to the methacryloyl alkene, undergoes sequential 5-exo-trig and 6-exo-trig cyclizations onto the indole, and the resulting cyclohexadienyl radical is oxidized (by Fe(III) or anode) to a cation that loses H+ to rearomatize and yield the polycycle.
10.1039_d5qo00353a.rate_determining_step: Not the C-H bond cleavage of substrates 1 or 2 (KIE ≈ 0.78 and 1.18 indicate non-RDS C-H steps); RDS not explicitly assigned but consistent with radical addition/cyclization or mediator turnover.

key_intermediates.name: α-allyl methylene carbanion (2-1) from cathodic-driven HFIP deprotonation
Adduct radical 3-1 after first radical addition to N-methacryloylindole alkene
Cyclohexadienyl (indolyl) radical 3-3 after 6-exo-trig cyclization onto indole C2

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed, reaction suppressed (Scheme 5c-1).

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: No desired product; BHT-radical adduct 31 isolated in 65%, supporting a C-radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Product yield dropped to 17%; DPE-trapped adduct 32 isolated in 53%, confirming radical intermediacy.

probe_reagents.name: [D]2-HFIP
probe_reagents.observation: 60% deuterium incorporation at α-position of 2 ([D]-2), indicating rapid reversible C-H cleavage of the α-allyl methylene.

probe_reagents.name: [D/H]-2 (α-deuterated methylene)
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 0.78 (inverse KIE), C-H cleavage of 2 is not rate-determining.

probe_reagents.name: [D/H]-1 (deuterated N-methacryloylindole)
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 1.18, small/negligible KIE — C-H cleavage of 1 not rate-determining.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of Cp2Fe + DMAP mixture
probe_reagents.observation: New oxidation peak at ~0.7 V (between Cp2Fe and DMAP alone) indicates a coordinated Fe(II)-DMAP species is formed and is the species oxidized at the anode.

10.1039_d5qo00353a.notes: Control experiments establish Cp2Fe as the essential mediator (Cu/Mn/Ni salts inactive) and DMAP as critical ligand; replacing DMAP with pyridine or N,N-dimethylaniline severely lowers yield. Reaction tolerant of air and water. Electron-poor indoles (CF3, NO2) unreactive, consistent with the cation/radical addition onto the indole π-system requiring sufficient nucleophilicity. The α-allyl methylene must be disubstituted at the γ-alkene (monosubstituted variants M1-M3 failed), and aryl esters (M4) failed, attributed to instability/steric hindrance of the alkyl radical intermediate.

10.1039_d3qo00290j_si.rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical radical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-allylated 2-arylbenzimidazoles with CF3SO2Na to afford CF3-substituted benzimidazo-fused products
10.1039_d3qo00290j_si.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (C cloth anode, 1.5 mA) coupled with eosin Y photocatalysis under 455 nm to generate CF3 radical from CF3SO2Na; photoexcited eosin Y oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3• (with SO2 extrusion), and the anode regenerates the ground-state photocatalyst
10.1039_d3qo00290j_si.electron_flow: Photoexcited eosin Y oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3• (releasing SO2); CF3• adds to the terminal alkene of 1a giving a C-radical, which cyclizes onto the pendant arene. The resulting cyclohexadienyl radical is oxidized (anode and/or eosin Y•+) to a cation, and deprotonation rearomatizes to product 3a; the anode regenerates eosin Y.

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical from CF3SO2Na (Langlois reagent) after SO2 loss
C-centered alkyl radical from CF3 addition to the allyl alkene of 1a
Cyclized cationic intermediate (radical-polar crossover; aryl-stabilized carbocation/Wheland-type)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: 0% yield of 3a; reaction fully suppressed

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Trace 3a; reaction suppressed

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace 3a; CF3-DPE adduct 18 isolated in 36%, confirming CF3 radical

probe_reagents.name: p-dinitrobenzene (pDNB)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% yield; SET pathway suppressed

probe_reagents.name: CuCl2
probe_reagents.observation: Trace 3a; single-electron pathway implicated

probe_reagents.name: CCl4
probe_reagents.observation: Trace 3a; consistent with SET intermediate

10.1039_d3qo00290j_si.notes: Reaction profiles compare std conditions vs 'no PC, no light' and 'no electricity', supporting that both photocatalysis and electrolysis are required (synergistic photoelectrocatalysis). DPE trapping experiment isolates CF3-DPE adduct 18 (36%), providing direct evidence for a CF3 radical intermediate. Radical-polar crossover (oxidation of cyclohexadienyl radical to cation followed by rearomatization) is invoked for the cyclization step.

10.1002_cssc.202402224.rxn_class_summary: Ferrocene-mediated electrochemical dehydrogenative (3+2) annulation of 1,1-disubstituted alkenes with 1,3-diesters (dimethyl 2-benzylmalonates) to give gamma-butyrolactones
10.1002_cssc.202402224.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of ferrocene (Cp2Fe) to Cp2Fe+ at the carbon felt anode acts as a redox mediator that oxidizes the malonate carbanion to a C-centered radical; cathodic reduction of H2O at the Pt cathode generates OH- (used as base) and H2
10.1002_cssc.202402224.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+ while cathode reduces H2O to OH- + H2; OH- deprotonates the 1,3-diester and Cp2Fe+ performs SET on the resulting carbanion to give a malonyl radical, which adds to the 1,1-diarylalkene to form a benzylic radical; a second SET by Cp2Fe+ generates a benzylic cation that is trapped by hydroxide, and the resulting alcohol lactonizes onto an ester (loss of MeOH) to give the gamma-butyrolactone.

key_intermediates.name: Malonate carbon anion (from deprotonation of benzyl dimethyl malonate by OH-)
Radical A: malonyl C-radical (alpha-carbon radical of benzyl dimethyl malonate after SET oxidation by Cp2Fe+)
Radical B: benzylic radical from addition of A to 1,1-diphenylethylene
Carbocation C: benzylic cation after second SET oxidation of B by Cp2Fe+
Benzyl alcohol intermediate D (after OH- addition to C)
gamma-Butyrolactone product (after intramolecular lactonization with loss of MeOH)

probe_reagents.name: H2 18-O (isotopically labeled water)
probe_reagents.observation: 18O-labeled product 1' isolated in 22% yield (86% 18O incorporation) using MeOH solvent with H2(18O) additive, showing the lactone oxygen originates from water, not from substrate or methanol

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, and Cp2Fe
probe_reagents.observation: No oxidation of 1a or 2a in 0-1.0 V vs Ag/AgCl; Cp2Fe shows reversible wave at ~0.45 V; catalytic current of Cp2Fe increases with added 1a (with base), consistent with electron transfer from the malonate anion to Cp2Fe+

probe_reagents.name: Controlled-potential electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: At 0.3 V vs Ag/AgCl (below Cp2Fe oxidation) only 9% product after 20 h; at 0.6 V (above Cp2Fe oxidation) 80% product in 1.67 h, confirming Cp2Fe-mediated pathway

10.1002_cssc.202402224.notes: X-ray structure (CCDC 2382826) of product 7 confirms gamma-butyrolactone connectivity. The mediator avoids direct anodic oxidation of the malonate, which is proposed to cause overoxidation; mono-substituted electron-rich alkenes give difunctionalized methoxy products instead of lactones (alternative trapping by MeOH on the cation), and benzyl acrylate gives a (4+2) product, indicating the radical-polar crossover step depends on alkene electronics.

10.1002_cssc.201803058.rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical difluoromethylation of acrylamides using CF2HSO2NHNHBoc, giving alkene hydroxydifluoromethylation with secondary N-aryl acrylamides and olefinic C-H difluoromethylation with tertiary acrylamides.
10.1002_cssc.201803058.electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the sulfonyl hydrazide reagent (CF2HSO2NHNHBoc, 1) generates a diazene intermediate that decomposes to release SO2, N2, CO2, tBu• and the key •CF2H radical; a second anodic oxidation converts the carbon radical adduct to a carbocation; cathode evolves H2.
10.1002_cssc.201803058.electron_flow: Anode oxidizes sulfonyl hydrazide 1 (2e−, −2H+) to diazene I, which fragments to •CF2H; the radical adds to the acrylamide β-carbon, the resulting α-carbonyl C-radical is oxidized at the anode to carbocation II/III, which either is trapped by H2O (secondary amides, via aza-oxyallyl cation IV → α-hydroxy amide) or undergoes Z-selective proton elimination (tertiary amides → olefinic C-H difluoromethylation product); protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: diazene from sulfonyl hydrazide oxidation (I, CF2HSO2N=NBoc)
α-carbonyl benzylic C-radical from CF2H addition to acrylamide (I-adduct)
aza-oxyallyl cation IV (equilibrium tautomer of IIIA)

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O (18O-labeled water as cosolvent)
probe_reagents.observation: [18O]-3 obtained in 40% yield with 79% 18O incorporation, confirming the hydroxyl oxygen in the alkene difunctionalization product originates from H2O.

probe_reagents.name: tertiary N,N-diisopropyl acrylamide 31 (substrate probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Under alkene-difunctionalization conditions, tertiary acrylamide 31 gave only C-H functionalization product 25 (63%) with no hydroxylated product, demonstrating that amide N-substitution controls cation fate (H2O addition vs proton elimination).

10.1002_cssc.201803058.notes: Divergence between alkene difunctionalization (secondary N-aryl amides) and C-H functionalization (tertiary amides) is rationalized by equilibrium of α-amido cation IIIA with aza-oxyallyl cation IV, which favors nucleophilic addition of water over proton loss. The Z-selectivity in the C-H functionalization pathway is rationalized by proton elimination via conformer IIIB1 (favored) rather than the sterically encumbered IIIB2. No radical traps (TEMPO/BHT), CV, EPR, or DFT reported.
